methods.name: ansys.aedt.core.modeler.cad.object_3d.Object3d.chamfer
documentation:
Add a chamfer to the selected edges in 3D/vertices in 2D.

# Parameters

vertices

: List of vertices to chamfer.

edges

: List of edges to chamfer.

left_distance

: Left distance from the edge. The default is `1`.

right_distance

: Right distance from the edge. The default is `None`.

angle

: Angle value for chamfer types 2 and 3. The default is `0`.

chamfer_type

: Type of the chamfer. Options are:
  : - 0 - Symmetric
    - 1 - Left Distance-Right Distance
    - 2 - Left Distance-Angle
    - 3 - Right Distance-Angle

  The default is `0`.

# Returns

bool

: `True` when successful, `False` when failed.

# References

```pycon
>>> oEditor.Chamfer
```

Manages object attributes for the AEDT 3D Modeler.

# Parameters

primitives

: Inherited parent object.

name : str

# Examples

Basic usage demonstrated with an HFSS design:

```pycon
>>> from ansys.aedt.core import Hfss
>>> aedtapp = Hfss()
>>> prim = aedtapp.modeler
```

Create a part, such as box, to return an {class}`ansys.aedt.core.modeler.Object3d.Object3d`.

```pycon
>>> id = prim.create_box([0, 0, 0],[10, 10, 5],"Mybox","Copper")
>>> part = prim[id]
```

documentation:

input_list : List
    C# list to convert to a Python list.

Returns
-------
List
    Converted Python list.

methods.name: ansys.aedt.core.modeler.geometry_operators.GeometryOperators.parse_dim_arg
documentation:
Convert a number and unit to a float.
Angles are converted in radians.

# Parameters

string

: String to convert. For example, `"2mm"`. The default is `None`.

scale_to_unit

: Units for the value to convert. For example, `"mm"`.

variable_manager

: Try to parse formula and returns numeric value.
  The default is `None`.

# Returns

float

: Value for the converted value and units. For example, `0.002`.

# Examples

Parse `'"2mm"'`.

```pycon
>>> from ansys.aedt.core.modeler.geometry_operators import GeometryOperators as go
>>> go.parse_dim_arg('2mm')
>>> 0.002
```

Use the optional argument `scale_to_unit` to specify the destination unit.

```pycon
>>> go.parse_dim_arg('2mm', scale_to_unit='mm')
>>> 2.0
```

methods.name: ansys.aedt.core.modeler.geometry_operators.GeometryOperators.distance_vector
documentation:
Evaluate the vector distance between point `p` and a line defined by two points, `a` and `b`.

:::{note}
he formula is `d = (a-p)-((a-p)dot p)n`, where `a` is a point of the line (either `a` or `b`)
and `n` is the unit vector in the direction of the line.
:::

methods.name: ansys.aedt.core.modeler.geometry_operators.GeometryOperators.atan2
documentation:
Implementation of atan2 that does not suffer from the following issues:
math.atan2(0.0, 0.0) = 0.0
math.atan2(-0.0, 0.0) = -0.0
math.atan2(0.0, -0.0) = 3.141592653589793
math.atan2(-0.0, -0.0) = -3.141592653589793
and returns always 0.0.

Parameters
----------
y : float
    Y-axis value for atan2.

x : float
    X-axis value for atan2.

Returns
-------
float

methods.name: ansys.aedt.core.modeler.geometry_operators.GeometryOperators.q_prod
documentation:
Evaluate the product of two quaternions, `p` and `q`, defined as:
p = p0 + p' = p0 + ip1 + jp2 + kp3.
q = q0 + q' = q0 + iq1 + jq2 + kq3.
r = pq = p0q0 - p' • q' + p0q' + q0p' + p' x q'.

# Parameters

p

: List of `[p1, p2, p3, p4]` coordinates for quaternion `p`.

q

: List of `[p1, p2, p3, p4]` coordinates for quaternion `q`.

# Returns

List

: List of [r1, r2, r3, r4] coordinates for the result quaternion.

methods.name: ansys.aedt.core.modeler.geometry_operators.GeometryOperators.q_rotation
documentation:
Evaluate the rotation of a vector, defined by a quaternion.
Evaluated as:
`"q = q0 + q' = q0 + iq1 + jq2 + kq3"`,
`"w = qvq* = (q0^2 - |q'|^2)v + 2(q' • v)q' + 2q0(q' x v)"`.

# Parameters

v

: List of `[v1, v2, v3]` coordinates for the vector.

q

: List of `[q1, q2, q3, q4]` coordinates for the quaternion.

# Returns

List

: List of `[w1, w2, w3]` coordinates for the result vector `w`.

methods.name: ansys.aedt.core.modeler.geometry_operators.GeometryOperators.q_rotation_inv
documentation:
Evaluate the inverse rotation of a vector that is defined by a quaternion.

It can also be the rotation of the coordinate frame with respect to the vector.

> q = q0 + q' = q0 + iq1 + jq2 + kq3
> q\* = q0 - q' = q0 - iq1 - jq2 - kq3
> w = q\*vq

# Parameters

v

: List of `[v1, v2, v3]` coordinates for the vector.

q

: List of `[q1, q2, q3, q4]` coordinates for the quaternion.

# Returns

List

: List of `[w1, w2, w3]` coordinates for the vector.

methods.name: ansys.aedt.core.modeler.geometry_operators.GeometryOperators.get_polygon_centroid
documentation:
Evaluate the centroid of a polygon defined by its points.

pts

# Returns

List

: List of [x,y,z] coordinates for the centroid of the polygon.

methods.name: ansys.aedt.core.modeler.geometry_operators.GeometryOperators.orient_polygon
documentation:
Orient a polygon clockwise or counterclockwise. The vertices should be already ordered either way.
Use this function to change the orientation.
The polygon is represented by its vertices coordinates.

# Parameters

x

: List of x coordinates of the vertices. Length must be >= 1.
  Degenerate polygon with only 2 points is also accepted, in this case the points are returned unchanged.

y

: List of y coordinates of the vertices. Must be of the same length as x.

clockwise

: If `True` the polygon is oriented clockwise, if `False` it is oriented counterclockwise.
  Default is `True`.

# Returns

List of List

: Lists of oriented vertices.

methods.name: ansys.aedt.core.modeler.geometry_operators.GeometryOperators.v_angle_sign
documentation:
Evaluate the signed angle between two geometry vectors.
The sign is evaluated respect to the normal to the plane containing the two vectors as per the following rule.
In case of opposite vectors, it returns an angle equal to 180deg (always positive).
Assuming that the plane normal is normalized (vb == 1), the signed angle is simplified.
For the right-handed rotation from Va to Vb:
\- atan2((va x Vb) . vn, va . vb).
For the left-handed rotation from Va to Vb:
\- atan2((Vb x va) . vn, va . vb).

# Parameters

va

: List of `[x, y, z]` coordinates for the first vector.

vb

: List of `[x, y, z]` coordinates for the second vector.

vn

: List of `[x, y, z]` coordinates for the plane normal.

right_handed

: Whether to consider the right-handed rotation from va to vb. The default is `True`.
  When `False`, left-hand rotation from va to vb is considered.

# Returns

float

: Angle in radians.

methods.name: ansys.aedt.core.modeler.geometry_operators.GeometryOperators.v_angle_sign_2D
documentation:
Evaluate the signed angle between two 2D geometry vectors.
Iit the 2D version of the `GeometryOperators.v_angle_sign` considering vn = [0,0,1].
In case of opposite vectors, it returns an angle equal to 180deg (always positive).

# Parameters

va

: List of `[x, y]` coordinates for the first vector.

vb

: List of `[x, y]` coordinates for the second vector.

right_handed

: Whether to consider the right-handed rotation from Va to Vb. The default is `True`.
  When `False`, left-hand rotation from Va to Vb is considered.

# Returns

float

: Angle in radians.

methods.name: ansys.aedt.core.modeler.geometry_operators.GeometryOperators.point_in_polygon
documentation:
Determine if a point is inside, outside the polygon or at exactly at the border.

The method implements the radial algorithm (<https://es.wikipedia.org/wiki/Algoritmo_radial>)

This version supports also self-intersecting polygons.

point

: List of `[x, y]` coordinates.

polygon

: \[[x1, x2, ..., xn],[y1, y2, ..., yn]\]

tolerance

: tolerance used for the algorithm. Default value is 1e-8.

# Returns

int
: - `-1` When the point is outside the polygon.
  - `0` When the point is exactly on one of the sides of the polygon.
  - `1` When the point is inside the polygon.

methods.name: ansys.aedt.core.modeler.geometry_operators.GeometryOperators.is_point_in_polygon
documentation:
Determine if a point is inside or outside a polygon, both located on the same plane.

The method implements the radial algorithm (<https://es.wikipedia.org/wiki/Algoritmo_radial>)

point

: List of `[x, y]` coordinates.

polygon

: \[[x1, x2, ..., xn],[y1, y2, ..., yn]\]

# Returns

bool

: `True` if the point is inside the polygon or exactly on one of its sides.
  `False` otherwise.

methods.name: ansys.aedt.core.modeler.geometry_operators.GeometryOperators.are_segments_intersecting
documentation:
Determine if the two segments a and b are intersecting.

a1

: First point of segment a. List of `[x, y]` coordinates.

a2

: Second point of segment a. List of `[x, y]` coordinates.

b1

: First point of segment b. List of `[x, y]` coordinates.

b2

: Second point of segment b. List of `[x, y]` coordinates.

include_collinear

: If `True` two segments are considered intersecting also if just one end lies on the other segment.
  Default is `True`.

# Returns

bool

: `True` if the segments are intersecting.
  `False` otherwise.

methods.name: ansys.aedt.core.modeler.geometry_operators.GeometryOperators.is_segment_intersecting_polygon
documentation:
Determine if a segment defined by two points `a` and `b` intersects a polygon.
Points on the vertices and on the polygon boundaries are not considered intersecting.

a

: First point of the segment. List of `[x, y]` coordinates.

b

: Second point of the segment. List of `[x, y]` coordinates.

polygon

: \[[x1, x2, ..., xn],[y1, y2, ..., yn]\]

# Returns

float

: `True` if the segment intersect the polygon. `False` otherwise.

methods.name: ansys.aedt.core.modeler.geometry_operators.GeometryOperators.is_perpendicular

documentation:

methods.name: ansys.aedt.core.modeler.geometry_operators.GeometryOperators.point_segment_distance
documentation:
Calculate the distance between a point `p` and a segment defined by two points `a` and `b`.

# Parameters

p

: List of `[x, y, z]` coordinates for the reference point `p`.

a

: List of `[x, y, z]` coordinates for the first point of the segment.

methods.name: ansys.aedt.core.modeler.geometry_operators.GeometryOperators.find_largest_rectangle_inside_polygon
documentation:
Find the largest area rectangles of arbitrary orientation in a polygon.

Implements the algorithm described by Rubén Molano, et al.
*"Finding the largest area rectangle of arbitrary orientation in a closed contour"*, published in
*Applied Mathematics and Computation*.
<https://doi.org/10.1016/j.amc.2012.03.063>.
(<https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S0096300312003207>)

# Parameters

polygon

: \[[x1, x2, ..., xn],[y1, y2, ..., yn]\]

partition_max_order

: Order of the lattice partition used to find the quasi-lattice polygon that approximates `polygon`.
  Default is `16`.

# Returns

List of List

: List containing the rectangles points. Return all rectangles found.
  List is in the form: \[\[[x1, y1],[x2, y2],...\],\[[x1, y1],[x2, y2],...\],...\].

methods.name: ansys.aedt.core.modeler.geometry_operators.GeometryOperators.find_closest_points
documentation:
Given a list of points, finds the closest points to a reference point.
It returns a list of points because more than one can be found.
It works with 2D or 3D points. The tolerance used to evaluate the distance
to the reference point can be specified.

# Parameters

points_list

: List of points. The points can be defined in 2D or 3D space.

reference_point

: The reference point. The point can be defined in 2D or 3D space (same as points_list).

tol

: The tolerance used to evaluate the distance. Default is `1e-6`.

# Returns

List of List

methods.name: ansys.aedt.core.modeler.geometry_operators.GeometryOperators.mirror_point
documentation:
Mirror point about a plane defining by a point on the plane and a normal point.

Parameters
----------
start : list
    Point to be mirrored
reference : list
    The reference point. Point on the plane around which you want to mirror the object.
vector : list
    Normalized vector used for the mirroring.

Returns
-------
List
    List of the reflected point.
Manages geometry operators.

methods.name: ansys.aedt.core.modeler.modeler_pcb.Modeler3DLayout.colinear_heal
documentation:
Remove small edges of one or more primitives.

# Parameters

assignment

: One or more primitives to heal.

tolerance

: Tolerance value. The default is `0.1`.

# Returns

bool

: `True` when successful, `False` when failed.

# References

```pycon
>>> oEditor.Heal
```

# Examples

```pycon
>>> from ansys.aedt.core import Hfss3dLayout
>>> h3d=Hfss3dLayout(version="2021.2")
>>> h3d.modeler.layers.add_layer("TOP")
>>> l1=h3d.modeler.create_line("TOP",[[0,0],[100,0]],0.5)
>>> l2=h3d.modeler.create_line("TOP",[[100,0],[120,-35]],0.5)
>>> h3d.modeler.unite([l1,l2])
>>> h3d.modeler.colinear_heal("poly_2",0.25)
True
```

methods.name: ansys.aedt.core.modeler.modeler_pcb.Modeler3DLayout.expand
documentation:
Expand the object by a specific size.

# Parameters

assignment

: Name of the object.

size

: Size of the expansion. The default is `1`.

expand_type

: Type of the expansion. Options are `"ROUND"`, `"MITER"`, and
  `"CORNER"`. The default is `"ROUND"`.

replace_original

: Whether to replace the original object. The default is `False`, in which case
  a new object is created.

# Returns

str

: Name of the object.

# References

```pycon
>>> oEditor.Expand
```

# Examples

```pycon
>>> from ansys.aedt.core import Hfss3dLayout
>>> h3d=Hfss3dLayout(version="2021.2")
>>> h3d.modeler.layers.add_layer("TOP")
>>> h3d.modeler.create_rectangle("TOP", [20,20],[50,50], name="rect_1")
>>> h3d.modeler.create_line("TOP",[[25,25],[40,40]])
>>> out1 = h3d.modeler.expand("line_3")
>>> print(out1)
line_4
```

model_name

: Name of the model. The default is `None`, in which case the model name is the file name without an
  extension.

subcircuit_name

: Name of the subcircuit. The default is `None`, in which case the subcircuit name is the model name.

  AEDT pins.

```pycon
>>> from ansys.aedt.core import Hfss3dLayout
```

functions.name: ansys.aedt.core.modeler.advanced_cad.stackup_3d._replace_by_underscore
documentation:
Replace each character of a string by underscores.
This method is used to create Hfss variable relative to a material,
and so reformat the material name into the variable name format.

# Parameters

character

: The character to replace by underscore.

string

: The string where the replacement is made.

# Examples

```pycon
>>> from ansys.aedt.core.modeler.advanced_cad.stackup_3d import _replace_by_underscore
>>> name = "Duroid (tm)"
>>> name = _replace_by_underscore(" ", name)
>>> name = _replace_by_underscore("(", name)
>>> name = _replace_by_underscore(")", name)
```

Cast PyAEDT variable object to simplify getters and setters in Stackup3D.

# Parameters

application

: HFSS design or project where the variable is to be created.

name

: The name of the variable. If the name begins with an '\$', the variable will be a project variable.
  Otherwise, it will be a design variable.

expression

: Expression of the value.

# Examples

```pycon
>>> from ansys.aedt.core import Hfss
>>> from ansys.aedt.core.modeler.advanced_cad.stackup_3d import NamedVariable
>>> hfss = Hfss()
>>> my_frequency = NamedVariable(hfss, "my_frequency", "900000Hz")
>>> wave_length_formula = "c0/" + my_frequency.name
>>> my_wave_length = NamedVariable(hfss, "my_wave_length", wave_length_formula)
>>> my_permittivity = NamedVariable(hfss, "my_permittivity", "2.2")
>>> my_wave_length.expression = my_wave_length.expression + "/" + my_permittivity.name
```

classes.name: ansys.aedt.core.modeler.advanced_cad.stackup_3d.DuplicatedParametrizedMaterial
documentation:
Provides a class to duplicate a material and manage its duplication in PyAEDT and in AEDT.
For each material property a NamedVariable is created as attribute.

# Parameters

application

: HFSS design or project where the variable is to be created.

material_name

: The material name which will be cloned.

cloned_material_name

: The cloned material named

list_of_properties

: Currently unavailable, but this parameter could be used to select the properties which needs to be parametrized.
  Currently, the permittivity, permeability, conductivity, dielectric loss tangent and the magnetic loss tangent

  > are parametrized with a NamedVariable.

# Examples

```pycon
>>> from ansys.aedt.core import Hfss
>>> from ansys.aedt.core.modeler.advanced_cad.stackup_3d import DuplicatedParametrizedMaterial
>>> hfss = Hfss()
>>> my_copper = DuplicatedParametrizedMaterial(hfss, "copper", "my_copper")
>>> my_material_name = my_copper.material_name
>>> my_material = my_copper.material
>>> my_copper_conductivity = my_copper.conductivity
```

methods.name: ansys.aedt.core.modeler.advanced_cad.stackup_3d.Layer3D.add_patch
documentation:
Create a parametric patch.

# Parameters

frequency

: Frequency value for the patch calculation in Hz.

patch_width

: Patch width.

patch_length

: Patch length. The default is `None`.

patch_position_x

: Patch start x position.

patch_position_y

: Patch start y position. The default is `0.`

patch_name

: Patch name. The default is `None`.

axis

: Line orientation axis. The default is `"X"`.

# Returns

{class}`ansys.aedt.core.modeler.advanced_cad.stackup_3d.Patch`

# Examples

```pycon
>>> from ansys.aedt.core import Hfss
>>> from ansys.aedt.core.modeler.advanced_cad.stackup_3d import Stackup3D
>>> hfss = Hfss()
>>> my_stackup = Stackup3D(hfss, 2.5e9)
>>> gnd = my_stackup.add_ground_layer("gnd")
>>> my_stackup.add_dielectric_layer("diel1", thickness=1.5, material="Duroid (tm)")
>>> top = my_stackup.add_signal_layer("top")
>>> my_patch = top.add_patch(frequency=None, patch_width=51, patch_name="MLPatch")
>>> my_stackup.resize_around_element(my_patch)
```

methods.name: ansys.aedt.core.modeler.advanced_cad.stackup_3d.Layer3D.ml_patch
documentation:
Create a new parametric patch using machine learning algorithm rather than analytic formulas.

# Parameters

frequency

: Frequency value for patch calculation in Hz.

patch_width

: Patch width.

patch_position_x

: Patch start x position.

patch_position_y

: Patch start y position.

patch_name

: Patch name.

axis

: Line orientation axis.

# Returns

{class}`ansys.aedt.core.modeler.advanced_cad.stackup_3d.MachineLearningPatch`

# Examples

```pycon
>>> from ansys.aedt.core import Hfss
>>> from ansys.aedt.core.modeler.advanced_cad.stackup_3d import Stackup3D
>>> hfss = Hfss()
>>> my_stackup = Stackup3D(hfss, 2.5e9)
>>> gnd = my_stackup.add_ground_layer("gnd")
>>> my_stackup.add_dielectric_layer("diel1", thickness=1.5, material="Duroid (tm)")
>>> top = my_stackup.add_signal_layer("top")
>>> my_patch = top.ml_patch(frequency=None, patch_width=51, patch_name="MLPatch")
>>> my_stackup.resize_around_element(my_patch)
```

methods.name: ansys.aedt.core.modeler.advanced_cad.stackup_3d.Layer3D.add_trace
documentation:
Create a trace.

# Parameters

line_width

: Line width. It can be the physical width or the line impedance.

line_length

: Line length. It can be the physical length or the electrical length in degrees.

is_electrical_length

: Whether the line length is an electrical length or a physical length. The default
  is `False`, which means it is a physical length.

is_impedance

: Whether the line width is an impedance. The default is `False`, in which case
  the line width is a geometrical value.

line_position_x

: Line center start x position. The default is `0`.

line_position_y

: Line center start y position. The default is `0`.

line_name

: Line name. The default is `None`.

axis

: Line orientation axis. The default is `"X"`.

reference_system

: Line reference system. The default is `None`, in which case a new coordinate
  system is created.

frequency

: Frequency value for the line calculation in Hz. The default is `1e9`.

# Returns

{class}`ansys.aedt.core.modeler.advanced_cad.stackup_3d.Trace`

# Examples

```pycon
>>> from ansys.aedt.core import Hfss
>>> from ansys.aedt.core.modeler.advanced_cad.stackup_3d import Stackup3D
>>> hfss = Hfss(new_desktop=True)
>>> my_stackup = Stackup3D(hfss, 2.5e9)
>>> gnd = my_stackup.add_ground_layer("gnd")
>>> my_stackup.add_dielectric_layer("diel1", thickness=1.5, material="Duroid (tm)")
>>> top = my_stackup.add_signal_layer("top")
>>> my_trace = top.add_trace(line_width=2.5, line_length=22)
>>> my_stackup.resize_around_element(my_trace)
```

methods.name: ansys.aedt.core.modeler.advanced_cad.stackup_3d.Layer3D.add_polygon
documentation:
Create a polygon.

# Parameters

points

: Points list of [x,y] coordinates.

material

: Material name. The default is `"copper"`.

is_void

: Whether the polygon is a void. The default is `False`.
  On ground layers, it will act opposite of the Boolean value because the ground
  is negative.

poly_name

: Polygon name. The default is `None`.

# Returns

{class}`ansys.aedt.core.modeler.advanced_cad.stackup_3d.Polygon`

# Examples

```pycon
>>> from ansys.aedt.core import Hfss
>>> from ansys.aedt.core.modeler.advanced_cad.stackup_3d import Stackup3D
>>> hfss = Hfss()
>>> my_stackup = Stackup3D(hfss, 2.5e9)
>>> gnd = my_stackup.add_ground_layer("gnd")
>>> my_stackup.add_dielectric_layer("diel1", thickness=1.5, material="Duroid (tm)")
>>> top = my_stackup.add_signal_layer("top")
>>> my_polygon = top.add_polygon([[0, 0], [0, 1], [1, 1], [1, 0]])
>>> my_stackup.dielectric_x_position = "2mm"
>>> my_stackup.dielectric_y_position = "2mm"
>>> my_stackup.dielectric_length = "-3mm"
>>> my_stackup.dielectric_width = "-3mm"
```

Provides a class for a management of a parametric layer in 3D Modeler. The Layer3D class is not intended
to be used with its constructor, but by using the method "add_layer" available in the Stackup3D class.

# Parameters

stackup

: The stackup where the layers will be added.

app

: HFSS design or project where the variable is to be created.

name

: Name of the layer.

layer_type

: "S" for signal layers, "D" for dielectric layers, "G" for ground layers.

material_name

: The material name of the layer.

thickness

: The thickness of the layer.

fill_material

: In ground and signal layers, the dielectric material name which will fill the non-conductive areas of the layer.

index

: The number of the layer, starting from bottom to top.

frequency

: The layer frequency, it will be common to all geometric shapes on the layer.

# Examples

```pycon
>>> from ansys.aedt.core import Hfss
>>> from ansys.aedt.core.modeler.advanced_cad.stackup_3d import Stackup3D
>>> hfss = Hfss()
>>> my_stackup = Stackup3D(hfss, 2.5e9)
>>> my_layer = my_stackup.add_layer("my_layer")
>>> gnd = my_stackup.add_ground_layer("gnd")
>>> diel = my_stackup.add_dielectric_layer("diel1", thickness=1.5, material="Duroid (tm)")
>>> top = my_stackup.add_signal_layer("top")
```

# Parameters

# Returns

{class}`ansys.aedt.core.modeler.advanced_cad.stackup_3d.Padstack`

methods.name: ansys.aedt.core.modeler.advanced_cad.stackup_3d.Stackup3D.add_layer
documentation:
Add a new layer to the stackup.
The new layer can be a signal (S), ground (G), or dielectric (D).
The layer is entirely filled with the specified fill material. Anything will be drawn
material.

# Parameters

name

: Layer name.

layer_type

: Layer type. The default is `"S"`. Options are:

  > - `"D"` for "dielectric" layer
  > - `"G"` for "ground" layer
  > - `"S"` for "signal" layer

material_name

: Material name. The default is `"copper"`. The material is parametrized.

thickness

: Thickness value. The default is `0.035`. The thickness will be parametrized.

fill_material

: Fill material name. The default is `"FR4_epoxy"`. The fill material will be
  parametrized. This parameter is not valid for dielectrics.

frequency

: The layer frequency, it will be common to all geometric shapes on the layer. The default is None, so each
  shape must have their own frequency.

# Returns

{class}`ansys.aedt.core.modeler.advanced_cad.stackup_3d.Layer3D`

: Layer object.

# Examples

```pycon
>>> from ansys.aedt.core import Hfss
>>> from ansys.aedt.core.modeler.advanced_cad.stackup_3d import Stackup3D
>>> hfss = Hfss()
>>> my_stackup = Stackup3D(hfss, 2.5e9)
>>> my_layer = my_stackup.add_layer("my_layer")
```

methods.name: ansys.aedt.core.modeler.advanced_cad.stackup_3d.Stackup3D.add_signal_layer
documentation:
Add a new ground layer to the stackup.
A signal layer is positive. The layer is entirely filled with the fill material.
Anything will be drawn material.

# Parameters

name

: Layer name.

material

: Material name. Material will be parametrized.

thickness

: Thickness value. Thickness will be parametrized.

fill_material

: Fill Material name. Material will be parametrized.=

material

: Material name. Material will be parametrized. Default value is `"copper"`.

thickness

: Thickness value. Thickness will be parametrized. Default value is `0.035`.

fill_material

: Fill material name. Material will be parametrized. Default value is `"FR4_epoxy"`.

frequency

: The layer frequency, it will be common to all geometric shapes on the layer. The default is None, so each
  shape must have their own frequency.

# Returns

{class}`ansys.aedt.core.modeler.advanced_cad.stackup_3d.Layer3D`

: Layer object.

# Examples

```pycon
>>> from ansys.aedt.core import Hfss
>>> from ansys.aedt.core.modeler.advanced_cad.stackup_3d import Stackup3D
>>> hfss = Hfss()
>>> my_stackup = Stackup3D(hfss, 2.5e9)
>>> my_signal_layer = my_stackup.add_signal_layer("signal_layer")
```

methods.name: ansys.aedt.core.modeler.advanced_cad.stackup_3d.Stackup3D.add_dielectric_layer
documentation:
Add a new dielectric layer to the stackup.

# Parameters

name

: Layer name.

material

: Material name. The default is `"FR4_epoxy"`. The material will be parametrized.

thickness

: Thickness value. The default is `0.035`. The thickness will be parametrized.

frequency

: The layer frequency, it will be common to all geometric shapes on the layer. The default is None, so each
  shape must have their own frequency.

# Returns

{class}`ansys.aedt.core.modeler.advanced_cad.stackup_3d.Layer3D`

: Layer object.

# Examples

```pycon
>>> from ansys.aedt.core import Hfss
>>> from ansys.aedt.core.modeler.advanced_cad.stackup_3d import Stackup3D
>>> hfss = Hfss()
>>> my_stackup = Stackup3D(hfss, 2.5e9)
>>> my_dielectric_layer = my_stackup.add_dielectric_layer("diel", thickness=1.5, material="Duroid (tm)")
```

methods.name: ansys.aedt.core.modeler.advanced_cad.stackup_3d.Stackup3D.add_ground_layer
documentation:
Add a new ground layer to the stackup. A ground layer is negative.
The layer is entirely filled with metal. Any polygon will draw a void in it.

# Parameters

name

: Layer name.

material

: Material name. Material will be parametrized.

thickness

: Thickness value. Thickness will be parametrized.

fill_material

: Fill Material name. Material will be parametrized.

frequency

: The layer frequency, it will be common to all geometric shapes on the layer. The default is None, so each
  shape must have their own frequency.

# Returns

{class}`ansys.aedt.core.modeler.advanced_cad.stackup_3d.Layer3D`

: Layer Object.

# Examples

```pycon
>>> from ansys.aedt.core import Hfss
>>> from ansys.aedt.core.modeler.advanced_cad.stackup_3d import Stackup3D
>>> hfss = Hfss()
>>> my_stackup = Stackup3D(hfss, 2.5e9)
>>> my_ground_layer = my_stackup.add_ground_layer("gnd")
```

documentation:
Resize the stackup around objects created by a percentage offset.

Parameters
----------
percentage_offset : float
    Offset of resize. The value must be greater than 0.

Returns
-------
bool

methods.name: ansys.aedt.core.modeler.advanced_cad.stackup_3d.Stackup3D.resize_around_element
documentation:
Resize the stackup around parametrized objects and make it parametrize.

element

methods.name: ansys.aedt.core.modeler.advanced_cad.stackup_3d.Patch.create_probe_port
documentation:
Create a coaxial probe port for the patch.

# Parameters

reference_layer

: Reference layer (ground).

rel_x_offset

: Relative x-offset for probe feed.
  Provide a value between 0.0 and 1.0.
  Offset in the x-direction relative to the center of the patch.
  `0` places the probe at the center of the patch.
  `1` places the probe at the edge of the patch.
  Default: 0

rel_y_offset

: `0` places the probe at the center of the patch.
  `1` places the probe at the edge of the patch.
  Default: 0

d

: Default: 0.01

name

: Default value `"Probe"`

# Returns

bool

: `True` when successful, `False` when failed.

# Examples

```pycon
>>> from ansys.aedt.core import Hfss
>>> from ansys.aedt.core.modeler.advanced_cad.stackup_3d import Stackup3D
>>> hfss = Hfss()
>>> my_stackup = Stackup3D(hfss, 2.5e9)
>>> gnd = my_stackup.add_ground_layer("gnd")
>>> my_stackup.add_dielectric_layer("diel1", thickness=1.5, material="Duroid (tm)")
>>> top = my_stackup.add_signal_layer("top")
>>> my_patch = top.add_patch(frequency=None, patch_width=51, patch_name="MLPatch")
>>> my_stackup.resize_around_element(my_patch)
>>> my_patch.create_probe_port(gnd)
```

methods.name: ansys.aedt.core.modeler.advanced_cad.stackup_3d.Patch.create_lumped_port
documentation:
Create a parametrized lumped port.

# Parameters

opposite_side

axisdir

: Position of the port. It should be one of the values for `Application.AxisDir`,

methods.name: ansys.aedt.core.modeler.advanced_cad.stackup_3d.Patch.quarter_wave_feeding_line
documentation:
Create a Trace to feed the patch. The trace length is the quarter wavelength, and this width is calculated
to return the desired impedance.

# Parameters

impedance_to_adapt

: Impedance the feeding line must return. By default 50 Ohms.

# Returns

{class}`ansys.aedt.core.modeler.advanced_cad.stackup_3d.Trace`

# Examples

```pycon
>>> from ansys.aedt.core import Hfss
>>> from ansys.aedt.core.modeler.advanced_cad.stackup_3d import Stackup3D
>>> hfss = Hfss()
>>> my_stackup = Stackup3D(hfss, 2.5e9)
>>> gnd = my_stackup.add_ground_layer("gnd")
>>> my_stackup.add_dielectric_layer("diel1", thickness=1.5, material="Duroid (tm)")
>>> top = my_stackup.add_signal_layer("top")
>>> my_patch = top.add_patch(frequency=None, patch_width=51, patch_name="MLPatch")
>>> my_stackup.resize_around_element(my_patch)
>>> my_feeding_line = my_patch.quarter_wave_feeding_line()
>>> my_stackup.dielectric_x_position.expression = my_stackup.dielectric_x_position.expression +
>>> " - " + my_feeding_line.length.name
>>> my_stackup.dielectric_length.expression = my_stackup.dielectric_length.expression +
>>> " + " + my_feeding_line.length.name
```

methods.name: ansys.aedt.core.modeler.advanced_cad.stackup_3d.Patch.set_optimal_width
documentation:
Set the expression of the NamedVariable corresponding to the patch width, to an optimal expression.

# Examples

```pycon
>>> from ansys.aedt.core import Hfss
>>> from ansys.aedt.core.modeler.advanced_cad.stackup_3d import Stackup3D
>>> hfss = Hfss()
>>> my_stackup = Stackup3D(hfss, 2.5e9)
>>> gnd = my_stackup.add_ground_layer("gnd")
>>> my_stackup.add_dielectric_layer("diel1", thickness=1.5, material="Duroid (tm)")
>>> top = my_stackup.add_signal_layer("top")
>>> my_patch = top.add_patch(frequency=None, patch_width=51, patch_name="MLPatch")
>>> my_stackup.resize_around_element(my_patch)
>>> my_patch.set_optimal_width()
```

Patch Class in Stackup3D. Create a parametrized patch. It is preferable to use the add_patch method
in the class Layer3D than directly the class constructor.

# Parameters

application

: HFSS design or project where the variable is to be created.

frequency

: Target resonant frequency for the patch antenna. The default is `None`,
  in which case the patch frequency is that of the
  layer or of the stackup.

dx

: The patch width.

signal_layer

: The signal layer where the patch will be drawn.

dielectric_layer

: The dielectric layer between the patch and the ground layer. Its permittivity and thickness are used in
  prediction formulas.

dy

: The patch length. By default, it is None and so the length is calculated by prediction formulas.

patch_position_x

: Patch x position, by default it is 0.

patch_position_y

: Patch y position, by default it is 0.

patch_name

: Patch name, by default "patch".

reference_system

: Coordinate system of the patch. By default, None.

axis

: Patch length axis, by default "X".

# Examples

```pycon
>>> from ansys.aedt.core import Hfss
>>> from ansys.aedt.core.modeler.advanced_cad.stackup_3d import Stackup3D
>>> hfss = Hfss()
>>> stackup = Stackup3D(hfss)
>>> gnd = stackup.add_ground_layer("ground", material="copper", thickness=0.035, fill_material="air")
>>> dielectric = stackup.add_dielectric_layer("dielectric", thickness="0.5" + length_units, material="Duroid (tm)")
>>> signal = stackup.add_signal_layer("signal", material="copper", thickness=0.035, fill_material="air")
>>> patch = signal.add_patch(patch_length=9.57, patch_width=9.25, patch_name="Patch")
>>> stackup.resize_around_element(patch)
>>> pad_length = [3, 3, 3, 3, 3, 3]  # Air bounding box buffer in mm.
>>> region = hfss.modeler.create_region(pad_length,is_percentage=False)
>>> hfss.assign_radiation_boundary_to_objects(region)
>>> patch.create_probe_port(gnd, rel_x_offset=0.485)
```

methods.name: ansys.aedt.core.modeler.advanced_cad.stackup_3d.Trace.create_lumped_port
documentation:
Create a parametrized lumped port.

# Parameters

reference_layer

: Reference layer, which is the ground layer in most cases.

opposite_side

: Change the side where the port is created.

port_name

: Name of the lumped port.

axisdir

: Position of the port. It should be one of the values for `Application.AxisDir`,
  which are: `XNeg`, `YNeg`, `ZNeg`, `XPos`, `YPos`, and `ZPos`.
  The default is `Application.AxisDir.XNeg`.

# Returns

bool

: `True` when successful, `False` when failed.
  `True` when successful, `False` when failed.

# Examples

```pycon
>>> from ansys.aedt.core import Hfss
>>> from ansys.aedt.core.modeler.advanced_cad.stackup_3d import Stackup3D
>>> hfss = Hfss(new_desktop=True)
>>> my_stackup = Stackup3D(hfss, 2.5e9)
>>> gnd = my_stackup.add_ground_layer("gnd")
>>> my_stackup.add_dielectric_layer("diel1", thickness=1.5, material="Duroid (tm)")
>>> top = my_stackup.add_signal_layer("top")
>>> my_trace = top.add_trace(line_width=2.5, line_length=90, is_electrical_length=True)
>>> my_stackup.resize_around_element(my_trace)
>>> my_trace.create_lumped_port(gnd)
>>> my_trace.create_lumped_port(gnd, opposite_side=True)
```

Trace Class in Stackup3D. Create a parametrized trace. It is preferable to use the add_trace method
in the class Layer3D than directly the class constructor.

# Parameters

application

: HFSS design or project where the variable is to be created.

frequency

: The line frequency, it is used in prediction formulas. If it is None, the line frequency will be that of the
  layer or of the stackup.

line_width

: The line width. If it is None, it will calculate it from characteristic impedance of the line.

line_impedance

: The characteristic impedance of the line. If a line width is entered by the user, the characteristic impedance
  will be calculated from it.

signal_layer

: The signal layer where the line will be drawn.

dielectric_layer

: The dielectric layer between the line and the ground layer. Its permittivity and thickness are used in
  prediction formulas.

line_electrical_length

: The ratio between the line length and the wavelength in degree. By default 90 which is corresponding
  to the quarter of the wavelength. If it is None, it will be directly calculated from the line length entered
  by the user.

line_length

: The line length. By default, it is None and so the length is calculated by prediction formulas according to the
  electrical length.

line_position_x

: Line x position, by default it is 0.

line_position_y

: Line y position, by default it is 0.

line_name

: Line name, by default "line".

reference_system

: Coordinate system of the line. By default, None.

axis

: Line length axis, by default "X".

# Examples

```pycon
>>> from ansys.aedt.core import Hfss
>>> from ansys.aedt.core.modeler.advanced_cad.stackup_3d import Stackup3D
>>> hfss = Hfss(new_desktop=True)
>>> my_stackup = Stackup3D(hfss, 2.5e9)
>>> gnd = my_stackup.add_ground_layer("gnd")
>>> my_stackup.add_dielectric_layer("diel1", thickness=1.5, material="Duroid (tm)")
>>> top = my_stackup.add_signal_layer("top")
>>> my_trace = top.add_trace(line_width=2.5, line_length=22)
>>> my_stackup.resize_around_element(my_trace)
```

classes.name: ansys.aedt.core.modeler.advanced_cad.stackup_3d.Polygon

methods.name: ansys.aedt.core.modeler.advanced_cad.stackup_3d.Polygon.points_on_layer
documentation:
Object Bounding Box.

Returns
-------
List
    List of [x,y] coordinate of bounding box.

Polygon Class in Stackup3D. It is preferable to use the add_polygon method in the class Layer3D than directly
the class constructor.

# Parameters

application

: HFSS design or project where the variable is to be created.

point_list

: Points list of [x,y] coordinates.

signal_layer

: The signal layer where the line will be drawn.

poly_name

: Polygon name. The default is `poly`.

mat_name

: The polygon material name.

is_void

: Whether the polygon is a void. The default is `False`.
  On ground layers, it will act opposite of the Boolean value because the ground
  is negative.

reference_system

: Coordinate system of the polygon. By default, None.

# Examples

```pycon
>>> from ansys.aedt.core import Hfss
>>> from ansys.aedt.core.modeler.advanced_cad.stackup_3d import Stackup3D
>>> hfss = Hfss(new_desktop=True)
>>> my_stackup = Stackup3D(hfss, 2.5e9)
>>> gnd = my_stackup.add_ground_layer("gnd", thickness=None)
>>> my_stackup.add_dielectric_layer("diel1", thickness=1.5, material="Duroid (tm)")
>>> top = my_stackup.add_signal_layer("top", thickness=None)
>>> my_polygon = top.add_polygon([[0, 0], [0, 1], [1, 1], [1, 0]])
>>> my_stackup.dielectric_x_position = "2mm"
>>> my_stackup.dielectric_y_position = "2mm"
>>> my_stackup.dielectric_length = "-3mm"
>>> my_stackup.dielectric_width = "-3mm"
```

classes.name: ansys.aedt.core.modeler.advanced_cad.stackup_3d.MachineLearningPatch
documentation:
MachineLearningPatch Class in Stackup3D. Create an antenna whose length is predicted by a
machine learning algorithm.

The machine learning algorithm determines the length according to resonant frequency,
patch width, substrat thickness and relative permittivity. The other parameter have no or only a minor influence.
We can consider that the patch thickness has no influence as long as it is lower than 50 um,
for machine learning training is set to 35 um. The patch conductivity and other dielectric properties are
respectively those of copper and duroid (tm) for the machine learning training, but predictions work
regardless of the dielectric or the conductor. The predictions are, in the most of cases, better than the
predictions with formula used in the class Patch. The machine learning model used, is Support Vector Regression,
it is a classic model in the non-linear prediction, it can be used for other non-linear application.
Two models were created, one from 0.1 GHz to 1 GHz and another from 1 GHz to 10 GHz. The example of the creation of
these models is available in a PyAEDT example named Machine_learning_applied_to_Patch. The two databases and models
are available in PyAEDT in misc.

It is preferable to use the ml_patch method in the class Layer3D than directly the class constructor.

# Parameters

application

: HFSS design or project where the variable is to be created.

frequency

: The patch frequency, it is used in prediction formulas. If it is None, the patch frequency will be that of the
  layer or of the stackup. From 0.1 to 10 GHz.

dx

: The patch width. From O.5 to 1.5 of the optimal width value : c0 * 1000/(2 * f * sqrt((er + 1)/2))

signal_layer

: The signal layer where the patch will be drawn.

dielectric_layer

: The dielectric layer between the patch and the ground layer. Its permittivity and thickness are used in
  prediction formulas. Thickness must be from 0.003 to 0.05 of the wavelength in vacuum and relative permittivity
  from 1 to 12.

patch_position_x

: Patch x position, by default it is 0.

patch_position_y

: Patch y position, by default it is 0.

patch_name

: Patch name, by default "patch".

reference_system

: Coordinate system of the patch. By default, None.

axis

: Patch length axis, by default `"X"`.

# Examples

```pycon
>>> from ansys.aedt.core import Hfss
>>> from ansys.aedt.core.modeler.advanced_cad.stackup_3d import Stackup3D
>>> hfss = Hfss()
>>> my_stackup = Stackup3D(hfss, 2.5e9)
>>> gnd = my_stackup.add_ground_layer("gnd")
>>> my_stackup.add_dielectric_layer("diel1", thickness=1.5, material="Duroid (tm)")
>>> top = my_stackup.add_signal_layer("top")
>>> my_patch = top.ml_patch(frequency=None, patch_width=51, patch_name="MLPatch")
>>> my_stackup.resize_around_element(my_patch)
```

documentation:
Retrieve the list of objects that belongs to a specific layer.

Parameters

object_filter : str, list, optional
    Name of the category to include in search. Options are `"poly"`, `"circle"`,
    `"rect"`,`"line"`,`"arc"`, `"via"`,`"pin"` and `"component"`.
include_voids : bool, optional
    Either if include or not the voids in search.

Returns
-------
list

include_voids : bool, optional
    Either if include or not the voids in search.

list

documentation:
Create a `Padstack` object that can be used to create a padstack.

# Parameters

name

: Name of the padstack. The default is `"Padstack"`.

# Returns

{class}`ansys.aedt.core.modeler.cad.object_3dlayout.Padstack`

: Padstack object if a padstack with this name does not already exist.

methods.name: ansys.aedt.core.modeler.pcb.primitives_3d_layout.Primitives3DLayout.change_net_visibility
documentation:
Change the visibility of one or more nets.

# Parameters

assignment

: One or more nets to visualize. The default is `None`.
  If no nets are provided all the nets in the design will be selected.

visible

: Whether to make the selected nets visible.
  The default value is `False`.

# Returns

bool

: `True` when successful, `False` when failed.

# References

```pycon
>>> oEditor.SetNetVisible
```

methods.name: ansys.aedt.core.modeler.pcb.primitives_3d_layout.Primitives3DLayout.create_via
documentation:
Create a via based on an existing padstack.

# Parameters

padstack

: Name of the padstack. The default is `"PlanarEMVia"`.

x

: Position on the X axis. The default is `0`.

y

: Position on the Y axis. The default is `0`.

rotation

: Angle rotation in degrees. The default is `0`.

hole_diam

: Diameter of the hole. If `None` the default is `1`,
  in which case the override is disabled.

top_layer

: Top layer. If `None` the first layer is taken.

bot_layer

: Bottom layer. If `None` the last layer is taken.

name

: Name of the via. If `None` a random name is generated.

net

: Name of the net. The default is `None`, in which case no
  name is assigned.

# Returns

{class}`ansys.aedt.core.modeler.cad.object_3dlayout.Pins3DLayout` or bool

: Object via created when successful, `False` when failed.

# References

```pycon
>>> oEditor.CreateVia
```

methods.name: ansys.aedt.core.modeler.pcb.primitives_3d_layout.Primitives3DLayout.create_circle
documentation:
Create a circle on a layer.

# Parameters

layer

: Name of the layer.

x

: Position on the X axis.

y

: Position on the Y axis.

radius

: Radius of the circle.

name

: Name of the circle. The default is `None`, in which case the
  default name is assigned.

net

: Name of the net. The default is `None`, in which case the
  default name is assigned.

# Returns

{class}`ansys.aedt.core.modeler.cad.object_3dlayout.Circle3dLayout`

: Objects of the circle created when successful.

# References

```pycon
>>> oEditor.CreateCircle
```

methods.name: ansys.aedt.core.modeler.pcb.primitives_3d_layout.Primitives3DLayout.create_rectangle
documentation:
Create a rectangle on a layer.

# Parameters

layer

: Name of the layer.

origin

: Origin of the coordinate system in a list of `[x, y]` coordinates.

sizes

: Dimensions for the box in a list of `[x, y]` coordinates.

corner_radius : float, optional
angle : float, optional

> Angle rotation in degrees. The default is `0`.

name

: Name of the rectangle. The default is `None`, in which case the
  default name is assigned.

net

: Name of the net. The default is `None`, in which case the
  default name is assigned.

# Returns

{class}`ansys.aedt.core.modeler.cad.object_3dlayout.Rect3dLayout`

: Name of the rectangle created when successful.

# References

```pycon
>>> oEditor.CreateRectangle
```

methods.name: ansys.aedt.core.modeler.pcb.primitives_3d_layout.Primitives3DLayout.create_polygon
documentation:
Create a polygon on a specified layer.

# Parameters

layer

: Name of the layer.

point_list

: Origin of the coordinate system in a list of `[x, y]` coordinates.

units

: Polygon units. Default is modeler units.

name

: Name of the rectangle. The default is `None`, in which case the
  default name is assigned.

net

: Name of the net. The default is `None`, in which case the
  default name is assigned.

# Returns

{class}`ansys.aedt.core.modeler.cad.object_3dlayout.Polygons3DLayout`

: Object of the rectangle created when successful.

# References

```pycon
>>> oEditor.CreatePolygon
```

methods.name: ansys.aedt.core.modeler.pcb.primitives_3d_layout.Primitives3DLayout.create_polygon_void
documentation:
Create a polygon void on a specified layer.

# Parameters

layer

: Name of the layer.

points

: List of points in a list of `[x, y]` coordinates.

assignment

: Object Owner.

units

: Polygon units. Default is modeler units.

name

: Name of the rectangle. The default is `None`, in which case the
  default name is assigned.

# Returns

{class}`ansys.aedt.core.modeler.cad.object_3dlayout.Polygons3DLayout`

: Object of the rectangle created when successful.

# References

```pycon
>>> oEditor.CreatePolygon
```

methods.name: ansys.aedt.core.modeler.pcb.primitives_3d_layout.Primitives3DLayout.create_line
documentation:
Create a line based on a list of points.

# Parameters

layer

: Name of the layer to create the line on.

center_line_coordinates

: List of centerline coordinates in the form of `[x, y]`.

lw

: Line width. The default is `1`.

start_style :

: Starting style of the line. Options are:

  - `0` - Flat
  - `1` - Extended
  - `2` - Round

  The default is `0`.

end_style :

: Ending style of the line. The options are the same as
  those for `start_style`. The default is `0`.

name

: Name of the line. The default is `None`, in which case the
  default name is assigned.

net

: Name of the net. The default is `None`, in which case the
  default name is assigned.

# Returns

{class}`ansys.aedt.core.modeler.cad.object_3dlayout.Line3dLayout`

: Object of the line created when successful.

# References

```pycon
>>> oEditor.CreateLine
```

methods.name: ansys.aedt.core.modeler.pcb.primitives_3d_layout.Primitives3DLayout.number_with_units
documentation:
Convert a number to a string with units.

If value is a string, it's returned as is.

# Parameters

value

: Input number or string.

units

: Units for formatting. The default is `None`, which uses `"meter"`.

# Returns

str

: String concatenating the value and unit.

methods.name: ansys.aedt.core.modeler.pcb.primitives_3d_layout.Primitives3DLayout.place_3d_component
documentation:
Place an HFSS 3D component in HFSS 3D Layout.

# Parameters

component_path

: Full path to the A3DCOMP file.

number_of_terminals

: Number of ports in the 3D component. The default is `1`.

placement_layer

: Layer to place the component on. The default is `None`, in which case it is
  placed on top.

component_name

: Name of the component. The default is `None`, in which case a
  default name is assigned.

pos_x

: X placement. The default is `0`.

pos_y

: Y placement. The default is `0`.

create_ports

: Whether to expose 3D component ports. The default is `True`.

is_3d_placement

: Whether if the component is placed on a layer or arbitrary.

pos_z
: Z placement. When enabled, 3d placement will be automatically enabled too.

  : The default is `False`.

# Returns

> {class}`ansys.aedt.core.modeler.pcb.object_3d_layout.ComponentsSubCircuit3DLayout`

methods.name: ansys.aedt.core.modeler.pcb.primitives_3d_layout.Primitives3DLayout.create_component_on_pins
documentation:
Create a component based on a pin list.

# Parameters

pins

: Pins to include in the new component.

definition_name

: Name of the component definition. If no name is provided, a
  name is automatically assigned.

component_type

: Component type. The default is `"Other"`.

ref_des

: Reference designator. The default is `"U100"`.

# Returns

{class}`ansys.aedt.core.modeler.cad.object_3dlayout.Components3DLayout`

methods.name: ansys.aedt.core.modeler.pcb.primitives_3d_layout.Primitives3DLayout.create_text
documentation:
Create a text primitive object.

# Parameters

text

: Name for the text primitive object.

position

: Position of the text.

layer

: Layer where text will be placed. The default value is `"PostProcessing"`.

angle

: Angle of the text. The default value is `0`.

font_size

: Font size. The default value is `12`.

# Returns

str

: Name of the text primitive.

Manages primitives in HFSS 3D Layout.

This class is inherited in the caller application and is accessible through the primitives variable part
of modeler object( eg. `hfss3dlayout.modeler`).

# Parameters

modeler

: Name of the modeler.

# Examples

Basic usage demonstrated with an HFSS 3D Layout design:

```pycon
>>> from ansys.aedt.core import Hfss3dLayout
>>> aedtapp = Hfss3dLayout()
>>> prim = aedtapp.modeler
```

```pycon
>>> oEditor.CreateComponent
```

MaxwellCircuitComponents class.

This class is for managing all circuit components for MaxwellCircuit.

# Examples

Basic usage demonstrated with a MaxwellCircuit design:

```pycon
>>> from ansys.aedt.core import MaxwellCircuit
>>> aedtapp = MaxwellCircuit()
>>> prim = aedtapp.modeler.schematic
```

methods.name: ansys.aedt.core.modeler.pcb.object_3d_layout.Components3DLayout.set_die_type
documentation:
Set the die type.

# Parameters

die_type

: Die type. The default is `1`. Options are `0` for None, `1` for FlipChip, and
  `2` for WireBond.

orientation

: Die orientation. The default is `0`. Options are `0` for Chip Top and `1` for
  Chip Bottom.

height

: Die height valid for port setup. The default is `0`.

reference_offset

: Port reference offset. The default is `0`.

auto_reference

: Whether to automatically compute reference size. The default is `True`.

reference_x

: Reference x size for when `auto_reference=False`.

reference_y

: Reference y size for when `auto_reference=False`.

# Returns

bool

: `True` when successful, `False` when failed.

methods.name: ansys.aedt.core.modeler.pcb.object_3d_layout.Components3DLayout.set_solderball
documentation:
Set solderball on the active component.

The method applies to these component types: `Other`, `IC` and `IO`.

# Parameters

solderball_type

: Solderball type. The default is `"Cyl"`. Options are `"None"`, `"Cyl"`,
  and `"Sph"`.

diameter

: Ball diameter. The default is `"0.1mm"`.

mid_diameter

: Ball mid diameter. The default is `"0.1mm"`.

height

: Ball height. The default is height="0.2mm".

material

: Ball material. The default is `"solder"`.

reference_offset

: Reference offset for port creation. The default is `"0mm"`

# Returns

bool

: `True` when successful, `False` when failed or the wrong component type.

methods.name: ansys.aedt.core.modeler.pcb.object_3d_layout.Nets3DLayout.plot
documentation:
Plot a Net to Matplotlib 2D Chart.

# Parameters

layers

: Name of the layers to include in the plot. If `None` all the signal layers will be considered.

show_legend

: If `True` the legend is shown in the plot. (default)
  If `False` the legend is not shown.

save_plot

: If a path is specified the plot will be saved in this location.
  If `save_plot` is provided, the `show` parameter is ignored.

outline

: List of points of the outline to plot.

size

: Image size in pixel (width, height). Default value is `(2000, 1000)`

plot_components_on_top

: If `True` the components placed on top layer are plotted.
  If `False` the components are not plotted. (default)
  If nets and/or layers is specified, only the components belonging to the specified nets/layers are plotted.

plot_components_on_bottom

: If `True` the components placed on bottom layer are plotted.
  If `False` the components are not plotted. (default)
  If nets and/or layers is specified, only the components belonging to the specified nets/layers are plotted.

show

: Whether to show the plot or not. Default is `True`.
Contains Nets in HFSS 3D Layout.

documentation: Pad properties.

methods.name: ansys.aedt.core.modeler.pcb.object_3d_layout.Padstack.add_layer
documentation:
Create a layer in the padstack.

# Parameters

layer

: Name of layer. The default is `"Start"`.

pad_hole

: Pad hole object, which you can create with the {func}`add_hole` method.
  The default is `None`.

antipad_hole :

: Antipad hole object, which you can create with the {func}`add_hole` method.
  The default is `None`.

thermal_hole :

: Thermal hole object, which you can create with the {func}`add_hole` method.
  The default is `None`.

connx

: Connection in the X-axis direction. The default is `0.`

conny

: Connection in the Y-axis direction. The default is `0.`

conndir :

: Connection attach angle. The default is `0.`

# Returns

bool

: `True` when successful, `False` when failed.

methods.name: ansys.aedt.core.modeler.pcb.object_3d_layout.Padstack.add_hole
documentation:
Add a hole.

# Parameters

hole_type

: Type of the hole. Options are:

  - No" - no pad
  - "Cir" - Circle
  - "Sq" - Square
  - "Rct" - Rectangle
  - "Ov" - Oval
  - "Blt" - Bullet
  - "Ply" - Polygons
  - "R45" - Round 45 thermal
  - "R90" - Round 90 thermal
  - "S45" - Square 45 thermal
  - "S90" - Square 90 thermal

  The default is `"Cir"`.

sizes

: Array of sizes, which depends on the object. For example, a circle ias an array
  of one element. The default is `[1]`.

x :

: Position on the X axis. The default is `0`.

y :

: Position on the Y axis. The default is `0`.

rotation

methods.name: ansys.aedt.core.modeler.circuits.primitives_twin_builder.TwinBuilderComponents.create_periodic_waveform_source
documentation:
Create a periodic waveform source (non conservative real output).

# Parameters

name

: Name of the voltage source. The default is `None`.

type

: Type of the source [SINE, PULSE, TRAING, SAWTOOTH]. The default is `SINE`.

amplitude

: Amplitude of the waveform if periodic. The default is `100V`

freq

: Frequency of the periodic waveform. The default is `50Hz`.

phase

: Phase of the periodic waveform. The default is `0deg`.

offset

: Offset added to the amplitude of the periodic waveform. The default is `0`.

delay

: Delay before starting of the periodic waveform. The default is `0`.

location

: Position on the X axis and Y axis. The default value is `None`.

angle

: Angle of rotation in degrees. The default is `0`.

use_instance_id_netlist

: Whether to use the instance ID in the net list or not. The default is `False`.

# Returns

{class}`ansys.aedt.core.modeler.cad.object_3dcircuit.CircuitComponent`

: Circuit Component Object.

# References

```pycon
>>> oEditor.CreateComponent
```

methods.name: ansys.aedt.core.modeler.circuits.primitives_twin_builder.TwinBuilderComponents.create_component_from_sml
documentation:
Create and place a new component based on a .sml file.

# Parameters

input_file

: Path to .sml file.

model

: Model name to import.

pins_names

: List of model pins names.

# Returns

bool

: `True` when successful, `False` when failed.

# Examples

```pycon
>>> from ansys.aedt.core import TwinBuilder
>>> tb = TwinBuilder(version="2025.1")
>>> input_file = os.path.join("Your path", "test.sml")
>>> model = "Thermal_ROM_SML"
>>> pins_names = ["Input1_InternalHeatGeneration", "Input2_HeatFlow", "Output1_Temp1,Output2_Temp2"]
>>> tb.modeler.schematic.create_component_from_sml(input_file=model, model=model, pins_names=pins_names)
>>> tb.release_desktop(False, False)
```

methods.name: ansys.aedt.core.modeler.circuits.primitives_twin_builder.TwinBuilderComponents.update_quantity_value
documentation:
Change the quantity value of a component.

# Parameters

component_name

: Component name.

name

: Quantity name.

value

: Value of the quantity.

netlist_units

: Value of the netlist unit.

# Returns

bool

: `True` when successful, `False` when failed.

# Examples

```pycon
>>> from ansys.aedt.core import TwinBuilder
>>> tb = TwinBuilder(version="2025.1")
>>> G = 0.00254
>>> modelpath = "Simplorer Elements\Basic Elements\Tools\Time Functions:DATAPAIRS"
>>> source1 = tb.modeler.schematic.create_component("source1", "", modelpath, [20 * G, 29 * G])
>>> tb.modeler.schematic.update_quantity_value(source1.composed_name, "PERIO", "0")
>>> tb.release_desktop(False, False)
```

TwinBuilderComponents class.

This class is for managing all circuit components for Twin Builder.

# Parameters

parent :

modeler :

# Examples

Basic usage demonstrated with a Twin Builder design:

```pycon
>>> from ansys.aedt.core import Twin Builder
>>> aedtapp = TwinBuilder()
>>> prim = aedtapp.modeler.schematic
```

methods.name: ansys.aedt.core.modeler.circuits.primitives_circuit.CircuitComponents.create_touchstone_component
documentation:
Create a component from a Touchstone model.

# Parameters

model_name

: Name of the Touchstone model or full path to touchstone file.
  If full touchstone is provided then, new model will be created.

location

: Position on the X and Y axis.

angle

: Angle rotation in degrees. The default is `0`.

show_bitmap

: Show bitmap image of schematic component.
  The default value is `True`.

# Returns

{class}`ansys.aedt.core.modeler.cad.object_3dcircuit.CircuitComponent`

: Circuit Component Object.

# References

```pycon
>>> oModelManager.Add
>>> oComponentManager.Add
>>> oEditor.CreateComponent
```

# Examples

```pycon
>>> from ansys.aedt.core import Circuit
>>> cir = Circuit()
>>> comps = cir.modeler.components
>>> s_parameter_path = os.path.join("your_path", "s_param_file_name.s4p")
>>> circuit_comp = comps.create_touchstone_component(s_parameter_path, location=[0.0, 0.0], show_bitmap=False)
```

methods.name: ansys.aedt.core.modeler.circuits.primitives_circuit.CircuitComponents.create_component
documentation:
Create a component from a library.

# Parameters

name

: Name of the instance. The default is `None.`

component_library

: Name of the component library. The default is `"Resistors"`.

component_name

: Name of component in the library. The default is `"RES"`.

location

: Position on the X axis and Y axis.
  The default is `None`, in which case the component is placed in [0, 0].

angle

: Angle rotation in degrees. The default is `0`.

use_instance_id_netlist

: Whether to enable the instance ID in the net list.
  The default is `False`.

global_netlist_list

: The default is `None`, in which case an empty list is passed.

# Returns

{class}`ansys.aedt.core.modeler.cad.object_3dcircuit.CircuitComponent`

: Circuit Component Object.

# References

```pycon
>>> oEditor.CreateComponent
```

# Examples

```pycon
>>> from ansys.aedt.core import TwinBuilder
>>> aedtapp = TwinBuilder()
>>> cmp = aedtapp.modeler.schematic.create_component(component_library="",component_name="ExcitationComponent")
>>> cmp.set_property("ShowPin",True)
>>> aedtapp.release_desktop(True, True)
```

CircutComponents class.

Manages all circuit components for Nexxim and Twin Builder.

# Examples


documentation:
Connect schematic components.

# Parameters

assignment

: Component pin to attach.

page_name

: Page port name. The default value is `None`, in which case
  a name is automatically generated.

use_wire

: Whether to use wires or a page port to connect the pins.
  The default is `False`, in which case a page port is used. Note
  that if wires are used but not well placed, shorts can result.

wire_name

: Wire name used only when `user_wire=True`. The default is `""`.

clearance_units

: Number of snap units (100mil each) around the object to overcome pins and wires.

page_port_angle

: Page port angle on the source pin. The default is `None`, in which case
  the angle is automatically computed.

# Returns

bool

: `True` when successful, `False` when failed.

# References

```pycon
>>> oPadstackManager.CreatePagePort
```

methods.name: ansys.aedt.core.modeler.circuits.object_3d_circuit.CircuitComponent.change_symbol_pin_locations
documentation:
Change the locations of symbol pins.

# Parameters

pin_locations

: A dictionary with two keys: "left" and "right",
  each containing a list of pin names to be placed on the left and
  right sides of the symbol, respectively.

# Returns

bool

: `True` if pin locations were successfully changed, `False` otherwise.

# References

```pycon
>>> oSymbolManager.EditSymbolAndUpdateComps
```

# Examples

```pycon
>>> from ansys.aedt.core import Circuit
>>> cir = Circuit(my_project)
>>> cir.modeler.schematic_units = "mil"
>>> ts_path = os.path.join(current_path, "connector_model.s4p")
>>> ts_component = cir.modeler.schematic.create_touchstone_component(ts_path, show_bitmap=False)
>>> pin_locations = {"left":
...                 ['DDR_CH3_DM_DBI0_BGA_BE47', 'DDR_CH3_DM_DBI1_BGA_BJ50','DDR_CH3_DM_DBI1_DIE_12471'],
...                 "right": ['DDR_CH3_DM_DBI0_DIE_7976']}
>>> ts_component.change_symbol_pin_locations(pin_locations)
```

documentation:
Display wire properties.

# Parameters

name

: Wire name to display.
  Default value is `""`.

property_to_display

: Property to display. Choices are: `"NetName"`, `"PinCount"`, `"AlignMicrowavePorts"`,
  `"SchematicID"`, `"Segment0"`.
  Default value is `"NetName"`.

visibility

: Visibility type. Choices are `"Name"`, `"Value"`, `"Both"`, `"Evaluated Value"`,
  `"Evaluated Both"`.
  Default value is `"Name"`.

location

: Wire name location. Choices are `"Left"`, `"Top"`, `"Right"`, `"Bottom"`, `"Center"`.
  Default value is `"Top"`.

# Returns

bool

: `True` when successful, `False` when failed.
Creates and manipulates a wire.

methods.name: ansys.aedt.core.maxwellcircuit.MaxwellCircuit.create_schematic_from_netlist
documentation:
Create a circuit schematic from an HSpice net list.

Supported currently are:

- R
- L
- C
- Diodes

# Parameters

input_file

: Full path to the HSpice file.

# Returns

bool

: `True` when successful, `False` when failed.

methods.name: ansys.aedt.core.maxwellcircuit.MaxwellCircuit.export_netlist_from_schematic
documentation:
Create netlist from schematic circuit.

# Parameters

output_file

: File path to export the netlist to.

# Returns

str

: Netlist file path when successful, `False` when failed.

Provide the Maxwell Circuit application interface.

# Parameters

project

: Name of the project to select or the full path to the project
  or AEDTZ archive to open. The default is `None`, in which
  case an attempt is made to get an active project. If no
  projects are present, an empty project is created.

design

: Name of the design to select. The default is `None`, in
  which case an attempt is made to get an active design. If no
  designs are present, an empty design is created.

version

: Version of AEDT to use. The default is `None`. If `None`,
  the active setup is used or the latest installed version is
  used.
  Examples of input values are `232`, `23.2`,\`\`2023.2\`\`,\`\`"2023.2".

non_graphical

: Whether to launch AEDT in non-graphical mode. The default
  is `False`, in which case AEDT is launched in graphical mode.
  This parameter is ignored when a script is launched within AEDT.

new_desktop

: Whether to launch an instance of AEDT in a new thread, even if
  another instance of the `specified_version` is active on the
  machine. The default is `False`.

close_on_exit

: Whether to release AEDT on exit. The default is `False`.

student_version

: Whether to open the AEDT student version. The default is `False`.

machine

: Machine name to connect the oDesktop session to. This works only in 2022 R2
  and later. The remote server must be up and running with the command
  `"ansysedt.exe -grpcsrv portnum"`. If the machine is `"localhost"`, the server
  is also started if not present.

port

: Port number on which to start the oDesktop communication on an already existing server.
  This parameter is ignored when creating a new server. It works only in 2022 R2 or
  later. The remote server must be up and running with the command `"ansysedt.exe -grpcsrv portnum"`.

aedt_process_id

: Process ID for the instance of AEDT to point PyAEDT at. The default is
  `None`. This parameter is only used when `new_desktop = False`.

remove_lock

: Whether to remove lock to project before opening it or not.
  The default is `False`, which means to not unlock
  the existing project if needed and raise an exception.

# Examples

Create an instance of Maxwell Circuit and connect to an existing
Maxwell circuit design or create a new Maxwell circuit design if one does
not exist.

```pycon
>>> from ansys.aedt.core import MaxwellCircuit
>>> app = MaxwellCircuit()
```

Create an instance of Maxwell Circuit and link to a project named
`"projectname"`. If this project does not exist, create one with
this name.

```pycon
>>> app = MaxwellCircuit(projectname)
```

Create an instance of Maxwell Circuit and link to a design named
`"designname"` in a project named `"projectname"`.

```pycon
>>> app = MaxwellCircuit(projectname, designame)
```

Create an instance of Maxwell Circuit and open the specified
project, which is named `"myfile.aedt"`.

```pycon
>>> app = MaxwellCircuit("myfile.aedt")
```
