methods.name: ansys.aedt.core.modeler.modeler_3d.Modeler3D.create_3dcomponent
documentation:
Create a 3D component file.

# Parameters

input_file

: Full path to the A3DCOMP file.

name

: Name of the component. The default is `None`.

variables_to_include

: List of variables to include. The default is all variables.

assignment

: List of object names to export. The default is all object names.

boundaries

: List of Boundaries names to export. The default is all boundaries.

excitations

: List of Excitation names to export. The default is all excitations.

coordinate_systems

: List of Coordinate Systems to export. The default is the `reference_cs`.

reference_coordinate_system

: The Coordinate System reference. The default is `"Global"`.

is_encrypted

: Whether the component has encrypted protection. The default is `False`.

allow_edit

: Whether the component is editable with encrypted protection.
  The default is `False`.

security_message

: Security message to display when component is inserted.
  The default value is an empty string.

password

: Security password needed when adding the component.
  The default value is `None`.

edit_password

: Edit password.
  The default value is `None`.

password_type

: Password type. Options are `UserSuppliedPassword` and `InternalPassword`.
  The default is `UserSuppliedPassword`.

hide_contents

: List of object names to hide when the component is encrypted.
  If set to an empty list or `False`, all objects are visible.

replace_names

: Whether to replace objects and material names.
  The default is `False`.

component_outline

: Component outline. Value can either be `BoundingBox` or `None`.
  The default is `BoundingBox`.

export_auxiliary

: Whether to export the auxiliary file containing information about defined
  datasets and Icepak monitor objects. A destination file can be specified
  using a string.
  The default is `False`.

monitor_objects

: List of monitor objects' names to export. The default is the names of all
  monitor objects. This argument is relevant only if `auxiliary_dict_file`
  is not set to `False`.

datasets
: List of dataset names to export. The default is all datasets. This argument

  : is relevant only if `auxiliary_dict_file` is set to `True`.

native_components

: List of native_components names to export. The default is all
  native_components. This argument is relevant only if `auxiliary_dict_file`
  is set to `True`.

create_folder

: If the specified path to the folder where the 3D component should be saved
  does not exist, then create the folder. Default is `True`.

# Returns

bool

: `True` when successful, `False` when failed.

# References

```pycon
>>> oEditor.Create3DComponent
```

methods.name: ansys.aedt.core.modeler.modeler_3d.Modeler3D.replace_3dcomponent
documentation:
Replace with 3D component.

# Parameters

name

: Name of the component. The default is `None`.

variables_to_include

: List of variables to include. The default is `None`.

assignment

: List of object names to export. The default is all object names.

boundaries

: List of Boundaries names to export. The default is all boundaries.

excitations

: List of Excitation names to export. The default is all excitations.

coordinate_systems

: List of coordinate systems to export. The default is all coordinate systems.

reference_coordinate_system

: The coordinate system reference. The default is `"Global"`.

# Returns

{class}`ansys.aedt.core.modeler.components_3d.UserDefinedComponent`

: User-defined component object.

# References

```pycon
>>> oEditor.ReplaceWith3DComponent
```

methods.name: ansys.aedt.core.modeler.modeler_3d.Modeler3D.create_coaxial
documentation:
Create a coaxial.

# Parameters

origin

: List of `[x, y, z]` coordinates for the starting position.

axis

: Coordinate system AXIS (integer `0` for X, `1` for Y, `2` for Z) or
  the {class}`Application.AXIS` enumerator.

inner_radius

: Inner coax radius. The default is `1`.

outer_radius

: Outer coax radius. The default is `2`.

diel_radius

: Dielectric coax radius. The default is `1.8`.

length

: Coaxial length. The default is `10`.

mat_inner

: Material for the inner coaxial. The default is `"copper"`.

mat_outer

: Material for the outer coaxial. The default is `"copper"`.

mat_diel

: Material for the dielectric. The default is `"teflon_based"`.

# Returns

tuple

: Contains the inner, outer, and dielectric coax as
  {class}`ansys.aedt.core.modeler.Object3d.Object3d` objects.

# References

```pycon
>>> oEditor.CreateCylinder
>>> oEditor.AssignMaterial
```

# Examples

This example shows how to create a Coaxial Along X Axis waveguide.

```pycon
>>> from ansys.aedt.core import Hfss
>>> app = Hfss()
>>> position = [0,0,0]
>>> coax = app.modeler.create_coaxial(position,app.AXIS.X,inner_radius=0.5,outer_radius=0.8,diel_radius=0.78,
... length=50)
```

methods.name: ansys.aedt.core.modeler.modeler_3d.Modeler3D.create_waveguide
documentation:
Create a standard waveguide and optionally parametrize `W` and `H`.

Available models are WG0.0, WG0, WG1, WG2, WG3, WG4, WG5, WG6,
WG7, WG8, WG9, WG9A, WG10, WG11, WG11A, WG12, WG13, WG14,
WG15, WR102, WG16, WG17, WG18, WG19, WG20, WG21, WG22, WG24,
WG25, WG26, WG27, WG28, WG29, WG29, WG30, WG31, and WG32.

# Parameters

origin

: List of `[x, y, z]` coordinates for the original position.

wg_direction_axis

: Coordinate system axis (integer `0` for X, `1` for Y, `2` for Z) or
  the {class}`Application.AXIS` enumerator.

wgmodel

: Waveguide model. The default is `"WG0"`.

wg_length

: Waveguide length. The default is `100`.

wg_thickness

: Waveguide thickness. The default is `None`, in which case the
  thickness is `wg_height/20`.

wg_material

: Waveguide material. The default is `"aluminum"`.

parametrize_w

: Whether to parametrize `W`. The default is `False`.

parametrize_h

: Whether to parametrize `H`. The default is `False`.

create_sheets_on_openings

: Whether to create sheets on both openings. The default is `False`.

name

: Name of the waveguide. The default is `None`.

# Returns

tuple

: Tuple of {class}`Object3d <ansys.aedt.core.modeler.Object3d.Object3d>`
  objects created by the waveguide.

# References

```pycon
>>> oEditor.CreateBox
>>> oEditor.AssignMaterial
```

# Examples

This example shows how to create a WG9 waveguide.

```pycon
>>> from ansys.aedt.core import Hfss
>>> app = Hfss()
>>> position = [0, 0, 0]
>>> wg1 = app.modeler.create_waveguide(position, app.AXIS.,
...                                    wgmodel="WG9", wg_length=2000)
```

methods.name: ansys.aedt.core.modeler.modeler_3d.Modeler3D.create_conical_rings
documentation:
Create rings in a conical shape.

# Parameters

axis

: Coordinate system of the axis.

origin

: List of `[x, y, z]` coordinates for the center position
  of the bottom of the cone.

bottom_radius

: Bottom radius of the cone.

top_radius

: Top radius of the cone.

cone_height

: Height of the cone.

ring_height

: Ring height.

thickness

: Ring thickness. The default is `None`, in which case a 2D sheet is created.

name

: Name of the cone. The default is `None`, in which case
  the default name is assigned.

# Returns

list of {class}`ansys.aedt.core.modeler.cad.object_3d.Object3d`, bool

: List of 3D object or `False` if it fails.

# References

```pycon
>>> oEditor.CreatePolyline
>>> oEditor.SweepAroundAxis
>>> oEditor.ThickenSheet
```

# Examples

This example shows how to create rings along Z axis with a cone shape.

```pycon
>>> from ansys.aedt.core import Hfss
>>> app = Hfss()
>>> position = [0,0,0]
>>> cone_object = aedtapp.modeler.create_conical_rings(axis='Z', origin=[0, 0, 0],
...                                           bottom_radius=2, top_radius=3, cone_height=4, ring_height=0.1)
```

methods.name: ansys.aedt.core.modeler.modeler_3d.Modeler3D.objects_in_bounding_box
documentation:
Given a bounding box checks if objects, sheets and lines are inside it.

# Parameters

bounding_box

: List of coordinates of bounding box vertices.
  Bounding box is provided as [xmin, ymin, zmin, xmax, ymax, zmax].

check_solids

: Check solid objects.

check_lines

: Check line objects.

check_sheets

: Check sheet objects.

# Returns

list of {class}`ansys.aedt.core.modeler.cad.object_3d`

methods.name: ansys.aedt.core.modeler.modeler_3d.Modeler3D.import_nastran
documentation:
Import Nastran file into 3D Modeler by converting the faces to stl and reading it. The solids are
translated directly to AEDT format.

# Parameters

file_path

: Path to .nas file.

import_lines

: Whether to import the lines or only triangles. Default is `True`.

lines_thickness

: Whether to thicken lines after creation and it's default value.
  Every line will be parametrized with a design variable called `xsection_linename`.

import_as_light_weight

: Import the stl generatated as light weight. It works only on SBR+ and HFSS Regions. Default is `False`.

decimation

: Fraction of the original mesh to remove before creating the stl file. If set to `0.9`,
  this function tries to reduce the data set to 10% of its
  original size and removes 90% of the input triangles.

group_parts

: Whether to group imported parts by object ID. The default is `True`.

enable_planar_merge

: Whether to enable or not planar merge. It can be `"True"`, `"False"` or `"Auto"`.
  `"Auto"` will disable the planar merge if stl contains more than 50000 triangles.

save_only_stl

: Whether to import the model in HFSS or only generate the stl file.

preview

: Whether to preview the model in pyvista or skip it.

merge_angle

: Angle in radians for which faces will be considered planar. Default is `1e-3`.

# Returns

List of {class}`ansys.aedt.core.modeler.Object3d.Object3d`, dict

: New object created and nastran dictionary.

methods.name: ansys.aedt.core.modeler.modeler_3d.Modeler3D.import_from_openstreet_map
documentation:
Import OpenStreet Maps into AEDT.

# Parameters

latitude_longitude

: Latitude and longitude.

env_name

: Name of the environment used to create the scene. The default value is `"default"`.

terrain_radius

: Radius to take around center. The default value is `500`.

include_osm_buildings

: Either if include or not 3D Buildings. Default is `True`.

including_osm_roads

: Either if include or not road. Default is `True`.

import_in_aedt

: Either if import stl after generation or not. Default is `True`.

plot_before_importing

: Either if plot before importing or not. Default is `True`.

z_offset

: Road elevation offset. Default is `0`.

road_step

: Road simplification steps in meter. Default is `3`.

road_width

: Road width in meter. Default is `8`.

create_lightweigth_part

: Either if import as lightweight object or not. Default is `True`.

# Returns

dict

: Dictionary of generated infos.

methods.name: ansys.aedt.core.modeler.modeler_3d.Modeler3D.objects_segmentation
documentation:
Get segmentation of an object given the segmentation thickness or number of segments.

# Parameters

assignment

: List of objects to apply the segmentation to.
  It can either be a list of strings (object names), integers (object IDs), or
  a list of {class}`ansys.aedt.core.modeler.cad.object_3d.Object3d` classes.

segmentation_thickness

: Segmentation thickness.
  Model units are automatically assigned. The default is `None`.

segments

: Number of segments to segment the object to. The default is `None`.

apply_mesh_sheets

: Whether to apply mesh sheets to selected objects.
  Mesh sheets are needed in case the user would like to have additional layers
  inside the objects for a finer mesh and more accurate results. The default is `False`.

mesh_sheets

: Number of mesh sheets within one magnet segment.
  If nothing is provided and `apply_mesh_sheets=True`, the default value is `2`.

# Returns

dict or tuple

: Depending on value `apply_mesh_sheets` it returns either a dictionary or a tuple.
  If mesh sheets are applied the method returns a tuple where:
  \- First dictionary is the segments that the object has been divided into.
  \- Second dictionary is the mesh sheets eventually needed to apply the mesh.
  to inside the object. Keys are the object names, and values are respectively
  segments sheets and mesh sheets of the
  {class}`ansys.aedt.core.modeler.cad.object_3d.Object3d` class.
  If mesh sheets are not applied the method returns only the dictionary of
  segments that the object has been divided into.
  `False` is returned if the method fails.

methods.name: ansys.aedt.core.modeler.modeler_3d.Modeler3D.change_region_padding
documentation:
Change region padding settings.

# Parameters

padding_data

: Padding value (with unit if necessary). A list of padding values must have corresponding
  elements in `padding_type` and `direction` arguments.

padding_type

: Padding type. Available options are `"Percentage Offset"`, `"Transverse Percentage Offset"`,
  `"Absolute Offset"`, `"Absolute Position"`.

direction

: Direction to which apply the padding settings. A direction can be `"+X"`, `"-X"`,
  `` "+Y"` ``, `"-Y"`, `"+Z"` or `"-Z"`. Default is `None`, in which case all the
  directions are used (in the order written in the previous sentence).

region_name

: Region name. Default is `Region`.

# Returns

bool

: `True` if successful, else `None`.

# Examples

```pycon
>>> import ansys.aedt.core
>>> app = ansys.aedt.core.Icepak()
>>> app.modeler.change_region_padding("10mm", padding_type="Absolute Offset", direction="-X")
```

methods.name: ansys.aedt.core.modeler.modeler_3d.Modeler3D.change_region_coordinate_system
documentation:
Change region coordinate system.

# Parameters

assignment

: Region coordinate system. Default is `Global`.

name

: Region name. Default is `Region`.

# Returns

bool

: `True` if successful, else `None`.

# Examples

```pycon
>>> import ansys.aedt.core
>>> app = ansys.aedt.core.Icepak()
>>> app.modeler.create_coordinate_system(origin=[1, 1, 1], name="NewCS")
>>> app.modeler.change_region_coordinate_system(assignment="NewCS")
```

Provides the Modeler 3D application interface.

This class is inherited in the caller application and is accessible through the modeler variable
object. For example, `hfss.modeler`.

# Parameters

application : {class}`ansys.aedt.core.application.analysis_3d.FieldAnalysis3D`

# Examples

```pycon
>>> from ansys.aedt.core import Hfss
>>> hfss = Hfss()
>>> my_modeler = hfss.modeler
```

documentation:
Add a new Circuit subcircuit to the design.

# Parameters

location

: Position on the X axis and Y axis.

angle

: Angle rotation in degrees. The default is `None`.

name

: Name of the design. The default is `None`, in which case
  a unique name is generated.

nested_subcircuit_id

: ID of the nested subcircuit.
  Example `"U1"`.

# Returns

{class}`ansys.aedt.core.modeler.cad.object_3dcircuit.CircuitComponent`

: Circuit Component Object when successful or `False` when failed.

# Examples

```pycon
>>> from ansys.aedt.core import Circuit
>>> cir = Circuit()
```

documentation:
Add a new subcircuit to the design.

:::{note}
This works only in graphical mode.
:::

# Parameters

assignment

: Component to duplicate.

location

: Position on the X axis and Y axis.

angle

: Angle rotation in degrees. The default is `0`.

flip

: Whether the component should be flipped. The default value is `False`.

# Returns

when successful or `False` when failed.

methods.name: ansys.aedt.core.modeler.circuits.primitives_nexxim.NexximComponents.connect_components_in_series
documentation:
Connect schematic components in series.

# Parameters

assignment

: List of Components to connect. It can be a list of objects or component names.

use_wire

: Whether to use wires or a page port to connect the pins.
  The default is `True`, in which case wires are used. Note
  that if wires are not well placed, shorts can result.

# Returns

bool

: `True` when successful, `False` when failed.

# Examples

```pycon
>>> from ansys.aedt.core import Circuit
>>> circuit = Circuit()
>>> circuit.modeler.schematic_units = "mil"
>>> myind = circuit.modeler.schematic.create_inductor(value=1e-9,location=[0,0])
>>> myres = circuit.modeler.schematic.create_resistor(value=50,location=[100, 2000])
>>> circuit.modeler.schematic.connect_components_in_series([myind, myres])
```

methods.name: ansys.aedt.core.modeler.circuits.primitives_nexxim.NexximComponents.connect_components_in_parallel
documentation:
Connect schematic components in parallel.

# Parameters

assignment

: List of Components to connect. It can be a list of objects or component names.

# Returns

bool

: `True` when successful, `False` when failed.

# Examples

```pycon
>>> from ansys.aedt.core import Circuit
>>> circuit = Circuit()
>>> myind = circuit.modeler.schematic.create_inductor("L100",1e-9)
>>> myres = circuit.modeler.schematic.create_resistor("R100",50)
>>> circuit.modeler.schematic.connect_components_in_parallel([myind, myres.composed_name])
```

documentation:
Create an inductor.

# Parameters

: Name of the inductor. The default is `None`.

value

methods.name: ansys.aedt.core.modeler.circuits.primitives_nexxim.NexximComponents.add_subcircuit_dynamic_link
documentation:
Add a subcircuit from `HFSS`, `Q3d` or `2D Extractor` in circuit design.

# Parameters

pyaedt_app

: {class}`ansys.aedt.core.q3d.Hfss`.
  pyaedt application object to include. It could be an Hfss object, a Q3d object or a Q2d.

solution_name

: Name of the solution and sweep. The default is `"Setup1 : Sweep"`.

extrusion_length

: Extrusion length for 2D Models (q2d or Hfss) in model units. Default is `None`.

enable_cable_modeling

: Either if the Hfss Cable modeling has to be enabled for 2D subcircuits.

default_matrix

: Matrix to link to the subcircuit. Default to `"Original"`. It only applies to 2D Extractor and Q3D.

tline_port

: Port to be used for tramsission line. Only applies to Hfss.

name

: Component name.

# Returns

{class}`ansys.aedt.core.modeler.cad.object_3dcircuit.CircuitComponent`

: Circuit Component Object.

# References

```pycon
>>> oModelManager.Add
>>> oComponentManager.Add
>>> oDesign.AddCompInstance
>>> oDesign.AddDynamicLink
```

methods.name: ansys.aedt.core.modeler.circuits.primitives_nexxim.NexximComponents._add_subcircuit_link
documentation:
Add a subcircuit HFSS link.

# Parameters

comp_name

: Name of the subcircuit HFSS link.

pin_names

: List of the pin names.

source_project_path

: Path to the source project.

source_design_name

: Name of the design.

solution_name

: Name of the solution and sweep. The
  default is `"Setup1 : Sweep"`.

image_subcircuit_path

: Path of the Picture used in Circuit.
  Default is an HFSS Picture exported automatically.

model_type

: Dynamick Link type. Options are `Hfss`, `Q3d`, `Q2d`.

variables

: Dictionary of variables and default values of original design, if exists.

extrusion_length_q2d

: Extrusion length for 2D Models. Default is 10 (in model units).

matrix : list, optional
simulate_solutions : bool, optional

> Either if simulate or interpolate solutions.

# Returns

{class}`ansys.aedt.core.modeler.cad.object_3dcircuit.CircuitComponent`

: Circuit Component Object.

# References

```pycon
>>> oModelManager.Add
>>> oComponentManager.Add
>>> oDesign.AddCompInstance
```

methods.name: ansys.aedt.core.modeler.circuits.primitives_nexxim.NexximComponents.create_component_from_spicemodel
documentation:
Create and place a new component based on a spice .lib file.

# Parameters

input_file

: Path to .lib file.

model

: Model name to import. If `None` the first subckt in the lib file will be placed.

create_component

: If set to `True`, create a spice model component. Otherwise, only import the spice model.

location

: Position in the schematic of the new component.

symbol_path

: Path to the symbol library.
  Default value is `"Nexxim Circuit Elements\Nexxim_symbols:"`.

symbol

: Symbol name to replace the spice model with.
  Default value is an empty string which means the default symbol for spice is used.

# Returns

{class}`ansys.aedt.core.modeler.cad.object_3dcircuit.CircuitComponent`

: Circuit Component Object.

# Examples

```pycon
>>> from ansys.aedt.core import Circuit
>>> cir = Circuit(version="2023.2")
>>> model = os.path.join("Your path", "test.lib")
>>> cir.modeler.schematic.create_component_from_spicemodel(input_file=model,model="GRM1234",symbol="nexx_cap")
>>> cir.release_desktop(False, False)
```

# Returns

: Circuit Component Object.

Manages circuit components for Nexxim.

modeler

```pycon
>>> aedtapp = Circuit()
>>> prim = aedtapp.modeler.schematic
```

methods.name: ansys.aedt.core.modeler.cad.primitives.GeometryModeler.create_coordinate_system
documentation:
Create a coordinate system.

# Parameters

origin

: List of `[x, y, z]` coordinates for the origin of the
  coordinate system. The default is `None`, in which case
  `[0, 0, 0]` is used.

reference_cs

: Name of the reference coordinate system. The default is
  `"Global"`.

name

: Name of the coordinate system. The default is `None`.

mode

: Definition mode. Options are `"axis"`, `"axisrotation"`,
  `"view"`, `"zxz"`, and `"zyz"` The default
  is `"axis"`. You can also use the `ansys.aedt.core.generic.constants.CSMODE`
  enumerator.

  - If `mode="axis"`, specify the `x_pointing` and `y_pointing` parameters.
  - If `mode="axisrotation"`, specify the `theta` and `u` parameters.
  - If `mode="view"`, specify the `view` parameter.
  - If `mode="zxz"` or `mode="zyz"`, specify the `phi`, `theta`,
    and `psi` parameters.

  Parameters not needed by the specified mode are ignored.
  The default mode, `"axis"`, is a coordinate system parallel to the
  global coordinate system centered in the global origin.

view

: View for the coordinate system if `mode="view"`. Options
  are `"iso"`, `None`, `"XY"`, `"XZ"`, and `"XY"`. The
  default is `"iso"`. the `"rotate"` option is obsolete. You can
  also use the `ansys.aedt.core.generic.constants.VIEW` enumerator.

  :::{note}
  For backward compatibility, `mode="view"` and `view="rotate"` are the same as
  `mode="axis"`. Because the "rotate" option in the "view" mode is obsolete, use
  `mode="axis"` instead.
  :::

x_pointing

: List of the `[x, y, z]` coordinates specifying the X axis
  pointing in the global coordinate system if `mode="axis"`.
  The default is `[1, 0, 0]`.

y_pointing

: List of the `[x, y, z]` coordinates specifying the Y axis
  pointing in the global coordinate system if `mode="axis"`.
  The default is `[0, 1, 0]`.

phi

: Euler angle phi in degrees if `mode="zxz"` or `mode="zyz"`.
  The default is `0`.

theta

: Euler angle theta or rotation angle in degrees if `mode="zxz"`,
  `mode="zyz"`, or `mode="axisrotation"`. The default is `0`.

psi

: Euler angle psi in degrees if `mode="zxz"` or `mode="zyz"`.
  The default is `0`.

u

: List of the `[ux, uy, uz]` coordinates for the rotation axis
  if `mode="zxz"`. The default is `[1, 0, 0]`.

# Returns

{class}`ansys.aedt.core.modeler.Modeler.CoordinateSystem`

: Created coordinate system.

# References

```pycon
>>> oEditor.CreateRelativeCS
```

methods.name: ansys.aedt.core.modeler.cad.primitives.GeometryModeler.create_face_coordinate_system
documentation:
Create a face coordinate system.

The face coordinate has always the Z axis parallel to face normal.
The X and Y axis lie on the face plane.

# Parameters

face

: Face where the coordinate system is defined.

origin

: Coordinate system origin. The origin must belong to the face where the
  coordinate system is defined.

  - If a face is specified, the origin is placed on the face center. It must be
    the same as the `face` parameter.
  - If an edge is specified, the origin is placed on the edge midpoint.
  - If a vertex is specified, the origin is placed on the vertex.

axis_position

: Specify where the X or Y axis is pointing. The position must belong to the face where the
  coordinate system is defined.
  Select which axis is considered with the option `axis`.
  If a face is specified, the position is placed on the face center. It must be the same as `face`.
  If an edge is specified, the position is placed on the edce midpoint.
  If a vertex is specified, the position is placed on the vertex.

axis

: Select which axis is considered for positioning. Possible values are `"X"` and `"Y"`.
  The default is `"X"`.

name

: Name of the coordinate system. The default is `None`.

offset

: List of the `[x, y]` coordinates specifying the offset of the coordinate system origin.
  The offset specified in the face coordinate system reference.
  The default is `[0, 0]`.

rotation

: Rotation angle of the coordinate system around its Z axis. Angle is in degrees.
  The default is `0`.

always_move_to_end

: If `True` the Face Coordinate System creation operation will always be moved to the end of subsequent
  objects operation. This will guarantee that the coordinate system will remain solidal with the object
  face. If `False` the option "Always Move CS to End" is set to off. The default is `True`.

# Returns

{class}`ansys.aedt.core.modeler.Modeler.FaceCoordinateSystem`

methods.name: ansys.aedt.core.modeler.cad.primitives.GeometryModeler.create_object_coordinate_system
documentation:
Create an object coordinate system.

# Parameters

assignment

: Object to attach the object coordinate system to.

origin

: Refer to the origin where the object coordinate system is anchored.
  It can be:

  > - int in which case it refers to the entity Id.
  > - VertexPrimitive, EdgePrimitive, FacePrimitive in which case it refers to the entity type.
  > - list in which case it refers to the origin coordinate system `[x, y, z]`.

x_axis

: Entity that the x axis of the object coordinate system points to.
  It can be:

  > - int in which case it refers to the entity Id.
  > - VertexPrimitive, EdgePrimitive, FacePrimitive in which case it refers to the entity type.
  > - list in which case it refers to the point coordinate system `[x, y, z]` that the x axis points to.

y_axis

: Entity that the y axis of the object coordinate system points to.
  It can be:

  > - int in which case it refers to the entity Id.
  > - VertexPrimitive, EdgePrimitive, FacePrimitive in which case it refers to the entity type.
  > - list in which case it refers to the point coordinate system `[x, y, z]` that the y axis points to.

move_to_end

: If `True` the Coordinate System creation operation will always be moved to the end of subsequent
  objects operation. This will guarantee that the coordinate system will remain solidal with the object
  face. If `False` the option "Always Move CS to End" is set to off. The default is `True`.

reverse_x_axis

: Whether the x-axis is in the reverse direction.
  The default is `False`.

reverse_y_axis

: Whether the y-axis is in the reverse direction.
  The default is `False`.

name

: Name of the coordinate system. The default is `None`.

# Returns

bool

: `True` when successful, `False` when failed.

methods.name: ansys.aedt.core.modeler.cad.primitives.GeometryModeler.convert_to_selections
documentation:
Convert modeler objects.

This method converts modeler object or IDs to the corresponding
output according to the following scheme:

```




assignment

Return value
```

> `int` object name (str)
>
> : `Object3D` object name (str)
>   `FacePrimitive` int, face ID
>   `EdgePrimitive` int, edge ID
>   `str` return the same `str`

- If `object_id` is a list, a list is returned according

to the table. If `object_id` is a single value, a list
of `length == 1` is returned (default).

- If the second argument, `return_list`, is set to `False` (default), a

string is returned with elements separated by a comma (,)".

# Parameters

assignment

: One or more object IDs whose name will be returned. A list can contain
  both strings (object names) and integers (object IDs).

return_list

: Whether to return a list of the selections. The default is
  `False`, in which case a string of the selections is returned.
  If `True`, a list of the selections is returned.

# Returns

str or list

: Name of the objects corresponding to the one or more object IDs passed as arguments.

methods.name: ansys.aedt.core.modeler.cad.primitives.GeometryModeler.split
documentation:
Split a list of objects.
In case of 3D design possible splitting options are plane, Face Primitive, Edge Primitive or Polyline.
In case of 2D design possible splitting option is plane.

# Parameters

assignment

: One or more objects to split. A list can contain
  both strings (object names) and integers (object IDs).

plane

: Coordinate plane of the cut or the Application.PLANE object.
  The default value is `None`.
  Choices for the coordinate plane are `"XY"`, `"YZ"`, and `"ZX"`.
  If plane or tool parameter are not provided the method returns `False`.

sides

: Which side to keep. The default is `"Both"`, in which case
  all objects are kept after the split. Options are `"Both"`,
  `"NegativeOnly"`, and `"PositiveOnly"`.

tool

: > {class}`ansys.aedt.core.modeler.cad.elements_3d.EdgePrimitive`, optional

  For 3D design types is the name, ID, face, edge or polyline used to split the objects.
  For 2D design types is the name of the plane used to split the objects.
  The default value is `None`.
  If plane or tool parameter are not provided the method returns `False`.

split_crossing_objs

: Whether to split crossing plane objects.
  The default is `False`.

delete_invalid_objs

: Whether to delete invalid objects.
  The default is `True`.

# Returns

list of str

: List of split object names.

# References

```pycon
>>> oEditor.Split
```

methods.name: ansys.aedt.core.modeler.cad.primitives.GeometryModeler.duplicate_and_mirror
documentation:
Duplicate and mirror a selection.

# Parameters

assignment

methods.name: ansys.aedt.core.modeler.cad.primitives.GeometryModeler.find_port_faces
documentation:
Find the vacuums given a list of input sheets.

Starting from a list of input sheets, this method creates a list of output sheets
that represent the blank parts (vacuums) and the tool parts of all the intersections
of solids on the sheets. After a vacuum on a sheet is found, a port can be
created on it.

Parameters
----------
assignment : list
    List of input sheets names.

Returns
-------
List
    List of output sheets (`2x len(port_sheets)`).

methods.name: ansys.aedt.core.modeler.cad.primitives.GeometryModeler.import_3d_cad
documentation:
Import a CAD model.

# Parameters

input_file

: Full path and name of the CAD file.

healing

: Whether to perform healing. The default is `False`, in which
  case healing is not performed.

refresh_all_ids

: Whether to refresh all IDs after the CAD file is loaded. The
  default is `True`. Refreshing IDs can take a lot of time in
  a big project.

import_materials

: Either to import material names from the file or not if presents.

create_lightweigth_part

: Either to import lightweight or not.

group_by_assembly

: Either import by sub-assembly or individual parts. The default is `False`.

create_group

: Either to create a new group of imported objects. The default is `True`.

separate_disjoints_lumped_object

: Either to automatically separate disjoint parts. The default is `False`.

import_free_surfaces

: Either to import free surfaces parts. The default is `False`.

point_coicidence_tolerance

: Tolerance on point. Default is `1e-6`.

reduce_stl

: Whether to reduce the stl file on import or not. Default is `True`.

reduce_percentage

: Stl reduce percentage. Default is `0`.

reduce_error

: Stl error percentage during reduce operation. Default is `0`.

merge_planar_faces

: Stl automatic planar face merge during import. Default is `True`.

merge_angle

: Stl import angle in radians for which faces will be considered planar. Default is `2e-2`.

# Returns

> bool
>
> : `True` when successful, `False` when failed.

# References

```pycon
>>> oEditor.Import
```

methods.name: ansys.aedt.core.modeler.cad.primitives.GeometryModeler.heal_objects
documentation:
Repair invalid geometry entities for the selected objects within the specified tolerance settings.

# Parameters

assignment

: List of object names to analyze.

auto_heal

: Auto heal option. Default value is `True`.

tolerant_stitch

: Tolerant stitch for manual healing. The default is `True`.

simplify_geometry

: Simplify geometry for manual healing. The default is `True`.

tighten_gaps

: Tighten gaps for manual healing. The default is `True`.

heal_to_solid

: Heal to solid for manual healing. The default is `False`.

stop_after_first_stitch_error

: Stop after first stitch error for manual healing. The default is `False`.

max_stitch_tolerance

: Max stitch tolerance for manual healing. The default is `0.001`.

explode_and_stitch

: Explode and stitch for manual healing. The default is `True`.

geometry_simplification_tolerance

: Geometry simplification tolerance for manual healing in mm. The default is `1`.

maximum_generated_radius

: Maximum generated radius for manual healing in mm. The default is `1`.

simplify_type

: Simplify type for manual healing. The default is `0` which refers to `Curves`.
  Other available values are `1` for `Surfaces` and `2` for `Both`.

tighten_gaps_width

: Tighten gaps width for manual healing in mm. The default is `0.00001`.

remove_silver_faces

: Remove silver faces for manual healing. The default is `True`.

remove_small_edges

: Remove small edges faces for manual healing. The default is `True`.

remove_small_faces

: Remove small faces for manual healing. The default is `True`.

silver_face_tolerance

: Silver face tolerance for manual healing in mm. The default is `1`.

small_edge_tolerance

: Silver face tolerance for manual healing in mm. The default is `1`.

small_face_area_tolerance

: Silver face tolerance for manual healing in mm^2. The default is `1`.

bounding_box_scale_factor

: Bounding box scaling factor for manual healing. The default is `0`.

remove_holes

: Remove holes for manual healing. The default is `True`.

remove_chamfers

: Remove chamfers for manual healing. The default is\`\`True\`\`.

remove_blends

: Remove blends for manual healing. The default is `True`.

hole_radius_tolerance

: Hole radius tolerance for manual healing in mm. The default is `1`.

chamfer_width_tolerance

: Chamfer width tolerance for manual healing in mm. The default is `1`.

blend_radius_tolerance

: Blend radius tolerance for manual healing in mm. The default is `1`.

allowable_surface_area_change

: Allowable surface area for manual healing in mm. The default is `1`.

allowable_volume_change

: Allowable volume change for manual healing in mm. The default is `1`.

# Returns

bool

: `True` when successful, `False` when failed.

methods.name: ansys.aedt.core.modeler.cad.primitives.GeometryModeler.simplify_objects
documentation:
Simplify command to converts complex objects into simpler primitives which are easy to mesh and solve.

# Parameters

assignment

: List of object names to simplify.

simplify_type

: Simplify type. The default is `"Polygon Fit"`. Options are
  `"Bounding Box"`, `"Polygon Fit"`, and "Primitive Fit".

extrusion_axis

: Extrusion axis. The default is `"Auto"`.
  Options are `"Auto"`, `"X"`, `"Y"`, and `"Z"`.

clean_up

: Whether to clean up. The default is `True`.

allow_splitting

: Whether to allow splitting. The default is `True`.

separate_bodies

: Whether to separate bodies. The default is `True`.

clone_body

: Whether to clone the body. The default is `True`.

generate_primitive_history

: Whether to generate primitive history. The default is `False`.
  If `True`, the history for the selected objects is purged.


interior_points_on_arc

: Number points on curve. The default is `5`.

length_threshold_percentage

: Length threshold percentage. The default is `25`.

create_group_for_new_objects

: Create group for new objects. The default is `False`.

# Returns

bool

: `True` when successful, `False` when failed.

methods.name: ansys.aedt.core.modeler.cad.primitives.GeometryModeler.polyline_segment
documentation:
New segment of a polyline.

# Parameters

type

: Type of the object. Choices are `"Line"`, `"Arc"`, `"Spline"`,
  and `"AngularArc"`.

num_seg

: Number of segments for the types `"Arc"`, `"Spline"`, and
  `"AngularArc"`. The default is `0`. For the type
  `Line`, this parameter is ignored.

num_points

: Number of control points for the type `Spline`. For other
  types, this parameter
  is defined automatically.

arc_angle

: Sweep angle in radians or a valid value string. For example,
  `"35deg"` or `"Specific
  to type AngularArc"`.

arc_center

: List of values in model units or a valid value string. For
  example, a list of `[x, y, z]` coordinates or `"Specific to
  type AngularArc"`.

arc_plane

: Plane in which the arc sweep is performed in the active
  coordinate system `"XY"`, `"YZ"` or `"ZX"`. The default is
  `None`, in which case the plane is determined automatically
  by the first coordinate for which the starting point and
  center point have the same value.

# Returns

{class}`ansys.aedt.core.modeler.polylines.PolylineSegment`

methods.name: ansys.aedt.core.modeler.cad.primitives.GeometryModeler.create_polyline
documentation:
Draw a polyline object in the 3D modeler.

This method retrieves the
{class}`ansys.aedt.core.modeler.cad.primitives.Polyline` object, which has
additional methods for manipulating the polyline. For example,
you can use
{func}`ansys.aedt.core.modeler.cad.primitives.Polyline.insert_segment` to
insert a segment or
{attr}`ansys.aedt.core.modeler.cad.primitives.Polyline.id` to retrieve the
ID of the polyline object.

# Parameters

points

: Array of positions of each point of the polyline. A
  position is a list of 2D or 3D coordinates. Position
  coordinate values can be numbers or valid AEDT string
  expressions. For example, `[0, 1, 2]`, `["0mm", "5mm",
  "1mm"]`, or `["x1", "y1", "z1"]`.

segment_type

: The default behavior is to connect all points as
  `"Line"` segments. The default is `None`.
  Use a `"PolylineSegment"`, for `"Line"`, `"Arc"`, `"Spline"`,
  or `"AngularArc"`.
  A list of segment types (str or {class}`ansys.aedt.core.modeler.cad.primitives.PolylineSegment`) is
  valid for a compound polyline.

cover_surface

: The default is `False`.

close_surface

: The default is `False`, which automatically joins the
  starting and ending points.

name

: Name of the polyline. The default is `None`.

material

: Name of the material. The default is `None`, in which case the
  default material is assigned.

xsection_type

: Type of the cross-section. Options are `"Line"`, `"Circle"`,
  `"Rectangle"`, and `"Isosceles Trapezoid"`. The default is `None`.

xsection_orient

: Direction of the normal vector to the width of the cross-section.
  Options are `"X"`, `"Y"`, `"Z"`, and `"Auto"`. The default is
  `None`, which sets the direction to `"Auto"`.

xsection_width

: Width or diameter of the cross-section for all types. The
  default is `1`.

xsection_topwidth

: Top width of the cross-section for type `"Isosceles Trapezoid"` only.
  The default is `1`.

xsection_height

: Height of the cross-section for type `"Rectangle"` or `"Isosceles
  Trapezoid"` only. The default is `1`.

xsection_num_seg

: Number of segments in the cross-section surface for type `"Circle"`,
  `"Rectangle"`, or `"Isosceles Trapezoid"`. The default is `0`. The
  value must be `0` or greater than `2`.

xsection_bend_type

: Type of the bend for the cross-section. The default is
  `None`, in which case the bend type is set to
  `"Corner"`. For the type `"Circle"`, the bend type
  should be set to `"Curved"`.

non_model

: Either if the polyline will be created as model or unmodel object.

# Returns

ansys.aedt.core.modeler.polylines.Polyline

: Polyline object.

# References

```pycon
>>> oEditor.CreatePolyline
```

# Examples

Set up the desktop environment.

```pycon
>>> from ansys.aedt.core.modeler.cad.polylines import PolylineSegment
>>> from ansys.aedt.core import Desktop
>>> from ansys.aedt.core import Maxwell3d
>>> desktop=Desktop(version="2024.2", new_desktop=False)
>>> m3d = Maxwell3d()
>>> m3d.modeler.model_units = "mm"
```

Define some test data points.

```pycon
>>> test_points = [["0mm", "0mm", "0mm"], ["100mm", "20mm", "0mm"],
...                ["71mm", "71mm", "0mm"], ["0mm", "100mm", "0mm"]]
```

The default behavior assumes that all points are to be
connected by line segments. Optionally specify the name.

```pycon
>>> P1 = m3d.modeler.create_polyline(test_points,name="PL_line_segments")
```

Specify that the first segment is a line and the last three
points define a three-point arc.

```pycon
>>> P2 = m3d.modeler.create_polyline(test_points,segment_type=["Line", "Arc"],name="PL_line_plus_arc")
```

Redraw the 3-point arc alone from the last three points and
additionally specify five segments using `PolylineSegment`.

```pycon
>>> P3 = m3d.modeler.create_polyline(test_points[1:],
...                                  segment_type=PolylineSegment(segment_type="Arc", num_seg=7),
...                                  name="PL_segmented_arc")
```

Specify that the four points form a spline and add a circular
cross-section with a diameter of 1 mm.

```pycon
>>> P4 = m3d.modeler.create_polyline(test_points,segment_type="Spline",name="PL_spline",
...                              xsection_type="Circle",xsection_width="1mm")
```

Use the `PolylineSegment` object to specify more detail about
the individual segments. Create a center point arc starting
from the position `test_points[1]`, rotating about the
center point position `test_points[0]` in the XY plane.

```pycon
>>> start_point = test_points[1]
>>> center_point = test_points[0]
>>> segment_def = PolylineSegment(segment_type="AngularArc", arc_center=center_point,
...                                arc_angle="90deg", arc_plane="XY")
>>> m3d.modeler.create_polyline(start_point,segment_type=segment_def,name="PL_center_point_arc")
```

Create a spline using a list of variables for the coordinates of the points.

```pycon
>>> x0, y0, z0 = "0", "0", "1"
>>> x1, y1, z1 = "1", "3", "1"
>>> x2, y2, z2 = "2", "2", "1"
>>> P5 = m3d.modeler.create_polyline(points=[[x0, y0, z0], [x1, y1, z1], [x2, y2, z2]],
...                              segment_type="Spline",name="polyline_with_variables")
```

Create a closed geometry by specifying in `segment_type` a list of `PolylineSegments` including
`AngularArc` segments.

```pycon
>>> test_points_1 = [[0.4, 0, 0],
...                 [-0.4, -0.6, 0],
...                 [0.4, 0, 0]]
>>> P6 = m3d.modeler.create_polyline(points=test_points_1,
...                                  segment_type=[PolylineSegment(segment_type="AngularArc",
...                                                                arc_center=[0, 0, 0],
...                                                                arc_angle="180deg",
...                                                                arc_plane="XY"),
...                                                PolylineSegment(segment_type="Line"),
...                                                PolylineSegment(segment_type="AngularArc",
...                                                                arc_center=[0, -0.6, 0],
...                                                                arc_angle="180deg",
...                                                                arc_plane="XY"),
...                                                PolylineSegment(segment_type="Line")])
```

methods.name: ansys.aedt.core.modeler.cad.primitives.GeometryModeler.create_udp
documentation:
Create a user-defined primitive (UDP).

# Parameters

dll

: Name of the UDP DLL or Python file. The default for the file format
  is `".dll"`.

parameters :

: List of the UDP parameters.

library

: Name of the UDP library. The default is `"syslib"`.

name

: Name of the component. The default is `None`.

# Returns

{class}`ansys.aedt.core.modeler.cad.object_3d.Object3d`

: UDP object created.

# References

```pycon
>>> oEditor.CreateUserDefinedPart
```

# Examples

```pycon
>>> my_udp = self.aedtapp.modeler.create_udp(dll="RMxprt/ClawPoleCore",parameters=my_udpPairs,library="syslib")
<class 'ansys.aedt.core.modeler.Object3d.Object3d'>
```

methods.name: ansys.aedt.core.modeler.cad.primitives.GeometryModeler.update_udp
documentation:

assignment

: Name of the object to update.

operation

: Name of the operation used to create the object.

parameters

: List of the UDP parameters to update and their value.

# Returns

bool

: `True` when successful.

# References

```pycon
>>> oEditor.CreateUserDefinedPart
```

# Examples

```pycon
>>> self.aedtapp.modeler.update_udp(assignment="ClawPoleCore",operation="CreateUserDefinedPart",
...                                 parameters=[["Length","110mm"], ["DiaGap","125mm"]])
True
```

documentation:
Retrieve the two closest edges that are not perpendicular for two objects.

# Parameters

start_object

: Name of the starting object.

end_object

: Name of the ending object.

direction

: Direction of the port to which to give edges precedence when more than two couples
  are at the same distance. For example, for a coax or microstrip, precedence is given
  to the edges that are on the given axis direction, such as `"XNeg"`. Options are
  `"XNeg"`, `"XPos"`, `"YNeg"`, `` "YPos`" ``, `"ZNeg"`, and `"ZPos"`.
  The default is `0`.

# Returns

list

: List with two edges if present.

  The default is `True`.

axis

: Axis direction. Choices are `0` through `5`. The default is `0`.

start_object

: Name of the starting object. The default is `""`.

end_object

: Name of the ending object. The default is `""`.

# Returns

list

: List of two created edges.

methods.name: ansys.aedt.core.modeler.cad.primitives.GeometryModeler.get_edges_on_bounding_box
documentation:
Retrieve the edges of the sheets passed in the input that are lying on the bounding box.

This method creates new lines for the detected edges and returns the IDs of these lines.
If required, only colinear edges are returned.

# Parameters

assignment

: ID or name for one or more sheets.

return_colinear

: Whether to return only colinear edges. The default is `True`.
  If `False`, all edges on the bounding box are returned.

tolerance

: Geometric tolerance. The default is `1e-6`.

# Returns

list

: List of edge IDs lying on the bounding box.

methods.name: ansys.aedt.core.modeler.cad.primitives.GeometryModeler.get_edges_for_circuit_port_from_sheet
documentation:
Retrieve two edge IDs that are suitable for a circuit port from a sheet.

One edge belongs to the sheet passed in the input, and the second edge
is the closest edge's coplanar to the first edge (aligned to the XY, YZ,
or XZ plane). This method creates new lines for the detected edges and returns
the IDs of these lines.

This method accepts one or more sheet objects as input,
while the method {func}`Primitives.get_edges_for_circuit_port`
accepts a face ID.

# Parameters

assignment

: ID or name for one or more sheets.

xy_plane

: Whether the edge's pair are to be on the XY plane.
  The default is `True`.

yz_plane

: Whether the edge's pair are to be on the YZ plane.
  The default is `True`.

xz_plane

: Whether the edge's pair are to be on the XZ plane.
  The default is `True`.

allow_perpendicular

: Whether the edge's pair are to be perpendicular.
  The default is `False`.

tolerance

: Geometric tolerance. The default is `1e-6`.

# Returns

list

: List of edge IDs.

methods.name: ansys.aedt.core.modeler.cad.primitives.GeometryModeler.get_edges_for_circuit_port
documentation:
Retrieve two edge IDs suitable for the circuit port.

One edge belongs to the face ID passed in the input, and the second edge
is the closest edge's coplanar to the first edge (aligned to the XY, YZ,
or XZ plane). This method creates new lines for the detected edges and returns
the IDs of these lines.

This method accepts a face ID in the input, while the `get_edges_for_circuit_port_from_port`
method accepts one or more sheet objects.

# Parameters

assignment :

: ID of the face.

xy_plane

: Whether the edge's pair are to be on the XY plane.
  The default is `True`.

yz_plane

: Whether the edge's pair are to be on the YZ plane.
  The default is `True`.

xz_plane

: Whether the edge's pair are to be on the XZ plane.
  The default is `True`.

allow_perpendicular

: Whether the edge's pair are to be perpendicular.
  The default is `False`.

tolerance

: Geometric tolerance. The default is `1e-6`.

# Returns

list

: List of edge IDs.

methods.name: ansys.aedt.core.modeler.cad.primitives.GeometryModeler._check_material
documentation:
Check for a material name.

If a material name exists, it is assigned. Otherwise, the material
specified as the default is assigned.

# Parameters

material

: Name of the material.

default_material

: Name of the default material to assign if `material` does not exist.

threshold

: Threshold conductivity in S/m to distinguish dielectric from conductor.
  The default value is `100000`.

# Returns

(str, bool)

: Material name, Boolean True if the material is a dielectric, otherwise False.

methods.name: ansys.aedt.core.modeler.cad.primitives.GeometryModeler.convert_segments_to_line
documentation:
Convert a CreatePolyline list of segments to lines.

This method applies to splines and 3-point arguments.

# Parameters

assignment

: Specified for the object.

# Returns

bool

: `True` if successful, `False` if it fails.

# References

```pycon
>>> oEditor.ChangeProperty
```

# Examples

```pycon
>>> from ansys.aedt.core import Hfss
>>> aedtapp = Hfss()
>>> edge_object = aedtapp.modeler.create_object_from_edge("my_edge")
>>> aedtapp.modeler.generate_object_history(edge_object)
>>> aedtapp.modeler.convert_segments_to_line(edge_object.name)
```

methods.name: ansys.aedt.core.modeler.cad.primitives.PrimitivesBuilder._create_coordinate_system
documentation: Create a coordinate system defined in the object.

Create primitives from a JSON file or dictionary of properties.

# Parameters

app :

: Inherited parent object.

input_file

: Path to a JSON file containing primitive settings.

input_dict

: Dictionary containing primitive settings.

# Returns

{class}`ansys.aedt.core.modeler.cad.primitives.PrimitivesBuilder`

: Primitives builder object if successful.

# Examples

```pycon
>>> from ansys.aedt.core import Hfss
>>> from ansys.aedt.core.modeler.cad.primitives import PrimitivesBuilder
>>> aedtapp = Hfss()
>>> primitive_file = "primitives_file.json"
>>> primitives_builder = PrimitivesBuilder(aedtapp, input_file=primitive_file)
>>> primitives_builder.create(),,
>>> aedtapp.release_desktop()
```

methods.name: ansys.aedt.core.modules.design_xploration.CommonOptimetrics._add_goal
documentation:
Add an optimization goal to the setup.

# Parameters

optigoalname

: Name of the optimization goal.

reporttype

: Type of the report.

solution

: Type of the solution. The default is `None`.

domain

: Type of the domain. The default is "Sweep"''.

calculation

: Name of the calculation. The default is `""`.

calculation_type

: Type of the calculation. The default is `"discrete"`.

calc_val1

: First value for the calculation. The default is `""`.

calc_val2

: Second value for the calculation. The default is `""`.

condition

: The condition for the calculation. The default is `"=="`.

goal_value

: Value for the optimization goal. The default is `1`.

goal_weight

: Weight for the optimization goal. The default is `1`.

goal_name

: Name of the goal. The default is `None`.

# Returns

methods.name: ansys.aedt.core.modules.design_xploration.CommonOptimetrics.analyze
documentation:
Solve the active design.

# Parameters

cores

: Number of simulation cores. The default is `1`.

tasks

: Number of simulation tasks. The default is `1`.

gpus

: Number of simulation graphic processing units to use. The default is `0`.

acf_file

: Full path to the custom ACF file.

use_auto_settings

: Set `True` to use automatic settings for HPC. The option is only considered for setups
  that support automatic settings.

solve_in_batch

: Whether to solve the project in batch or not.
  If `True` the project will be saved, closed, solved and repened.

machine

: Name of the machine if remote. The default is `"localhost"`.

run_in_thread

: Whether to submit the batch command as a thread. The default is
  `False`.

revert_to_initial_mesh

: Whether to revert to initial mesh before solving or not. Default is `False`.

# Returns

bool

: `True` when successful, `False` when failed.

# References

```pycon
>>> oDesign.Analyze
```

methods.name: ansys.aedt.core.modules.design_xploration.SetupOpti.add_calculation
documentation:
Add a calculation to the setup.

# Parameters

calculation

: Name of the calculation.

ranges

: Dictionary of ranges with respective values.
  Values can be: `None` for all values, a List of Discrete Values, a tuple of start and stop range.
  It includes intrinsics like "Freq", "Time", "Theta", "Distance".
  The default is `None`, to be used e.g. in "Eigenmode" design type.

solution

: Type of the solution. The default is `None`, in which case the default
  solution is used.

context

: Calculation contexts. It can be a sphere, a matrix or a polyline.

subdesign_id

: Subdesign id for Circuit and HFSS 3D Layout objects.

polyline_points

: Number of points for Polyline context.

report_type

: Override the auto computation of Calculation Type.

# Returns

bool

: `True` when successful, `False` when failed.

# References

```pycon
>>> oModule.EditSetup
```

methods.name: ansys.aedt.core.modules.design_xploration.SetupOpti.add_goal
documentation:
Add a goal to the setup.

# Parameters

calculation

: Name of the calculation.

ranges

: Dictionary of ranges with respective values.
  Values can be: `None` for all values, a List of Discrete Values, a tuple of start and stop range.
  It includes intrinsics like "Freq", "Time", "Theta", "Distance".

variables

: List of variables to include in the optimization.

condition

: The default is `"<="`.

goal_value

: Value for the goal. The default is `1`.

goal_weight

: Value for the goal weight. The default is `1`.

solution

: Type of the solution. The default is `None`, in which case the default
  solution is used.

context

: Calculation contexts. It can be a sphere, a matrix or a polyline.

subdesign_id

: Subdesign id for Circuit and HFSS 3D Layout objects.

polyline_points

: Number of points for Polyline context.

report_type

: Override the auto computation of Calculation Type.

# Returns

bool

: `True` when successful, `False` when failed.

# References

```pycon
>>> oModule.EditSetup
```

methods.name: ansys.aedt.core.modules.design_xploration.SetupOpti.add_variation
documentation:
Add a new variable as input for the optimization and defines its ranges.

Parameters
----------
variable_name : str
    Name of the variable.
min_value : float
    Minimum Optimization Value for variable_name.
max_value : float
    Maximum Optimization Value for variable_name.
starting_point : float, optional
    Starting point for optimization. If None, default will be used.
min_step : float
    Minimum Step Size for optimization. If None, 1/100 of the range will be used.
max_step : float
    Maximum Step Size for optimization. If None, 1/10 of the range will be used.
use_manufacturable : bool
    Either if to use or not the Manufacturable values. Default is False.
levels : list, optional
    List of available manufacturer levels.

Returns
-------
bool
Sets up an optimization in Opimetrics.

methods.name: ansys.aedt.core.modules.design_xploration.OptimizationSetups.add
documentation:
Add a basic optimization analysis.
You can customize all options after the analysis is added.

# Parameters

calculation

: Name of the calculation.

ranges

: Dictionary of ranges with respective values.
  Values can be: a list of discrete values, a dict with tuple args of start and stop range.
  It includes intrinsics like "Freq", "Time", "Theta", "Distance".

variables

: List of variables to include in the optimization. By default all variables are included.

optimization_type

: Optimization Type.
  Possible values are `"Optimization"`, `"DXDOE"`,\`"DesignExplorer"`,`"Sensitivity"`,`"Statistical"\`
  and `"optiSLang"`.

condition

: The default is `"<="`.

goal_value

: Value for the goal. The default is `1`.

goal_weight

: Value for the goal weight. The default is `1`.

solution

: Type of the solution. The default is `None`, in which case the default
  solution is used.

name

: Name of the analysis. The default is `None`, in which case a
  default name is assigned.

context

: Calculation contexts. It can be a sphere, a matrix or a polyline.

subdesign_id

: Subdesign id for Circuit and HFSS 3D Layout objects.

polyline_points

: Number of points for Polyline context.

report_type

: Override the auto computation of Calculation Type.

# Returns

{class}`ansys.aedt.core.modules.design_xploration.SetupOpti`

: Optimization object.

# References

```pycon
>>> oModule.InsertSetup
```

Sets up optimizations. It includes Optimization, DOE and DesignXplorer Analysis.

# Examples

```pycon
>>> from ansys.aedt.core import Hfss
>>> app = Hfss()
>>> optimization_setup = app.optimizations
```

> Dict\[int, {class}`ansys.aedt.core.modules.layer_stackup.Layer`\]
>
> : Number of layers in the current stackup.

methods.name: ansys.aedt.core.modules.layer_stackup.Layers.add_layer
documentation:
Add a layer.

# Parameters

layer

: Name of the layer.

layer_type

: Type of the layer. The default is `"signal"`.

thickness

: Thickness with units. The default is `"0mm"`.

elevation

: Elevation with units. The default is `"0mm"`.

material

: Name of the material. The default is `"copper"`.

isnegative

: If `True`, the geometry on the layer is cut away from the layer. The default is `False`.

# Returns

{class}`ansys.aedt.core.modules.layer_stackup.Layer`

: Layer object.

methods.name: ansys.aedt.core.modules.layer_stackup.Layers.change_stackup_type
documentation:
Change the stackup type between Multizone, Overlap and Laminate.

Parameters
----------
mode : str, optional
    Stackup type. Default is `"Multizone"`. Options are `"Overlap"` and `"Laminate"`.
number_zones : int, optional
    Number of zones of multizone. By default all layers will be enabled in all zones.

Returns
-------
bool
    `True` if successful.

Manages stackup for the Circuit and HFSS 3D Layout tools.

# Parameters

modeler : {class}`ansys.aedt.core.modeler.modeler_pcb.Modeler3DLayout`

roughnessunits

: Units for the roughness of layers. The default is `"um"`.

# Examples

```pycon
>>> from ansys.aedt.core import Hfss3dLayout
>>> app = Hfss3dLayout()
>>> layers = app.modeler.layers
```

  a coarse, standard, or very accurate mesh.

enable_stair_step

: Whether to enable a stair step. The default is `True`.


methods.name: ansys.aedt.core.modules.mesh_icepak.IcepakMesh.assign_priorities
documentation:
Set objects priorities.

# Parameters

assignment

: List of lists of objects. Each list corresponds to one priority level from low to high.
  This means that the first list has the lowest priority while the last list
  has the highest priority. Objects not explicitly passed in the lists are assigned
  to a priority level lower than the objects in the first list.

# Returns

bool

: `True` when successful, "False" when failed.

# References

```pycon
>>> oEditor.UpdatePriorityList
```

# Examples

```pycon
>>> ipk.mesh.assign_priorities([["Box1", "Rectangle1"], ["Box2", "Fan1_1"], ["Heatsink1_1"]])
```

methods.name: ansys.aedt.core.modules.mesh_icepak.IcepakMesh.add_priority
documentation:
Add priority to objects.

:::{deprecated} 0.9.1
:::

Use {func}`assign_priorities` function instead.

# Parameters

entity_type

: Type of the entity. Options are `1` and `2`, which represent respectively
  an object and a component.

assignment

: List of 3D objects, which can include conductors and dielectrics.
  If a non-3D object is passed, it is excluded.

component

: Name of the component. The default is `None`.

priority

: Level of priority. The default is `3`.

# Returns

bool

: `True` when successful, `False` when failed.

# References

```pycon
>>> oEditor.UpdatePriorityList
```

# Examples

```pycon
>>> from ansys.aedt.core import Icepak
>>> app = Icepak()
>>> app.mesh.add_priority(entity_type=1,assignment=app.modeler.object_names,priority=3)
>>> app.mesh.add_priority(entity_type=2,component=app.modeler.user_defined_component_names[0],priority=2)
```

documentation:
Get the object `Mesh` for a given mesh operation name.

# Parameters

part_id

: Mesh operation name.

# Returns

{class}`ansys.aedt.core.mesh.meshoperations`

: Returns None if the part ID or the object name is not found.

# Examples

Basic usage demonstrated with an HFSS design:

```pycon
>>> from ansys.aedt.core import Hfss
>>> hfss = Hfss()
>>> cylinder = hfss.modeler.create_cylinder(0,[0, 0, 0],3,20,0)
>>> mr1 = hfss.mesh.assign_model_resolution(cylinder,1e-4,"ModelRes1")
>>> mr2 = hfss.mesh[mr1.name]
```

methods.name: ansys.aedt.core.modules.mesh.Mesh.meshoperation_names
documentation:
Return the available mesh operation names.

# Returns

List

: List of mesh operation names.

# Examples

Basic usage demonstrated with an HFSS design:

```pycon
>>> from ansys.aedt.core import Hfss
>>> hfss = Hfss()
>>> o = hfss.modeler.create_cylinder(0,[0, 0, 0],3,20,0)
>>> mr1 = hfss.mesh.assign_model_resolution(o,1e-4,"ModelRes1")
>>> mr2 = hfss.mesh.assign_model_resolution(o,1e-2,"ModelRes2")
>>> mesh_operations_names = hfss.mesh.meshoperation_names
```

methods.name: ansys.aedt.core.modules.mesh.Mesh.assign_surface_mesh
documentation:
Assign a surface mesh level to one or more objects.

# Parameters

assignment

: One or more names of the objects.

level

: Level of the surface mesh. Options are `1` through `10`

name

: Name of the mesh operation. The default is `None`.

# Returns

{class}`ansys.aedt.core.modules.mesh.MeshOperation`

: Mesh operation object.

# References

```pycon
>>> oModule.AssignTrueSurfOp
```

# Examples

Basic usage demonstrated with an HFSS design:

```pycon
>>> from ansys.aedt.core import Hfss
>>> hfss = Hfss()
>>> o = hfss.modeler.create_cylinder(0,[0, 0, 0],3,20,0)
>>> surface = hfss.mesh.assign_surface_mesh(o.id,3,"Surface")
```

methods.name: ansys.aedt.core.modules.mesh.Mesh.assign_surface_mesh_manual
documentation:
Assign a surface mesh to a list of faces.

# Parameters

assignment

: List of faces to apply the surface mesh to.

surface_deviation

: Surface deviation. The default is `None`. You can specify a float value, a number with units, or `"inf"`.

normal_dev

: Normal deviation. The default is `None`.

aspect_ratio

: Aspect ratio. The default is `None`.

name

: Name of the mesh operation. The default is `None`.

# Returns

{class}`ansys.aedt.core.modules.mesh.MeshOperation`

: Mesh operation object.

# References

```pycon
>>> oModule.AssignTrueSurfOp
```

# Examples

Basic usage demonstrated with an HFSS design:

```pycon
>>> from ansys.aedt.core import Hfss
>>> hfss = Hfss()
>>> o = hfss.modeler.create_cylinder(0,[0, 0, 0],3,20,0)
>>> surface = hfss.mesh.assign_surface_mesh_manual(o.id,1e-6,aspect_ratio=3,name="Surface_Manual")
```

methods.name: ansys.aedt.core.modules.mesh.Mesh.assign_initial_mesh_from_slider
documentation:
Assign a surface mesh level to an object.

# Parameters

level

: Level of the surface mesh. Options are `1` through `10`. The default is `5.`

method

: Meshing method. Options are `"Auto"`, `"AnsoftTAU"`, and `"AnsoftClassic"`
  The default is `"Auto"`.

usedynamicsurface

: Whether to use a dynamic surface. The default is `True`.

useflexmesh

: Whether to use a flexible mesh. The default is `False`.

applycurvilinear

: Whether to apply curvilinear elements. The default is `False`.

usefallback

: Whether to retain as a fallback. The default is `True`.

usephi

: Whether to use the Phi mesher for layered geometry.
  The default is `True`.

automodelresolution

: Whether to automatically calculate the resolution length
  based on each object's effective thickness. The default is `True`.

modelresolutionlength

: Resolution thickness with units if `automodelresolution=False`.
  The default `"0.0001mm"`.

# Returns

bool

: `True` when successful, `False` when failed.

# References

```pycon
>>> oModule.InitialMeshSettings
```

methods.name: ansys.aedt.core.modules.mesh.Mesh.generate_mesh

```pycon

```

# Examples

```pycon
>>> from ansys.aedt.core import Maxwell3d

>>> m3d.create_setup(setupname="Setup1")
```


methods.name: ansys.aedt.core.modules.mesh.Mesh.assign_cylindrical_gap
documentation:
Assign a cylindrical gap for a 2D or 3D design to enable a clone mesh and associated band mapping angle.

# Parameters

entity

: Object to assign cylindrical gap to.

name

: Name of the mesh. The default is `None`, in which
  case the default name is used.

clone_mesh

: Whether to clone the mesh. This parameter is valid only for 3D design.
  The default is `False`. If `True`, the solid bodies adjacent to the band
  are detected to identify the clone object.

band_mapping_angle

: Band mapping angle in degrees.
  The recommended band mapping angle (the angle the rotor rotates in one time step) typically
  equals the rotational speed multiplied by the time step.
  The band mapping angle must be between 0.0005 and 3 degrees.
  The default is `None`.

  - For a 2D design, if a value is provided, the option `Use band mapping angle`
    is automatically enabled.
  - For a 3D design, the `Clone Mesh` option has to be enabled first.

moving_side_layers

: Number of mesh layers on the moving side.
  The valid ranges are integers greater or equal to 1.
  This parameter is valid only for a 3D design.
  The default is `1`.

static_side_layers

: Number of mesh layers on the static side.
  The valid ranges are integers greater than or equal to 1.
  This parameter is valid only for a 3D design.
  The default is `1`.

# Returns

{class}`ansys.aedt.core.modules.mesh.MeshOperation` or bool

: Mesh operation object or `False` if it fails.

# References

```pycon
>>> oModule.AssignCylindricalGapOp
```

Manages AEDT mesh functions for 2D and 3D solvers (HFSS, Maxwell, and Q3D).

# Parameters

app : {class}`ansys.aedt.core.application.analysis_3d.FieldAnalysis3D`

# Examples

Basic usage demonstrated with an HFSS design:

```pycon
>>> from ansys.aedt.core import Hfss
>>> hfss = Hfss()
>>> cylinder = hfss.modeler.create_cylinder(0,[0, 0, 0],3,20,0)
>>> model_resolution = hfss.mesh.assign_model_resolution(cylinder,1e-4,"ModelRes1")
```

methods.name: ansys.aedt.core.modules.material.MatProperty.add_thermal_modifier_free_form
documentation:
Add a thermal modifier to a material property using a free-form formula.

# Parameters

formula

: Full formula to apply.

index

: Value for the index. The default is `0`.

# Returns

bool

: `True` when successful, `False` when failed.

# References

```pycon
>>> oDefinitionManager.EditMaterial
```

# Examples

```pycon
>>> from ansys.aedt.core import Hfss
>>> hfss = Hfss(version="2021.2")
>>> mat1 = hfss.materials.add_material("new_copper2")
>>> mat1.add_thermal_modifier_free_form("if(Temp > 1000cel, 1, if(Temp < -273.15cel, 1, 1))")
```

methods.name: ansys.aedt.core.modules.material.MatProperty.add_thermal_modifier_closed_form
documentation:
Add a thermal modifier to a material property using a closed-form formula.

# Parameters

tref

: Reference temperature. The default is `22`.

c1

: First coefficient value. The default is `0.0001`.

c2

: Second coefficient value. The default is `1e-6`.

tl

: Lower temperature limit. The default is `273.15`.

tu

: Upper temperature limit. The default is `1000`.

units

: Units for the reference temperature. The default
  is `"cel"`.

auto_calc

: Whether to calculate the lower and upper
  temperature limits automatically. The default is
  `True`.

tml

: Lower temperature limit when `auto_calc=True.`
  The default is `1000`.

tmu

: Upper temperature limit when `auto_calc=True.`
  The default is `1000`.

index

: Value for the index. The default is `0`.

# Returns

bool

: `True` when successful, `False` when failed.

# References

```pycon
>>> oDefinitionManager.EditMaterial
```

# Examples

```pycon
>>> from ansys.aedt.core import Hfss
>>> hfss = Hfss(version="2021.2")
>>> mat1 = hfss.materials.add_material("new_copper2")
>>> mat1.permittivity.add_thermal_modifier_closed_form(c1 = 1e-3)
```

methods.name: ansys.aedt.core.modules.material.MatProperty._set_non_linear
documentation:
Enable non-linear material.

: This is a private method, and should not be used directly.

# Parameters

x_unit

: X units. Defaults will be used if `None`.

y_unit

: Y units. Defaults will be used if `None`.

# Returns

bool

: `True` if succeeded.

# Examples

```pycon
>>> from ansys.aedt.core import Hfss
>>> hfss = Hfss(version="2023.2")
>>> B_value = [0.0, 0.1, 0.3, 0.4, 0.48, 0.55, 0.6, 0.61, 0.65]
>>> H_value = [0.0, 500.0, 1000.0, 1500.0, 2000.0, 2500.0, 3500.0, 5000.0, 10000.0]
>>> mat = hfss.materials.add_material("newMat")
>>> b_h_dataset = [[b, h] for b, h in zip(B_value, H_value)]
>>> mat.permeability = b_h_dataset
```

methods.name: ansys.aedt.core.modules.material.Material.material_appearance
documentation:
Material appearance specified as a list where the first three items are
RGB color and the fourth one is transparency.

# Returns

list

: Color of the material in RGB and transparency.
  Color values are in the range `[0, 255]`.
  Transparency is a float in the range `[0,1]`.

# Examples

Create a material with color `[0, 153, 153]` (darker cyan) and transparency `0.5`.

```pycon
>>> from ansys.aedt.core import Hfss
>>> hfss = Hfss(version="2021.2")
>>> mat1 = hfss.materials.add_material("new_material")
>>> appearance_props = mat1.material_appearance
>>> mat1.material_appearance = [0, 153, 153, 0.5]
```

methods.name: ansys.aedt.core.modules.material.Material.get_core_loss_coefficients
documentation:
Get electrical steel or power ferrite core loss coefficients at a given frequency.

# Parameters

points_at_frequency

: Dictionary where keys are the frequencies (in Hz) and values are lists of points (BP curve).
  If the core loss model is calculated at one frequency, this parameter must be provided as a
  dictionary with one key (single frequency in Hz) and values are lists of points at
  that specific frequency (BP curve).

core_loss_model_type

: Core loss model type. The default value is `"Electrical Steel"`.
  Options are `"Electrical Steel"` and `"Power Ferrite"`.

thickness

: Thickness provided as the value plus the unit.
  The default is `0.5mm`.

conductivity

: Material conductivity.
  The default is `0`.

coefficient_setup

: Core loss unit. The default is `"w_per_cubic_meter"`.
  Options are `"kw_per_cubic_meter"`, `"w_per_cubic_meter"`, `"w_per_kg"`,
  and `"w_per_lb"`.

# Returns

list

: List of core loss coefficients.
  Returns Kh, Kc, and Ke coefficients if the core loss model is `"Electrical Steel"`.
  Returns Cm, X, and Y if the core loss model is `"Power Ferrite"`.

# Examples

This example shows how to get core loss coefficients for Electrical Steel core loss model.

```pycon
>>> from ansys.aedt.core import Maxwell3d
>>> m3d = Maxwell3d()
>>> box = m3d.modeler.create_box([-10, -10, 0],[20, 20, 20],"box_to_split")
>>> box.material = "magnesium"
>>> coefficients = m3d.materials["magnesium"].get_core_loss_coefficients(
...                                                         points_at_frequency={60 : [[0, 0], [1, 3], [2, 7]]},
...                                                         thickness="0.5mm",conductivity=0)
>>> print(coefficients)
>>> m3d.release_desktop(True, True)
```

methods.name: ansys.aedt.core.modules.material.Material.set_coreloss_at_frequency
documentation:
Set electrical steel or power ferrite core loss model at one single frequency or at multiple frequencies.

# Parameters

points_at_frequency

: Dictionary where keys are the frequencies (in Hz) and values are lists of points (BP curve).
  If the core loss model is calculated at one frequency, this parameter must be provided as a
  dictionary with one key (single frequency in Hz) and values are lists of points at
  that specific frequency (BP curve).

kdc

: Coefficient considering the DC flux bias effects

cut_depth

: Equivalent cut depth.
  You use this parameter to consider the manufacturing effects on core loss computation.
  The default value is `"1mm"`.

thickness

: Thickness specified in terms of the value plus the unit.
  The default is `"0.5mm"`.

conductivity

: Conductivity. The unit is S/m.
  The default is `"0 S/m"`.

coefficient_setup

: Core loss unit. The default is `"w_per_cubic_meter"`.
  Options are `"kw_per_cubic_meter"`, `"w_per_cubic_meter"`, `"w_per_kg"`,
  and `"w_per_lb"`.

core_loss_model_type

: Core loss model type. The default value is `"Electrical Steel"`.
  Options are `"Electrical Steel"` and `"Power Ferrite"`.

# Returns

bool

: `True` when successful, `False` when failed.

# References

```pycon
>>> oDefinitionManager.EditMaterial
```

# Examples

This example shows how to set a core loss model for a material in case material properties are calculated for
core losses at one frequency or core losses versus frequencies (core losses multicurve data).
The first case shows how to set properties for core losses at one frequency:

```pycon
>>> from ansys.aedt.core import Maxwell3d
>>> m3d = Maxwell3d()
>>> box = m3d.modeler.create_box([-10, -10, 0],[20, 20, 20],"box_to_split")
>>> box.material = "magnesium"
>>> m3d.materials["magnesium"].set_coreloss_at_frequency(
                                            ... points_at_frequency={60 : [[0,0], [1,3.5], [2,7.4]]}
                                            ... )
>>> m3d.release_desktop(True, True)
```

The second case shows how to set properties for core losses versus frequencies:

```pycon
>>> from ansys.aedt.core import Maxwell3d
>>> m3d = Maxwell3d()
>>> box = m3d.modeler.create_box([-10, -10, 0],[20, 20, 20],"box_to_split")
>>> box.material = "magnesium"
>>> m3d.materials["magnesium"].set_coreloss_at_frequency(
                                            ... points_at_frequency={60 : [[0,0], [1,3.5], [2,7.4]],
                                            ...                      100 : [[0,0], [1,8], [2,9]],
                                            ...                      150 : [[0,0], [1,10], [2,19]]}
                                            ... )
>>> m3d.release_desktop(True, True)
```

methods.name: ansys.aedt.core.modules.material.Material.set_electrical_steel_coreloss
documentation:
Set electrical steel core loss.

# Parameters

kh

: Hysteresis core loss coefficient.
  The default is `0`.

kc

: Eddy-current core loss coefficient.
  The default is `0`.

ke

: Excess core loss coefficient.
  The default is `0`.

kdc

: Coefficient considering the DC flux bias effects.
  The default is `0`.

cut_depth

: Equivalent cut depth considering manufacturing effects on core loss computation.
  The default value is `"1mm"`.

# Returns

bool

methods.name: ansys.aedt.core.modules.material_lib.Materials.add_material

When the added material object is returned, you can customize
the material. This method does not update the material.

name

properties

```pycon
>>> from ansys.aedt.core import Hfss
```


methods.name: ansys.aedt.core.modules.material_lib.Materials.duplicate_material
documentation:
Duplicate a material.

# Parameters

material

: Name of the material.

name

: Name for the copy of the material. If a new name is not specified,
  the new material name is `material_name` plusa "\_clone"\`\` suffix.

properties

: List of properties to parameterize when the material is duplicated.
  Parameterized properties have project scope. Options are:

  - `'permittivity'`
  - `'permeability'`
  - `'conductivity'`
  - '`dielectric_loss_tan'`
  - '`magnetic_loss_tan'`

# Returns

{class}`ansys.aedt.core.modules.material.Material`

# References

```pycon
>>> oDefinitionManager.AddMaterial
```

# Examples

```pycon
>>> from ansys.aedt.core import Hfss
>>> hfss = Hfss()
>>> hfss.materials.add_material("MyMaterial")
>>> hfss.materials.duplicate_material("MyMaterial","MyMaterial2")
```

methods.name: ansys.aedt.core.visualization.post.farfield_exporter.FfdSolutionDataExporter.__create_geometries
documentation: Export the geometry in OBJ format.

Class to enable export of embedded element pattern data from HFSS.

An instance of this class is returned from the
{meth}`ansys.aedt.core.Hfss.get_antenna_data` method. This method allows creation of
the embedded
element pattern files for an antenna that have been solved in HFSS. The
`metadata_file` properties can then be passed as arguments to
instantiate an instance of the
{class}`ansys.aedt.core.ansys.aedt.core.visualization.advanced.farfield_visualization.FfdSolutionData` class for
subsequent analysis and postprocessing of the array data.

Note that this class is derived from the {class}`FfdSolutionData` class and can be used directly for
far-field postprocessing and array analysis, but it remains a property of the
{class}`ansys.aedt.core.Hfss` application.

# Parameters

app

: HFSS application instance.

sphere_name

: Infinite sphere to use.

setup_name

: Name of the setup. Make sure to build a setup string in the form of `"SetupName : SetupSweep"`.

frequencies

: Frequency list to export. Specify either a list of strings with units or a list of floats in Hertz units.
  For example, `["9GHz", 9e9]`.

variations

: Dictionary of all families including the primary sweep. The default value is `None`.

overwrite

: Whether to overwrite the existing far field solution data. The default is `True`.

export_touchstone

: Whether to export touchstone file. The default is `False`. Working from 2024 R1.

set_phase_center_per_port

: Set phase center per port location. The default is `True`.

# Examples

```pycon
>>> from ansys.aedt.core
>>> app = ansys.aedt.core.Hfss(version="2023.2", design="Antenna")
>>> setup_name = "Setup1 : LastAdaptive"
>>> frequencies = [77e9]
>>> sphere = "3D"
>>> data = app.get_antenna_data(frequencies,setup_name,sphere)
>>> data.plot_3d(quantity_format="dB10")
```

methods.name: ansys.aedt.core.modules.solve_sweeps.SweepHFSS.add_subrange
documentation:
Add a range to the sweep.

# Parameters

range_type

: Type of the range. Options are `"LinearCount"`,
  `"LinearStep"`, `"LogScale"`, and `"SinglePoints"`.

start

: Starting frequency.

end

: Stopping frequency. The default value is `None`. A value is required for
  `range_type="LinearCount"|"LinearStep"|"LogScale"`.

count

: Frequency count or frequency step. The default is `None`. A value is required for
  `range_type="LinearCount"|"LinearStep"|"LogScale"`.

unit

: Unit of the frequency. For example, `"MHz` or `"GHz"`. The default is `"GHz"`.

save_single_fields

: Whether to save the fields of the single point. The default is `False`.
  This parameter is used only for `range_type="SinglePoints"`.

clear

: Whether to suppress all other subranges except the current one under creation.
  The default value is `False`.

# Returns

bool

: `True` when successful, `False` when failed.

# Examples

Create a setup in an HFSS design and add multiple sweep ranges.

```pycon
>>> setup = hfss.create_setup(name="MySetup")
>>> sweep = setup.add_sweep()
>>> sweep.change_type("Interpolating")
>>> sweep.change_range("LinearStep", 1.1, 2.1, 0.4, "GHz")
>>> sweep.add_subrange("LinearCount",1,1.5,5,"MHz")
>>> sweep.add_subrange("LogScale",1,3,10,"GHz")
```
