methods.name: ansys.aedt.core.modules.solve_sweeps.SweepHFSS._get_args
documentation:
Get arguments.

# Parameters

props

: Dictionary of the properties. The default is `None`, in which
  case the default properties are retrieved.

# Returns

dict

: Dictionary of the properties.

Initializes, creates, and updates sweeps in HFSS.

# Parameters

setup

: Setup to use for the analysis.

name

: Name of the sweep.

sweep_type

: Type of the sweep. Options are `"Fast"`, `"Interpolating"`,
  and `"Discrete"`. The default is `"Interpolating"`.

props

: Dictionary of the properties. The default is `None`, in which case
  the default properties are retrieved.

# Examples

```pycon
>>> hfss = Hfss(version=version, project=proj, design=gtemDesign, solution_type=solutiontype,
                name=name, new_desktop=False, close_on_exit=False)
>>> hfss_setup = hfss.setups[0]
>>> hfss_sweep = SweepHFSS(hfss_setup, 'Sweep', sweep_type ='Interpolating', props=None)
```

documentation:
Change the range of the sweep.

# Parameters

range_type

: Type of the subrange. Options are `"LinearCount"`, `"SinglePoint"`,
  `"LinearStep"`, and `"LogScale"`.

start

: Starting frequency.

end

: Stopping frequency. The default is `None`. A value is required
  for these subranges: `"LinearCount"`, `"LinearStep"`, and `"LogScale"`.

count

: Frequency count or frequency step. The default is `None`.
  A value is required for these subranges: `"LinearCount"`,
  `"LinearStep"`, and `"LogScale"`.

unit

: Unit of the frequency. For example, `"MHz` or `"GHz"`. The default is `"GHz"`.

# Returns

bool

: `True` when successful, `False` when failed.

methods.name: ansys.aedt.core.modules.solve_setup.CommonSetup.analyze
documentation:
Solve the active design.

# Parameters

cores

: Number of simulation cores. The default is `1`.

tasks

: Number of simulation tasks. The default is `1`.

gpus

: Number of simulation graphic processing units to use. The default is `0`.

acf_file

: Full path to the custom ACF file.

use_auto_settings

: Set `True` to use automatic settings for HPC. The option is only considered for setups
  that support automatic settings.

solve_in_batch

: Whether to solve the project in batch or not.
  If `True` the project will be saved, closed, and solved.

machine

: Name of the machine if remote. The default is `"localhost"`.

run_in_thread

: Whether to submit the batch command as a thread. The default is
  `False`.

revert_to_initial_mesh

: Whether to revert to initial mesh before solving or not. Default is `False`.

blocking

: Whether to block script while analysis is completed or not. It works from AEDT 2023 R2.
  Default is `True`.

# Returns

bool

: `True` when successful, `False` when failed.

# References

```pycon
>>> oDesign.Analyze
```

methods.name: ansys.aedt.core.modules.solve_setup.CommonSetup.get_solution_data
documentation:
Get a simulation result from a solved setup and cast it in a `SolutionData` object.
Data to be retrieved from Electronics Desktop are any simulation results available in that
specific simulation context.
Most of the argument have some defaults which works for most of the `Standard` report quantities.

# Parameters

expressions

: One or more formulas to add to the report. Example is value `"dB(S(1,1))"` or a list of values.
  Default is `None` which will return all traces.

domain

: Plot Domain. Options are "Sweep" for frequency domain related results and "Time" for transient related data.

variations

: Dictionary of all families including the primary sweep.
  The default is `None` which will use the nominal variations of the setup.

primary_sweep_variable

: Name of the primary sweep. The default is `"None"` which, depending on the context,
  will internally assign the primary sweep to:
  1\. `Freq` for frequency domain results,
  2\. `Time` for transient results,
  3\. `Theta` for radiation patterns,
  4\. `distance` for field plot over a polyline.

report_category

: Category of the Report to be created. If `None` default data Report will be used.
  The Report Category can be one of the types available for creating a report depend on the simulation setup.
  For example for a Far Field Plot in HFSS the UI shows the report category as "Create Far Fields Report".
  The report category will be in this case "Far Fields".
  Depending on the setup different categories are available.
  If `None` default category will be used (the first item in the Results drop down menu in AEDT).
  To get the list of available categories user can use method `available_report_types`.

context

: This is the context of the report.
  The default is `None`. It can be:
  1\. `None`
  2\. Infinite Sphere name for Far Fields Plot.
  3\. Dictionary. If dictionary is passed, key is the report property name and value is property value.

polyline_points

: Number of points on which to create the report for plots on polylines.
  This parameter is valid for `Fields` plot only.

math_formula

: One of the available AEDT mathematical formulas to apply. For example, `abs, dB`.

sweep

: Name of the sweep adaptive setup to get solutions from. the default is `LastAdaptive`.

# Returns

{class}`ansys.aedt.core.modules.solutions.SolutionData`

: Solution Data object.

# References

```pycon
>>> oModule.GetSolutionDataPerVariation
```

# Examples

```pycon
>>> from ansys.aedt.core import Hfss
>>> aedtapp = Hfss()
>>> aedtapp.post.create_report("dB(S(1,1))")
```

```pycon
>>> variations = aedtapp.available_variations.nominal_w_values_dict
>>> variations["Theta"] = ["All"]
>>> variations["Phi"] = ["All"]
>>> variations["Freq"] = ["30GHz"]
>>> data1 = aedtapp.post.get_solution_data("GainTotal", aedtapp.nominal_adaptive,
...                                        variations=variations, primary_sweep_variable="Phi",
...                                        report_category="Far Fields", context="3D")
```

```pycon
>>> data2 =aedtapp.post.get_solution_data("S(1,1)",aedtapp.nominal_sweep,variations=variations)
>>> data2.plot()
```

```pycon
>>> from ansys.aedt.core import Maxwell2d
>>> maxwell_2d = Maxwell2d()
>>> data3 = maxwell_2d.post.get_solution_data("InputCurrent(PHA)",domain="Time",primary_sweep_variable="Time")
>>> data3.plot("InputCurrent(PHA)")
```

```pycon
>>> from ansys.aedt.core import Circuit
>>> circuit = Circuit()
>>> context = {"algorithm": "FFT", "max_frequency": "100MHz", "time_stop": "2.5us", "time_start": "0ps"}
>>> spectralPlotData = circuit.post.get_solution_data(expressions="V(Vprobe1)", domain="Spectral",
...                                                   primary_sweep_variable="Spectrum", context=context)
```

methods.name: ansys.aedt.core.modules.solve_setup.CommonSetup.create_report
documentation:
Create a report in AEDT. It can be a 2D plot, 3D plot, polar plot, or data table.

# Parameters

expressions

: One or more formulas to add to the report. Example is value = `"dB(S(1,1))"`.

domain

: Plot Domain. Options are "Sweep", "Time", "DCIR".

variations

: Dictionary of all families including the primary sweep. The default is `{"Freq": ["All"]}`.

primary_sweep_variable

: Name of the primary sweep. The default is `"Freq"`.

secondary_sweep_variable

: Name of the secondary sweep variable in 3D Plots.

report_category

: Category of the Report to be created. If `None` default data Report will be used.
  The Report Category can be one of the types available for creating a report depend on the simulation setup.
  For example for a Far Field Plot in HFSS the UI shows the report category as "Create Far Fields Report".
  The report category will be in this case "Far Fields".
  Depending on the setup different categories are available.
  If `None` default category will be used (the first item in the Results drop down menu in AEDT).

plot_type

: The format of Data Visualization. Default is `Rectangular Plot`.

context

: The default is `None`. It can be `None`, `"Differential Pairs"`,\`"RL"`` ,
  `"Sources" ``, `"Vias"`,\`"Bondwires"`` , `"Probes" `` for Hfss3dLayout or
  Reduce Matrix Name for Q2d/Q3d solution or Infinite Sphere name for Far Fields Plot.

name

: Name of the plot. The default is `None`.

polyline_points

: Number of points for creating the report for plots on polylines.

subdesign_id

: Specify a subdesign ID to export a Touchstone file of this subdesign to.
  This parameter is valid only for a circuit.
  The default value is `None`.

sweep

: Name of the sweep adaptive setup to get solutions from. The default is `LastAdaptive`.

# Returns

{class}`ansys.aedt.core.modules.report_templates.Standard`

: `True` when successful, `False` when failed.

# References

```pycon
>>> oModule.CreateReport
```

# Examples

```pycon
>>> from ansys.aedt.core import Circuit
>>> aedtapp = Circuit()
>>> aedtapp.post.create_report("dB(S(1,1))")
```

```pycon
>>> variations = aedtapp.available_variations.nominal_w_values_dict
>>> aedtapp.post.setups[0].create_report("dB(S(1,1))",variations=variations,primary_sweep_variable="Freq")
```

```pycon
>>> aedtapp.post.create_report("S(1,1)",variations=variations,plot_type="Smith Chart")
```

methods.name: ansys.aedt.core.modules.solve_setup.Setup.enable_expression_cache
documentation:
Enable an expression cache.

# Parameters

expressions

: One or more formulas to add to the expression cache.

report_type

: Type of the report for the expression. The default is `Fields`. If a list of expressions
  is supplied, supply a corresponding list of report types.

intrinsics

: Intrinsic functions for the expressions. The default is `""`. If a list of expressions
  is supplied, a corresponding list of intrinsic functions must be supplied.

isconvergence

: Whether the expression is in the convergence criteria. The default is `True`.
  If a list of expressions is supplied, a corresponding list of Boolean values must be
  supplied.

isrelativeconvergence

: The default is `True`.

conv_criteria :

: The default is `1`.

use_cache_for_pass

: Use cache for pass.
  Default value is `True`.

use_cache_for_freq

: Use cache for frequency.
  Default value is `True`.

# Returns

bool

: `True` when successful, `False` when failed.

# References

```pycon
>>> oModule.EditSetup
```

methods.name: ansys.aedt.core.modules.solve_setup.Setup.add_mesh_link
documentation:
Import mesh from a source design solution to the target design.

# Parameters

design

: Name of the source design.

solution

: Name of the source design solution in the format `"name : solution_name"`.
  If `None`, the default value is `name : LastAdaptive`.

parameters

: Dictionary of the "mapping" variables from the source design.
  If `None`, the default is `appname.available_variations.nominal_w_values_dict`.

project

: Name of the project with the design. The default is `"This Project*"`.
  However, you can supply the full path and name to another project.

force_source_to_solve

: Default value is `True`.

preserve_partner_solution

: Default value is `True`.

apply_mesh_operations

: Apply mesh operations in target design on the imported mesh.
  Default value is `True`.

adapt_port

: Perform port adapt/seeding in target solve setup.
  Default value is `True`.

# Returns

bool

: `True` when successful, `False` when failed.

# References

```pycon
>>> oModule.EditSetup
```

# Examples

```pycon
>>> from ansys.aedt.core import Maxwell3d
>>> m3d = Maxwell3d(design="source_design")
>>> m3d.create_setup(name="setup1")
The target design is duplicated from the source design and made it active design.
>>> m3d.duplicate_design(name="source_design", save_after_duplicate=True)
The mesh link is assigned to the target design analysis setup.
>>> m3d.setups[0].add_mesh_link(design="source_design", solution=m3d.nominal_adaptive)
>>> m3d.release_desktop()
```

methods.name: ansys.aedt.core.modules.solve_setup.Setup.start_continue_from_previous_setup
documentation:
Start or continue from a previously solved setup.

# Parameters

design

: Name of the design.

solution

: Name of the solution in the format `"name : solution_name"`.
  For example, `"Setup1 : Transient", "MySetup : LastAdaptive"`.

map_variables_by_name

: Whether variables are mapped by name from the source design. The default is
  `True`.

parameters

: Dictionary of the parameters. This parameter is not considered if
  `map_variables_by_name=True`. If `None`, the default is
  `appname.available_variations.nominal_w_values_dict`.

project

: Name of the project with the design. The default is `"This Project*"`.
  However, you can supply the full path and name to another project.

force_source_to_solve

: The default is `True`.

preserve_partner_solution

: The default is `True`.

# Returns

bool

: `True` when successful, `False` when failed.

# References

```pycon
>>> oModule.EditSetup
```

# Examples

```pycon
>>> m2d = ansys.aedt.core.Maxwell2d()
>>> setup = m2d.get_setup("Setup1")
>>> setup.start_continue_from_previous_setup(design="IM",solution="Setup1 : Transient")
```

methods.name: ansys.aedt.core.modules.solve_setup.SetupCircuit.add_sweep_points
documentation:
Add a linear count sweep to existing Circuit Setup.

# Parameters

sweep_variable

: Variable to which the sweep belongs. Default is `"Freq`.

sweep_points
: Sweep points to apply linear sweep. It can be a list or single points.

  : Points can be float or str. If `str` then no units will be applied.

end_point float or str, optional

: End Point of Linear Count sweep. If `str` then no units will be applied.

units

: Sweeps Units. It will be ignored if strings are provided as start_point or end_point

override_existing_sweep

: Define if existing sweep on the same variable has to be overridden or kept and added to this new sweep.

# Returns

bool

: `True` is succeeded.

# References

```pycon
>>> oModule.EditLinearNetworkAnalysis
>>> oModule.EditDCAnalysis
>>> oModule.EditTransient
>>> oModule.EditQuickEyeAnalysis
>>> oModule.EditVerifEyeAnalysis
>>> oModule.EditAMIAnalysis
```

methods.name: ansys.aedt.core.modules.solve_setup.SetupCircuit.add_sweep_count
documentation:
Add a step sweep to existing Circuit Setup. It can be `"Linear"`, `"Decade"` or `"Octave"`.

# Parameters

sweep_variable

: Variable that the sweep belongs to. The default is `"Freq`.

start

: Start point of the linear count sweep. The default is `1`.
  If a string `str` is specified, no units are applied.

stop

: End point of the linear count sweep. The default is `100`.
  If a string is specified, no units are applied.

count

: Number of points. Default is `100`.

units

: Sweeps Units. It will be ignored if strings are provided as start_point or end_point.

count_type

: Count Type. Default is `"Linear"`. It can be also `"Decade"` or `"Octave"`.

override_existing_sweep

: Define if existing sweep on the same variable has to be overridden or kept and added to this new sweep.

# Returns

bool

: `True` is succeeded.

# References

```pycon
>>> oModule.EditLinearNetworkAnalysis
>>> oModule.EditDCAnalysis
>>> oModule.EditTransient
>>> oModule.EditQuickEyeAnalysis
>>> oModule.EditVerifEyeAnalysis
>>> oModule.EditAMIAnalysis
```

methods.name: ansys.aedt.core.modules.solve_setup.SetupCircuit.add_sweep_step
documentation:
Add a linear count sweep to existing Circuit Setup.

# Parameters

sweep_variable

: Variable to which the sweep belongs. Default is `"Freq`.

start

: Start point of the linear count sweep. The default is `1`.
  If a string `str` is specified, no units are applied.

stop

: End point of the linear count sweep. The default is `100`.
  If a string is specified, no units are applied.

step_size

: Step size of the sweep. The default is `1`.
  If a string is specified, no units are applied.

units

: Sweeps Units. It will be ignored if strings are provided as start_point or end_point.

override_existing_sweep

: Define if existing sweep on the same variable has to be overridden or kept and added to this new sweep.

# Returns

bool

: `True` is succeeded.

# References

```pycon
>>> oModule.EditLinearNetworkAnalysis
>>> oModule.EditDCAnalysis
>>> oModule.EditTransient
>>> oModule.EditQuickEyeAnalysis
>>> oModule.EditVerifEyeAnalysis
>>> oModule.EditAMIAnalysis
```

methods.name: ansys.aedt.core.modules.solve_setup.SetupCircuit.get_solution_data
documentation:
Get a simulation result from a solved setup and cast it in a `SolutionData` object.
Data to be retrieved from Electronics Desktop are any simulation results available in that
specific simulation context.
Most of the argument have some defaults which works for most of the `Standard` report quantities.

# Parameters

expressions

: One or more formulas to add to the report. Example is value `"dB(S(1,1))"` or a list of values.
  Default is `None` which will return all traces.

domain

: Plot Domain. Options are "Sweep" for frequency domain related results and "Time" for transient related data.

variations

: Dictionary of all families including the primary sweep.
  The default is `None` which will use the nominal variations of the setup.

primary_sweep_variable

: Name of the primary sweep. The default is `"None"` which, depending on the context,
  will internally assign the primary sweep to:
  1\. `Freq` for frequency domain results,
  2\. `Time` for transient results,
  3\. `Theta` for radiation patterns,
  4\. `distance` for field plot over a polyline.

report_category

: Category of the Report to be created. If `None` default data Report will be used.
  The Report Category can be one of the types available for creating a report depend on the simulation setup.
  For example for a Far Field Plot in HFSS the UI shows the report category as "Create Far Fields Report".
  The report category will be in this case "Far Fields".
  Depending on the setup different categories are available.
  If `None` default category will be used (the first item in the Results drop down menu in AEDT).
  To get the list of available categories user can use method `available_report_types`.

context

: This is the context of the report.
  The default is `None`. It can be:
  1\. `None`
  2\. `"Differential Pairs"`
  3\. Reduce Matrix Name for Q2d/Q3d solution
  4\. Infinite Sphere name for Far Fields Plot.
  5\. Dictionary. If dictionary is passed, key is the report property name and value is property value.

math_formula

: One of the available AEDT mathematical formulas to apply. For example, `abs, dB`.

polyline_points

: Number of points on which to create the report for plots on polylines.
  This parameter is valid for `Fields` plot only.

sweep

: One of the available AEDT mathematical formulas to apply. For example, `abs, dB`.

# Returns

{class}`ansys.aedt.core.modules.solutions.SolutionData`

: Solution Data object.

# References

```pycon
>>> oModule.GetSolutionDataPerVariation
```

methods.name: ansys.aedt.core.modules.solve_setup.SetupCircuit.create_report
documentation:
Create a report in AEDT. It can be a 2D plot, 3D plot, polar plots or data tables.

# Parameters

expressions

: One or more formulas to add to the report. Example is value = `"dB(S(1,1))"`.

domain

: Plot Domain. Options are "Sweep", "Time", "DCIR".

variations

: Dictionary of all families including the primary sweep. The default is `{"Freq": ["All"]}`.

primary_sweep_variable

: Name of the primary sweep. The default is `"Freq"`.

secondary_sweep_variable

: Name of the secondary sweep variable in 3D Plots.

report_category

: Category of the Report to be created. If `None` default data Report will be used.
  The Report Category can be one of the types available for creating a report depend on the simulation setup.
  For example for a Far Field Plot in HFSS the UI shows the report category as "Create Far Fields Report".
  The report category will be in this case "Far Fields".
  Depending on the setup different categories are available.
  If `None` default category will be used (the first item in the Results drop down menu in AEDT).

plot_type

: The format of Data Visualization. Default is `Rectangular Plot`.

context

: The default is `None`. It can be `None`, `"Differential Pairs"`,\`"RL"`` ,
  `"Sources" ``, `"Vias"`,\`"Bondwires"`` , `"Probes" `` for Hfss3dLayout or
  Reduce Matrix Name for Q2d/Q3d solution or Infinite Sphere name for Far Fields Plot.

name

: Name of the plot. The default is `None`.

polyline_points

: Number of points for creating the report for plots on polylines.

subdesign_id

: Specify a subdesign ID to export a Touchstone file of this subdesign. Valid for Circuit Only.
  The default value is `None`.

context : str, optional

# Returns

{class}`ansys.aedt.core.modules.report_templates.Standard`

: `True` when successful, `False` when failed.

# References

```pycon
>>> oModule.CreateReport
```

Manages a circuit setup.

# Parameters

app

: Inherited app object.

solution_type

: Type of the setup.

name

: Name of the setup. The default is `"MySetupAuto"`.

is_new_setup

: Whether to create the setup from a template. The default is `True.`
  If `False`, access is to the existing setup.

methods.name: ansys.aedt.core.modules.solve_setup.SetupHFSS.set_tuning_offset
documentation:
Set derivative variable to a specific offset value.

This method adjusts the tuning ranges for derivative variables in the design, allowing for specific offset
values to be applied. If a variable is not specified in the `offsets` dictionary,
its offset is set to `0` by default. Each value must be within ±10% of the nominal
value of the corresponding variable.

# Parameters

offsets

: Dictionary where keys are variable names and values are the corresponding offset values to be applied.

# Returns

bool

: `True` when successful, `False` when failed.

# References

```pycon
>>> oDesign.SetTuningRanges
```

# Examples

```pycon
>>> from ansys.aed.core import Hfss
>>> hfss = Hfss()
>>> hfss["der_var"] = "1mm"
>>> setup = hfss.create_setup(setup_type=1)
>>> setup.add_derivatives("der_var")
>>> hfss.analyze()
>>> setup.set_tuning_offset({"der_var": 0.05})
```

methods.name: ansys.aedt.core.modules.solve_setup.SetupHFSS.create_frequency_sweep
documentation:
Create a sweep with the specified number of points.

# Parameters

unit

: Unit of the frequency.. The default is `None`, in which case the default desktop units are used.

start_frequency

: Starting frequency of the sweep. The default is `1.0`.
  If a unit is passed with number, such as `"1MHz"`, the unit is ignored.

stop_frequency

: Stopping frequency of the sweep. The default is `10.0`.
  If a unit is passed with number, such as "1MHz", the unit is ignored.

num_of_freq_points

: Number of frequency points in the range. The default is `401` for
  a sweep type of `"Interpolating"` or `"Fast"`. The default is `5` for a sweep
  type of `"Discrete"`.

name

: Name of the sweep. The default is `None`, in which
  case a name is automatically assigned.

save_fields

: Whether to save the fields. The default is `True`.

save_rad_fields

: Whether to save the radiating fields. The default is `False`.

sweep_type

: Type of the sweep. Options are `"Fast"`, `"Interpolating"`,
  and `"Discrete"`. The default is `"Discrete"`.

interpolation_tol

: Error tolerance threshold for the interpolation
  process. The default is `0.5`.

interpolation_max_solutions

: Maximum number of solutions evaluated for the interpolation process.
  The default is `250`.

# Returns

{class}`ansys.aedt.core.modules.solve_sweeps.SweepHFSS` or bool

: Sweep object if successful, `False` otherwise.

# References

```pycon
>>> oModule.InsertFrequencySweep
```

# Examples

Create a setup named `"LinearCountSetup"` and use it in a linear count sweep
named `"LinearCountSweep"`.

```pycon
>>> setup = hfss.create_setup("LinearCountSetup")
>>> linear_count_sweep = setup.create_linear_count_sweep(,,
>>> type(linear_count_sweep)
<class 'from ansys.aedt.core.modules.setup_templates.SweepHFSS'>
```

methods.name: ansys.aedt.core.modules.solve_setup.SetupHFSS.create_linear_step_sweep
documentation:
Create a Sweep with a specified frequency step.

# Parameters

unit

: Unit of the frequency. For example, `"MHz` or `"GHz"`.

start_frequency

: Starting frequency of the sweep. The default is `0.1`.

stop_frequency

: Stopping frequency of the sweep. The default is `2.0`.

step_size

: Frequency size of the step. The default is `0.05`.

name

: Name of the sweep. The default is `None`, in which
  case a name is automatically assigned.

save_fields

: Whether to save the fields. The default is `True`.

save_rad_fields

: Whether to save the radiating fields. The default is `False`.

sweep_type

: Whether to create a `"Discrete"`,\`\`"Interpolating"\`\` or `"Fast"` sweep.
  The default is `"Discrete"`.

# Returns

{class}`ansys.aedt.core.modules.solve_sweeps.SweepHFSS` or bool

: Sweep object if successful, `False` otherwise.

# References

```pycon
>>> oModule.InsertFrequencySweep
```

# Examples

Create a setup named `"LinearStepSetup"` and use it in a linear step sweep
named `"LinearStepSweep"`.

```pycon
>>> setup = hfss.create_setup("LinearStepSetup")
>>> linear_step_sweep = setup.create_linear_step_sweep(name="LinearStepSweep",
...                                                   unit="MHz", start_frequency=1.1e3,
...                                                   stop_frequency=1200.1, step_size=153.8)
>>> type(linear_step_sweep)
<class 'from ansys.aedt.core.modules.setup_templates.SweepHFSS'>
```

methods.name: ansys.aedt.core.modules.solve_setup.SetupHFSS.create_single_point_sweep
documentation:
Create a Sweep with a single frequency point.

# Parameters

unit

: Unit of the frequency. For example, `"MHz` or `"GHz"`.

freq

: Frequency of the single point or list of frequencies to create distinct single points.

name

: Name of the sweep. The default is `None`, in which
  case a name is automatically assigned.

save_single_field

: Whether to save the fields of the single point. The default is `True`.
  If a list is specified, the length must be the same as the frequency length.

save_fields

: Whether to save the fields for all points and subranges defined in the sweep. The default is `False`.

save_rad_fields

: Whether to save only the radiating fields. The default is `False`.

# Returns

{class}`ansys.aedt.core.modules.solve_sweeps.SweepHFSS` or bool

: Sweep object if successful, `False` otherwise.

# References

```pycon
>>> oModule.InsertFrequencySweep
```

# Examples

Create a setup named `"LinearStepSetup"` and use it in a single point sweep
named `"SinglePointSweep"`.

```pycon
>>> setup = hfss.create_setup("LinearStepSetup")
>>> single_point_sweep = setup.create_single_point_sweep(name="SinglePointSweep", unit="MHz", freq=1.1e3)
>>> type(single_point_sweep)
<class 'from ansys.aedt.core.modules.setup_templates.SweepHFSS'>
```

bool

: `True` when successful, `False` when failed.

# References

```pycon
>>> oModule.EditSetup
```

methods.name: ansys.aedt.core.modules.solve_setup.SetupHFSSAuto.set_tuning_offset
documentation:
Set derivative variable to a specific offset value.

This method adjusts the tuning ranges for derivative variables in the design, allowing for specific offset
values to be applied. If a variable is not specified in the `offsets` dictionary,
its offset is set to `0` by default. Each value must be within ±10% of the nominal
value of the corresponding variable.

# Parameters

offsets

: Dictionary where keys are variable names and values are the corresponding offset values to be applied.

# Returns

bool

: `True` when successful, `False` when failed.

# References

```pycon
>>> oDesign.SetTuningRanges
```

# Examples

```pycon
>>> from ansys.aed.core import Hfss
>>> hfss = Hfss()
>>> hfss["der_var"] = "1mm"
>>> setup = hfss.create_setup(setup_type=0)
>>> setup.add_derivatives("der_var")
>>> hfss.analyze()
>>> setup.set_tuning_offset({"der_var": 0.05})
```

methods.name: ansys.aedt.core.modules.solve_setup.SetupHFSSAuto.add_subrange
documentation:
Add a subrange to the sweep.

# Parameters

range_type

: Type of the subrange. Options are `"LinearCount"`,
  `"LinearStep"`, and `"LogScale"`.

start

: Starting frequency.

end

: Stopping frequency.

count

: Frequency count or frequency step.

unit

: Frequency Units.

clear

: Either if the subrange has to be appended to existing ones or replace them.

# Returns

bool

: `True` when successful, `False` when failed.

methods.name: ansys.aedt.core.modules.solve_setup.SetupHFSSAuto.enable_adaptive_setup_single
documentation:
Enable HFSS single frequency setup.

# Parameters

frequency

: Frequency to set the adaptive convergence at.
  The default is `None`, in which case the value in the setup is
  not updated. You can specify a float value (GHz) or a string.

max_passes

: Maximum number of adaptive passes. The default is `None` which will not update the value in setup.

max_delta_s

: Delta S convergence criteria. The default is `None` which will not update the value in setup.

# Returns

bool

: `True` when successful, `False` when failed.

methods.name: ansys.aedt.core.modules.solve_setup.SetupHFSSAuto.enable_adaptive_setup_broadband
documentation:
Enable HFSS broadband setup.

# Parameters

low_frequency

: Lower frequency to set the adaptive convergence at.
  You can specify a float value (GHz) or a string.

high_frequency

: Lower frequency to set the adaptive convergence at. You can
  specify a float value (GHz) or a string.

max_passes

: Maximum number of adaptive passes. The default is `6`.

max_delta_s

: Delta S Convergence criteria. The default is `0.02`.

# Returns

bool

: `True` when successful, `False` when failed.

methods.name: ansys.aedt.core.modules.solve_setup.SetupHFSSAuto.enable_adaptive_setup_multifrequency
documentation:
Enable HFSS multi-frequency setup.

# Parameters

frequencies

: Frequency at which to set the adaptive convergence. You can enter list entries
  as float values in GHz or as strings.

max_delta_s

: Delta S convergence criteria. The default is `0.02`.

# Returns

bool

: `True` when successful, `False` when failed.

Initializes, creates, and updates an HFSS SBR+ or HFSS Auto setup.

app

: Inherited app object.

solution_type

: Type of the setup.

name

: Name of the setup. The default is `"MySetupAuto"`.

is_new_setup

: Whether to create the setup from a template. The default is `True`.

classes.name: ansys.aedt.core.modules.solve_setup.SetupMaxwell

methods.name: ansys.aedt.core.modules.solve_setup.SetupMaxwell.add_eddy_current_sweep
documentation:
Create a Maxwell Eddy Current Sweep.

# Parameters

sweep_type

: Type of the subrange. Options are `"LinearCount"`,
  `"LinearStep"`, `"LogScale"` and `"SinglePoints"`.

start_frequency

: Starting frequency.

stop_frequency

: Stopping frequency. Required for `range_type="LinearCount"|"LinearStep"|"LogScale"`.

step_size

: Frequency count or frequency step. Required for `range_type="LinearCount"|"LinearStep"|"LogScale"`.

units

: Unit of the frequency. For example, `"MHz` or `"GHz"`. The default is `"Hz"`.

clear

: If set to `True`, all other subranges will be suppressed except the current one under creation.
  Default value is `False`.

save_all_fields

: Save fields at all frequency points to save fields for the entire set of sweep ranges.
  Default is `True`.

# Returns

bool

: `True` if successful, `False` if it fails.

methods.name: ansys.aedt.core.modules.solve_setup.SetupMaxwell.enable_control_program
documentation:
Enable control program option is solution setup.
Provide externally created executable files, or Python (.py) scripts that are called after each time step,
and allow you to control the source input, circuit elements, mechanical quantities, time step,
and stopping criteria, based on the updated solutions.

# Parameters

control_program_path

: File path of control program.

control_program_args

: Arguments to pass to control program.
  Default value is `" "`.

call_after_last_step

: If `True` the control program is called after the simulation is completed.
  Default value is `False`.

# Returns

bool

: `True` if successful, `False` if it fails.

# Notes

By default a control program script will be called by the pre-installed Python interpreter:
`<install_path>\Win64\commonfiles\CPython\37\winx64\Release\python\python.exe`.
However, the user can specify a custom Python interpreter to be used by setting following environment variable:
`EM_CTRL_PROG_PYTHON_PATH=<path_to\python.exe>`

# References

```pycon
>>> oModule.EditSetup
```

Initializes, creates, and updates an HFSS setup.

# Parameters

app

: Inherited app object.

solution_type

: Type of the setup.

name

: Name of the setup. The default is `"MySetupAuto"`.

is_new_setup

: Whether to create the setup from a template. The default is `True`.
  If `False`, access is to the existing setup.

classes.name: ansys.aedt.core.modules.solve_setup.SetupQ3D

methods.name: ansys.aedt.core.modules.solve_setup.SetupQ3D.create_frequency_sweep
documentation:
Create a sweep with the specified number of points.

# Parameters

unit

: Frequency units. The default is `None`, in which case the default desktop units are used.

start_frequency

: Starting frequency of the sweep. The default is `0.0`.
  If a unit is passed with the number, such as\`\`"1MHz", the unit is ignored.

stop_frequency

: Stopping frequency of the sweep. The default is `20.0`.
  If a unit is passed with the number, such as `"1MHz"`, the unit is ignored.

num_of_freq_points

: Number of frequency points in the range. The default is `401` for
  a sweep type of `"Interpolating"` or `"Fast"`. The default is `5` for a sweep
  type of `"Discrete"`.

name

: Name of the sweep. The default is `None`, in which
  case a name is automatically assigned.

save_fields

: Whether to save the fields. The default is `True`.

sweep_type

: Type of the sweep. Options are `"Fast"`, `"Interpolating"`,
  and `"Discrete"`. The default is `"Discrete"`.

interpolation_tol

: Error tolerance threshold for the interpolation
  process. The default is `0.5`.

interpolation_max_solutions

: Maximum number of solutions evaluated for the interpolation process.
  The default is `250`.

# Returns

{class}`ansys.aedt.core.modules.solve_sweeps.SweepQ3D` or bool

: Sweep object if successful, `False` otherwise.

# References

```pycon
>>> oModule.InsertFrequencySweep
```

# Examples

```pycon
>>> from ansys.aedt.core import Q3d
>>> q3d = Q3d()
>>> setup = q3d.create_setup("LinearCountSetup")
>>> sweep = setup.create_frequency_sweep(unit="GHz", start_frequency=0.5,
...                                     stop_frequency=1.5, name="Sweep1")
>>> q3d.release_desktop(True, True)
```

methods.name: ansys.aedt.core.modules.solve_setup.SetupQ3D.create_linear_step_sweep
documentation:
Create a sweep with a specified frequency step.

# Parameters

unit

: Unit of the frequency. The default is `"GHz"`.

start_frequency

: Starting frequency of the sweep. The default is `0.0`.

stop_frequency

: Stopping frequency of the sweep. The default is `2.0`.

step_size

: Frequency size of the step. The default is `0.05`.

name

: Name of the sweep. The default is `None`, in which
  case a name is automatically assigned.

save_fields

: Whether to save the fields. The default is `True`.

sweep_type

: Whether to create a `"Discrete"` or\`\`"Interpolating"\`\` sweep.
  The default is `"Discrete"`.

# Returns

{class}`ansys.aedt.core.modules.solve_sweeps.SweepQ3D` or bool

: Sweep object if successful, `False` otherwise.

# References

```pycon
>>> oModule.InsertFrequencySweep
```

# Examples

Create a setup named `"LinearStepSetup"` and use it in a linear step sweep
named `"LinearStepSweep"`.
\>>> from ansys.aedt.core import Q3d
\>>> q3d = Q3d()
\>>> setup = q3d.create_setup("LinearStepSetup")
\>>> linear_step_sweep = setup.create_linear_step_sweep(name="LinearStepSweep",
... unit="MHz", start_frequency=1.1e3,
... stop_frequency=1200.1, step_size=153.8)
\>>> type(linear_step_sweep)
\>>> q3d.release_desktop(True, True)

methods.name: ansys.aedt.core.modules.solve_setup.SetupQ3D.create_single_point_sweep
documentation:
Create a sweep with a single frequency point.

# Parameters

unit

: Unit of the frequency. The default is `"GHz"`.

freq

: One or more frequencies for creating distinct single points.
  The default is `1.0`.

name

: Name of the sweep. The default is `None`, in which
  case a name is automatically assigned.

save_single_field

: Whether to save the fields of the single point. The default is `True`.
  If a list is specified, the length must be the same as the
  frequency length.

save_fields

: Whether to save the fields for all points and subranges defined in the sweep. The default is `False`.

# Returns

{class}`ansys.aedt.core.modules.solve_sweeps.SweepQ3D` or bool

: Sweep object if successful, `False` otherwise.

# References

```pycon
>>> oModule.InsertFrequencySweep
```

# Examples

Create a setup named `"SinglePointSetup"` and use it in a single point sweep
named `"SinglePointSweep"`.
\>>> from ansys.aedt.core import Q3d
\>>> q3d = Q3d()
\>>> setup = q3d.create_setup("SinglePointSetup")
\>>> single_point_sweep = setup.create_single_point_sweep(
... name="SinglePointSweep",
... unit="MHz", freq=1.1e3)
\>>> type(single_point_sweep)
\>>> q3d.release_desktop(True, True)

methods.name: ansys.aedt.core.modules.solve_setup.SetupQ3D.add_sweep
documentation:
Add a sweep to the project.

# Parameters

name

: Name of the sweep. The default is `None`, in which
  case a name is automatically assigned.

sweep_type

: Type of the sweep. The default is `"Interpolating"`.

# Returns

{class}`ansys.aedt.core.modules.solve_sweeps.SweepHFSS` or
{class}`ansys.aedt.core.modules.solve_sweeps.SweepMatrix`

> Sweep object.

# References

```pycon
>>> oModule.InsertFrequencySweep
```

methods.name: ansys.aedt.core.modules.solve_setup.SetupQ3D.get_sweep
documentation:
Get the frequency sweep object of a given sweep.

# Parameters

name

: Name of the sweep. The default is `None`, in which case the
  first sweep is used.

# Returns

{class}`ansys.aedt.core.modules.solve_sweeps.SweepQ3D` or
{class}`ansys.aedt.core.modules.solve_sweeps.SweepMatrix`

# Examples

```pycon
>>> from ansys.aedt.core import Q3d
>>> q3d = Q3d()
>>> setup = q3d.create_setup()
>>> sweep = setup.create_frequency_sweep(name="Sweep1")
>>> sweep.add_subrange("LinearCount",0,10,1,"Hz")
>>> sweep.add_subrange("LogScale",10,1E8,100,"Hz")
>>> sweep = setup.get_sweep("Sweep1")
>>> q3d.release_desktop(True, True)
```

methods.name: ansys.aedt.core.modules.solve_setup.SetupIcepak.start_continue_from_previous_setup
documentation:
Start or continue from a previously solved setup.

# Parameters

design

: Name of the design.

solution

: Name of the solution in the format `"name : solution_name"`.
  For example, `"Setup1 : Transient"`, `"Setup1 : SteadyState"`.

map_variables_by_name

: Whether variables are mapped by name from the source design. The default is
  `True`.

parameters

: Dictionary of the parameters. This argument is not considered if
  `map_variables_by_name=True`. If `None`, the default is
  `appname.available_variations.nominal_w_values_dict`.

project

: Name of the project with the design. The default is `"This Project*"`.
  However, you can supply the full path and name to another project.

force_source_to_solve

: The default is `True`.

preserve_partner_solution

: The default is `True`.

frozen_flow

: Whether to freeze the flow to the previous solution. The default is `False`.

# Returns

bool

: `True` when successful, `False` when failed.

# References

```pycon
>>> oModule.EditSetup
```

# Examples

```pycon
>>> ipk = ansys.aedt.core.Icepak()
>>> setup = ipk.get_setup("Setup1")
>>> setup.start_continue_from_previous_setup(design="IcepakDesign1",solution="Setup1 : SteadyState")
```

methods.name: ansys.aedt.core.visualization.post.post_circuit.PostProcessorCircuit.create_ami_initial_response_plot
documentation:
Create an AMI initial response plot.

# Parameters

setup

: Name of the setup.

ami_name

: AMI probe name to use.

variation_list_w_value

: List of variations with relative values.

plot_type

: String containing the report type. Default is `"Rectangular Plot"`. It can be `"Data Table"`,
  ``` "Rectangular Stacked Plot"``or any of the other valid AEDT Report types.
  The default is ``"Rectangular Plot" ```.

plot_initial_response

: Set either to plot the initial input response. Default is `True`.

plot_intermediate_response

: Set whether to plot the intermediate input response. Default is `False`.

plot_final_response

: Set whether to plot the final input response. Default is `False`.

plot_name

: Plot name. The default is `None`, in which case
  a unique name is automatically assigned.

# Returns

str

: Name of the plot.

methods.name: ansys.aedt.core.visualization.post.post_circuit.PostProcessorCircuit.sample_waveform
documentation:
Sampling a waveform at clock times plus half unit interval.

# Parameters

waveform_data

: Waveform data.

waveform_sweep

: Waveform sweep data.

waveform_unit

: Waveform units. The default values is `V`.

waveform_sweep_unit

: Time units. The default value is `s`.

unit_interval

: Unit interval in seconds. The default is `1e-9`.

clock_tics

: List with clock tics. The default is `None`, in which case the clock tics from
  the AMI receiver are used.

pandas_enabled

: Whether to enable the Pandas data format. The default is `False`.

# Returns

list or {class}`pandas.Series`

: Sampled waveform in `Volts` at different times in `seconds`.

# Examples

```pycon
>>> from ansys.aedt.core import Circuit
>>> circuit = Circuit()
>>> circuit.post.sample_ami_waveform(name,probe_name,source_name,circuit.available_variations.nominal)
```

methods.name: ansys.aedt.core.visualization.post.post_circuit.PostProcessorCircuit.sample_ami_waveform
documentation:
Sampling a waveform at clock times plus half unit interval.

# Parameters

setup

: Name of the setup.

probe

: Name of the AMI probe.

source

: Name of the AMI source.

variation_list_w_value

: Variations with relative values.

unit_interval

: Unit interval in seconds. The default is `1e-9`.

ignore_bits

: Number of initial bits to ignore. The default is `0`.

plot_type

: Report type. The default is `None`, in which case all report types are generated.
  Options for a specific report type are `"InitialWave"`, `"WaveAfterSource"`,
  `"WaveAfterChannel"`, and `"WaveAfterProbe"`.

clock_tics

: List with clock tics. The default is `None`, in which case the clock tics from
  the AMI receiver are used.

# Returns

list

: Sampled waveform in `Volts` at different times in `seconds`.

# Examples

```pycon
>>> circuit = Circuit()
>>> circuit.post.sample_ami_waveform(setupname,probe_name,source_name,circuit.available_variations.nominal)
```

app

  `_modeler`, `_desktop`, `_odesign`, and `logger`.

methods.name: ansys.aedt.core.visualization.post.compliance.VirtualComplianceGenerator.add_report
documentation:
Add Com parameters computed by SpiSim into the configuration.

# Parameters

design_name

: Design name.

config_file

: Full path to `cfg` file.

traces

: List of traces to compute com parameters.

report_type

: Report Type.

pass_fail

: Whether if to compute pass fail on this parameter or not.

group_plots

: Whether if to group all the traces in the same plot or not.

name

: Name of the report.

project

methods.name: ansys.aedt.core.visualization.post.post_icepak.PostProcessorIcepak.get_fans_operating_point
documentation:
Get the operating point of the fans in the design.

# Parameters

export_file

: Name of the file to save the operating point of the fans to. The default is
  `None`, in which case the filename is automatically generated.

setup_name

: Setup name to determine the operating point of the fans. The default is
  `None`, in which case the first available setup is used.

time_step

: Time, with units, at which to determine the operating point of the fans. The default
  is `None`, in which case the first available timestep is used. This parameter is
  only relevant in transient simulations.

variation

: Design variation to determine the operating point of the fans from. The default is
  `None`, in which case the nominal variation is used.

# Returns

list

: First element of the list is the CSV filename. The second and third elements
  are the quantities with units describing the operating point of the fans.
  The fourth element is a dictionary with the names of the fan instances
  as keys and lists with volumetric flow rates and pressure rise floats associated
  with the operating point as values.

# References

```pycon
>>> oModule.ExportFanOperatingPoint
```

# Examples

```pycon
>>> from ansys.aedt.core import Icepak
>>> ipk = Icepak()
>>> ipk.create_fan()
>>> filename, vol_flow_name, p_rise_name, op_dict= ipk.get_fans_operating_point()
```

methods.name: ansys.aedt.core.visualization.post.post_icepak.PostProcessorIcepak.evaluate_faces_quantity
documentation:
Export the field surface output.

# Parameters

faces

: List of faces to apply.

quantity

: Name of the quantity to export.

side

: Which side of the mesh face to use. The default is `Default`.
  Options are `"Adjacent"`, `"Combined"`, and `"Default"`.

setup_name

: Name of the setup and name of the sweep. For example, `"IcepakSetup1 : SteatyState"`.
  The default is `None`, in which case the active setup and active sweep are used.

variations

: Dictionary of parameters defined for the specific setup with values. The default is `{}`.

ref_temperature

: Reference temperature to use for heat transfer coefficient computation. The default is `""`.

time

: Timestep to get the data from. Default is `"0s"`.

# Returns

dict

: Output dictionary, which depending on the quantity chosen, contains one
  of these sets of keys:

  - `"Min"`, `"Max"`, `"Mean"`, `"Stdev"`, and `"Unit"`
  - `"Total"` and `"Unit"`

# References

```pycon
>>> oModule.ExportFieldsSummary
```

methods.name: ansys.aedt.core.visualization.post.post_icepak.PostProcessorIcepak.evaluate_boundary_quantity
documentation:
Export the field output on a boundary.

# Parameters

boundary

: Name of boundary to perform the computation on.

quantity

: Name of the quantity to export.

side

: Side of the mesh face to use. The default is `"Default"`.
  Options are `"Adjacent"`, `"Combined"`, and `"Default"`.

volume

: Whether to compute the quantity on the volume or on the surface.
  The default is `False`, in which case the quantity will be evaluated
  only on the surface .

setup_name

: Name of the setup and name of the sweep. For example, `"IcepakSetup1 : SteatyState"`.
  The default is `None`, in which case the active setup and active sweep are used.

variations

: Dictionary of parameters defined for the specific setup with values. The default is `{}`.

ref_temperature

: Reference temperature to use for heat transfer coefficient computation. The default is `""`.

time

: Timestep to get the data from. Default is `"0s"`.

# Returns

dict

: Output dictionary, which depending on the quantity chosen, contains one
  of these sets of keys:
  \- `"Min"`, `"Max"`, `"Mean"`, `"Stdev"`, and `"Unit"`
  \- `"Total"` and `"Unit"`

# References

```pycon
>>> oModule.ExportFieldsSummary
```

Manages the specific Icepak postprocessing functions.

Some functionalities are available only when AEDT is running in the graphical mode.

    scale, arrow, and marker settings. Dictionary syntax must
    be the same of the AEDT file.

methods.name: ansys.aedt.core.visualization.post.field_data.FieldPlot.get_points_value
documentation:
Get points data from field plot.

:::{note}
This method is working only if the associated field plot is currently visible.
:::

:::{note}
This method does not work in non-graphical mode.
:::

# Parameters

points

: List with [x,y,z] coordinates of a point or list of lists of points or
  dictionary with keys containing point names and for each key the point
  coordinates [x,y,z].

filename

: Full path or relative path with filename.
  Default is `None` in which case no file is exported.

visibility

: Whether to keep the markers visible in the UI. Default is `False`.

# Returns

dict or pd.DataFrame

: Dict containing 5 keys: point names, x,y,z coordinates and the quantity probed.
  Each key is associated with a list with the same length of the argument points.
  If pandas is installed, the output is a pandas DataFrame with point names as
  index and coordinates and quantity as columns.

methods.name: ansys.aedt.core.visualization.post.field_data.FieldPlot.export_image
documentation:
Export the active plot to an image file.

:::{note}
There are some limitations on HFSS 3D Layout plots.
:::

full_path

: Path for saving the image file. PNG and GIF formats are supported.
  The default is `None` which export file in working_directory.

width

: Plot Width.

height

: Plot height.

orientation

: View of the exported plot. Options are `isometric`,
  `top`, `bottom`, `right`, `left`, `front`,
  `back`, and any custom orientation.

display_wireframe

: Whether the objects has to be put in wireframe mode. Default is `True`.

selections

: Objects to fit for the zoom on the exported image.
  Default is None in which case all the objects in the design will be shown.
  One important note is that, if the fieldplot extension is larger than the
  selection extension, the fieldplot extension will be the one considered
  for the zoom of the exported image.

show_region

: Whether to include the air region in the exported image. Default is `True`.

show_grid

: Whether to display the background grid in the exported image.
  Default is `True`.

show_axis

: Whether to display the axis triad in the exported image. Default is `True`.

show_ruler

: Whether to display the ruler in the exported image. Default is `True`.

# Returns

str

: Full path to exported file if successful.

# References

```pycon
>>> oModule.ExportPlotImageToFile
>>> oModule.ExportModelImageToFile
>>> oModule.ExportPlotImageWithViewToFile
```

methods.name: ansys.aedt.core.visualization.post.field_data.FieldPlot.export_image_from_aedtplt
documentation:
Save an image of the active plot using PyVista.

:::{note}
This method only works if the CPython with PyVista module is installed.
:::

# Parameters

export_path

: Path where image will be saved.
  The default is `None` which export file in working_directory.

view

: View to export. Options are `"isometric"`, `"xy"`, `"xz"`, `"yz"`.

plot_mesh

: Plot mesh.

scale_min

: Scale output min.

scale_max

: Scale output max.

# Returns

str

: Full path to exported file if successful.

# References

```pycon
>>> oModule.UpdateAllFieldsPlots
>>> oModule.UpdateQuantityFieldsPlots
>>> oModule.ExportFieldPlot
```

Provides for creating and editing field plots.

methods.name: ansys.aedt.core.visualization.post.spisim.SpiSim.compute_erl
documentation:
Compute effective return loss (ERL) using Ansys SPISIM from S-parameter file.

# Parameters

config_file

: Configuration file to use as a reference. The default is `None`, in
  which case this parameter is ignored.

port_order

: Whether to use "`EvenOdd`" or "`Incremental`" numbering for S4P files.
  The default is `None`. This parameter is ignored if there are more than four ports.

specify_through_ports

: Input and output ports to compute the ERL on. Those are ordered like `[inp, inneg, outp, outneg]`.
  The default is `None`. This parameter is ignored if there are more than four ports.

bandwidth

: Application bandwidth in hertz (Hz), which is the inverse of one UI (unit interval). The value
  can be a float or a string with the unit ("m", "g"). The default is `30e9`.

tdr_duration

: Time domain reflectometry (TDR) duration in seconds, meaning how long the TDR tailed data should be applied.
  The default is `5`.

z_terminations

: Z-terminations (Z11 and Z22) when TDR is calculated. The default is `50`.

transition_time

: Transition time: how fast (slew rate) input pulse transit from 0 to Vcc volt. The default is "`10p`".

fixture_delay

: Fixture delay: delay when input starts transition from 0 to Vcc. The default is `500e-12`.

input_amplitude

: Input amplitude: Vcc volt of step input. The default is `1.0`.

ber

: Specified BER: At what threshold ERL is calculated. The default is `1e-4`.

pdf_bin_size

: PDF bin size: how to quantize the superimposed value. The default is `1e-5`.

signal_loss_factor

: Signal loss factor (Beta). For more information, see the SPISIM Help. The default is `1.7e9`.

permitted_reflection

: Permitted reflection (Rho). For more information, see the SPISIM Help. The default is `0.18`.

reflections_length

: Length of the reflections: how many UI will be used to calculate ERL. The default is `1000`.

modulation_type

: Modulations type: signal modulation type "`NRZ`" or "`PAM4`". The default is "`NRZ`".

# Returns

bool or float

: Effective return loss from the spisimExe command, `False` when failed.

methods.name: ansys.aedt.core.visualization.post.spisim.SpiSim.compute_com
documentation:
Compute Channel Operating Margin. Only COM ver3.4 is supported.

# Parameters

standard

: Name of the standard to apply. Supported stdnards are as below.
  COM_CUSTOM = 0
  COM_50GAUI_1_C2C = 1
  COM_100GAUI_2_C2C = 2
  COM_200GAUI_4 = 3
  COM_400GAUI_8 = 4
  COM_100GBASE_KR4 = 5
  COM_100GBASE_KP4 = 6

config_file

: Config file to use.

port_order

: Whether to use "`EvenOdd`" or "`Incremental`" numbering for S4P files. The default is `EvenOdd`.
  The default is `None`. This parameter is ignored if there are more than four ports.

fext_s4p

: Fext touchstone file to use.

next_s4p

: Next touchstone file to use.

out_folder

: Output folder where to save report.

# Returns

methods.name: ansys.aedt.core.visualization.post.spisim.DataSet.wave
documentation:
Retrieves the trace data.

# Returns

{class}`numpy.array`

: The trace values.

This is the base class for storing all traces of a RAW file. Returned by the get_trace() or by the get_axis()
methods.
Normally the user doesn't have to be aware of this class. It is only used internally to encapsulate the different
implementations of the wave population.
Data can be retrieved directly by using the [] operator.
If numpy is available, the numpy vector can be retrieved by using the get_wave() method.
The parameter whattype defines what is the trace representing in the simulation, Voltage, Current a Time or
Frequency.

Manages Native Component data and execution.

# Parameters

app

: An AEDT application from `ansys.aedt.core.application`.

component_type

: Type of the component.

component_name

: Name of the component.

props

: Properties of the boundary.

# Examples

in this example the par_beam returned object is a `ansys.aedt.core.modules.boundary.NativeComponentObject`
\>>> from ansys.aedt.core import Hfss
\>>> hfss = Hfss(solution_type="SBR+")
\>>> ffd_file ="path/to/ffdfile.ffd"
\>>> par_beam = hfss.create_sbr_file_based_antenna(ffd_file)
\>>> par_beam.native_properties["Size"] = "0.1mm"
\>>> par_beam.update()
\>>> par_beam.delete()

methods.name: ansys.aedt.core.modules.boundary.BoundaryObject.update_assignment
documentation:
Update the boundary assignment.

# Returns

bool

: `True` when successful, `False` when failed.

Manages boundary data and execution.

# Parameters

app

: An AEDT application from `ansys.aedt.core.application`.

name

: Name of the boundary.

props

: Properties of the boundary.

boundarytype

: Type of the boundary.

# Examples

Create a cylinder at the XY working plane and assign a copper coating of 0.2 mm to it. The Coating is a boundary
operation and coat will return a `ansys.aedt.core.modules.boundary.BoundaryObject`

```pycon
>>> from ansys.aedt.core import Hfss
>>> hfss =Hfss()
>>> origin = hfss.modeler.Position(0, 0, 0)
>>> inner = hfss.modeler.create_cylinder(hfss.PLANE.XY,origin,3,200,0,"inner")
>>> inner_id = hfss.modeler.get_obj_id("inner",)
>>> coat = hfss.assign_coating([inner_id],"copper",use_thickness=True,thickness="0.2mm")
```

Manages parameters data and execution.

# Parameters

app

: Either `Maxwell3d` or `Maxwell2d` application.

name

: Name of the boundary.

props

: Properties of the boundary.

boundarytype

: Type of the boundary.

# Examples

Create a matrix in Maxwell3D return a `ansys.aedt.core.modules.boundary.BoundaryObject`

```pycon
>>> from ansys.aedt.core import Maxwell2d
>>> maxwell_2d = Maxwell2d()
>>> coil1 = maxwell_2d.modeler.create_rectangle([8.5,1.5, 0],[8, 3],True,"Coil_1","vacuum")
>>> coil2 = maxwell_2d.modeler.create_rectangle([8.5,1.5, 0],[8, 3],True,"Coil_2","vacuum")
>>> maxwell_2d.assign_matrix(["Coil_1", "Coil_2"])
```

In this example the sphere1 returned object is a `ansys.aedt.core.modules.boundary.FarFieldSetup`
\>>> from ansys.aedt.core import Hfss

\>>> sphere1.props["ThetaStop"] = "90deg"
\>>> sphere1.props["ThetaStep"] = "2deg"
\>>> sphere1.delete()

methods.name: ansys.aedt.core.modules.boundary.Matrix.get_sources_for_plot
documentation:
Return a list of source of specified matrix ready to be used in plot reports.

# Parameters

get_self_terms

: Either if self terms have to be returned or not.

get_mutual_terms

: Either if mutual terms have to be returned or not.

first_element_filter

: Filter to apply to first element of equation. It accepts `*` and `?` as special characters.

second_element_filter

: Filter to apply to second element of equation. It accepts `*` and `?` as special characters.

category

: Plot category name as in the report. Eg. "C" is category Capacitance.
  Matrix `CATEGORIES` property can be used to map available categories.

# Returns

list

# Examples

```pycon
>>> from ansys.aedt.core import Q3d
>>> q3d = Q3d(project_path)
>>> q3d.matrices[0].get_sources_for_plot(first_element_filter="Bo?1",
...                                      second_element_filter="GND*", category="DCL")
```

methods.name: ansys.aedt.core.modules.boundary.NetworkObject.add_internal_node
documentation:
Add an internal node to the network.

# Parameters

name

: Name of the node.

power

: String, float, or dictionary containing the value of the assignment.
  If a float is passed, the `"W"` unit is used. A dictionary can be
  passed to use temperature-dependent or transient
  assignments.

mass

: Value of the mass assignment. This parameter is relevant only
  if the solution is transient. If a float is passed, the `"Kg"` unit
  is used. The default is `None`, in which case `"0.001kg"` is used.

specific_heat

: Value of the specific heat assignment. This parameter is
  relevant only if the solution is transient. If a float is passed,
  the `"J_per_Kelkg"` unit is used. The default is ``` None`, in
  which case ``"1000J_per_Kelkg" ``` is used.

# Returns

bool

: `True` when successful, `False` when failed.

# Examples

```pycon
>>> import ansys.aedt.core
>>> app = ansys.aedt.core.Icepak()
>>> network = ansys.aedt.core.modules.boundary.Network(app)
>>> network.add_internal_node("TestNode", {"Type": "Transient",
>>>                                        "Function": "Linear", "Values": ["0.01W", "1"]})
```

methods.name: ansys.aedt.core.modules.boundary.NetworkObject.add_boundary_node
documentation:
Add a boundary node to the network.

# Parameters

name

: Name of the node.

assignment_type

: Type assignment. Options are `"Power"` and `"Temperature"`.

value

: String, float, or dictionary containing the value of the assignment.
  If a float is passed the `"W"` or `"cel"` unit is used, depending on
  the selection for the `assignment_type` parameter. If `"Power"`
  is selected for the type, a dictionary can be passed to use
  temperature-dependent or transient assignment.

# Returns

bool

: `True` if successful.

# Examples

```pycon
>>> import ansys.aedt.core
>>> app = ansys.aedt.core.Icepak()
>>> network = ansys.aedt.core.modules.boundary.Network(app)
>>> network.add_boundary_node("TestNode", "Temperature", 2)
>>> ds = app.create_dataset1d_design("Test_DataSet",[1, 2, 3],[3, 4, 5])
>>> network.add_boundary_node("TestNode", "Power", {"Type": "Temp Dep",
>>>                                                       "Function": "Piecewise Linear",
>>>                                                       "Values": "Test_DataSet"})
```

methods.name: ansys.aedt.core.modules.boundary.NetworkObject.add_face_node
documentation:
Create a face node in the network.

# Parameters

assignment

: Face ID.

name

: Name of the node. Default is `None`.

thermal_resistance

: Thermal resistance value and unit. Default is `"NoResistance"`.

material

: Material specification (required if `thermal_resistance="Compute"`).
  Default is `None`.

thickness

: Thickness value and unit (required if `thermal_resistance="Compute"`).
  If a float is passed, `"mm"` unit is automatically used. Default is `None`.

resistance

: Resistance value and unit (required if `thermal_resistance="Specified"`).
  If a float is passed, `"cel_per_w"` unit is automatically used. Default is `None`.

# Returns

bool

: True if successful.

# Examples

```pycon
>>> import ansys.aedt.core
>>> app = ansys.aedt.core.Icepak()
>>> network = ansys.aedt.core.modules.boundary.Network(app)
>>> box = app.modeler.create_box([5, 5, 5],[20, 50, 80])
>>> faces_ids = [face.id for face in box.faces]
>>> network.add_face_node(faces_ids[0])
>>> network.add_face_node(faces_ids[1],name="TestNode",thermal_resistance="Compute",
...                       material="Al-Extruded",thickness="2mm")
>>> network.add_face_node(faces_ids[2],name="TestNode",thermal_resistance="Specified",resistance=2)
```

methods.name: ansys.aedt.core.modules.boundary.NetworkObject.add_nodes_from_dictionaries
documentation:
Add nodes to the network from dictionary.

# Parameters

nodes

: A dictionary or list of dictionaries containing nodes to add to the network. Different
  node types require different key and value pairs:

  - Face nodes must contain the `"ID"` key associated with an integer containing the face ID.
    Optional keys and values pairs are:

    - `"ThermalResistance"`: a string specifying the type of thermal resistance.
      : Options are `"NoResistance"` (default), `"Compute"`, and `"Specified"`.
    - `"Thickness"`: a string with the thickness value and unit (required if `"Compute"`

    is selected for `"ThermalResistance"`).
    \- `"Material"`: a string with the name of the material (required if `"Compute"` is
    selected for `"ThermalResistance"`).
    \- `"Resistance"`: a string with the resistance value and unit (required if

    > `"Specified"` is selected for `"ThermalResistance"`).

    - `"Name"`: a string with the name of the node. If not
      : specified, a name is generated automatically.

  - Internal nodes must contain the following keys and values pairs:

    - `"Name"`: a string with the node name
    - `"Power"`: a string with the assigned power or a dictionary for transient or

    temperature-dependent assignment
    Optional keys and values pairs:
    \- `"Mass"`: a string with the mass value and unit
    \- `"SpecificHeat"`: a string with the specific heat value and unit

  - Boundary nodes must contain the following keys and values pairs:

    - `"Name"`: a string with the node name
    - `"ValueType"`: a string specifying the type of value (`"Power"` or

    `"Temperature"`)
    Depending on the `"ValueType"` choice, one of the following keys and values pairs must
    be used:
    \- `"Power"`: a string with the power value and unit or a dictionary for transient or
    temperature-dependent assignment
    \- `"Temperature"`: a string with the temperature value and unit or a dictionary for
    transient or temperature-dependent assignment
    According to the `"ValueType"` choice, `"Power"` or `"Temperature"` key must be
    used. Their associated value a string with the value and unit of the quantity prescribed or
    a dictionary for transient or temperature dependent assignment.

  All the temperature dependent or thermal dictionaries should contain three keys:
  `"Type"`, `"Function"`, and `"Values"`. Accepted `"Type"` values are:
  `"Temp Dep"` and `"Transient"`. Accepted `"Function"` are: `"Linear"`,
  `"Power Law"`, `"Exponential"`, `"Sinusoidal"`, `"Square Wave"`, and
  `"Piecewise Linear"`. `"Temp Dep"` only support the latter. `"Values"`
  contains a list of strings containing the parameters required by the `"Function"`
  selection (e.g. `"Linear"` requires two parameters: the value of the variable at t=0
  and the slope of the line). The parameters required by each `Function` option is in
  Icepak documentation. The parameters must contain the units where needed.

# Returns

bool

: `True` if successful. `False` otherwise.

# Examples

```pycon
>>> import ansys.aedt.core
>>> app = ansys.aedt.core.Icepak()
>>> network = ansys.aedt.core.modules.boundary.Network(app)
>>> box = app.modeler.create_box([5, 5, 5],[20, 50, 80])
>>> faces_ids = [face.id for face in box.faces]
>>> nodes_dict = [
>>>         {"FaceID": faces_ids[0]},
>>>         {"Name": "TestNode", "FaceID": faces_ids[1],
>>>          "ThermalResistance": "Compute", "Thickness": "2mm"},
>>>         {"FaceID": faces_ids[2], "ThermalResistance": "Specified", "Resistance": "2cel_per_w"},
>>>         {"Name": "Junction", "Power": "1W"}]
>>> network.add_nodes_from_dictionaries(nodes_dict)
```

methods.name: ansys.aedt.core.modules.boundary.NetworkObject.add_link
documentation:
Create links in the network object.

# Parameters

node1

: String containing one of the node names that the link is connecting or an integer
  containing the ID of the face. If an ID is used and the node associated with the
  corresponding face is not created yet, it is added automatically.

node2

: String containing one of the node names that the link is connecting or an integer
  containing the ID of the face. If an ID is used and the node associated with the
  corresponding face is not created yet, it is added atuomatically.

value

: String containing the value and unit of the connection. If a float is passed, an
  R-Link is added to the network and the `"cel_per_w"` unit is used.

name

: Name of the link. The default is `None`, in which case a name is
  automatically generated.

# Returns

bool

: `True` when successful, `False` when failed.

# Examples

```pycon
>>> import ansys.aedt.core
>>> app = ansys.aedt.core.Icepak()
>>> network = ansys.aedt.core.modules.boundary.Network(app)
>>> box = app.modeler.create_box([5, 5, 5],[20, 50, 80])
>>> faces_ids = [face.id for face in box.faces]
>>> connection = {"Name": "LinkTest", "Connection": [faces_ids[1], faces_ids[0]], "Value": "1cel_per_w"}
>>> network.add_links_from_dictionaries(connection)
```

methods.name: ansys.aedt.core.modules.boundary.NetworkObject.add_links_from_dictionaries
documentation:
Create links in the network object.

# Parameters

connections

: Dictionary or list of dictionaries containing the links between nodes. Each dictionary
  consists of these elements:

  - `"Link"`: a three-item list consisting of the two nodes that the link is connecting and
    : the value with unit of the link. The node of the connection can be referred to with the
      name (str) or face ID (int). The link type (resistance, heat transfer coefficient, or
      mass flow) is determined automatically from the unit.
  - `"Name"` (optional): a string specifying the name of the link.

# Returns

bool

: `True` if successful.

# Examples

```pycon
>>> import ansys.aedt.core
>>> app = ansys.aedt.core.Icepak()
>>> network = ansys.aedt.core.modules.boundary.Network(app)
>>> box = app.modeler.create_box([5, 5, 5],[20, 50, 80])
>>> faces_ids = [face.id for face in box.faces]
>>> [network.add_face_node(faces_ids[i]) for i in range(2)]
>>> connection = {"Name": "LinkTest", "Link": [faces_ids[1], faces_ids[0], "1cel_per_w"]}
>>> network.add_links_from_dictionaries(connection)
```

classes.name: ansys.aedt.core.modules.boundary.BoundaryDictionary

methods.name: ansys.aedt.core.modules.boundary.BoundaryDictionary.props
documentation: Dictionary that defines all the boundary condition properties.

Handles Icepak transient and temperature-dependent boundary condition assignments.

# Parameters

assignment_type

: Type of assignment represented by the class. Options are `` "Temp Dep"` ``
  and `"Transient"`.

function_type

: Variation function to assign. If `assignment_type=="Temp Dep"`,
  the function can only be `"Piecewise Linear"`. Otherwise, the function can be
  `"Exponential"`, `"Linear"`, `"Piecewise Linear"`, `"Power Law"`,
  `"Sinusoidal"`, and `"Square Wave"`.

classes.name: ansys.aedt.core.modules.boundary.LinearDictionary
documentation:
Manages linear conditions assignments, which are children of the `BoundaryDictionary` class.

This class applies a condition `y` dependent on the time `t`:

: `y=a+b*t`

# Parameters

intercept

: Value of the assignment condition at the initial time, which
  corresponds to the coefficient `a` in the formula.

slope

: Slope of the assignment condition, which
  corresponds to the coefficient `b` in the formula.

classes.name: ansys.aedt.core.modules.boundary.PowerLawDictionary
documentation:
Manages power law condition assignments, which are children of the ``BoundaryDictionary`` class.

 This class applies a condition ``y`` dependent on the time ``t``:
     ``y=a+b*t^c``

 Parameters
 ----------
 intercept : str
     Value of the assignment condition at the initial time, which
     corresponds to the coefficient ``a`` in the formula.
 coefficient : str
     Coefficient that multiplies the power term, which
     corresponds to the coefficient ``b`` in the formula.
 scaling_exponent : str
     Exponent of the power term, which
     corresponds to the coefficient ``c`` in the formula.

classes.name: ansys.aedt.core.modules.boundary.ExponentialDictionary
documentation:
Manages exponential condition assignments, which are children of the `BoundaryDictionary` class.

This class applies a condition `y` dependent on the time `t`:

: `y=a+b*exp(c*t)`

# Parameters

vertical_offset

: Vertical offset summed to the exponential law, which
  corresponds to the coefficient `a` in the formula.

coefficient

: Coefficient that multiplies the exponential term, which
  corresponds to the coefficient `b` in the formula.

exponent_coefficient

: Coefficient in the exponential term, which
  corresponds to the coefficient `c` in the formula.

classes.name: ansys.aedt.core.modules.boundary.SinusoidalDictionary
documentation:
Manages sinusoidal condition assignments, which are children of the `BoundaryDictionary` class.

This class applies a condition `y` dependent on the time `t`:

: `y=a+b*sin(2*pi(t-t0)/T)`

# Parameters

vertical_offset

: Vertical offset summed to the sinusoidal law, which
  corresponds to the coefficient `a` in the formula.

vertical_scaling

: Coefficient that multiplies the sinusoidal term, which
  corresponds to the coefficient `b` in the formula.

period

: Period of the sinusoid, which
  corresponds to the coefficient `T` in the formula.

period_offset

: Offset of the sinusoid, which
  corresponds to the coefficient `t0` in the formula.

classes.name: ansys.aedt.core.visualization.post.field_summary.FieldSummary

methods.name: ansys.aedt.core.visualization.post.field_summary.FieldSummary.add_calculation
documentation:
Add an entry in the field summary calculation requests.

# Parameters

entity
: Type of entity to perform the calculation on. Options are

  : `"Boundary"`, `"Monitor`", and `"Object"`.
    (`"Monitor"` is available in AEDT 2024 R1 and later.)

geometry

: Location to perform the calculation on. Options are
  `"Surface"` and `"Volume"`.

geometry_name

: Objects to perform the calculation on. If a list is provided,
  the calculation is performed on the combination of those
  objects.

quantity

: Quantity to compute.

normal

: Coordinate values for direction relative to normal. The default is `""`,
  in which case the normal to the face is used.

side

: String containing which side of the face to use. The default is
  `"Default"`. Options are `"Adjacent"`, `"Combined"`, and
  `` "Default"` ``.

mesh

: Surface meshes to use. The default is `"All"`. Options are `"All"` and
  `"Reduced"`.

ref_temperature

: Reference temperature to use in the calculation of the heat transfer
  coefficient. The default is `"AmbientTemp"`.

time

: Timestep to get the data from. Default is `"0s"`.

# Returns

bool

: `True` when successful, `False` when failed.

methods.name: ansys.aedt.core.visualization.post.field_summary.FieldSummary.get_field_summary_data
documentation:
Get field summary output computation.

# Parameters

setup

: Setup name to use for the computation. The
  default is `None`, in which case the nominal variation is used.

variation

: Dictionary containing the design variation to use for the computation.
  The default is `{}`, in which case nominal variation is used.

intrinsics

: Intrinsic values to use for the computation. The default is `""`,
  which is suitable when no frequency needs to be selected.

pandas_output

: Whether to use pandas output. The default is `False`, in
  which case the dictionary output is used.

# Returns

dict or pandas.DataFrame

: Output type depending on the Boolean `pandas_output` parameter.
  The output consists of information exported from the field summary.

methods.name: ansys.aedt.core.visualization.post.field_summary.FieldSummary.export_csv
documentation:
Get the field summary output computation.

# Parameters

output_file

: Path and filename to write the output file to.

setup

: Setup name to use for the computation. The
  default is `None`, in which case the nominal variation is used.

variations

: Dictionary containing the design variation to use for the computation.
  The default is `{}`, in which case the nominal variation is used.

intrinsics

: Intrinsic values to use for the computation. The default is `""`,
  which is suitable when no frequency needs to be selected.

# Returns

bool

: `True` when successful, `False` when failed.
Provides Icepak field summary methods.

methods.name: ansys.aedt.core.visualization.post.monitor_icepak.Monitor.assign_point_monitor
documentation:
Create and assign a point monitor.

# Parameters

point_position

: List of the `[x, y, z]` coordinates for the point or name of the point.
  Also, multiple monitor assignment with list of list with coordinates or list
  of strings with points names.

monitor_quantity

: Quantity being monitored. The default is `"Temperature"`.

monitor_name

: Name of the monitor. The default is `None`, in which case
  the name is randomly generated.

# Returns

str or list

: Monitor name or list of monitor names when successful, `False` when failed.

# References

```pycon
>>> oModule.AssignPointMonitor
```

# Examples

Create two temperature monitor at the points `[0, 0, 0]` and `[1, 1, 1]`.

```pycon
>>> icepak.monitor.assign_point_monitor([[0,0,0], [1, 1, 1]], monitor_name="monitor1")
['monitor1', 'monitor2']
```


# Parameters

monitor_quantity

: Quantity being monitored. The default is `"Temperature"`.

monitor_name

  the name is randomly generated.

# Returns

str or list

: Monitor name or list of monitor names when successful, `False` when failed.

# References

```pycon
>>> oModule.AssignFaceMonitor
```

# Examples

Create a rectangle named `"Surface1"` and assign a temperature monitor to that surface.

```pycon
>>> surface = icepak.modeler.create_rectangle(icepak.PLANE.XY,[0, 0, 0],[10, 20],name="Surface1")
>>> icepak.assign_surface_monitor("Surface1", monitor_name="monitor")
'monitor'
```

Create a box named `"BlockBox1"` and assign a temperature monitor point to that object.

```pycon
>>> box = icepak.modeler.create_box([1, 1, 1],[3, 3, 3],"BlockBox1","copper")

```

methods.name: ansys.aedt.core.visualization.post.rcs_exporter.MonostaticRCSExporter.__create_geometries
documentation: Export the geometry in OBJ format.

Class to enable export of radar cross-section (RCS) data from HFSS.

An instance of this class is returned from the
{meth}`pyaedt.Hfss.get_monostatic_rcs` method. This class creates a
`metadata_file` that can be passed as argument to instantiate an instance of the
{class}`pyaedt.generic.rcs_visualization.MonostaticRCSData` class for subsequent analysis and postprocessing.

Note that this class is derived from the {class}`MonostaticRCSData` class and can be used directly for
RCS postprocessing, but it remains as a property of the {class}`pyaedt.Hfss` application.

# Parameters

app

: HFSS application instance.

setup_name

: Name of the setup. Make sure to build a setup string in the form of `"SetupName : SetupSweep"`.

frequencies

: Frequency list to export. Specify either a list of strings with units or a list of floats in Hertz units.
  For example, `["9GHz", 9e9]`.

expression

: Monostatic expression name. The default value is `"ComplexMonostaticRCSTheta"`.

variations

: Dictionary of all families including the primary sweep. The default value is `None`.

overwrite

: Whether to overwrite the existing far field solution data. The default is `True`.

# Examples

```pycon
>>> import ansys.aedt.core
>>> app = ansys.aedt.core.Hfss(version="2023.2", design="Antenna")
>>> setup_name = "Setup1 : LastAdaptive"
>>> frequencies = [77e9]
>>> sphere = "3D"
>>> data = app.get_monostatic_rcs(frequencies,setup_name,sphere)
>>> data.plot_3d(quantity_format="dB10")
```

methods.name: ansys.aedt.core.visualization.post.solution_data.SolutionData.get_report_plotter
documentation:
Get the `ReportPlotter` on the specified curves.

# Parameters

curves

: Trace names.

formula

: Trace formula. Default is `None` which takes the real part of the trace.

to_radians

: Whether is data has to be converted to radians or not. Default is `False`.
