{class}`ansys.aedt.core.visualization.plot.matplotlib.ReportPlotter`

: Report plotter class.

methods.name: ansys.aedt.core.visualization.post.solution_data.SolutionData.plot
documentation:
Create a matplotlib figure based on a list of data.

# Parameters

curves

: Curves to be plotted. The default is `None`, in which case
  the first curve is plotted.

formula

: Mathematical formula to apply to the plot curve. The default is `None`,
  in which case only real value of the data stored in the solution data is plotted.
  Options are `"abs"`, `"db10"`, `"db20"`, `"im"`, `"mag"`, `"phasedeg"`,
  `"phaserad"`, and `"re"`.

size

: Image size in pixels (width, height).

show_legend

: Whether to show the legend. The default is `True`.
  This parameter is ignored if the number of curves to plot is
  greater than 15.

x_label

: Plot X label.

y_label

: Plot Y label.

title

: Plot title label.

snapshot_path

: Full path to image file if a snapshot is needed.

is_polar

: Set to `True` if this is a polar plot.

show

: Whether if show the plot or not. Default is set to `True`.

# Returns

{class}`ansys.aedt.core.visualization.plot.matplotlib.ReportPlotter`

: Matplotlib class object.

methods.name: ansys.aedt.core.visualization.post.solution_data.SolutionData.plot_3d
documentation:
Create a matplotlib 3D figure based on a list of data.

# Parameters

curve

: Curve to be plotted. If None, the first curve will be plotted.

x_axis

: X-axis sweep. The default is `"Theta"`.

y_axis

: Y-axis sweep. The default is `"Phi"`.

x_label

: Plot X label.

y_label

: Plot Y label.

title

: Plot title label.

formula

: Mathematical formula to apply to the plot curve. The default is `None`.
  Options are `` "abs"` ``, `"db10"`, `"db20"`, `"im"`, `"mag"`, `"phasedeg"`,
  `"phaserad"`, and `"re"`.

size

: Image size in pixels (width, height). The default is `(2000, 1000)`.

snapshot_path

: Full path to image file if a snapshot is needed.
  The default is `None`.

show

: Whether if show the plot or not. Default is set to `True`.

# Returns

{class}`matplotlib.figure.Figure`

: Matplotlib figure object.

methods.name: ansys.aedt.core.visualization.post.solution_data.SolutionData.ifft

methods.name: ansys.aedt.core.visualization.post.fields_calculator.FieldsCalculator.add_expression
documentation:
Add named expression.

# Parameters

calculation

: Calculation type.
  If provided as a string, it has to be a name defined in the expression_catalog.toml.
  If provided as a dict, it has to contain all the necessary arguments to define an expression.
  For reference look at the expression_catalog.toml.

assignment
: :class:ansys.aedt.core.modeler.cad.FacePrimitive

  : Name of the object to add the named expression from.

name

: Name of the named expression. The default is `None`.

# Returns

str, bool

: Named expression when successful, `False` when failed.

# Examples

```pycon
>>> from ansys.aedt.core import Hfss
>>> hfss = Hfss()
>>> poly = hfss.modeler.create_polyline([[0, 0, 0], [1, 0, 1]], name="Polyline1")
>>> my_expression = {
...        "name": "test",
...        "description": "Voltage drop along a line",
...        "design_type": ["HFSS", "Q3D Extractor"],
...        "fields_type": ["Fields", "CG Fields"],
...        "solution_type": "",
...        "primary_sweep": "Freq",
...        "assignment": "",
...        "assignment_type": ["Line"],
...        "operations": ["Fundamental_Quantity('E')",
...                        "Operation('Real')",
...                        "Operation('Tangent')",
...                        "Operation('Dot')",
...                        "EnterLine('assignment')",
...                        "Operation('LineValue')",
...                        "Operation('Integrate')",
...                        "Operation('CmplxR')"],
...        "report": ["Data Table", "Rectangular Plot"],
...    }
>>> expr_name = hfss.post.fields_calculator.add_expression(my_expression, "Polyline1")
>>> hfss.release_desktop(False, False)
```

```pycon
>>> expr_name = hfss.post.fields_calculator.add_expression("voltage_line", "Polyline1")

```

methods.name: ansys.aedt.core.visualization.post.fields_calculator.FieldsCalculator.calculator_write
documentation:
Save the content of the stack register for future reuse in a later Field Calculator session.

# Parameters

expression

: Expression name.
  The expression must exist already in the named expressions list in AEDT Fields Calculator.

output_file

: File path to save the stack entry to.
  File extension must be either `.fld` or `.reg`.

setup

: Solution name.
  If not provided the nominal adaptive solution is taken.

intrinsics

: Intrinsics variables provided as a dictionary.
  Key is the variable name and value is the variable value.
  These are typically: frequency, time and phase.
  If it is a dictionary, keys depend on the solution type and can be expressed as:
  \- `"Freq"`.
  \- `"Time"`.
  \- `"Phase"`.
  The default is `None` in which case the intrinsics value is automatically computed based on the setup.

# Returns

bool

: `True` when successful, `False` when failed.

# Examples

```pycon
>>> from ansys.aedt.core import Hfss
>>> hfss = Hfss()
>>> poly = hfss.modeler.create_polyline([[0, 0, 0], [1, 0, 1]], name="Polyline1")
>>> expr_name = hfss.post.fields_calculator.add_expression("voltage_line", "Polyline1")
>>> file_path = os.path.join(hfss.working_directory, "my_expr.fld")
>>> hfss.post.fields_calculator.calculator_write("voltage_line", file_path, hfss.nominal_adaptive)
>>> hfss.release_desktop(False, False)
```

methods.name: ansys.aedt.core.visualization.post.fields_calculator.FieldsCalculator.write

methods.name: ansys.aedt.core.visualization.post.fields_calculator.FieldsCalculator.export
documentation:
Export the field quantity at the top of the register to a file, mapping it to a grid of points.

Two options are available for defining the grid points on which to export:
\- Input grid points from file : Maps the field quantity to a customized grid of points.

> Before using this command, you must create a file containing the points
> and units.

- Calculate grid points
  : You specify the dimensions and spacing of the grid in the x, y, and z directions,
    with units. The initial units are taken from the model.
    Other grid options are: Cylindrical, in which case rho, phi and z directions must be
    specified, or Spherical, in which case r, theta and phi directions must be
    specified.

If you want to adopt the first option you must provide either the file containing the grid of points
or a list of sample points in `sample_points`. In the latter case, a new file is created
in the working directory called "temp_points.pts" that will be automatically written with the data points
provided and consequently imported.
If you want to adopt the second option you must provide the grid type (Cartesian, Cylindrical or Spherical).
If `grid_center`, `grid_start` or `grid_stop` are not provided the default values are used.

# Parameters

quantity

: Name of the quantity to export.

solution

: Name of the solution in the format `"solution : sweep"`. The default is `None`.

variations

: Dictionary of all variation variables with their values.
  The default is `None`.

output_file

: Full path and name to save the file to.
  The default is `None`, in which case the file is exported
  to the working directory.

assignment

: List of objects to export. The default is `"AllObjects"`.

objects_type

: Type of objects to export. The default is `"Vol"`.
  Options are `"Surf"` for surface and `"Vol"` for
  volume.

intrinsics

: Intrinsic variables required to compute the field before the export.
  These are typically: frequency, time and phase.
  It can be provided either as a dictionary or as a string.
  If it is a dictionary, keys depend on the solution type and can be expressed in lower or camel case as:
  \- `"Freq"` or `"Frequency"`
  \- `"Time"`
  \- `"Phase"`
  If it is a string, it can either be `"Freq"` or `"Time"` depending on the solution type.
  The default is `None` in which case the intrinsics value is automatically computed based on the setup.

phase

: Field phase. The default is `None`.
  This argument is deprecated. Please use `intrinsics` and provide the phase as a dictionary key instead.

sample_points

: Name of the file with sample points or list of the sample points.

export_with_sample_points

: Whether to include the sample points in the file to export.
  The default is `True`.

reference_coordinate_system

: Reference coordinate system in the file to export.
  The default is `"Global"`.

export_in_si_system

: Whether the provided sample points are defined in the SI system or model units.
  The default is `True`.

export_field_in_reference

: Whether to export the field in reference coordinate system.
  The default is `True`.

grid_type

: Type of the grid to export. The options are:
  \- `Cartesian`
  \- `Cylindrical`
  \- `Spherical`

grid_center

: The `[x, y, z]` coordinates for the center of the grid.
  The default is `[0, 0, 0]`. This parameter is disabled if `gridtype=
  "Cartesian"`.

grid_start

: The `[x, y, z]` coordinates for the starting point of the grid.
  The default is `[0, 0, 0]`.

grid_stop

: The `[x, y, z]` coordinates for the stopping point of the grid.
  The default is `[0, 0, 0]`.

grid_step

: The `[x, y, z]` coordinates for the step size of the grid.
  The default is `[0, 0, 0]`.

is_vector

: Whether the quantity is a vector. The default is `False`.

# Returns

bool or str

: The path to the exported field file when successful, `False` when failed.

methods.name: ansys.aedt.core.visualization.post.fields_calculator.FieldsCalculator.__has_lines
documentation: Check if a list has lines.

Provides the Advanced fields calculator methods.

Provide methods to add, load and delete named expressions on top of the
already existing ones in AEDT Fields calculator.

# Parameters

app :

: Inherited parent object.

# Examples

Custom expressions can be added as dictionary on-the-fly:

```pycon
>>> from ansys.aedt.core import Hfss
>>> hfss = Hfss()
>>> poly = hfss.modeler.create_polyline([[0, 0, 0], [1, 0, 1]], name="Polyline1")
>>> my_expression = {
...                     "name": "test",
...                     "description": "Voltage drop along a line",
...                     "design_type": ["HFSS", "Q3D Extractor"],
...                     "fields_type": ["Fields", "CG Fields"],
...                     "solution_type": "",
...                     "primary_sweep": "Freq",
...                     "assignment": "",
...                     "assignment_type": ["Line"],
...                     "operations": ["Fundamental_Quantity('E')",
...                     "Operation('Real')",
...                     "Operation('Tangent')",
...                     "Operation('Dot')",
...                     "EnterLine('assignment')",
...                     "Operation('LineValue')",
...                     "Operation('Integrate')",
...                     "Operation('CmplxR')"],
...                     "report": ["Data Table", "Rectangular Plot"],
...                 }
>>> expr_name = hfss.post.fields_calculator.add_expression(my_expression, "Polyline1")
>>> hfss.release_desktop(False, False)
```

or they can be added from the `expression_catalog.toml`:

```pycon
>>> from ansys.aedt.core import Hfss
>>> hfss = Hfss()
>>> poly = hfss.modeler.create_polyline([[0, 0, 0], [1, 0, 1]], name="Polyline1")
>>> expr_name = hfss.post.fields_calculator.add_expression("voltage_line", "Polyline1")
>>> hfss.release_desktop(False, False)
```

display_type

  : is used. In most cases, this default type is `"Rectangular Plot"`.

solution

: Report setup. The default is `None`, in which case the first
  nominal adaptive solution is used.

context

: Report category. The default is `None`, in which case the first default context
  is used. For Maxwell 2D/3D eddy current solution types, the report category
  can be provided as a dictionary, where the key is the matrix name and the value
  the reduced matrix.

is_siwave_dc

: Whether the setup is SIwave DCIR. The default is `False`.

# Returns

list

# References

```pycon
>>> oModule.GetAllCategories
```

methods.name: ansys.aedt.core.visualization.post.common.PostProcessorCommon.available_report_quantities
documentation:
Compute the list of all available report quantities of a given report quantity category.

# Parameters

report_category

: Report Category. The default is `None`, in which case the default category is used.

display_type

: Report Display Type.
  The default is `None`, in which case the default type is used.
  In most of the cases the default type is "Rectangular Plot".

solution

: Report Setup.
  The default is `None`, in which case the first nominal adaptive solution is used.

quantities_category

: The category that the quantities belong to.
  It must be one of the `available_quantities_categories` method.
  The default is `None`, in which case the first default quantity is used.

context

: Report Context.
  The default is `None`, in which case the default context is used.
  For Maxwell 2D/3D Eddy Current solution types this can be provided as a dictionary
  where the key is the matrix name and value the reduced matrix.

is_siwave_dc

: Whether if the setup is SIwave DCIR or not. Default is `False`.

differential_pairs

: Whether if return differential pairs traces or not. Default is `False`.

# Returns

list

# References

```pycon
>>> oModule.GetAllQuantities
```

# Examples

The example shows how to get report expressions for a Maxwell design with Eddy current solution.
The context has to be provided as a dictionary where the key is the name of the original matrix
and the value is the name of the reduced matrix.
\>>> from ansys.aedt.core import Maxwell3d
\>>> m3d = Maxwell3d(solution_type="EddyCurrent")
\>>> rectangle1 = m3d.modeler.create_rectangle(0, [0.5, 1.5, 0], [2.5, 5], name="Sheet1")
\>>> rectangle2 = m3d.modeler.create_rectangle(0, [9, 1.5, 0], [2.5, 5], name="Sheet2")
\>>> rectangle3 = m3d.modeler.create_rectangle(0, [16.5, 1.5, 0], [2.5, 5], name="Sheet3")
\>>> m3d.assign_current(rectangle1.faces[0], amplitude=1, name="Cur1")
\>>> m3d.assign_current(rectangle2.faces[0], amplitude=1, name="Cur2")
\>>> m3d.assign_current(rectangle3.faces[0], amplitude=1, name="Cur3")
\>>> L = m3d.assign_matrix(assignment=["Cur1", "Cur2", "Cur3"], matrix_name="Matrix1")
\>>> out = L.join_series(sources=["Cur1", "Cur2"], matrix_name="ReducedMatrix1")
\>>> expressions = m3d.post.available_report_quantities(report_category="EddyCurrent",
... display_type="Data Table",
... context={"Matrix1": "ReducedMatrix1"})
\>>> m3d.release_desktop(False, False)

methods.name: ansys.aedt.core.visualization.post.common.PostProcessorCommon.export_report_to_file
documentation:
Export a 2D Plot data to a file.

This method leaves the data in the plot (as data) as a reference
for the Plot after the loops.

# Parameters

output_dir

: Path to the directory of exported report

plot_name

: Name of the plot to export.

extension
: Extension of export , one of
  : - (CSV) .csv
    - (Tab delimited) .tab
    - (Post processor format) .txt
    - (Ensight XY data) .exy
    - (Anosft Plot Data) .dat
    - (Ansoft Report Data Files) .rdat

unique_file

: If set to True, generates unique file in output_dit

uniform

: Whether the export uniform points to the file. The
  default is `False`.

start

: Start range with units for the sweep if the `uniform` parameter
  is set to `True`.

end

: End range with units for the sweep if the `uniform` parameter
  is set to `True`.

step

: Step range with units for the sweep if the `uniform` parameter is
  set to `True`.

use_trace_number_format

: Whether to use trace number formats and use separate columns for curve. The default is `False`.

# Returns

str

: Path of exported file.

# References

```pycon
>>> oModule.ExportReportDataToFile
>>> oModule.ExportUniformPointsToFile
>>> oModule.ExportToFile
```

# Examples

```pycon
>>> from ansys.aedt.core import Circuit
>>> cir = Circuit("my_project.aedt")
>>> report = cir.post.create_report("MyScattering")
>>> cir.post.export_report_to_file("C:\temp", "MyTestScattering", ".csv")
```

methods.name: ansys.aedt.core.visualization.post.common.PostProcessorCommon.create_report
documentation:
Create a report in AEDT. It can be a 2D plot, 3D plot, polar plot, or a data table.

# Parameters

expressions

: One or more formulas to add to the report. Example is value = `"dB(S(1,1))"`.

setup_sweep_name

: Setup name with the sweep. The default is `""`.

domain

: Plot Domain. Options are "Sweep", "Time", "DCIR".

variations

: Dictionary of all families including the primary sweep. The default is `{"Freq": ["All"]}`.

primary_sweep_variable

: Name of the primary sweep. The default is `"Freq"`.

secondary_sweep_variable

: Name of the secondary sweep variable in 3D Plots.

report_category

: Category of the Report to be created. If `None` default data Report is used.
  The Report Category can be one of the types available for creating a report depend on the simulation setup.
  For example for a Far Field Plot in HFSS the UI shows the report category as "Create Far Fields Report".
  The report category is "Far Fields" in this case.
  Depending on the setup different categories are available.
  If `None` default category is used (the first item in the Results drop down menu in AEDT).

plot_type

: The format of Data Visualization. Default is `Rectangular Plot`.

context

: The default is `None`.
  \- For HFSS 3D Layout, options are `"Bondwires"`, `"Differential Pairs"`,
  `None`, `"Probes"`, `"RL"`, `"Sources"`, and `"Vias"`.
  \- For Q2D or Q3D, specify the name of a reduced matrix.
  \- For a far fields plot, specify the name of an infinite sphere.
  \- For Maxwell 2D/3D Eddy Current solution types this can be provided as a dictionary
  where the key is the matrix name and value the reduced matrix.

plot_name

: Name of the plot. The default is `None`.

polyline_points

: Number of points to create the report for plots on polylines on.

subdesign_id

: Specify a subdesign ID to export a Touchstone file of this subdesign. Valid for Circuit Only.
  The default value is `None`.

# Returns

{class}`ansys.aedt.core.modules.report_templates.Standard`

: `True` when successful, `False` when failed.

# References

```pycon
>>> oModule.CreateReport
```

# Examples

```pycon
>>> from ansys.aedt.core import Hfss
>>> hfss = Hfss()
>>> hfss.post.create_report("dB(S(1,1))")
>>> variations = hfss.available_variations.nominal_w_values_dict
>>> variations["Theta"] = ["All"]
>>> variations["Phi"] = ["All"]
>>> variations["Freq"] = ["30GHz"]
>>> hfss.post.create_report(expressions="db(GainTotal)",
...                            setup_sweep_name=hfss.nominal_adaptive,
...                            variations=variations,
...                            primary_sweep_variable="Phi",
...                            secondary_sweep_variable="Theta",
...                            report_category="Far Fields",
...                            plot_type="3D Polar Plot",
...                            context="3D")
>>> hfss.post.create_report("S(1,1)",hfss.nominal_sweep,variations=variations,plot_type="Smith Chart")
>>> hfss.release_desktop(False, False)
```

```pycon
>>> from ansys.aedt.core import Maxwell2d
>>> m2d = Maxwell2d()
>>> m2d.post.create_report(expressions="InputCurrent(PHA)",
...                               domain="Time",
...                               primary_sweep_variable="Time",
...                               plot_name="Winding Plot 1")
>>> m2d.release_desktop(False, False)
```

```pycon
>>> from ansys.aedt.core import Maxwell3d
>>> m3d = Maxwell3d(solution_type="EddyCurrent")
>>> rectangle1 = m3d.modeler.create_rectangle(0, [0.5, 1.5, 0], [2.5, 5], name="Sheet1")
>>> rectangle2 = m3d.modeler.create_rectangle(0, [9, 1.5, 0], [2.5, 5], name="Sheet2")
>>> rectangle3 = m3d.modeler.create_rectangle(0, [16.5, 1.5, 0], [2.5, 5], name="Sheet3")
>>> m3d.assign_current(rectangle1.faces[0], amplitude=1, name="Cur1")
>>> m3d.assign_current(rectangle2.faces[0], amplitude=1, name="Cur2")
>>> m3d.assign_current(rectangle3.faces[0], amplitude=1, name="Cur3")
>>> L = m3d.assign_matrix(assignment=["Cur1", "Cur2", "Cur3"], matrix_name="Matrix1")
>>> out = L.join_series(sources=["Cur1", "Cur2"], matrix_name="ReducedMatrix1")
>>> expressions = m3d.post.available_report_quantities(report_category="EddyCurrent",
...                                                    display_type="Data Table",
...                                                    context={"Matrix1": "ReducedMatrix1"})
>>> report = m3d.post.create_report(
...    expressions=expressions,
...    context={"Matrix1": "ReducedMatrix1"},
...    plot_type="Data Table",
...    plot_name="reduced_matrix")
>>> m3d.release_desktop(False, False)
```

methods.name: ansys.aedt.core.visualization.post.common.PostProcessorCommon.get_solution_data
documentation:
Get a simulation result from a solved setup and cast it in a `SolutionData` object.
Data to be retrieved from Electronics Desktop are any simulation results available in that
specific simulation context.
Most of the argument have some defaults which works for most of the `Standard` report quantities.

# Parameters

expressions

: One or more formulas to add to the report. Example is value `"dB(S(1,1))"` or a list of values.
  Default is `None` which returns all traces.

setup_sweep_name

: Name of the setup. The default is `None`, in which case the `nominal_adaptive`
  setup is used. Be sure to build a setup string in the form of
  `"SetupName : SetupSweep"`, where `SetupSweep` is the sweep name to
  use in the export or `LastAdaptive`.

domain

: Plot Domain. Options are "Sweep" for frequency domain related results and "Time" for transient related data.

variations

: Dictionary of all families including the primary sweep.
  The default is `None` which uses the nominal variations of the setup.

primary_sweep_variable

: Name of the primary sweep. The default is `"None"` which, depending on the context,
  internally assigns the primary sweep to:
  1\. `Freq` for frequency domain results,
  2\. `Time` for transient results,
  3\. `Theta` for radiation patterns,
  4\. `distance` for field plot over a polyline.

report_category

: Category of the Report to be created. If `None` default data Report is used.
  The Report Category can be one of the types available for creating a report depend on the simulation setup.
  For example for a Far Field Plot in HFSS the UI shows the report category as "Create Far Fields Report".
  The report category is "Far Fields" in this case.
  Depending on the setup different categories are available.
  If `None` default category is used (the first item in the Results drop down menu in AEDT).
  To get the list of available categories user can use method `available_report_types`.

context

: This is the context of the report.
  The default is `None`. It can be:
  1\. `None`
  2\. `"Differential Pairs"`
  3\. Reduce Matrix Name for Q2d/Q3d solution
  4\. Infinite Sphere name for Far Fields Plot.
  5\. Dictionary. If dictionary is passed, key is the report property name and value is property value.
  6\. For Maxwell 2D/3D eddy current solution types, this can be provided as a dictionary,
  where the key is the matrix name and value the reduced matrix.

subdesign_id

: Subdesign ID for exporting a Touchstone file of this subdesign.
  This parameter is valid for `Circuit` only.
  The default value is `None`.

polyline_points

: Number of points on which to create the report for plots on polylines.
  This parameter is valid for `Fields` plot only.

math_formula

: One of the available AEDT mathematical formulas to apply. For example, `abs, dB`.

# Returns

{class}`ansys.aedt.core.modules.solutions.SolutionData`

: Solution Data object.

# References

```pycon
>>> oModule.GetSolutionDataPerVariation
```

# Examples

```pycon
>>> from ansys.aedt.core import Hfss
>>> hfss = Hfss()
>>> hfss.post.create_report("dB(S(1,1))")
>>> variations = hfss.available_variations.nominal_w_values_dict
>>> variations["Theta"] = ["All"]
>>> variations["Phi"] = ["All"]
>>> variations["Freq"] = ["30GHz"]
>>> data1 = hfss.post.get_solution_data(
...    "GainTotal",
...    hfss.nominal_adaptive,
...    variations=variations,
...    primary_sweep_variable="Phi",
...    secondary_sweep_variable="Theta",
...    context="3D",
...    report_category="Far Fields",
...)
```

```pycon
>>> data2 =hfss.post.get_solution_data(
...    "S(1,1)",
...    hfss.nominal_sweep,
...    variations=variations,
...)
>>> data2.plot()
>>> hfss.release_desktop(False, False)
```

```pycon
>>> from ansys.aedt.core import Maxwell2d
>>> m2d = Maxwell2d()
>>> data3 = m2d.post.get_solution_data(
...     "InputCurrent(PHA)", domain="Time", primary_sweep_variable="Time",
... )
>>> data3.plot("InputCurrent(PHA)")
>>> m2d.release_desktop(False, False)
```

```pycon
>>> from ansys.aedt.core import Circuit
>>> circuit = Circuit()
>>> context = {"algorithm": "FFT", "max_frequency": "100MHz", "time_stop": "2.5us", "time_start": "0ps"}
>>> spectralPlotData = circuit.post.get_solution_data(expressions="V(Vprobe1)", domain="Spectral",
...                                                   primary_sweep_variable="Spectrum", context=context)
>>> circuit.release_desktop(False, False)
```

```pycon
>>> from ansys.aedt.core import Maxwell3d
>>> m3d = Maxwell3d(solution_type="EddyCurrent")
>>> rectangle1 = m3d.modeler.create_rectangle(0, [0.5, 1.5, 0], [2.5, 5], name="Sheet1")
>>> rectangle2 = m3d.modeler.create_rectangle(0, [9, 1.5, 0], [2.5, 5], name="Sheet2")
>>> rectangle3 = m3d.modeler.create_rectangle(0, [16.5, 1.5, 0], [2.5, 5], name="Sheet3")
>>> m3d.assign_current(rectangle1.faces[0], amplitude=1, name="Cur1")
>>> m3d.assign_current(rectangle2.faces[0], amplitude=1, name="Cur2")
>>> m3d.assign_current(rectangle3.faces[0], amplitude=1, name="Cur3")
>>> L = m3d.assign_matrix(assignment=["Cur1", "Cur2", "Cur3"], matrix_name="Matrix1")
>>> out = L.join_series(sources=["Cur1", "Cur2"], matrix_name="ReducedMatrix1")
>>> expressions = m3d.post.available_report_quantities(report_category="EddyCurrent",
...                                                    display_type="Data Table",
...                                                    context={"Matrix1": "ReducedMatrix1"})
>>> data = m2d.post.get_solution_data(expressions=expressions, context={"Matrix1": "ReducedMatrix1"})
>>> m3d.release_desktop(False, False)
```

methods.name: ansys.aedt.core.visualization.post.common.PostProcessorCommon.create_report_from_configuration
documentation:
Create a report based on a JSON file, TOML file, RPT file, or dictionary of properties.

# Parameters

input_file

: Path to the JSON, TOML, or RPT file containing report settings.

report_settings

: Dictionary containing report settings.

solution_name

: Setup name to use.

matplotlib

: Whether if use AEDT or ReportPlotter to generate the plot. Eye diagrams are not supported.

# Returns

{class}`ansys.aedt.core.modules.report_templates.Standard`

: Report object if succeeded.

# Examples

Create report from JSON file.
\>>> from ansys.aedt.core import Hfss
\>>> hfss = Hfss()
\>>> hfss.post.create_report_from_configuration(r'C:tempmy_report.json',
... solution_name="Setup1 : LastAdpative")

Create report from RPT file.
\>>> from ansys.aedt.core import Hfss
\>>> hfss = Hfss()
\>>> hfss.post.create_report_from_configuration(r'C:tempmy_report.rpt')

Create report from dictionary.
\>>> from ansys.aedt.core import Hfss
\>>> from ansys.aedt.core.generic.general_methods import read_json
\>>> hfss = Hfss()
\>>> dict_vals = read_json("Report_Simple.json")
\>>> hfss.post.create_report_from_configuration(report_settings=dict_vals)

Manages the main AEDT postprocessing functions.

This class is inherited in the caller application and is accessible through the post variable( eg. `hfss.post` or
`q3d.post`).

:::{note}
Some functionalities are available only when AEDT is running in
the graphical mode.
:::

# Parameters

app

: Inherited parent object. The parent object must provide the members
  `_modeler`, `_desktop`, `_odesign`, and `logger`.

# Examples

```pycon
>>> from ansys.aedt.core import Q3d
>>> q3d = Q3d()
>>> q.post.get_solution_data(domain="Original")
```

documentation:
Create a standard statistical AMI contour plot.

# Parameters

expressions

: Expression to add into the report. The expression can be any of the available formula
  you can enter into the Electronics Desktop Report Editor.

setup

: Name of the setup. The default is `None`, in which case the `nominal_adaptive`
  setup is used. Be sure to build a setup string in the form of
  `"SetupName : SetupSweep"`, where `SetupSweep` is either the sweep

  > name to use in the export or `LastAdaptive`.

quantity_type

: For AMI analysis only, the quantity type. The default is `3`. Options are:

  - `0` for Initial Wave
  - `1` for Wave after Source
  - `2` for Wave after Channel
  - `3` for Wave after Probe.

# Returns

{class}`ansys.aedt.core.modules.report_templates.AMIConturEyeDiagram`

# Examples

```pycon
>>> from ansys.aedt.core import Circuit
>>> cir= Circuit()
>>> new_eye = cir.post.reports_by_category.statistical_eye_contour("V(Vout)")
>>> new_eye.unit_interval = "1e-9s"
>>> new_eye.time_stop = "100ns"
>>> new_eye.create()
```

quantity_type

statistical_analysis

functions.name: ansys.aedt.core.visualization.advanced.sbrplus.hdm_utils.sort_bundle
documentation:
In-place sorting utility for hdm ray exports.

Ray exports are not guaranteed to always be in a predetermined order,
because of the non-deterministic multi-threaded SBR+ solver implementation.

SBR+ rays will be sorted by source launch point, UTD bright point if present,
and first-bounce ray hit point. In the case of monostatic PW incidence, the launch
point is represented by the sweep angle index.

Creeping rays will be sorted by source launch point, geodesic origin,
and first-footprint current location. In the case of monostatic PW incidence, the launch
point is represented by the sweep angle index.

```
param bundle

SBR+ or CW bundle from hdm_parser

param str monoPW_attrib

sweep angle index argument name for monostatic PW illumination
```

y_units

: Y points units. The default is `"mV"`.

enable_limits

: Whether to enable the upper and lower limits. The default is `False`.

upper_limit

: Upper limit if limits are enabled. The default is `500`.

lower_limit

color

  Each color value must be an integer in a range from 0 to 255.

: Mask time offset with units. The default is `"0ns"`.

methods.name: ansys.aedt.core.visualization.post.post_common_3d.PostProcessor3D.get_scalar_field_value
documentation:
Use the field calculator to Compute Scalar of a Field.

# Parameters

quantity

: Name of the quantity to export. For example, `"Temp"`.

scalar_function

: The name of the scalar function. For example, `"Maximum"`, `"Integrate"`.
  The default is `"Maximum"`.

solution

: Name of the solution in the format `"solution : sweep"`. The default is `None`.

variations

: Dictionary of all variation variables with their values.
  e.g. `['power_block:=', ['0.6W'], 'power_source:=', ['0.15W']]`
  The default is `None`.

is_vector

: Whether the quantity is a vector. The default is `False`.

intrinsics

: Intrinsic variables required to compute the field before the export.
  These are typically: frequency, time and phase.
  It can be provided either as a dictionary or as a string.

  If it is a dictionary, keys depend on the solution type and can be expressed as:
  \- `"Freq"` or `"Frequency"`.
  \- `"Time"`.
  \- `"Phase"`.

  If it is a string, it can either be `"Freq"` or `"Time"` depending on the solution type.
  The default is `None` in which case the intrinsics value is automatically computed based on the setup.

phase

: Field phase. The default is `None`.

object_name

: Name of the object. For example, `"Box1"`.
  The default is `"AllObjects"`.

object_type

: Type of the object - `"volume"`, `"surface"`, `"point"`.
  The default is `"volume"`.

adjacent_side

: To query quantity value on adjacent side for object_type = "surface", pass `True`.
  The default is `False`.

# Returns

float

: Scalar field value.

# References

```pycon
>>> oModule.EnterQty
>>> oModule.CopyNamedExprToStack
>>> oModule.CalcOp
>>> oModule.EnterQty
>>> oModule.EnterVol
>>> oModule.ClcEval
>>> GetTopEntryValue
```

# Examples

```pycon
>>> from ansys.aedt.core import Hfss
>>> aedtapp = Hfss()
>>> # Intrinsics is explicitly provided as a dictionary.
>>> intrinsics = {"Freq": "5GHz", "Phase": "180deg"}
>>> min_value = aedtapp.post.get_scalar_field_value(quantity_name, "Minimum", setup_name, intrinsics=intrinsics)
>>> plot1 = aedtapp.post.create_fieldplot_cutplane(cutlist, quantity_name, setup_name, intrinsics=intrinsics)
>>> # Intrinsics is provided as a string. Phase is automatically assigned to 0deg.
>>> min_value = aedtapp.post.get_scalar_field_value(quantity_name, "Minimum", setup_name, intrinsics="5GHz")
>>> plot1 = aedtapp.post.create_fieldplot_cutplane(cutlist, quantity_name, setup_name, intrinsics="5GHz")
>>> # Intrinsics is provided as a dictionary. Phase is automatically assigned to 0deg.
>>> intrinsics = {"Freq": "5GHz"}
>>> min_value = aedtapp.post.get_scalar_field_value(quantity_name, "Minimum", setup_name, intrinsics=intrinsics)
>>> plot1 = aedtapp.post.create_fieldplot_cutplane(cutlist, quantity_name, setup_name, intrinsics=intrinsics)
>>> # Intrinsics is not provided and is automatically computed from the setup.
>>> min_value = aedtapp.post.get_scalar_field_value(quantity_name, "Minimum", setup_name)
>>> plot1 = aedtapp.post.create_fieldplot_cutplane(cutlist, quantity_name, setup_name)
```

methods.name: ansys.aedt.core.visualization.post.post_common_3d.PostProcessor3D.export_field_file_on_grid
documentation:
Use the field calculator to create a field file on a grid based on a solution and variation.

# Parameters

quantity

: Name of the quantity to export. For example, `"Temp"`.

solution

: Name of the solution in the format `"solution : sweep"`. The default is `None`.

variations

: Dictionary of all variation variables with their values.
  The default is `None`.

file_name

: Full path and name to save the file to.
  The default is `None`, in which case the file is exported
  to the working directory.

grid_type

: Type of the grid to export. The default is `"Cartesian"`.

grid_center

: The `[x, y, z]` coordinates for the center of the grid.
  The default is `[0, 0, 0]`. This parameter is disabled if `gridtype=
  "Cartesian"`.

grid_start

: The `[x, y, z]` coordinates for the starting point of the grid.
  The default is `[0, 0, 0]`.

grid_stop

: The `[x, y, z]` coordinates for the stopping point of the grid.
  The default is `[0, 0, 0]`.

grid_step

: The `[x, y, z]` coordinates for the step size of the grid.
  The default is `[0, 0, 0]`.

is_vector

: Whether the quantity is a vector. The default is `False`.

intrinsics

: Intrinsic variables required to compute the field before the export.
  These are typically: frequency, time and phase.
  It can be provided either as a dictionary or as a string.
  If it is a dictionary, keys depend on the solution type and can be expressed in lower or camel case as:
  \- `"Freq"` or `"Frequency"`.
  \- `"Time"`.
  \- `"Phase"`.
  If it is a string, it can either be `"Freq"` or `"Time"` depending on the solution type.
  The default is `None` in which case the intrinsics value is automatically computed based on the setup.

phase

: Field phase. The default is `None`.

export_with_sample_points

: Whether to include the sample points in the file to export.
  The default is `True`.

reference_coordinate_system

: Reference coordinate system in the file to export.
  The default is `"Global"`.

export_in_si_system

: Whether the provided sample points are defined in the SI system or model units.
  The default is `True`.

export_field_in_reference

: Whether to export the field in reference coordinate system.
  The default is `True`.

# Returns

str

: Field file path when succeeded.

# References

```pycon
>>> oModule.EnterQty
>>> oModule.CopyNamedExprToStack
>>> oModule.CalcOp
>>> oModule.EnterQty
>>> oModule.EnterVol
>>> oModule.ExportOnGrid
```

# Examples

```pycon
>>> from ansys.aedt.core import Hfss
>>> hfss = Hfss()
>>> var = hfss.available_variations.nominal_w_values
>>> setup = "Setup1 : LastAdaptive"
>>> path = "Field.fld"
>>> hfss.post.export_field_file_on_grid("E",setup,var,path,'Cartesian',[0, 0, 0],intrinsics="8GHz")
```

methods.name: ansys.aedt.core.visualization.post.post_common_3d.PostProcessor3D.export_field_file
documentation:
Use the field calculator to create a field file based on a solution and variation.

# Parameters

quantity

: Name of the quantity to export. For example, `"Temp"`.

solution

: Name of the solution in the format `"solution: sweep"`.
  The default is `None`.

variations

: Dictionary of all variation variables with their values.
  The default is `None`.

output_file

: Full path and name to save the file to.
  The default is `None` which export a file named `"<setup_name>.fld"` in working_directory.

assignment

: List of objects to export. The default is `"AllObjects"`.

objects_type

: Type of objects to export. The default is `"Vol"`.
  Options are `"Surf"` for surface and `"Vol"` for
  volume.

intrinsics

: Intrinsic variables required to compute the field before the export.
  These are typically: frequency, time and phase.
  It can be provided either as a dictionary or as a string.
  If it is a dictionary, keys depend on the solution type and can be expressed in lower or camel case as:
  \- `"Freq"` or `"Frequency"`
  \- `"Time"`
  \- `"Phase"`
  If it is a string, it can either be `"Freq"` or `"Time"` depending on the solution type.
  The default is `None` in which case the intrinsics value is automatically computed based on the setup.

phase

: Field phase. The default is `None`.
  This argument is deprecated. Please use `intrinsics` and provide the phase as a dictionary key instead.

sample_points_file

: Name of the file with sample points. The default is `None`.

sample_points

: List of the sample points. The default is `None`.

export_with_sample_points

: Whether to include the sample points in the file to export.
  The default is `True`.

reference_coordinate_system

: Reference coordinate system in the file to export.
  The default is `"Global"`.

export_in_si_system

: Whether the provided sample points are defined in the SI system or model units.
  The default is `True`.

export_field_in_reference

: Whether to export the field in reference coordinate system.
  The default is `True`.

# Returns

bool

: `True` when successful, `False` when failed.

# References

```pycon
>>> oModule.EnterQty
>>> oModule.CopyNamedExprToStack
>>> oModule.CalcOp
>>> oModule.EnterQty
>>> oModule.EnterVol
>>> oModule.CalculatorWrite
>>> oModule.ExportToFile
```

# Examples

```pycon
>>> from ansys.aedt.core import Maxwell3d
>>> m3d = Maxwell3d()
>>> # Intrinsics is provided as a string.
>>> fld_file1 = "test_fld_hfss1.fld"
>>> hfss_app.post.export_field_file(quantity="Mag_E", output_file=fld_file1, assignment="Box1",
>>>                                 intrinsics="1GHz", phase="5deg")
>>> # Intrinsics is provided as dictionary. Phase is automatically assigned to 0deg.
>>> fld_file2 =  "test_fld_hfss2.fld"
>>> hfss_app.post.export_field_file(quantity="Mag_E", output_file=fld_file2, assignment="Box1",
>>>                                intrinsics={"frequency":"1GHz"})
>>> # Intrinsics is provided as dictionary. Phase is provided.
>>> hfss_app.post.export_field_file(quantity="Mag_E", output_file=fld_file2, assignment="Box1",
>>>                                 intrinsics={"frequency":"1GHz", "phase":"30deg"})
>>> # Intrinsics is not provided. It is computed from the setup arguments.
>>>  hfss_app.post.export_field_file(quantity="Mag_E", output_file=fld_file2, assignment="Box1",
>>>                                     )
```

methods.name: ansys.aedt.core.visualization.post.post_common_3d.PostProcessor3D.create_fieldplot_line
documentation:
Create a field plot of the line.

# Parameters

assignment

: List of polylines to plot.

quantity

: Name of the quantity to plot.

setup

: Name of the setup. The default is `None`, in which case the `nominal_adaptive`
  setup is used. Be sure to build a setup string in the form of
  `"SetupName : SetupSweep"`, where `SetupSweep` is the sweep name to
  use in the export or `LastAdaptive`.

intrinsics

: Intrinsic variables required to compute the field before the export.
  These are typically: frequency, time and phase.
  It can be provided either as a dictionary or as a string.
  If it is a dictionary, keys depend on the solution type and can be expressed in lower or camel case as:
  \- `"Freq"` or `"Frequency"`.
  \- `"Time"`.
  \- `"Phase"`.
  If it is a string, it can either be `"Freq"` or `"Time"` depending on the solution type.
  The default is `None` in which case the intrinsics value is automatically computed based on the setup.

plot_name

: Name of the field plot to create.

field_type

: Field type to plot. Valid only for Q3D Field plots.

# Returns

type

: Plot object.

# References

```pycon
>>> oModule.CreateFieldPlot
```

# Examples

```pycon
>>> from ansys.aedt.core import Hfss
>>> aedtapp = Hfss()
>>> # Intrinsics is provided as a dictionary.
>>> intrinsics = {"Freq": "5GHz", "Phase": "180deg"}
>>> min_value = aedtapp.post.get_scalar_field_value(quantity_name, "Minimum", setup_name, intrinsics=intrinsics)
>>> plot1 = aedtapp.post.create_fieldplot_line("Polyline1", quantity_name, setup_name, intrinsics=intrinsics)
>>> # Intrinsics is provided as a string. Phase is automatically assigned to 0deg.
>>> min_value = aedtapp.post.get_scalar_field_value(quantity_name, "Minimum", setup_name, intrinsics="5GHz")
>>> plot1 = aedtapp.post.create_fieldplot_line("Polyline1", quantity_name, setup_name, intrinsics="5GHz")
>>> # Intrinsics is provided as a dictionary. Phase is automatically assigned to 0deg.
>>> intrinsics = {"Freq": "5GHz"}
>>> min_value = aedtapp.post.get_scalar_field_value(quantity_name, "Minimum", setup_name, intrinsics=intrinsics)
>>> plot1 = aedtapp.post.create_fieldplot_line("Polyline1", quantity_name, setup_name, intrinsics=intrinsics)
>>> # Intrinsics is not provided and is computed from the setup.
>>> min_value = aedtapp.post.get_scalar_field_value(quantity_name, "Minimum", setup_name)
>>> plot1 = aedtapp.post.create_fieldplot_line("Polyline1", quantity_name, setup_name)
```

  \- `"Freq"` or `"Frequency"`.
  \- `"Time"`.
  \- `"Phase"`.
  If it is a string, it can either be `"Freq"` or `"Time"` depending on the solution type.
  The default is `None` in which case the intrinsics value is automatically computed based on the setup.

plot_name

```pycon
>>> from ansys.aedt.core import Maxwell2d
```

methods.name: ansys.aedt.core.visualization.post.post_common_3d.PostProcessor3D.create_fieldplot_layers
documentation:
Create a field plot of stacked layer plot.
This plot is valid from AEDT 2023 R2 and later in HFSS 3D Layout. Nets can be used as a filter.
Dielectrics will be included into the plot.
It works when a layout components in 3d modeler is used.

# Parameters

layers

: List of layers to plot. For example:
  `["Layer1","Layer2"]`. If empty list is provided
  all layers are considered.

quantity

: Name of the quantity to plot.

setup

: Name of the setup. The default is `None`, in which case the `nominal_adaptive`
  setup is used. Make sure to build a setup string in the form of
  `"SetupName : SetupSweep"`, where `SetupSweep` is the sweep name to
  use in the export or `LastAdaptive`.

nets

: List of nets to filter the field plot. Optional.

plot_on_surface

: Whether if the plot has to be on surfaces or inside the objects.
  It is applicable only to layout components. Default is `True`.

intrinsics

: Intrinsic variables required to compute the field before the export.
  These are typically: frequency, time and phase.
  It can be provided either as a dictionary or as a string.
  If it is a dictionary, keys depend on the solution type and can be expressed in lower or camel case as:

  - `"Freq"` or `"Frequency"`.
  - `"Time"`.
  - `"Phase"`.

  If it is a string, it can either be `"Freq"` or `"Time"` depending on the solution type.
  The default is `None` in which case the intrinsics value is automatically computed based on the setup.

name

: Name of the field plot to create.

# Returns

:class:`ansys.aedt.core.modules.solutions.FieldPlot` or bool

: Plot object.

# References

```pycon
>>> oModule.CreateFieldPlot
```

  use in the export or `LastAdaptive`.

layers

  `["Layer1","Layer2"]`. If empty list is provided
  all layers are considered.

intrinsics

: Intrinsic variables required to compute the field before the export.

  It can be provided either as a dictionary or as a string.
  If it is a dictionary, keys depend on the solution type and can be expressed in lower or camel case as:

  - `"Freq"` or `"Frequency"`.
  - `"Time"`.
  - `"Phase"`.

  If it is a string, it can either be `"Freq"` or `"Time"` depending on the solution type.
  The default is `None` in which case the intrinsics value is automatically computed based on the setup.

plot_on_surface

: Whether if the plot has to be on surfaces or inside the objects.
  It is applicable only to layout components. Default is `True`.


  - `"Time"`.
  - `"Phase"`.

  If it is a string, it can either be `"Freq"` or `"Time"` depending on the solution type.
  The default is `None` in which case the intrinsics value is automatically computed based on the setup.

plot_on_surface

: Whether if the plot has to be on surfaces or inside the objects.
  It is applicable only to layout components. Default is `True`.

plot_name

: Name of the field plot to create.

# Returns

:class:`ansys.aedt.core.modules.solutions.FieldPlot`

: Plot object.

# References

```pycon
>>> oModule.CreateFieldPlot
```

methods.name: ansys.aedt.core.visualization.post.post_common_3d.PostProcessor3D.create_fieldplot_cutplane
documentation:
Create a field plot of cut planes.

# Parameters

assignment

: List of cut planes to plot.

quantity

: Name of the quantity to plot.

setup

: Name of the setup. The default is `None`, in which case the `nominal_adaptive` setup
  is used. Be sure to build a setup string in the form of `"SetupName : SetupSweep"`,
  where `SetupSweep` is the sweep name to use in the export or `LastAdaptive`.

intrinsics

: Intrinsic variables required to compute the field before the export.
  These are typically: frequency, time and phase.
  It can be provided either as a dictionary or as a string.
  If it is a dictionary, keys depend on the solution type and can be expressed in lower or camel case as:

  - `"Freq"` or `"Frequency"`.
  - `"Time"`.
  - `"Phase"`.

  If it is a string, it can either be `"Freq"` or `"Time"` depending on the solution type.
  The default is `None` in which case the intrinsics value is automatically computed based on the setup.

plot_name

: Name of the field plot to create.

filter_objects

: Objects list on which filter the plot.
  The default value is `None`, in which case an empty list is passed.

field_type

: Field type to plot. This parameter is valid only for Q3D field plots.

# Returns

:class:`ansys.aedt.core.modules.solutions.FieldPlot`

: Plot object.

# References

```pycon
>>> oModule.CreateFieldPlot
```

# Examples

```pycon
>>> from ansys.aedt.core import Hfss
>>> aedtapp = Hfss()
>>> # Intrinsics is provided as a dictionary.
>>> intrinsics = {"Freq": "5GHz", "Phase": "180deg"}
>>> min_value = aedtapp.post.get_scalar_field_value(quantity_name, "Minimum", setup_name, intrinsics=intrinsics)
>>> plot1 = aedtapp.post.create_fieldplot_cutplane(cutlist, quantity_name, setup_name, intrinsics=intrinsics)
>>> # Intrinsics is provided as a string. Phase is automatically assigned to 0deg.
>>> min_value = aedtapp.post.get_scalar_field_value(quantity_name, "Minimum", setup_name, intrinsics="5GHz")
>>> plot1 = aedtapp.post.create_fieldplot_cutplane(cutlist, quantity_name, setup_name, intrinsics="5GHz")
>>> # Intrinsics is provided as a dictionary. Phase is automatically assigned to 0deg.
>>> intrinsics = {"Freq": "5GHz"}
>>> min_value = aedtapp.post.get_scalar_field_value(quantity_name, "Minimum", setup_name, intrinsics=intrinsics)
>>> plot1 = aedtapp.post.create_fieldplot_cutplane(cutlist, quantity_name, setup_name, intrinsics=intrinsics)
>>> # Intrinsics is not provided and is computed from the setup.
>>> min_value = aedtapp.post.get_scalar_field_value(quantity_name, "Minimum", setup_name)
>>> plot1 = aedtapp.post.create_fieldplot_cutplane(cutlist, quantity_name, setup_name)
```

methods.name: ansys.aedt.core.visualization.post.post_common_3d.PostProcessor3D.export_field_jpg
documentation:
Export a field plot and coordinate system to a JPG file.

# Parameters

file_name

: Full path and name to save the JPG file to.

plot_name

: Name of the plot.

folder_name

: Name of the folder plot.

orientation

: Name of the orientation to apply. The default is `"isometric"`.

width

: Plot Width. The default is `1920`.

height

: Plot Height. The default is `1080`.

display_wireframe

: Display wireframe. The default is `True`.

selections
: List of objects to include in the plot.

  : Supported in 3D Field Plots only starting from 23R1.

show_axis

: Whether to show the axes. The default is `True`.
  Supported in 3D Field Plots only starting from 23R1.

show_grid

: Whether to show the grid. The default is `True`.
  Supported in 3D Field Plots only starting from 23R1.

show_ruler

: Whether to show the ruler. The default is `True`.
  Supported in 3D Field Plots only starting from 23R1.

show_region

: Whether to show the region or not. The default is `Default`.
  Supported in 3D Field Plots only starting from 23R1.

# Returns

bool

: `True` when successful, `False` when failed.

# References

```pycon
>>> oModule.ExportPlotImageToFile
>>> oModule.ExportModelImageToFile
```

methods.name: ansys.aedt.core.visualization.post.post_common_3d.PostProcessor3D.export_model_picture
documentation:
Export a snapshot of the model to a `JPG` file.

:::{note}
This method works only when AEDT is running in the graphical mode.
:::

# Parameters

full_name

: Full Path for exporting the image file. The default is `None`, in which case working_dir is used.

show_axis

: Whether to show the axes. The default is `True`.

show_grid

: Whether to show the grid. The default is `True`.

show_ruler

: Whether to show the ruler. The default is `True`.

show_region

: Whether to show the region or not. The default is `Default`.

selections

: Whether to export image of a selection or not. Default is `None`.

field_selections

: List of Fields plots to add to the image. Default is `None`. `"all"` for all field plots.

orientation

: Picture orientation. Orientation can be one of `"top"`, `"bottom"`, `"right"`, `"left"`,
  `"front"`, `"back"`, `"trimetric"`, `"dimetric"`, `"isometric"`, or a custom
  orientation that you added to the Orientation List.

width

: Export image picture width size in pixels. Default is 0 which takes the desktop size.

height

: Export image picture height size in pixels. Default is 0 which takes the desktop size.

# Returns

str

: File path of the generated JPG file.

# References

```pycon
>>> oEditor.ExportModelImageToFile
```

# Examples

```pycon
>>> from ansys.aedt.core import Q3d
>>> q3d = Q3d(non_graphical=False)
>>> output_file = q3d.post.export_model_picture(full_name=os.path.join(q3d.working_directory, "images1.jpg"))
```

methods.name: ansys.aedt.core.visualization.post.post_common_3d.PostProcessor3D.get_far_field_data
documentation:
Generate far field data using the `GetSolutionDataPerVariation()` method.

This method returns the data `solData`, `ThetaVals`,
`PhiVals`, `ScanPhiVals`, `ScanThetaVals`, and
`FreqVals`.

# Parameters

expressions

: One or more formulas to add to the report. The default is `"GainTotal"`.

setup_sweep_name

: Name of the setup for computing the report. The default is `""`,
  in which case the nominal sweep is used.

domain

: Context type (sweep or time). The default is `"Infinite Sphere1"`.

sweeps

: Dictionary of variables and values. The default is `{"Freq": ["All"]}`.

# Returns

{class}`ansys.aedt.core.modules.solutions.SolutionData`

# References

```pycon
>>> oModule.GetSolutionDataPerVariation
```

methods.name: ansys.aedt.core.visualization.post.post_common_3d.PostProcessor3D.export_model_obj
documentation:
Export the model.

# Parameters

assignment

: List of strings with names of objects to export. Default is `None` in which
  case export every model object except 3D ones and vacuum and air objects.

export_path

: Full path of the exported OBJ file.

export_as_multiple_objects

: Whether to export the model as multiple objects or not. Default is `False`
  in which case the model is exported as single object.

air_objects

: Whether to export air and vacuum objects. The default is `False`.

# Returns

list

: Paths for OBJ files.

methods.name: ansys.aedt.core.visualization.post.post_common_3d.PostProcessor3D.export_mesh_obj
documentation:
Export the mesh in AEDTPLT format.
The mesh has to be available in the selected setup.
If a parametric model is provided, you can choose the mesh to export by providing a specific set of variations.
This method applies only to `Hfss`, `Q3d`, `Q2D`, `Maxwell3d`, `Maxwell2d`, `Icepak`
and `Mechanical` objects. This method is calling `create_fieldplot_surface` to create a mesh plot and
`export_field_plot` to export it as `aedtplt` file.

# Parameters

setup

: Name of the setup. The default is `None`, in which case the `nominal_adaptive`
  setup is used. Be sure to build a setup string in the form of
  `"SetupName : SetupSweep"`, where `SetupSweep` is the sweep name to
  use in the export or `LastAdaptive`.

intrinsics

: Intrinsic variables required to compute the field before the export.
  These are typically: frequency, time and phase.
  It can be provided either as a dictionary or as a string.
  If it is a dictionary, keys depend on the solution type and can be expressed in lower or camel case as:
  \- `"Freq"` or `"Frequency"`.
  \- `"Time"`.
  \- `"Phase"`.
  If it is a string, it can either be `"Freq"` or `"Time"` depending on the solution type.
  The default is `None` in which case the intrinsics value is automatically computed based on the setup.

export_air_objects

: Whether to include vacuum objects for the copied objects.
  The default is `False`.

on_surfaces

: Whether to create a mesh on surfaces or on the volume. The default is `True`.

# Returns

str

: File Generated with full path.

# Examples

```pycon
>>> from ansys.aedt.core import Hfss
>>> hfss = Hfss()
>>> hfss.analyze()
>>> # Export report using defaults.
>>> hfss.post.export_mesh_obj(setup=None,intrinsics=None)
>>> # Export report using arguments.
>>> hfss.post.export_mesh_obj(setup="MySetup : LastAdaptive",intrinsics={"w1":"5mm", "l1":"3mm"})
```

methods.name: ansys.aedt.core.visualization.post.post_common_3d.PostProcessor3D.power_budget
documentation:
Power budget calculation.

# Parameters

units

: Output power units. The default is `"W"`.

temperature

: Temperature to calculate the power. The default is `22`.

output_type

: Output data presentation. The default is `"component"`.
  The options are `"component"`, or `"boundary"`.
  `"component"` returns the power based on each component.
  `"boundary"` returns the power based on each boundary.

# Returns

dict, float

: Dictionary with the power introduced on each boundary and total power.

# References

```pycon
>>> oEditor.ChangeProperty
```

# Parameters

max_frequency

ray_density

number_of_bounces

: Maximum number of bounces. Default is `5`.

multi_bounce

: Whether if enable or not Multi-Bounce ray density control. Default is `False`.

mbrd_max_sub_division

: Maximum number of MBRD subdivisions. Default is `2`.

shoot_utd

: Whether if enable or UTD Rays shooting or not. Default is `False`.

incident_theta

: Incident plane wave theta. Default is `"0deg"`.

incident_phi

: Incident plane wave phi. Default is `"0deg"`.

is_vertical_polarization

shoot_filter_type

: Shooter Type. Default is `"All Rays"`. Options are `"Rays by index"`, `"Rays in box"`.

ray_index_start

: Ray index start. Valid only if `"Rays by index"` is chosen. Default is `0`.

ray_index_stop

: Ray index stop. Valid only if `"Rays by index"` is chosen. Default is `1`.

ray_index_step

: Ray index step. Valid only if `"Rays by index"` is chosen. Default is `1`.

ray_box

  generated Class for more customization before plot.

export_path

plot_as_separate_objects

methods.name: ansys.aedt.core.visualization.post.post_common_3d.PostProcessor3D.plot_field_from_fieldplot
documentation:
Export a field plot to an image file (JPG or PNG) using Python PyVista.

This method does not support streamlines plot.

:::{note}
The PyVista module rebuilds the mesh and the overlap fields on the mesh.
:::

# Parameters

plot_name

: Name of the field plot to export.

project_path

: Path for saving the image file. The default is `""`.

mesh_plot

: Whether to create and plot the mesh over the fields. The default is `False`.

image_format

: Format of the image file. Options are `"jpg"`, `"png"`, `"svg"`, and `"webp"`.
  The default is `"jpg"`.

view

: View to export. Options are `"isometric"`, `"xy"`, `"xz"`, `"yz"`.

plot_label

: Type of the plot. The default is `"Temperature"`.

plot_folder

: Plot folder to update before exporting the field.
  The default is `None`, in which case all plot folders are updated.

show

: Export Image without plotting on UI.

scale_min

: Fix the Scale Minimum value.

scale_max

: Fix the Scale Maximum value.

plot_cad_objs

: Whether to include objects in the plot. The default is `True`.

log_scale

: Whether to plot fields in log scale. The default is `True`.

dark_mode

: Whether to display the model in dark mode or not. The default is `False`.

show_grid

: Whether to display the axes grid or not. The default is `False`.

show_bounding

: Whether to display the axes bounding box or not. The default is `False`.

show_legend

: Whether to display the legend. The default is `True`.

plot_as_separate_objects

: Whether to plot each object separately, which can require
  more time to export from AEDT. The default is `True`.

file_format

: File format to export the plot to. The default is ``` "case".
  Options are ``"aedtplt" ``` and `"case"`.
  If the active design is a Q3D design, the file format is automatically
  set to `"fldplt"`.

# Returns

{class}`ansys.aedt.core.generic.plot.ModelPlotter`

: Model Object.

methods.name: ansys.aedt.core.visualization.post.post_common_3d.PostProcessor3D.plot_field
documentation:
Create a field plot using Python PyVista and export to an image file (JPG or PNG).

:::{note}
The PyVista module rebuilds the mesh and the overlap fields on the mesh.
:::

# Parameters

quantity

: Quantity to plot. For example, `"Mag_E"`.

assignment

: One or more objects or faces to apply the field plot to.

plot_type

: Plot type. The default is `Surface`. Options are
  `"CutPlane"`, `"Surface"`, and `"Volume"`.

setup

: Setup and sweep name on which create the field plot. Default is None for nominal setup usage.

intrinsics

: Intrinsic variables required to compute the field before the export.
  These are typically: frequency, time and phase.
  It can be provided either as a dictionary or as a string.
  If it is a dictionary, keys depend on the solution type and can be expressed as:

  - `"Freq"` or `"Frequency"`.
  - `"Time"`.
  - `"Phase"`.

  If it is a string, it can either be `"Freq"` or `"Time"` depending on the solution type.
  The default is `None` in which case the intrinsics value is automatically computed based on the setup.

mesh_on_fields

: Whether to create and plot the mesh over the fields. The
  default is `False`.

view

: View to export. Options are `"isometric"`, `"xy"`, `"xz"`, `"yz"`.

plot_label

: Type of the plot. The default is `"Temperature"`.

show

: Export Image without plotting on UI.

scale_min

: Fix the Scale Minimum value.

scale_max

: Fix the Scale Maximum value.

plot_cad_objs

: Whether to include objects in the plot. The default is `True`.

log_scale

: Whether to plot fields in log scale. The default is `False`.

export_path

: Image export path. Default is `None` to not export the image.

image_format

: Format of the image file. Options are `"jpg"`, `"png"`, `"svg"`, and `"webp"`.
  The default is `"jpg"`.

keep_plot_after_generation

: Either to keep the Field Plot in AEDT after the generation is completed. Default is `False`.

dark_mode

: Whether to display the model in dark mode or not. The default is `False`.

show_grid

: Whether to display the axes grid or not. The default is `False`.

show_bounding

: Whether to display the axes bounding box or not. The default is `False`.

show_legend

: Whether to display the legend or not. The default is `True`.

filter_objects

: Objects list for filtering the `CutPlane` plots.

plot_as_separate_objects

: Plot each object separately. It may require more time to export from AEDT.

# Returns

{class}`ansys.aedt.core.generic.plot.ModelPlotter`

: Model Object.

methods.name: ansys.aedt.core.visualization.post.post_common_3d.PostProcessor3D.plot_animated_field
documentation:
Create an animated field plot using Python PyVista and export to a gif file.

:::{note}
The PyVista module rebuilds the mesh and the overlap fields on the mesh.
:::

# Parameters

quantity

: Quantity to plot (for example, `"Mag_E"`).

assignment

: One or more objects or faces to apply the field plot to.

plot_type

: Plot type. The default is `Surface`. Options are
  `"CutPlane"`, `"Surface"`, and `"Volume"`.

setup

: Setup and sweep name on which create the field plot. Default is None for nominal setup usage.

intrinsics

: Intrinsic variables required to compute the field before the export.
  These are typically: frequency, time and phase.
  It can be provided either as a dictionary or as a string.
  If it is a dictionary, keys depend on the solution type and can be expressed as:
  \- `"Freq"` or `"Frequency"`
  \- `"Time"`
  \- `"Phase"`

  If it is a string, it can either be `"Freq"` or `"Time"` depending on the solution type.
  The default is `None` in which case the intrinsics value is automatically computed based on the setup.

variation_variable

: Variable to vary. The default is `"Phi"`.

variations

: List of variation values with units. The default is `["0deg"]`.

view

: View to export. Options are `"isometric"`, `"xy"`, `"xz"`, `"yz"`.

show

: Export Image without plotting on UI.

scale_min

: Fix the Scale Minimum value.

scale_max

: Fix the Scale Maximum value.

plot_cad_objs

: Whether to include objects in the plot. The default is `True`.

log_scale

: Whether to plot fields in log scale. The default is `True`.

zoom

: Zoom factor.

export_gif

: Whether to export an animated gif or not. The default is `False`.

export_path

: Image export path. Default is `None` to not `working_directory` will be used to save the image.

force_opacity_value

: Opacity value between 0 and 1 to be applied to all model.
  If `None` aedt opacity will be applied to each object.

dark_mode

: Whether to display the model in dark mode or not. The default is `False`.

show_grid

: Whether to display the axes grid or not. The default is `False`.

show_bounding

: Whether to display the axes bounding box or not. The default is `False`.

show_legend

: Whether to display the legend or not. The default is `True`.

filter_objects

: Objects list for filtering the `CutPlane` plots.
  The default is `None` in which case an empty list is passed.

# Returns

{class}`ansys.aedt.core.generic.plot.ModelPlotter`

: Model Object.

methods.name: ansys.aedt.core.visualization.post.post_common_3d.PostProcessor3D.animate_fields_from_aedtplt
documentation:
Generate a field plot to an image file (JPG or PNG) using PyVista.

:::{note}
The PyVista module rebuilds the mesh and the overlap fields on the mesh.
:::

# Parameters

plot_name

: Name of the plot or the name of the object.

plot_folder

: Name of the folder in which the plot resides. The default
  is `None`.

variation_variable

: Variable to vary. The default is `"Phase"`.

variations

: List of variation values with units. The default is
  `["0deg"]`.

project_path

: Path for the export. The default is `""`, in which case the file is exported
  to the working directory.

export_gif

: Whether to export the GIF file. The default is `False`.

show

: Generate the animation without showing an interactive plot. The default is `True`.

dark_mode

: Whether to display the model in dark mode or not. The default is `False`.

show_grid

: Whether to display the axes grid or not. The default is `False`.

show_bounding

: Whether to display the axes bounding box or not. The default is `False`.

# Returns

{class}`ansys.aedt.core.generic.plot.ModelPlotter`

: Model Object.

methods.name: ansys.aedt.core.visualization.post.post_common_3d.PostProcessor3D.create_3d_plot
documentation:
Create a 3D plot using Matplotlib.

# Parameters

solution_data

: Input data for the solution.

nominal_sweep

: Name of the nominal sweep. The default is `None`.

nominal_value

: Value for the nominal sweep. The default is `None`.

primary_sweep

: Primary sweep. The default is `"Theta"`.

secondary_sweep

: Secondary sweep. The default is `"Phi"`.

snapshot_path

: Full path to image file if a snapshot is needed.
  The default is `None`.

show

: Whether if show the plot or not. Default is set to `True`.

# Returns

> bool
>
> : `True` when successful, `False` when failed.

methods.name: ansys.aedt.core.visualization.post.post_common_3d.PostProcessor3D.plot_scene
documentation:
Plot the current model 3D scene with overlapping animation coming from a file list and save the gif.

# Parameters

frames

: File list containing animation frames to plot in CSV format or
  path to a text index file containing the full path to CSV files.

gif_path

: Full path for outputting the GIF file.

norm_index

: Frame to use to normalize your images.
  Data is already saved as dB : 100 for usual traffic scenes.

dy_rng

: Specify how many dB below you would like to specify the range_min.
  Tweak this a couple of times with small number of frames.

fps

: Frames per Second.

show

: Either if show or only export gif.

view

: View to export. Options are `"isometric"`, `"xy"`, `"xz"`, and `"yz"`.
  The default is `"isometric"`.

zoom

: Default zoom. Default Value is `2`.

convert_fields_in_db

: Either if convert the fields before plotting in dB. Default Value is `False`.

log_multiplier

: Field multiplier if field in dB. Default Value is `10.0`.

# Returns

Manages the main AEDT postprocessing functions.

The inherited `AEDTConfig` class contains all `_desktop`
hierarchical calls needed for the class initialization data
`_desktop` and the design types `"HFSS"`, `"Icepak"`, and
`"HFSS3DLayout"`.

:::{note}
Some functionalities are available only when AEDT is running in
the graphical mode.
:::

# Parameters

app

: Inherited parent object. The parent object must provide the members
  `_modeler`, `_desktop`, `_odesign`, and `logger`.

# Examples

Basic usage demonstrated with an HFSS, Maxwell, or any other design:

```pycon
>>> from ansys.aedt.core import Hfss
>>> hfss = Hfss()
>>> post = hfss.post
```

functions.name: ansys.aedt.core.visualization.advanced.misc.convert_nearfield_data
documentation:
Convert a near field data folder to hfss `nfd` file and link it to `and` file.

Parameters
----------
dat_folder : str
    Full path to the folder containing near fields data.
    Folder will contain 24 files in the following format: `data_Ex_ymin.dat`. Same for H Fields.
frequency : float, int, str
    Frequency in `GHz`.
invert_phase_for_lower_faces : bool
    Add 180 deg for all fields at 'negative' faces (xmin, ymin, zmin).
output_folder : str, optional
    Output folder where files will be saved.

Returns
-------
str
    Full path to `.and` file.

methods.name: ansys.aedt.core.visualization.advanced.rcs_visualization.MonostaticRCSData.data_conversion_function
documentation:
RCS data conversion function.
The available functions are:

- `"dB10"`: Converts the data to decibels using base 10 logarithm.
- `"dB20"`: Converts the data to decibels using base 20 logarithm.
- `"abs"`: Computes the absolute value of the data.
- `"real"`: Computes the real part of the data.
- `"imag"`: Computes the imaginary part of the data.
- `"norm"`: Normalizes the data to have values between 0 and 1.
- `"ang"`: Computes the phase angle of the data in radians.
- `"ang_deg"`: Computes the phase angle of the data in degrees.

methods.name: ansys.aedt.core.visualization.advanced.rcs_visualization.MonostaticRCSData.__init_rcs
documentation:
Load monostatic radar cross-section data.

# Returns

bool

: `True` when successful, `False` when failed.

Provides monostatic radar cross-section (RCS) data.

Read monostatic RCS metadata in a JSON file generated by {func}`MonostaticRCSExporter` and return the
Python interface to plot and analyze the RCS data.

# Parameters

input_file

: Metadata information in a JSON file.

# Examples

```pycon
>>> from ansys.aedt.core import Hfss
>>> from ansys.aedt.core.generic.rcs_visualization import MonostaticRCSData
>>> app = Hfss(version="2023.2", design="Antenna")
>>> data = app.get_rcs_data()
>>> metadata_file = data.metadata_file
>>> app.release_desktop()
>>> rcs_data = MonostaticRCSData(input_file=metadata_file)
```

methods.name: ansys.aedt.core.visualization.advanced.rcs_visualization.MonostaticRCSPlotter.plot_rcs
documentation:
Create a 2D plot of the monostatic RCS.

# Parameters

primary_sweep

: X-axis variable. The default is `"IWavePhi"`. Options are `"Freq"`, `"IWavePhi"` and `"IWaveTheta"`.

secondary_sweep

: X-axis variable. The default is `"IWavePhi"`. Options are `"Freq"`, `"IWavePhi"` and `"IWaveTheta"`.

secondary_sweep_value

: List of cuts on the secondary sweep to plot. The default is `0`. Options are
  `"all"`, a single value float, or a list of float values.

title

: Plot title. The default is `"RectangularPlot"`.

output_file

: Full path for the image file. The default is `None`, in which case an image in not exported.

show

: Whether to show the plot. The default is `True`.
  If `False`, the Matplotlib instance of the plot is shown.

is_polar

: Whether this plot is a polar plot. The default is `True`.

show_legend

: Whether to display the legend or not. The default is `True`.

size

: Image size in pixel (width, height).

# Returns

{class}`ansys.aedt.core.visualization.plot.matplotlib.ReportPlotter`

: PyAEDT matplotlib figure object.

output_file

: Full path for the image file. The default is `None`, in which case an image in not exported.

: Whether to show the plot. The default is `True`.
  If `False`, the Matplotlib instance of the plot is shown.

size

: Image size in pixel (width, height).

# Returns

{class}`matplotlib.pyplot.Figure`

: Matplotlib figure object.
  If `show=True`, a Matplotlib figure instance of the plot is returned.
  If `show=False`, the plotted curve is returned.

methods.name: ansys.aedt.core.visualization.advanced.rcs_visualization.MonostaticRCSPlotter.plot_scene
documentation:
Plot the 3D scene including models, annotations, and results.

This method visualizes the 3D scene by rendering the mesh objects under the "model",
"annotations", and "results" categories stored in `self.all_scene_actors`. The meshes
are rendered using a default PyVista plotter.

# Parameters

show

: Whether to immediately display the plot. If `True`, the plot will be displayed using `plotter.show()`.
  If `False`, the `plotter` object is returned for further customization before rendering.
  The default is `True`.

# Returns

pyvista.Plotter or None

: Returns the `Plotter` object if `show` is set to `False`. If `show` is `True`,
  the plot is displayed and no value is returned.

methods.name: ansys.aedt.core.visualization.advanced.rcs_visualization.MonostaticRCSPlotter.add_rcs
documentation:
Add a 3D RCS representation to the current scene.

This function normalizes and visualizes RCS data on a spherical coordinate grid
(theta, phi), mapping it to 3D Cartesian coordinates (x, y, z). The RCS values are
color-mapped and added as a mesh to the current scene actors.

# Parameters

color_bar

: Color mapping to be applied to the RCS data. It can be a color (`"blue"`,
  `"green"`, ...) or a colormap (`"jet"`, `"viridis"`, ...). The default is `"jet"`.

methods.name: ansys.aedt.core.visualization.advanced.rcs_visualization.MonostaticRCSPlotter.add_range_profile_settings
documentation:
Add a 3D range profile setting representation to the current scene.

This function visualizes a 3D range profile with a main line representing the range axis
and tick marks indicating distance intervals. The profile includes visual elements like
a disc at the far end and a cone at the starting point. These elements help to display
a reference range profile in the 3D scene.

# Parameters

size_range

: Total size of the range in `meters`. It determines the length of the range
  profile. The default is `10.0`.

range_resolution

: Resolution of the range in `meters`, representing the distance between each tick mark along
  the range profile. The default is `0.1`.

tick_color

: Color of the tick marks along the range profile. The default is black (`"#000000"`).

methods.name: ansys.aedt.core.visualization.advanced.rcs_visualization.MonostaticRCSPlotter.add_waterfall_settings
documentation:
Add a 3D waterfall setting representation to the current scene.

This function visualizes a 3D "waterfall" pattern that represents angular data across
a circular arc in a spherical coordinate system. The arc covers an angular extent defined
by the `aspect_ang_phi` parameter, with optional tick marks along the arc. A cone is added
to indicate the endpoint of the angular sweep.

# Parameters

aspect_ang_phi

: The angular extent of the arc in degrees. It defines the total angle (in degrees) over
  which the circular arc spans. The default is `360.0` degrees (full circle).

phi_num

: The number of tick marks to be placed along the arc. The default is `10`.

tick_color

: Color of the tick marks. The default is black (`"#000000"`).

methods.name: ansys.aedt.core.visualization.advanced.rcs_visualization.MonostaticRCSPlotter.add_isar_2d_settings
documentation:
Add a 2D ISAR (Inverse Synthetic Aperture Radar) visualization to the current 3D scene.

This function creates a 2D range and cross-range profile in a 3D scene. It includes a main
line to represent the range axis, tick marks to indicate distance intervals, and additional
lines to mark the azimuth. The visualization aids in representing ISAR settings for both
range and cross-range dimensions.

# Parameters

size_range

: The total size of the range axis in meters. This sets the overall length of the
  range axis. The default is `10.0 meters`.

range_resolution

: Resolution of the range axis in meters, specifying the spacing between each tick mark.
  The default is `0.1 meters`.

size_cross_range

: The total size of the cross-range axis in meters. This sets the width of the cross-range
  axis. The default is `10.0 meters`.

cross_range_resolution

: Resolution of the cross-range axis in meters, specifying the spacing between each tick mark
  along the azimuth axis. The default is `0.1 meters`.

tick_color

: Color of the tick marks along both the range and cross-range axes. The default is
  black ("#000000").

methods.name: ansys.aedt.core.visualization.advanced.rcs_visualization.MonostaticRCSPlotter.add_range_profile
documentation:
Add the 3D range profile.

This function visualizes a 3D range profile, which represents the RCS data
as a function of range. The profile is rendered as a plot in the 3D scene using spherical coordinates
(phi, theta) mapped into Cartesian coordinates (x, y). The range profile can be visualized in different
plot types such as line plots or color-mapped surfaces.

# Parameters

plot_type
: The type of plot to create for the range profile. It can be `"Line"`, `"Ribbon"`, `"Rotated"`,

  : `"Extruded"`, `"Plane V"`, `` "Plane H"` ``, and `` "Projection"` ``. The default is `"Line"`.

color_bar

: Color mapping to be applied to the RCS data. It can be a color (`"blue"`,
  `"green"`, ...) or a colormap (`"jet"`, `"viridis"`, ...). The default is `"jet"`.

documentation: Add the 3D waterfall.

methods.name: ansys.aedt.core.visualization.advanced.rcs_visualization.MonostaticRCSPlotter.stretch_data
documentation:
Stretches and scales the input data to a specified range.

This method normalizes the input data between its minimum and maximum values and then applies
a linear transformation using the formula: `scaled_data = (data - min) / (max - min) * m + b`.
The parameters `m` and `b` control the scaling and shifting of the normalized data.

# Parameters

data

: The input data array or series to be stretched.

scaling_factor

: The scaling factor applied to the normalized data.

offset

: The offset added to the scaled data after normalization.

# Returns

numpy.ndarray or pandas.Series

: Transformed data

# Example:

```pycon
>>> data = np.array([1, 2, 3, 4, 5])
>>> stretched_data = stretch_data(data,2,1)
>>> print(stretched_data)
[1.  1.5 2.  2.5 3. ]
```

documentation: Add a mesh to the plotter with additional options.

methods.name: ansys.aedt.core.visualization.advanced.rcs_visualization.MonostaticRCSPlotter.__get_model_extent
documentation:
Calculate the 3D extent of the model by evaluating the bounding box dimensions
of each mesh object in the scene.

This method retrieves the maximum and minimum coordinates in the x, y, and z
directions for all mesh objects stored under the "model" key in `self.all_scene_actors`.
The bounding box of each mesh is assessed, and the overall bounds for the entire
model are determined by taking the min/max values from these individual bounding boxes.

methods.name: ansys.aedt.core.visualization.advanced.rcs_visualization.MonostaticRCSPlotter.__get_geometry
documentation: Get 3D meshes.

Provides monostatic radar cross-section (RCS) plot functionalities.

# Parameters

rcs_data

: Monostatic RCS data object.

# Examples

```pycon
>>> from ansys.aedt.core import Hfss
>>> from ansys.aedt.core.generic.rcs_visualization import MonostaticRCSData
>>> from ansys.aedt.core.generic.rcs_visualization import MonostaticRCSPlotter
>>> app = Hfss(version="2023.2", design="Antenna")
>>> data = app.get_rcs_data()
>>> metadata_file = data.metadata_file
>>> app.release_desktop()
>>> rcs_data = MonostaticRCSData(input_file=metadata_file)
>>> rcs_plotter = MonostaticRCSPlotter(rcs_data)
```
