functions.name: ansys.aedt.core.visualization.advanced.touchstone_parser.check_touchstone_files
documentation:
Check passivity and causality for all Touchstone files included in the folder.

# Parameters

input_dir

: Folder path. The default is `""`.

passivity

: Whether the passivity check is enabled, The default is `True`.

causality

: Whether the causality check is enabled. The default is `True`.

# Returns

dict

: Dictionary with the SNP file name as the key and a list if the passivity and/or causality checks are enabled.
  The first element in the list is a str with `"passivity"` or `"causality"` as a value. The second element
  is a Boolean that is set to `True` when the criteria passed or `False` otherwise. The last element
  is a string with the log information.

methods.name: ansys.aedt.core.visualization.advanced.touchstone_parser.TouchstoneData.get_insertion_loss_index

ports are shorted.

Plot all insertion losses. The first frequency point is used to determine whether two
ports are shorted.

plot

methods.name: ansys.aedt.core.visualization.advanced.touchstone_parser.TouchstoneData.get_mixed_mode_touchstone_data
documentation:
Transform network from single ended parameters to generalized mixed mode parameters.

num_of_diff_ports

: The number of differential ports.

port_ordering

: The current port ordering. Options are `"1234"`, `"1324"`. The default
  is `1234`.

# Returns

class:`ansys.aedt.core.generic.touchstone_parser.TouchstoneData`

methods.name: ansys.aedt.core.visualization.advanced.touchstone_parser.TouchstoneData.get_return_loss_index
documentation:
Get the list of all the Returnloss from a list of exctitations.

If no excitation is provided it will provide a full list of return losses.

Example: excitation_names ["1","2"] is_touchstone_expression=False output ["S(1,1)", S(2,2)]
Example: excitation_names ["S(1,1)","S(1,2)", S(2,2)] is_touchstone_expression=True output ["S(1,1)", S(2,2)]

Parameters
----------

excitation_name_prefix :
     (Default value = '')

Returns
-------
list
    List of index couples representing return losses of excitations.

methods.name: ansys.aedt.core.visualization.advanced.touchstone_parser.TouchstoneData.get_insertion_loss_index_from_prefix
documentation:
Get the list of all the Insertion Losses from prefix.

Parameters
----------
tx_prefix : str
    Prefix for TX (eg. "DIE").
rx_prefix : str
    Prefix for RX (eg. "BGA").

Returns
-------
list
    List of index couples representing Insertion Losses of excitations.

methods.name: ansys.aedt.core.visualization.advanced.touchstone_parser.TouchstoneData.get_next_xtalk_index
documentation:
Get the list of all the Near End XTalk a list of excitation. Optionally prefix can
be used to retrieve driver names.
Example: excitation_names ["1", "2", "3"] output ["S(1,2)", "S(1,3)", "S(2,3)"].

Parameters
----------
tx_prefix :
    prefix for TX (eg. "DIE") (Default value = "")

Returns
-------
list
    List of index couples representing Near End XTalks.

methods.name: ansys.aedt.core.visualization.advanced.touchstone_parser.TouchstoneData.get_fext_xtalk_index_from_prefix
documentation:
Get the list of all the Far End XTalk from a list of exctitations and a prefix that will
be used to retrieve driver and receivers names.
If skip_same_index_couples is true, the tx and rx with same index
position will be considered insertion losses and excluded from the list.

# Parameters

tx_prefix

: prefix for TX (eg. "DIE")

rx_prefix

: prefix for RX (eg. "BGA")

skip_same_index_couples

: Boolean ignore TX and RX couple with same index. The default value is `True`.

# Returns

list

: List of index couples representing Far End XTalks.

methods.name: ansys.aedt.core.visualization.advanced.touchstone_parser.TouchstoneData.plot_next_xtalk_losses
documentation:
Plot all next crosstalk curves.

Parameters
----------
Returns
-------
bool

methods.name: ansys.aedt.core.visualization.advanced.touchstone_parser.TouchstoneData.plot_fext_xtalk_losses
documentation:
Plot all fext crosstalk curves.

# Parameters

tx_prefix

: prefix for TX (eg. "DIE")

rx_prefix

: prefix for RX (eg. "BGA")

skip_same_index_couples

: Boolean ignore TX and RX couple with same index. The default value is `True`.

# Returns

bool

methods.name: ansys.aedt.core.visualization.advanced.touchstone_parser.TouchstoneData.get_worst_curve
documentation:
Analyze a solution data object with multiple curves and find the worst curve.
Take the mean of the magnitude over the frequency range.

# Parameters

freq_min

: Minimum frequency to analyze in GHz (None to 0). Default value is `None`.

freq_max

: Maximum frequency to analyze in GHz (None to max freq). Default value is `None`.

worst_is_higher

: Worst curve is the one with higher mean value. Default value is `True`.

curve_list

: List of [m,n] index of curves on which to search. None to search on all curves. Default value is `None`.

plot

: Whether to plot or not the chart.

# Returns

tuple

: Worst element, dictionary of ordered expression.
Contains data information from Touchstone Read call.

documentation:

: Style for the limit line. The default is `None`. You can also use

: Number of pixels for the hatch. The default is `None`.

  The default is `None`.

bool

: `True` when successful, `False` when failed.

methods.name: ansys.aedt.core.visualization.report.common.CommonReport.edit_grid
documentation:
Edit the grid settings for the plot.

# Parameters

minor_x

: Whether to enable the minor X grid. The default is `True`.

minor_y

: Whether to enable the minor Y grid. The default is `True`.

major_x

: Whether to enable the major X grid. The default is `True`.

major_y

: Whether to enable the major Y grid. The default is `True`.

style_minor

: Minor grid style. The default is `"Solid"`.

style_major

: Major grid style. The default is `"Solid"`.

minor_color

: Minor grid (R, G, B) color. The default is `(0, 0, 0)`.
  Each color value must be an integer in a range from 0 to 255.

major_color

: Major grid (R, G, B) color. The default is `(0, 0, 0)`.
  Each color value must be an integer in a range from 0 to 255.

# Returns

bool

: `True` when successful, `False` when failed.

documentation:
Edit the X-axis settings.

# Parameters

: Font name. The default is `"Arial"`.

font_size

: Font size. The default is `12`.

italic

: Whether to use italic type. The default is `False`.

bold

: Whether to use bold type. The default is `False`.

color

: Font (R, G, B) color. The default is `(0, 0, 0)`. Each color value
  must be an integer in a range from 0 to 255.

label

: Label for the Y axis. The default is `None`.

display_units

: Whether to display units. The default is `True`.

# Returns

bool

: `True` when successful, `False` when failed.

Edit the X-axis scaling settings.

linear_scaling

: Whether to use the linear scale. The default is `True`.

min_scale

: Minimum scale value with units. The default is `None`.

max_scale

: Maximum scale value with units. The default is `None`.

minor_tick_divs

: Minor tick division. The default is `5`.

min_spacing

: Minimum spacing with units. The default is `None`.

units

: Units in the plot. The default is `None`.

# Returns

bool

: `True` when successful, `False` when failed.

plot_color

functions.name: ansys.aedt.core.visualization.advanced.farfield_visualization.export_pyaedt_antenna_metadata
documentation:
Obtain PyAEDT metadata JSON file from AEDT metadata XML file or embedded element pattern TXT file.

# Parameters

input_file

: Full path to the XML or TXT file.

output_dir

: Full path to save the file to.

variation

: Label to identify corresponding variation.

model_info : dict, optional
power : dict, optional

> Dictionary with information of the incident power for each frequency.
> The default is `None`, in which case an empty dictionary is applied.
> From AEDT 2024.1, this information is available from the XML input file.
> For example, the dictionary format for a two element farfield
> data = power[1000000000.0]["IncidentPower"]
> data = [1, 0.99]

touchstone_file

: Touchstone file name. The default is `None`.

# Returns

str

: Metadata JSON file.

methods.name: ansys.aedt.core.visualization.advanced.farfield_visualization.FfdSolutionData.plot_contour
documentation:
Create a contour plot of a specified quantity in Matplotlib.

# Parameters

quantity

: Far field quantity to plot. The default is `"RealizedGain"`.
  Available quantities are: `"RealizedGain"`, `"RealizedGain_Phi"`, `"RealizedGain_Theta"`,
  `"rEPhi"`, `"rETheta"`, and `"rETotal"`.

phi

: Phi scan angle in degrees. The default is `0`.

theta

: Theta scan angle in degrees. The default is `0`.

title

: Plot title. The default is `"RectangularPlot"`.

quantity_format

: Conversion data function.
  Available functions are: `"abs"`, `"ang"`, `"dB10"`, `"dB20"`, `"deg"`, `"imag"`, `"norm"`,
  and `"real"`.

output_file

: Full path for the image file. The default is `None`, in which case the file is not exported.

levels

: Color map levels. The default is `64`.

show

: Whether to show the plot. The default is `True`. If `False`, the Matplotlib
  instance of the plot is shown.

polar

: Generate the plot in polar coordinates. The default is `True`. If `False`, the plot
  generated is rectangular.

max_theta

: Maximum theta angle for plotting. The default is `180`, which plots the far-field data for
  all angles. Setting `max_theta` to 90 limits the displayed data to the upper
  hemisphere, that is (0 < theta < 90).

# Returns

{class}`ansys.aedt.core.visualization.plot.matplotlib.ReportPlotter`

: Matplotlib figure object.

# Examples

```pycon
>>> from ansys.aedt.core
>>> app = ansys.aedt.core.Hfss(version="2024.2", design="Antenna")
>>> setup_name = "Setup1 : LastAdaptive"
>>> frequencies = [77e9]
>>> sphere = "3D"
>>> data = app.get_antenna_data(frequencies,setup_name,sphere)
>>> data.plot_contour()
```

methods.name: ansys.aedt.core.visualization.advanced.farfield_visualization.FfdSolutionData.plot_cut
documentation:
Create a 2D plot of a specified quantity in Matplotlib.

# Parameters

quantity

: Quantity to plot. The default is `"RealizedGain"`.
  Available quantities are: `"RealizedGain"`, `"RealizedGain_Theta"`, `"RealizedGain_Phi"`,
  `"rETotal"`, `"rETheta"`, and `"rEPhi"`.

primary_sweep

: X-axis variable. The default is `"phi"`. Options are `"phi"` and `"theta"`.

secondary_sweep_value

: List of cuts on the secondary sweep to plot. The default is `0`. Options are
  `"all"`, a single value float, or a list of float values.

phi

: Phi scan angle in degrees. The default is `0`.

theta

: Theta scan angle in degrees. The default is `0`.

title

: Plot title. The default is `"RectangularPlot"`.

quantity_format

: Conversion data function.
  Available functions are: `"abs"`, `"ang"`, `"dB10"`, `"dB20"`, `"deg"`, `"imag"`, `"norm"`,
  and `"real"`.

output_file

: Full path for the image file. The default is `None`, in which case an image in not exported.

show

: Whether to show the plot. The default is `True`.
  If `False`, the Matplotlib instance of the plot is shown.

is_polar

: Whether this plot is a polar plot. The default is `True`.

show_legend

: Whether to display the legend or not. The default is `True`.

# Returns

{class}`ansys.aedt.core.visualization.plot.matplotlib.ReportPlotter`

: Matplotlib figure object.
  If `show=True`, a Matplotlib figure instance of the plot is returned.
  If `show=False`, the plotted curve is returned.

# Examples

```pycon
>>> from ansys.aedt.core
>>> app = ansys.aedt.core.Hfss(version="2023.2", design="Antenna")
>>> setup_name = "Setup1 : LastAdaptive"
>>> frequencies = [77e9]
>>> sphere = "3D"
>>> data = app.get_antenna_data(frequencies,setup_name,sphere)
>>> data.plot_cut(theta=20)
```

methods.name: ansys.aedt.core.visualization.advanced.farfield_visualization.FfdSolutionData.plot_3d_chart
documentation:
Create a 3D chart of a specified quantity in Matplotlib.

# Parameters

quantity

: Far field quantity to plot. The default is `"RealizedGain"`.
  Available quantities are: `"RealizedGain"`, `"RealizedGain_Phi"`, `"RealizedGain_Theta"`,
  `"rEPhi"`, `"rETheta"`, and `"rETotal"`.

phi

: Phi scan angle in degree. The default is `0`.

theta

: Theta scan angle in degree. The default is `0`.

title

: Plot title. The default is `"3D Plot"`.

quantity_format

: Conversion data function.
  Available functions are: `"abs"`, `"ang"`, `"dB10"`, `"dB20"`, `"deg"`, `"imag"`, `"norm"`,
  and `"real"`.

output_file

: Full path for the image file. The default is `None`, in which case a file is not exported.

show

: Whether to show the plot. The default is `True`.
  If `False`, the Matplotlib instance of the plot is not shown.

# Returns

{class}`ansys.aedt.core.visualization.plot.matplotlib.ReportPlotter`

: Matplotlib figure object.

# Examples

```pycon
>>> from ansys.aedt.core
>>> app = ansys.aedt.core.Hfss(version="2023.2", design="Antenna")
>>> setup_name = "Setup1 : LastAdaptive"
>>> frequencies = [77e9]
>>> sphere = "3D"
>>> data = app.get_antenna_data(frequencies,setup_name,sphere)
>>> data.polar_plot_3d(theta=10)
```

methods.name: ansys.aedt.core.visualization.advanced.farfield_visualization.FfdSolutionData.plot_3d
documentation:
Create a 3D polar plot of the geometry with a radiation pattern in PyVista.

# Parameters

quantity

: Quantity to plot. The default is `"RealizedGain"`.
  Available quantities are: `"RealizedGain"`, `"RealizedGain_Theta"`, `"RealizedGain_Phi"`,
  `"rETotal"`, `"rETheta"`, and `"rEPhi"`.

quantity_format

: Conversion data function.
  Available functions are: `"abs"`, `"ang"`, `"dB10"`, `"dB20"`, `"deg"`, `"imag"`, `"norm"`,
  and `"real"`.

output_file

: Full path for the image file. The default is `None`, in which case a file is not exported.

rotation

: Far field rotation matrix. The matrix contains three vectors, around x, y, and z axes.
  The default is `[[1., 0., 0.], [0., 1., 0.], [0., 0., 1.]]`.

show

: Whether to show the plot. The default is `True`.

show_as_standalone

: Whether to show a plot as standalone. The default is `False`.

pyvista_object

: PyVista instance defined externally. The default is `None`.

background

: Background color if a list is passed or background picture if a string is passed.
  The default is `None`.

scale_farfield

: List with minimum and maximum values of the scale slider. The default is
  `None`.

show_beam_slider

: Whether the Theta and Phi scan slider is active. The default is `True`.

show_geometry :

: Whether to show the geometry. The default is `True`.

# Returns

bool or {class}`Pyvista.Plotter`

: `True` when successful. The {class}`Pyvista.Plotter` is returned when `show` and
  `image_path` are `False`.

# Examples

```pycon
>>> from ansys.aedt.core
>>> app = ansys.aedt.core.Hfss(version="2023.2", design="Antenna")
>>> setup_name = "Setup1 : LastAdaptive"
>>> frequencies = [77e9]
>>> sphere = "3D"
>>> data = app.get_antenna_data(setup=setup_name,sphere=sphere)
>>> data.plot_3d(quantity_format="dB10")
```

    Element index.

Provides antenna far-field data.

Read element pattern information in a JSON file generated by {func}`FfdSolutionDataExporter` and return the
Python interface to plot and analyze the far-field data.

# Parameters

input_file

: Metadata information in a JSON file.

frequency

: Active frequency in hertz (Hz). The default is `None`, in which case the first frequency is active.

variation

: Label to identify corresponding variation.

model_info : dict, optional
incident_power : dict, optional

> Dictionary with information of the incident power for each frequency.
> The default is `None`, in which case an empty dictionary is applied.
> From AEDT 2024.1, this information is available from the XML input file.
> For example, the dictionary format for a two element farfield
> data = incident_power["1GHz"]
> data = [1, 0.99]

touchstone_file

: Touchstone file name. The default is `None`.

# Examples

```pycon
>>> from ansys.aedt.core
>>> from ansys.aedt.core.visualization.advanced.farfield_visualization import FfdSolutionData
>>> app = ansys.aedt.core.Hfss(version="2023.2", design="Antenna")
>>> data = app.get_antenna_data()
>>> metadata_file = data.metadata_file
>>> app.release_desktop()
>>> farfield_data = FfdSolutionData(input_file=metadata_file)
>>> farfield_data.plot_3d(quantity_format="dB10")
```

Provides for updating far field data.

This class is used to interact with the far field Theta and Phi scan.

# Parameters

farfield_data

: Far field solution data instance.

farfield_quantity

: Quantity to plot. The default is `"RealizedGain"`.
  Available quantities are: `"RealizedGain"`, `"RealizedGain_Phi"`, `"RealizedGain_Theta"`,
  `"rEPhi"`, `"rETheta"`, and `"rETotal"`.

quantity_format

: Conversion data function.
  Available functions are: `"abs"`, `"ang"`, `"dB10"`, `"dB20"`, `"deg"`, `"imag"`, `"norm"`,

  > and `"real"`.

methods.name: ansys.aedt.core.twinbuilder.TwinBuilder.add_q3d_dynamic_component
documentation:
Add a Q2D or Q3D dynamic component to a Twin Builder design.

# Parameters

source_project

: Source project name or project path.

source_design_name

: Source design name.

setup

: Setup name.

sweep

: Sweep name.

coupling_matrix_name

: Coupling matrix name.

model_depth

: 2D model depth specified as value with unit.
  To be provided if design is Q2D.
  The default value is `1meter`

maximum_order

: The Maximum Order value specifies the highest order state space
  system that you can choose while fitting to represent the system behavior.
  A lower order may lead to less accuracy but faster simulation.
  The default value is `10000`.

error_tolerance

: Error Tolerance sets the error tolerance for S-Matrix fitting.
  The default value is `0.005`.

z_ref

: Sets the value of the Z (ref) in ohms.
  The default value is `50`.

state_space_dynamic_link_type

: Q3D state space dynamic link type.
  Possible options are:

  > - `S` for S parameters link type.
  > - `RLGC` for RLGC Parameters link type.
  > - `EQ` for Equivalent Circuit.

  The default value is `RLGC`.

component_name

: Component name.
  If not provided a generic name with prefix "SimpQ3DData" will be given.

save_project

: Save project after use.
  The default value is `True`.

# Returns

{class}`ansys.aedt.core.modeler.cad.object_3dcircuit.CircuitComponent` or bool

: Circuit component object if successful or `False` if fails.

# References

```pycon
>>> oComponentManager.AddDynamicNPortData
```

# Examples

Create an instance of Twin Builder.

```pycon
>>> from ansys.aedt.core import TwinBuilder
>>> tb = TwinBuilder()
```

Add a Q2D dynamic link component.

```pycon
>>> tb.add_q3d_dynamic_component("Q2D_ArmouredCableExample", "2D_Extractor_Cable", "MySetupAuto", "sweep1",
...                              "Original","100mm")
>>> tb.release_desktop()
```

methods.name: ansys.aedt.core.twinbuilder.TwinBuilder.add_excitation_model
documentation:
Use the excitation component to assign output quantities in a Twin Builder design to a windings
in a Maxwell design.
This method works only with AEDT 2025 R1 and later.

# Parameters

project

: Name or path to the project to provide.

design

: Name of the design to import the excitations from.

use_default_values

: Whether to use the default values for the start and stop times for the chosen TR setup.
  The default value is `True`.

setup

: Name of the Twinbuilder setup.
  If not provided, the default value is the first setup in the design.

start

: Start time provided as value + units.
  The default value is `None`.
  If not provided and `use_default_values=True`, the value is chosen from the TR setup.

stop

: Stop time provided as value + units.
  The default value is `None`.
  If not provided and `use_default_values=True`, the value is chosen from the TR setup.

export_uniform_points

: Whether Twin Builder is to perform linear interpolation to uniformly space out time and data points.
  The interpolation is based on the step size provided. The default is `False`.

export_uniform_points_step

: Step size to use for the uniform interpolation.
  The default value is `1E-5`.

excitations

: List of excitations to extract from the Maxwell design.
  It is a dictionary where the keys are the excitation names and value a list
  containing respectively:
  \- The excitation value to assign to the winding, provided as a string.
  \- A boolean whether to enable the component or not.
  \- The excitation type. Possible options are `Current` or `Voltage`.
  \- A boolean to enable the pin. If `True` the pin will be used to make connection on the schematic
  and the excitation value will be zeroed, since the expectation is that the value is provided
  through schematic connections.
  To know which excitations will be extracted from the Maxwell design use
  `app.excitations_by_type["Winding Group"]` where `app` is the Maxwell instance.
  If not provided, the method automatically retrieves the excitations from the Maxwell Design
  and sets the default excitation settings.

# Returns

{class}`pyaedt.modeler.cad.object3dcircuit.CircuitComponent` or bool

: Circuit component object if successful or `False` if fails.

# References

```pycon
>>> oComponentManager.AddExcitationModel
```

# Example

```pycon
>>> from ansys.aedt.core import TwinBuilder
>>> tb = TwinBuilder(specified_version="2025.1")
>>> maxwell_app = tb.desktop_class[[project_name, "my_maxwell_design"]]
>>> excitations = {}
>>> for e in maxwell_app.excitations_by_type["Winding Group"]:
...    excitations[e.name] = ["20", True, e.props["Type"], False]
>>> comp = tb.add_excitation_model(project=project_name, design="my_maxwell_design", excitations=excitations)
>>> tb.release_desktop(False, False)
```

Provides the Twin Builder application interface.

# Parameters

project

: Name of the project to select or the full path to the project
  or AEDTZ archive to open. The default is `None`, in which
  case an attempt is made to get an active project. If no
  projects are present, an empty project is created.

design

: Name of the design to select. The default is `None`, in
  which case an attempt is made to get an active design. If no
  designs are present, an empty design is created.

solution_type

: Solution type to apply to the design. The default is
  `None`, in which case the default type is applied.

setup

: Name of the setup to use as the nominal. The default is
  `None`, in which case the active setup is used or
  nothing is used.

version

: Version of AEDT to use. The default is `None`, in which
  case the active setup or latest installed version is
  used.
  Examples of input values are `232`, `23.2`,\`\`2023.2\`\`,\`\`"2023.2".

non_graphical

: Whether to launch AEDT in non-graphical mode. The default
  is `False`, in which case AEDT is launched in graphical mode.
  This parameter is ignored when a script is launched within AEDT.

new_desktop

: Whether to launch an instance of AEDT in a new thread, even if
  another instance of the `specified_version` is active on the
  machine. The default is `False`.

close_on_exit

: Whether to release AEDT on exit. The default is `False`.

student_version

: Whether to open the AEDT student version. The default is `False`.

machine

: Machine name to connect the oDesktop session to. This works only in 2022 R2
  or later. The remote server must be up and running with the command
  `"ansysedt.exe -grpcsrv portnum"`. If the machine is `"localhost"`,
  the server also starts if not present.

port

: Port number on which to start the oDesktop communication on an already existing server.
  This parameter is ignored when creating a new server. It works only in 2022 R2 or later.
  The remote server must be up and running with command `"ansysedt.exe -grpcsrv portnum"`.

aedt_process_id

: Process ID for the instance of AEDT to point PyAEDT at. The default is
  `None`. This parameter is only used when `new_desktop = False`.

remove_lock

: Whether to remove lock to project before opening it or not.
  The default is `False`, which means to not unlock
  the existing project if needed and raise an exception.

# Examples

Create an instance of Twin Builder and connect to an existing
Maxwell design or create a new Maxwell design if one does not
exist.

```pycon
>>> from ansys.aedt.core import TwinBuilder
>>> app = TwinBuilder()
```

Create a instance of Twin Builder and link to a project named
`"projectname"`. If this project does not exist, create one with
this name.

```pycon
>>> app = TwinBuilder(projectname)
```

Create an instance of Twin Builder and link to a design named
`"designname"` in a project named `"projectname"`.

```pycon
>>> app = TwinBuilder(projectname, designame)
```

Create an instance of Twin Builder and open the specified
project, which is named `"myfile.aedt"`.

```pycon
>>> app = TwinBuilder("myfile.aedt")
```

functions.name: ansys.aedt.core.visualization.plot.matplotlib.plot_matplotlib
documentation:
Create a matplotlib plot based on a list of data.

# Parameters

plot_data

: List of plot data. Every item has to be in the following format
  For type `fill`: `[x points, y points, color, label, alpha, type=="fill"]`.
  For type `path`: `[vertices, codes, color, label, alpha, type=="path"]`.
  For type `contour`: `[vertices, codes, color, label, alpha, line_width, type=="contour"]`.

size

: Image size in pixel (width, height). Default is `(1920, 1440)`.

show_legend

: Either to show legend or not. Default is `True`.

xlabel

: Plot X label. Default is `""`.

ylabel

: Plot Y label. Default is `""`.

title

: Plot Title label. Default is `""`.

snapshot_path

: Full path to image file if a snapshot is needed. Default is `None`.

x_limits

: List of x limits (left and right). Default is `None`.

y_limits

: List of y limits (bottom and top). Default is `None`.

axis_equal

: > Whether to show the same scale on both axis or have a different scale based on plot size.

  Default is `False`.

annotations

: List of annotations to add to the plot. The format is [x, y, string, dictionary of font options].
  Default is `None`.

show

: Whether to show the plot or return the matplotlib object. Default is `True`.

# Returns

{class}`matplotlib.pyplot.Figure`

: Matplotlib figure object.

functions.name: ansys.aedt.core.visualization.plot.matplotlib.plot_contour
documentation:
Create a Matplotlib figure contour based on a list of data.

:::{deprecated} 0.11.1 Use :class:`ReportPlotter` instead.
:::

# Parameters

plot_data

: List of plot data. Each item of the list a numpy array. The list has the following format:
  `[data, x points, y points]`.

size

: Image size in pixel (width, height). The default is `(2000,1600)`.

xlabel

: Plot X label. The default value is `""`.

ylabel

: Plot Y label. The default value is `""`.

title

: Plot Title label. The default value is `""`.

polar

: Generate the plot in polar coordinates. The default is `True`. If `False`, the plot
  generated is rectangular.

levels

: Color map levels. The default is `64`.

max_theta

: Maximum theta angle for plotting. It applies only for polar plots.
  The default is `180`, which plots the data for all angles.
  Setting `max_theta` to 90 limits the displayed data to the upper
  hemisphere, that is (0 < theta < 90).

color_bar

: Color bar title. The default is `None` in which case the color bar is not included.

snapshot_path

: Full path to image file if a snapshot is needed.
  The default value is `None`.

show

: Whether to show the plot or return the matplotlib object. Default is `True`.

# Returns

{class}`ansys.aedt.core.visualization.plot.matplotlib.ReportPlotter`

: Matplotlib class object.

methods.name: ansys.aedt.core.visualization.plot.matplotlib.ReportPlotter.plot_polar
documentation:
Create a Matplotlib polar plot based on a list of data.

# Parameters

traces

: Trace or traces to be plotted. It can be the trace name, the trace id or a list of those.

to_polar

: Whether if cartesian data has to be converted to polar before the plot or can be used as is.

snapshot_path

: Full path to the image file if a snapshot is needed.

show

: Whether to render the figure. The default is `True`. If `False`, the
  figure is not drawn.

is_degree

: Whether if data source are in degree or not. Default is `True`.

figure

: An existing Matplotlib `Figure` to which the plot is added.
  If not provided, a new `Figure` and `Axes` object are created.

# Returns

{class}`matplotlib.pyplot.Figure`

: Matplotlib figure object.

methods.name: ansys.aedt.core.visualization.plot.matplotlib.ReportPlotter.plot_3d
documentation:
Create a Matplotlib 3D plot based on a list of data.

# Parameters

trace

: Trace index or name on which create the 3D Plot.

snapshot_path

: Full path to image file if a snapshot is needed.

show

: Whether to show the plot or return the matplotlib object. Default is `True`.

color_map_limits

: Color map minimum and maximum values.

is_polar

: Whether if the plot will be polar or not. Polar plot will hide axes and grids. Default is `True`.

# Returns

{class}`matplotlib.pyplot.Figure`

: Matplotlib figure object.

methods.name: ansys.aedt.core.visualization.plot.matplotlib.ReportPlotter.plot_2d
documentation:
Create a Matplotlib figure based on a list of data.

# Parameters

traces

: Trace or traces to be plotted. It can be the trace name, the trace id or a list of those.

snapshot_path

: Full path to image file if a snapshot is needed.
  The default value is `None`.

show

: Whether to show the plot or return the matplotlib object. Default is `True`.

figure

: An existing Matplotlib `Figure` to which the plot is added.
  If not provided, a new `Figure` and `Axes` object are created.

# Returns

{class}`matplotlib.pyplot.Figure`

: Matplotlib figure object.

methods.name: ansys.aedt.core.visualization.plot.matplotlib.ReportPlotter.plot_contour
documentation:
Create a Matplotlib figure contour based on a list of data.

# Parameters

trace

: Trace index on which create the 3D Plot.

polar

: Generate the plot in polar coordinates. The default is `True`. If `False`, the plot
  generated is rectangular.

levels

: Color map levels. The default is `64`.

max_theta

: Maximum theta angle for plotting. It applies only for polar plots.
  The default is `180`, which plots the data for all angles.
  Setting `max_theta` to 90 limits the displayed data to the upper
  hemisphere, that is (0 < theta < 90).

color_bar

: Color bar title. The default is `None` in which case the color bar is not included.

snapshot_path

: Full path to image file if a snapshot is needed.
  The default value is `None`.

show

: Whether to show the plot or return the matplotlib object. Default is `True`.

# Returns

{class}`matplotlib.pyplot.Figure`

: Matplotlib figure object.
Matplotlib Report manager.

: Fields coordinates units. The default value is `"meter"`.

opacity

: Value between 0 to 1 of opacity. The default value is `1`.

: Color map of field plot. The default value is `"rainbow"`.

: Name of the field. The default value is `"Field"`.

tolerance

: Delauny tolerance value used for interpolating points. The default value is `1e-3`.

headers

: Number of lines to of the file containing header info that has to be removed.
  The default value is `2`.

methods.name: ansys.aedt.core.visualization.plot.pyvista.ModelPlotter.close
documentation: Close the render window.

Manages the data to be plotted with `pyvista`.

# Examples

This Class can be instantiated within Pyaedt (with plot_model_object or different field plots
and standalone).
Here an example of standalone project

```pycon
>>> model = ModelPlotter()
>>> model.add_object(r'D:\Simulation\antenna.obj', (200,20,255), 0.6, "in")
>>> model.add_object(r'D:\Simulation\helix.obj', (0,255,0), 0.5, "in")
>>> model.add_field_from_file(r'D:\Simulation\helic_antenna.csv', True, "meter", 1)
>>> model.background_color = (0,0,0)
>>> model.plot()
```

And here an example of animation:

```pycon
>>> model = ModelPlotter()
>>> model.add_object(r'D:\Simulation\antenna.obj', (200,20,255), 0.6, "in")
>>> model.add_object(r'D:\Simulation\helix.obj', (0,255,0), 0.5, "in")
>>> frames = [r'D:\Simulation\helic_antenna.csv', r'D:\Simulation\helic_antenna_10.fld',
...           r'D:\Simulation\helic_antenna_20.fld', r'D:\Simulation\helic_antenna_30.fld',
...           r'D:\Simulation\helic_antenna_40.fld']
>>> model.gif_file = r"D:\Simulation\animation.gif"
>>> model.animate()
```

methods.name: ansys.aedt.core.application.analysis_nexxim.FieldAnalysisCircuit.create_setup
documentation:
Create a setup.

# Parameters

name

: Name of the new setup. The default is `"MySetupAuto"`.

setup_type

: Type of the setup. The default is `None`, in which case
  the default type is applied.

kwargs

: Extra arguments to set up the circuit.
  Available keys depend on the setup chosen.
  For more information, see
  {doc}`../SetupTemplatesCircuit`.

# Returns

{class}`ansys.aedt.core.modules.solve_setup.SetupCircuit`

: Setup object.

# References

```pycon
>>> oModule.AddLinearNetworkAnalysis
>>> oModule.AddDCAnalysis
>>> oModule.AddTransient
>>> oModule.AddQuickEyeAnalysis
>>> oModule.AddVerifEyeAnalysis
>>> oModule.AddAMIAnalysis
```

# Examples

```pycon
>>> from ansys.aedt.core import Circuit
>>> app = Circuit()
>>> app.create_setup(name="Setup1",setup_type=app.SETUPS.NexximLNA,Data="LINC 0GHz 4GHz 501")
```

FieldCircuitAnalysis class.

This class is for circuit analysis setup in Nexxim.

It is automatically initialized by a call from an application,
such as HFSS or Q3D. See the application function for its
parameter definitions.

classes.name: ansys.aedt.core.application.analysis_hf.ScatteringMethods

methods.name: ansys.aedt.core.application.analysis_hf.ScatteringMethods.get_all_sparameter_list
documentation:
List of all S parameters for a list of excitations.

# Parameters

excitation_names

: List of excitations. The default is `None`, in which case
  the S parameters for all excitations are to be provided.
  For example, `["1", "2"]`.

# Returns

list

: Strings representing the S parameters of the excitations.
  For example, `["S(1, 1)", "S(1, 2)", S(2, 2)]`.

methods.name: ansys.aedt.core.application.analysis_hf.ScatteringMethods.get_all_return_loss_list
documentation:
Get a list of all return losses for a list of excitations.

# Parameters

excitations

: List of excitations. The default is `None`, in which case
  the return losses for all excitations are provided.
  For example `["1", "2"]`.

excitation_name_prefix

: Prefix to add to the excitation names. The default is `""`,

math_formula

: One of the available AEDT mathematical formulas to apply. For example, `abs, dB`.

nets

: List of nets to filter the output. The default is `None`, in which case all parameters are returned.

# Returns

list of str

: List of strings representing the return losses of the excitations.
  For example, `["S(1, 1)", S(2, 2)]`.

# References

```pycon
>>> oEditor.GetAllPorts
```

methods.name: ansys.aedt.core.application.analysis_hf.ScatteringMethods.get_all_insertion_loss_list
documentation:
Get a list of all insertion losses from two lists of excitations (driver and receiver).

# Parameters

drivers

: List of drivers. The default is `[]`. For example, `["1"]`.

receivers

: List of receivers. The default is `[]`. The number of drivers equals
  the number of receivers. For example, `["2"]`.

drivers_prefix_name

: Prefix to add to driver names. For example, `"DIE"`. The default is `""`.

receivers_prefix_name

: Prefix to add to receiver names. For example, `"BGA"`. The default is `""`.

math_formula

: One of the available AEDT mathematical formulas to apply. For example, `abs, dB`.

nets

: List of nets to filter the output. The default is `None`, in which
  case all parameters are returned.

# Returns

list of str

: List of strings representing insertion losses of the excitations.
  For example, `["S(1,2)"]`.

# References

```pycon
>>> oEditor.GetAllPorts
```

methods.name: ansys.aedt.core.application.analysis_hf.ScatteringMethods.get_next_xtalk_list
documentation:
Get a list of all the near end XTalks from a list of excitations (driver and receiver).

# Parameters

drivers

: List of drivers. The default is `None`. For example,
  `["1", "2", "3"]`.

drivers_prefix_name

: Prefix to add to driver names. For example, `"DIE"`. The default is `""`.

math_formula

: One of the available AEDT mathematical formulas to apply. For example, `abs, dB`.

nets

: List of nets to filter the output. The default is `None`, in which case
  all parameters are returned.

# Returns

list of str

: List of strings representing near end XTalks of the excitations.
  For example, `["S(1, 2)", "S(1, 3)", "S(2, 3)"]`.

# References

```pycon
>>> oEditor.GetAllPorts
```

methods.name: ansys.aedt.core.application.analysis_hf.ScatteringMethods.get_fext_xtalk_list
documentation:
Geta list of all the far end XTalks from two lists of excitations (driver and receiver).

# Parameters

drivers

: List of drivers. The default is `[]`. For example,
  `["1", "2"]`.

receivers

: List of receivers. The default is `[]`. For example,
  `["3", "4"]`.

drivers_prefix_name

: Prefix for driver names. For example, `"DIE"`. The default is `""`.

receivers_prefix_name

: Prefix for receiver names. For examples, `"BGA"` The default is `""`.

skip_same_index_couples

: Whether to skip driver and receiver couples with the same index position.
  The default is `True`, in which case the drivers and receivers
  with the same index position are considered insertion losses and
  excluded from the list.

math_formula

: One of the available AEDT mathematical formulas to apply. For example, `abs, dB`.

nets

: List of nets to filter the output. The default is `None`, in which case all
  parameters are returned.

# Returns

list of str

: List of strings representing the far end XTalks of the excitations.
  For example, `["S(1, 4)", "S(2, 3)"]`.

# References

```pycon
>>> oEditor.GetAllPorts
```

methods.name: ansys.aedt.core.application.analysis_hf.ScatteringMethods.get_touchstone_data
documentation:
Return a Touchstone data plot.

# Parameters

setup

: Name of the setup.

sweep

: Name of the sweep. The default value is `None`.

variations

: Dictionary of variation names. The default value is `None`.

# Returns

{class}`ansys.aedt.core.generic.touchstone_parser.TouchstoneData`

: Class containing all requested data.

# References

```pycon
>>> oModule.GetSolutionDataPerVariation
```

methods.name: ansys.aedt.core.application.analysis_hf.ScatteringMethods.export_touchstone
documentation:
Export a Touchstone file.

# Parameters

setup

: Name of the setup that has been solved.

sweep

: Name of the sweep that has been solved.

output_file

: Full path and name for the Touchstone file.
  The default is `None`, in which case the Touchstone file is exported to
  the working directory.

variations

: List of all parameter variations. For example, `["$AmbientTemp", "$PowerIn"]`.
  The default is `None`.

variations_value

: List of all parameter variation values. For example, `["22cel", "100"]`.
  The default is `None`.

renormalization

: Perform renormalization before export.
  The default is `False`.

impedance

: Real impedance value in ohm, for renormalization, if not specified considered 50 ohm.
  The default is `None`.

gamma_impedance_comments

: Include Gamma and Impedance values in comments.
  The default is `False`.

# Returns

str

: Filename when successful, `False` when failed.

# References

```pycon
>>> oDesign.ExportNetworkData
```

Class containing all methods related to scattering matrix management that are common to Hfss, Circuit and
Hfss3dLayout classes.

methods.name: ansys.aedt.core.application.analysis_3d_layout.FieldAnalysis3DLayout.create_setup
documentation:
Create a setup.

# Parameters

name

: Name of the new setup. The default is `"MySetupAuto"`.

setup_type

: Type of the setup. The default is `None`, in which case
  the default type is applied.

kwargs

: Extra arguments for setup settings.
  Available keys depend on the setup chosen. For more
  information, see {doc}`../SetupTemplates3DLayout`.

# Returns

{class}`ansys.aedt.core.modules.solve_setup.Setup3DLayout`

# References

```pycon
>>> oModule.Add
```

# Examples

```pycon
>>> from ansys.aedt.core import Hfss3dLayout
>>> app = Hfss3dLayout()
>>> app.create_setup(name="Setup1",MeshSizeFactor=2,SingleFrequencyDataList__AdaptiveFrequency="5GHZ")
```

methods.name: ansys.aedt.core.application.analysis_3d_layout.FieldAnalysis3DLayout.get_setup
documentation:
Retrieve a setup.

# Parameters

name

: Name of the setup.

setup_type

: Type of the setup. The default is `None`, in which case
  the default type is applied.

# Returns

{class}`ansys.aedt.core.modules.solve_setup.Setup3DLayout`

: Setup object.

methods.name: ansys.aedt.core.application.analysis_3d_layout.FieldAnalysis3DLayout.delete_setup
documentation:
Delete a setup.

# Parameters

name

: Name of the setup.

# Returns

bool

: `True` when successful, `False` when failed.

# References

```pycon
>>> oModule.Delete
```

# Examples

Create a setup and then delete it.

```pycon
>>> import ansys.aedt.core
>>> hfss3dlayout = ansys.aedt.core.Hfss3dLayout()
>>> setup1 = hfss3dlayout.create_setup(name='Setup1')
>>> hfss3dlayout.delete_setup()
...
PyAEDT INFO: Sweep was deleted correctly.
```

Manages 3D field analysis setup in HFSS 3D Layout.

This class is automatically initialized by an application call from this
3D tool. See the application function for parameter definitions.

# Parameters

application

: 3D application that is to initialize the call.

projectname

: Name of the project to select or the full path to the project
  or AEDTZ archive to open. The default is `None`, in which
  case an attempt is made to get an active project. If no
  projects are present, an empty project is created.

designname

: Name of the design to select. The default is `None`, in
  which case an attempt is made to get an active design. If no
  designs are present, an empty design is created.

solution_type

: Solution type to apply to the design. The default is
  `None`, in which case the default type is applied.

setup_name

: Name of the setup to use as the nominal. The default is
  `None`, in which case the active setup is used or
  nothing is used.

version

: Version of AEDT to use. The default is `None`, in which case
  the active version or latest installed version is used.

non_graphical

: Whether to run AEDT in the non-graphical mode. The default
  is `False`, in which case AEDT is launched in the graphical mode.

new_desktop

: Whether to launch an instance of AEDT in a new thread, even if
  another instance of the `specified_version` is active on the
  machine. The default is `False`.

close_on_exit

: Whether to release AEDT on exit. The default is `False`.

student_version

: Whether to enable the student version of AEDT. The default
  is `False`.

aedt_process_id

: Specifies by process ID the instance of AEDT to point PyAEDT at.
  This parameter is only used when `new_desktop=False`.

ic_mode

: Whether to set the design to IC mode. The default is `None`, which means to retain the
  existing setting.

remove_lock

: Whether to remove lock to project before opening it or not.
  The default is `False`, which means to not unlock
  the existing project if needed and raise an exception.

documentation: Modeler object.

Provides the Circuit Netlist (CircuitNetlist) interface.
Circuit Netlist Editor has no setup, solution, analysis or postprocessor
It is automatically initialized by Application call.
Refer to Application function for inputs definition

# Parameters

project

: Name of the project to select or the full path to the project
  or AEDTZ archive to open. The default is `None`, in which
  case an attempt is made to get an active project. If no
  projects are present, an empty project is created.

design

: Name of the design to select. The default is `None`, in
  which case an attempt is made to get an active design. If no
  designs are present, an empty design is created.

version

: Version of AEDT to use. The default is `None`. If `None`,
  the active setup is used or the latest installed version is
  used.

non_graphical

: Whether to launch AEDT in the non-graphical mode. The default
  is `False`, in which case AEDT is launched in the graphical mode.

new_desktop

: Whether to launch an instance of AEDT in a new thread, even if
  another instance of the `specified_version` is active on the
  machine. The default is `False`.

close_on_exit

: Whether to release AEDT on exit. The default is `False`.

student_version

: Whether to open the AEDT student version. The default is `False`.

aedt_process_id

: Only used when `new_desktop = False`, specifies by process ID which instance
  of Electronics Desktop to point PyAEDT at.

remove_lock

: Whether to remove lock to project before opening it or not.
  The default is `False`, which means to not unlock
  the existing project if needed and raise an exception.

methods.name: ansys.aedt.core.application.analysis_3d.FieldAnalysis3D.plot
documentation:
Plot the model or a subset of objects.

# Parameters

assignment

: List of objects to plot. The default is `None`, in which case all objects
  are exported.

show

: Whether to show the plot after generation. The default is `True`. If
  `False`, the generated class is returned for more customization before
  plot generation.

output_file

: Output file path to save the image to. If `None` no image will be saved.

plot_as_separate_objects

: Whether to plot each object separately. The default is `True`, which may
  require more time to export from AEDT.

plot_air_objects

: Whether to also plot air and vacuum objects. The default is `True`.

force_opacity_value

: Opacity value between 0 and 1 to applied to all the models. The
  default is `None`, which means the AEDT opacity is applied to each object.

clean_files

: Whether to clean created files after plot generation. The default is `False`,
  which means that the cache is maintained in the model object that is returned.

view

: View to export. Options are `"isometric"`, `"xy"`, `"xz"`, `"yz"`.
  The default is `"isometric"`.

show_legend

: Whether to display the legend or not. The default is `True`.

dark_mode

: Whether to display the model in dark mode or not. The default is `False`.

show_grid

: Whether to display the axes grid or not. The default is `False`.

show_bounding

: Whether to display the axes bounding box or not. The default is `False`.

# Returns

{class}`ansys.aedt.core.generic.plot.ModelPlotter`

: Model Object.

methods.name: ansys.aedt.core.application.analysis_3d.FieldAnalysis3D.assign_material
documentation:
Assign a material to one or more objects.

# Parameters

assignment

: One or more objects to assign materials to.

material

: Material to assign. If this material is not present, it is
  created.

# Returns

bool

: `True` when successful, `False` when failed.

# References

```pycon
>>> oEditor.AssignMaterial
```

# Examples

The method {func}`assign_material` is used to assign a material to a list of objects.

Open a design and create the objects.

```pycon
>>> from ansys.aedt.core import Hfss
>>> hfss = Hfss()
>>> box1 = hfss.modeler.create_box([10, 10, 10],[4, 5, 5])
>>> box2 = hfss.modeler.create_box([0, 0, 0],[2, 3, 4])
>>> cylinder1 = hfss.modeler.create_cylinder(orientation="X",origin=[5, 0, 0],radius=1,height=20)
>>> cylinder2 = hfss.modeler.create_cylinder(orientation="Z",origin=[0, 0, 5],radius=1,height=10)
```

Assign the material `"copper"` to all the objects.

```pycon
>>> objects_list = [box1, box2, cylinder1, cylinder2]
>>> hfss.assign_material(objects_list,"copper")
```

The method also accepts a list of object names.

```pycon
>>> obj_names_list = [box1.name, box2.name, cylinder1.name, cylinder2.name]
>>> hfss.assign_material(obj_names_list,"aluminum")
```

methods.name: ansys.aedt.core.application.analysis_3d.FieldAnalysis3D.import_dxf
documentation:
Import a DXF file.

# Parameters

file_path

: Path to the DXF file.

layers

: List of layer names to import. To get the dxf_layers in the DXF file,
  you can call the `get_dxf_layers` method.

auto_detect_close

: Whether to check polylines to see if they are closed.
  The default is `True`. If a polyline is closed, the modeler
  creates a polygon in the design.

self_stitch

: Whether to join multiple straight line segments to form polylines.
  The default is `True`.

self_stitch_tolerance

: Self stitch tolerance value. The default is `0`.

scale

: Scaling factor. The default is `0.001`. The units are `mm`.

defeature_geometry

: Whether to defeature the geometry to reduce complexity.
  The default is `False`.

defeature_distance

: Defeature tolerance distance. The default is `0`.

round_coordinates

: Whether to rounds all imported data to the number
  of decimal points specified by the next parameter.
  The default is `False`.

round_num_digits

: Number of digits to which to round all imported data.
  The default is `4`.

write_poly_with_width_as_filled_poly

: Imports wide polylines as polygons. The default is `False`.

import_method

: Whether the import method is `Script` or `Acis`.
  The default is `1`, which means that the `Acis` is used.

sheet_bodies_2d

: Whether importing as 2D sheet bodies causes imported objects to
  be organized in terms of 2D sheets. The default is `True`.

# Returns

bool

: `True` when successful, `False` when failed.

# References

```pycon
>>> oEditor.ImportDXF
```

methods.name: ansys.aedt.core.application.analysis_3d.FieldAnalysis3D.import_gds_3d
documentation:
Import a GDSII file.

# Parameters

input_file

: Path to the GDS file.

mapping_layers

: Dictionary keys are GDS layer numbers, and the value is a tuple with the thickness and elevation.

units

: Length unit values. The default is `"um"`.

import_method

: GDSII import method. The default is `1`. Options are:

  - `0` for script.
  - `1` for Parasolid.

# Returns

bool

: `True` when successful, `False` when failed.

# References

```pycon
>>> oEditor.ImportGDSII
```

# Examples

Import a GDS file in an HFSS 3D project.

```pycon
>>> gds_path = r"C:\temp\gds1.gds"
>>> from ansys.aedt.core import Hfss
>>> hfss = Hfss()
>>> gds_number = {7: (100, 10), 9: (110, 5)}
>>> hfss.import_gds_3d(gds_path,gds_number,units="um",import_method=1)
```

Manages 3D field analysis setup in HFSS, Maxwell 3D, and Q3D.

This class is automatically initialized by an application call from one of
the 3D tools. See the application function for parameter definitions.

# Parameters

application

: 3D application that is to initialize the call.

projectname

: Name of the project to select or the full path to the project
  or AEDTZ archive to open. The default is `None`, in which
  case an attempt is made to get an active project. If no
  projects are present, an empty project is created.

designname

: Name of the design to select. The default is `None`, in
  which case an attempt is made to get an active design. If no
  designs are present, an empty design is created.

solution_type

: Solution type to apply to the design. The default is
  `None`, in which case the default type is applied.

setup_name

: Name of the setup to use as the nominal. The default is
  `None`, in which case the active setup is used or
  nothing is used.

version

: Version of AEDT to use. The default is `None`, in which case
  the active version or latest installed version is used.

non_graphical

: Whether to run AEDT in non-graphical mode. The default
  is `False`, in which case AEDT is launched in the graphical mode.

new_desktop

: Whether to launch an instance of AEDT in a new thread, even if
  another instance of the `specified_version` is active on the
  machine. The default is `False`.

close_on_exit

: Whether to release AEDT on exit. The default is `False`.

student_version

: Whether to enable the student version of AEDT. The default
  is `False`.

aedt_process_id

: Only used when `new_desktop = False`, specifies by process ID which instance
  of Electronics Desktop to point PyAEDT at.

remove_lock

: Whether to remove lock to project before opening it or not.
  The default is `False`, which means to not unlock
  the existing project if needed and raise an exception.

This module contains these classes: `CSVDataset`, `DataSet`, `Expression`, `Variable`, and `VariableManager`.

functions.name: ansys.aedt.core.application.variables.generate_validation_errors
documentation:
From the given property names, expected settings and actual settings, return a list of validation errors.
If no errors are found, an empty list is returned. The validation of values such as "10mm"
ensures that they are close to within a relative tolerance.
For example an expected setting of "10mm", and actual of "10.000000001mm" will not yield a validation error.
For values with no numerical value, an equivalence check is made.

Parameters
----------
property_names : List[str]
    List of property names.
expected_settings : List[str]
    List of the expected settings.
actual_settings : List[str]
    List of actual settings.

Returns
-------
List[str]
    A list of validation errors for the given settings.

methods.name: ansys.aedt.core.application.variables.CSVDataset.next
documentation: Yield the next row.

Reads in a CSV file and extracts data, which can be augmented with constant values.

# Parameters

csv_file

: Input file consisting of delimited data with the first line as the header.
  The CSV value includes the header and data, which supports AEDT units information
  such as `"1.23Wb"`. You can also augment the data with constant values.

separator

: Value to use for the delimiter. The default is\`\`None\`\` in which case a comma is
  assumed.

units_dict

: Dictionary consisting of `{Variable Name: unit}` to rescale the data
  if it is not in the desired unit system.

append_dict

: Dictionary consisting of `{New Variable Name: value}` to add variables
  with constant values to all data points. This dictionary is used to add
  multiple sweeps to one result file.

valid_solutions

: The default is `True`.

invalid_solutions

: The default is `False`.

methods.name: ansys.aedt.core.application.variables.VariableManager.decompose
documentation:
Decompose a variable string to a floating with its unit.

# Parameters

variable : str

# Returns

tuple

: The float value of the variable and the units exposed as a string.

# Examples

```pycon
>>> hfss = Hfss()
>>> print(hfss.variable_manager.decompose("5mm"))
>>> (5.0, 'mm')
>>> hfss["v1"] = "3N"
>>> print(hfss.variable_manager.decompose("v1"))
>>> (3.0, 'N')
>>> hfss["v2"] = "2*v1"
>>> print(hfss.variable_manager.decompose("v2"))
>>> (6.0, 'N')
```

methods.name: ansys.aedt.core.application.variables.VariableManager.set_variable
documentation:
Set the value of a design property or project variable.

# Parameters

name

: Name of the design property or project variable
  (`$var`). If this variable does not exist, a new one is
  created and a value is set.

expression

: Valid string expression within the AEDT design and project
  structure. For example, `"3*cos(34deg)"`.

read_only

: Whether to set the design property or project variable to
  read-only. The default is `False`.

hidden

: Whether to hide the design property or project variable. The
  default is `False`.

description

: Text to display for the design property or project variable in the
  `Properties` window. The default is `None`.

sweep

: Allows you to designate variables to include in solution indexing as a way to
  permit faster post-processing.
  Variables with the Sweep check box cleared are not used in solution indexing.
  The default is `True`.

overwrite

: Whether to overwrite an existing value for the design
  property or project variable. The default is `False`, in
  which case this method is ignored.

is_post_processing
: Whether to define a postprocessing variable.

  : The default is `False`, in which case the variable is not used in postprocessing.

circuit_parameter
: Whether to define a parameter in a circuit design or a local parameter.

  : The default is `True`, in which case a circuit variable is created as a parameter default.

# Returns

bool

: `True` when successful, `False` when failed.

# References

```pycon
>>> oProject.ChangeProperty
>>> oDesign.ChangeProperty
```

# Examples

```pycon
>>> from ansys.aedt.core import Maxwell3d
>>> aedtapp = Maxwell3d(specified_version="2024.2")
```

Set the value of design property `p1` to `"10mm"`,
creating the property if it does not already eixst.

```pycon
>>> aedtapp.variable_manager.set_variable("p1",expression="10mm")
```

Set the value of design property `p1` to `"20mm"` only if
the property does not already exist.

```pycon
>>> aedtapp.variable_manager.set_variable("p1",expression="20mm",overwrite=False)
```

Set the value of design property `p2` to `"10mm"`,
creating the property if it does not already exist. Also make
it read-only and hidden and add a description.

```pycon
>>> aedtapp.variable_manager.set_variable(name="p2",
...                                       expression="10mm",
...                                       read_only=True,
...                                       hidden=True,
...                                       description="This is the description of this variable.")
```

Set the value of the project variable `$p1` to `"30mm"`,
creating the variable if it does not exist.

```pycon
>>> aedtapp.variable_manager.set_variable["$p1"] == "30mm"
```

methods.name: ansys.aedt.core.application.variables.VariableManager.delete_unused_variables
documentation:
Delete unused design and project variables.

# Returns

bool

: `True` when successful, `False` when failed.

Manages design properties and project variables.

Design properties are the local variables in a design. Project
variables are defined at the project level and start with `$`.

This class provides access to all variables or a subset of the
variables. Manipulation of the numerical or string definitions of
variable values is provided in the
{class}`ansys.aedt.core.application.variables.Variable` class.

# Parameters

variables

: Dictionary of all design properties and project variables in
  the active design.

design_variables

: Dictionary of all design properties in the active design.

project_variables

: Dictionary of all project variables available to the active
  design (key by variable name).

dependent_variables

: Dictionary of all dependent variables available to the active
  design (key by variable name).

independent_variables

: Dictionary of all independent variables (constant numeric
  values) available to the active design (key by variable name).

independent_design_variables : dict

independent_project_variables : dict

variable_names

: One or more variable names.

project_variable_names

: One or more project variable names.

design_variable_names

: One or more design variable names.

dependent_variable_names

: All dependent variable names within the project.

independent_variable_names

: All independent variable names within the project. These can
  be sweep variables for optimetrics.

independent_project_variable_names

: All independent project variable names within the
  project. These can be sweep variables for optimetrics.

independent_design_variable_names

: All independent design properties (local variables) within the
  project. These can be sweep variables for optimetrics.

# See Also

ansys.aedt.core.application.variables.Variable

# Examples

```pycon
>>> from ansys.aedt.core.maxwell import Maxwell3d
>>> from ansys.aedt.core.desktop import Desktop
>>> d = Desktop()
>>> aedtapp = Maxwell3d()
```

Define some test variables.

```pycon
>>> aedtapp["Var1"] = 3
>>> aedtapp["Var2"] = "12deg"
>>> aedtapp["Var3"] = "Var1 * Var2"
>>> aedtapp["$PrjVar1"] = "pi"
```

Get the variable manager for the active design.

```pycon
>>> v = aedtapp.variable_manager
```

Get a dictionary of all project and design variables.

```pycon
>>> v.variables
{'Var1': <ansys.aedt.core.application.variables.Variable at 0x2661f34c448>,
 'Var2': <ansys.aedt.core.application.variables.Variable at 0x2661f34c308>,
 'Var3': <ansys.aedt.core.application.variables.Expression at 0x2661f34cb48>,
 '$PrjVar1': <ansys.aedt.core.application.variables.Expression at 0x2661f34cc48>}
```

Get a dictionary of only the design variables.

```pycon
>>> v.design_variables
{'Var1': <ansys.aedt.core.application.variables.Variable at 0x2661f339508>,
 'Var2': <ansys.aedt.core.application.variables.Variable at 0x2661f3415c8>,
 'Var3': <ansys.aedt.core.application.variables.Expression at 0x2661f341808>}
```

Get a dictionary of only the independent design variables.

```pycon
>>> v.independent_design_variables
{'Var1': <ansys.aedt.core.application.variables.Variable at 0x2661f335d08>,
 'Var2': <ansys.aedt.core.application.variables.Variable at 0x2661f3557c8>}
```

methods.name: ansys.aedt.core.application.variables.Variable.evaluated_value
documentation:
String value.

The numeric value with the unit is concatenated and returned as a string. The numeric display
in the modeler and the string value can differ. For example, you might see `10mm` in the
modeler and see `10.0mm` returned as the string value.

methods.name: ansys.aedt.core.application.variables.Variable.decompose
documentation:
Decompose a variable value to a floating with its unit.

# Returns

tuple

: The float value of the variable and the units exposed as a string.

# Examples

```pycon
>>> hfss = Hfss()
>>> hfss["v1"] = "3N"
>>> print(hfss.variable_manager["v1"].decompose("v1"))
>>> (3.0, 'N')
```

methods.name: ansys.aedt.core.application.variables.Variable.rescale_to
documentation:
Rescale the expression to a new unit within the current unit system.

# Parameters

units

: Units to rescale to.

# Examples

```pycon
>>> from ansys.aedt.core.application.variables import Variable
```

```pycon
>>> v = Variable("10W")
>>> assert v.numeric_value == 10
>>> assert v.units == "W"
>>> v.rescale_to("kW")
>>> assert v.numeric_value == 0.01
>>> assert v.units == "kW"
```

methods.name: ansys.aedt.core.application.variables.Variable.format
documentation:
Retrieve the string value with the specified numerical formatting.

# Parameters

format

: Format for the numeric value of the string. For example, `'06.2f'`. For
  more information, see the [PyFormat documentation](https://pyformat.info/).

# Returns

str

: String value with the specified numerical formatting.

# Examples

```pycon
>>> from ansys.aedt.core.application.variables import Variable
```

```pycon
>>> v = Variable("10W")
>>> assert v.format("f") == '10.000000W'
>>> assert v.format("06.2f") == '010.00W'
>>> assert v.format("6.2f") == ' 10.00W'
```

methods.name: ansys.aedt.core.application.variables.Variable.__mul__
documentation:
Multiply the variable with a number or another variable and return a new object.

        Parameters
        ----------
        other : numbers.Number or variable
            Object to be multiplied.

        Returns
        -------
        type
            Variable.

        Examples
        --------
        >>> from ansys.aedt.core.application.variables import Variable

        Multiply ``'Length1'`` by unitless ``'None'``` to obtain ``'Length'``.
        A numerical value is also considered to be unitless.

import ansys.aedt.core.generic.constants        >>> v1 = Variable("10mm")
        >>> v2 = Variable(3)
        >>> result_1 = v1 * v2
        >>> result_2 = v1 * 3
        >>> assert result_1.numeric_value == 30.0
        >>> assert result_1.unit_system == "Length"
        >>> assert result_2.numeric_value == result_1.numeric_value
        >>> assert result_2.unit_system == "Length"

        Multiply voltage times current to obtain power.

import ansys.aedt.core.generic.constants        >>> v3 = Variable("3mA")
        >>> v4 = Variable("40V")
        >>> result_3 = v3 * v4
        >>> assert result_3.numeric_value == 0.12
        >>> assert result_3.units == "W"
        >>> assert result_3.unit_system == "Power"

methods.name: ansys.aedt.core.application.variables.Variable.__add__
documentation:
Add the variable to another variable to return a new object.

# Parameters

other

: Object to be multiplied.

# Returns

type

: Variable.

# Examples

```pycon
>>> from ansys.aedt.core.application.variables import Variable
>>> import ansys.aedt.core.generic.constants
>>> v1 = Variable("3mA")
>>> v2 = Variable("10A")
>>> result = v1 + v2
>>> assert result.numeric_value == 10.003
>>> assert result.units == "A"
>>> assert result.unit_system == "Current"
```

methods.name: ansys.aedt.core.application.variables.Variable.__sub__
documentation:
Subtract another variable from the variable to return a new object.

# Parameters

other

: Object to be subtracted.

# Returns

type

: Variable.

# Examples

```pycon
>>> import ansys.aedt.core.generic.constants
>>> from ansys.aedt.core.application.variables import Variable
>>> v3 = Variable("3mA")
>>> v4 = Variable("10A")
>>> result_2 = v3 - v4
>>> assert result_2.numeric_value == -9.997
>>> assert result_2.units == "A"
>>> assert result_2.unit_system == "Current"
```

methods.name: ansys.aedt.core.application.variables.Variable.__truediv__
documentation:
Divide the variable by a number or another variable to return a new object.

# Parameters

other

: Object by which to divide.

# Returns

type

: Variable.

# Examples

Divide a variable with units `"W"` by a variable with units `"V"` and automatically
resolve the new units to `"A"`.

```pycon
>>> from ansys.aedt.core.application.variables import Variable
>>> import ansys.aedt.core.generic.constants
>>> v1 = Variable("10W")
>>> v2 = Variable("40V")
>>> result = v1 / v2
>>> assert result_1.numeric_value == 0.25
>>> assert result_1.units == "A"
>>> assert result_1.unit_system == "Current"
```
Python 3.x version

methods.name: ansys.aedt.core.application.variables.Variable.__div__
documentation: Python 2.7 version

methods.name: ansys.aedt.core.application.variables.Variable.__rtruediv__
documentation:
Divide another object by this object.

# Parameters

other

: Object to divide by.

# Returns

type

: Variable.

# Examples

Divide a number by a variable with units `"s"` and automatically determine that
the result is in `"Hz"`.

```pycon
>>> import ansys.aedt.core.generic.constants
>>> from ansys.aedt.core.application.variables import Variable
>>> v = Variable("1s")
>>> result = 3.0 / v
>>> assert result.numeric_value == 3.0
>>> assert result.units == "Hz"
>>> assert result.unit_system == "Freq"
```

Stores design properties and project variables and provides operations to perform on them.

# Parameters

value

: Numerical value of the variable in SI units.

units

: Units for the value.

# Examples

```pycon
>>> from ansys.aedt.core.application.variables import Variable
```

Define a variable using a string value consistent with the AEDT properties.

```pycon
>>> v = Variable("45mm")
```

Define an unitless variable with a value of 3.0.

```pycon
>>> v = Variable(3.0)
```

Define a variable defined by a numeric result and a unit string.

```pycon
>>> v = Variable(3.0 * 4.5, units="mm")
>>> assert v.numeric_value = 13.5
>>> assert v.units = "mm"
```

methods.name: ansys.aedt.core.application.analysis_maxwell_circuit.AnalysisMaxwellCircuit.modeler
documentation:
Design oModeler.

# Returns

{class}`ansys.aedt.core.modeler.schematic.ModelerMaxwellCircuit`

Provides the Maxwell Circuit (MaxwellCircuit) interface.
Maxwell Circuit Editor has no setup, solution, analysis or postprocessor
It is automatically initialized by Application call (Maxwell Circuit).
Refer to Application function for inputs definition

# Parameters

project

: Name of the project to select or the full path to the project
  or AEDTZ archive to open. The default is `None`, in which
  case an attempt is made to get an active project. If no
  projects are present, an empty project is created.

design

: Name of the design to select. The default is `None`, in
  which case an attempt is made to get an active design. If no
  designs are present, an empty design is created.

version

: Version of AEDT to use. The default is `None`. If `None`,
  the active setup is used or the latest installed version is
  used.

non_graphical

: Whether to launch AEDT in the non-graphical mode. The default
  is `False`, in which case AEDT is launched in the graphical mode.

new_desktop

: Whether to launch an instance of AEDT in a new thread, even if
  another instance of the `specified_version` is active on the
  machine. The default is `False`.

close_on_exit

: Whether to release AEDT on exit. The default is `False`.

student_version

: Whether to open the AEDT student version. The default is `False`.

aedt_process_id

: Only used when `new_desktop = False`, specifies by process ID which instance
  of Electronics Desktop to point PyAEDT at.

remove_lock

: Whether to remove lock to project before opening it or not.
  The default is `False`, which means to not unlock
  the existing project if needed and raise an exception.

methods.name: ansys.aedt.core.application.analysis.Analysis.get_traces_for_plot
documentation:
Retrieve a list of traces of specified designs ready to use in plot reports.

# Parameters

get_self_terms

: Whether to return self terms. The default is `True`.

get_mutual_terms

: Whether to return mutual terms. The default is `True`.

first_element_filter

: Filter to apply to the first element of the equation.
  This parameter accepts `*` and `?` as special characters. The default is `None`.

second_element_filter

: Filter to apply to the second element of the equation.
  This parameter accepts `*` and `?` as special characters. The default is `None`.

category

: Plot category name as in the report (including operator).
  The default is `"dB(S)"`, which is the plot category name for capacitance.

differential_pairs

: Differential pairs defined. The default is `None` in which case an empty list is set.

# Returns

list

: List of traces of specified designs ready to use in plot reports.

# Examples

```pycon
>>> from ansys.aedt.core import Hfss3dLayout
>>> hfss = Hfss3dLayout(project_path)
>>> hfss.get_traces_for_plot(first_element_filter="Bo?1",
...                          second_element_filter="GND*", category="dB(S")
>>> hfss.get_traces_for_plot(differential_pairs=['Diff_U0_data0','Diff_U1_data0','Diff_U1_data1'],
...                          first_element_filter="*_U1_data?",
...                          second_element_filter="*_U0_*", category="dB(S")
```

methods.name: ansys.aedt.core.application.analysis.Analysis.export_results
documentation:
Export all available reports to a file, including profile, and convergence and sNp when applicable.

# Parameters

analyze

: Whether to analyze before export. Solutions must be present for the design.

export_folder

: Full path to the project folder. The default is `None`, in which case the
  working directory is used.

matrix_name

: Matrix to specify to export touchstone file.
  The default is `Original`, in which case default matrix is taken.
  This argument applies only to 2DExtractor and Q3D setups where Matrix reduction is computed
  and needed to export touchstone file.

matrix_type

: Type of matrix to export. The default is `S` to export a touchstone file.
  Available values are `S`, `Y`, `Z`. `Y` and `Z` matrices will be exported as tab file.

touchstone_format

: Touchstone format. The default is `MagPahse`.
  Available values are: `MagPahse`, `DbPhase`, `RealImag`.

length

: Length of the model to export. The default is `1meter`.

impedance

: Real impedance value in ohms, for renormalization. The default is `50`.

touchstone_number_precision

: Touchstone number of digits precision. The default is `15`.

include_gamma_comment

: Specifies whether to include Gamma and Impedance comments. The default is `True`.

support_non_standard_touchstone_extension

: Specifies whether to support non-standard Touchstone extensions for mixed reference impedance.
  The default is `False`.

variations

: List of variation values with units. The default is all variations.

# Returns

list

: List of all exported files.

# References

```pycon
>>> oModule.GetAllPortsList
>>> oDesign.ExportProfile
>>> oModule.ExportToFile
>>> oModule.ExportConvergence
>>> oModule.ExportNetworkData
```

# Examples

```pycon
>>> from ansys.aedt.core import Hfss
>>> aedtapp = Hfss()
>>> aedtapp.analyze()
>>> exported_files = aedtapp.export_results()
```

methods.name: ansys.aedt.core.application.analysis.Analysis.analyze
documentation:
Solve the active design.

# Parameters

setup

: Setup to analyze. The default is `None`, in which case all
  setups are solved.

cores

: Number of simulation cores. Default is `4` which is the number of cores available in license.

tasks

: Number of simulation tasks. The default is `1`.
  In bach solve, set `tasks` to `-1` to apply auto settings and distributed mode.

gpus

: Number of simulation graphic processing units to use. The default is `0`.

acf_file

: Full path to the custom ACF file.

use_auto_settings

: Set `True` to use automatic settings for HPC. The option is only considered for setups
  that support automatic settings.

solve_in_batch

: Whether to solve the project in batch or not.
  If `True` the project will be saved, closed, solved and repened.

machine

: Name of the machine if remote. The default is `"localhost"`.

run_in_thread

: Whether to submit the batch command as a thread. The default is
  `False`.

revert_to_initial_mesh

: Whether to revert to initial mesh before solving or not. Default is `False`.

blocking

: Whether to block script while analysis is completed or not. It works from AEDT 2023 R2.
  Default is `True`.

# Returns

bool

: `True` when successful, `False` when failed.

# References

```pycon
>>> oDesign.Analyze
```

methods.name: ansys.aedt.core.application.analysis.Analysis.analyze_setup
documentation:
Analyze a design setup.

# Parameters

name

: Name of the setup, which can be an optimetric setup or a simple setup.
  The default is `None`, in which case all setups are solved.

cores

: Number of simulation cores. The default is `4`.

tasks

: Number of simulation tasks. The default is `1`.

gpus

: Number of simulation graphics processing units. The default is `0`.

acf_file

: Full path to the custom ACF file. The default is `None`.

use_auto_settings

: Either use or not auto settings in task/cores. It is not supported by all Setup.

num_variations_to_distribute

: Number of variations to distribute. For this to take effect `use_auto_settings` must be set to `True`.

allowed_distribution_types

: List of strings. Each string represents a distribution type. The default value `None` does nothing.
  An empty list `[]` disables all types.

revert_to_initial_mesh

: Whether to revert to initial mesh before solving or not. Default is `False`.

blocking

: Whether to block script while analysis is completed or not. It works from AEDT 2023 R2.
  Default is `True`.

# Returns

bool

: `True` when successful, `False` when failed.

# References

```pycon
>>> oDesign.Analyze
```

  which exports the file to the working directory.

variations

  The default is `None`.

  The default is `None`.

renormalization

  The default is `False`.

  The default is `None`.

comments

: Include Gamma and Impedance values in comments.
  The default is `False`.

: file name when successful, `False` when failed.

methods.name: ansys.aedt.core.application.analysis.Analysis.value_with_units
documentation:
Combine a number and a string containing the modeler length unit in a single
string e.g. "1.2mm".
If the units are not specified, the model units are used.
If value is a string (like containing an expression), it is returned as is.

Parameters
----------
value : float, int, str
    Value of the number or string containing an expression.
units : str, optional
    Units to combine with value. Valid values are defined in the native API documentation.
    Some common examples are:
    "in": inches
    "cm": centimeter
    "um": micron
    "mm": millimeter
    "meter": meters
    "mil": 0.001 inches (mils)
    "km": kilometer
    "ft": feet
units_system : str, optional
    Unit system. Default is `"Length"`.

Returns
-------
str
    String that combines the value and the units (e.g. "1.2mm").

inner_classes.name: ansys.aedt.core.application.analysis.Analysis.AxisDir
documentation: Contains constants for the axis directions.

Contains all common analysis functions.

This class is inherited in the caller application and is accessible through it ( eg. `hfss.method_name`).

It is automatically initialized by a call from an application, such as HFSS or Q3D.
See the application function for its parameter descriptions.

# Parameters

application

: Application that is to initialize the call.

projectname

: Name of the project to select or the full path to the project
  or AEDTZ archive to open.

designname

: Name of the design to select.

solution_type

: Solution type to apply to the design.

setup_name

: Name of the setup to use as the nominal.

specified_version

: Version of AEDT to use.

NG

: Whether to run AEDT in the non-graphical mode.

new_desktop

: Whether to launch an instance of AEDT in a new thread, even if
  another instance of the `specified_version` is active on the
  machine.

close_on_exit

: Whether to release AEDT on exit.

student_version

: Whether to enable the student version of AEDT.

aedt_process_id

: Only used when `new_desktop = False`, specifies by process ID which instance
  of Electronics Desktop to point PyAEDT at.

ic_mode

: Whether to set the design to IC mode. The default is `None`, which means to retain the
  existing setting. This parameter applies only to HFSS 3D Layout.

remove_lock

: Whether to remove lock to project before opening it or not.
  The default is `False`, which means to not unlock
  the existing project if needed and raise an exception.
