methods.name: ansys.aedt.core.rmxprt.Rmxprt.import_configuration
documentation:
Parse a json file and assign all the properties to the Rmxprt design.

# Parameters

input_file

: Full path to json file to be used.

# Returns

bool

: `True` when successful, `False` when failed.

Provides the RMxprt app interface.

# Parameters

project

: Name of the project to select or the full path to the project
  or AEDTZ archive to open. The default is `None`, in which
  case an attempt is made to get an active project. If no
  projects are present, an empty project is created.

design

: Name of the design to select. The default is `None`, in
  which case an attempt is made to get an active design. If no
  designs are present, an empty design is created.

solution_type

: Solution type to apply to the design. The default is
  `None`, in which case the default type is applied.

model_units

: Model units.

setup

: Name of the setup to use as the nominal. The default is
  `None`, in which case the active setup is used or
  nothing is used.

version

: Version of AEDT to use. The default is `None`, in which case
  the active setup is used or the latest installed version is
  used.
  Examples of input values are `232`, `23.2`,\`\`2023.2\`\`,\`\`"2023.2".

non_graphical

: Whether to launch AEDT in non-graphical mode. The default
  is `False`, in which case AEDT is launched in graphical mode.
  This parameter is ignored when a script is launched within AEDT.

new_desktop

: Whether to launch an instance of AEDT in a new thread, even if
  another instance of the `specified_version` is active on the
  machine. The default is `False`.

close_on_exit

: Whether to release AEDT on exit. The default is `False`.

student_version

: Whether to open the AEDT student version. The default is `False`.

machine

: Machine name to connect the oDesktop session to. This works only in 2022 R2 or
  later. The remote server must be up and running with the command
  `"ansysedt.exe -grpcsrv portnum"`. If the machine is `"localhost"`, the
  server also starts if not present.

port

: Port number on which to start the oDesktop communication on an already existing server.
  This parameter is ignored when creating a new server. It works only in 2022 R2 or later.
  The remote server must be up and running with the command `"ansysedt.exe -grpcsrv portnum"`.

aedt_process_id

: Process ID for the instance of AEDT to point PyAEDT at. The default is
  `None`. This parameter is only used when `new_desktop = False`.

remove_lock

: Whether to remove lock to project before opening it or not.
  The default is `False`, which means to not unlock
  the existing project if needed and raise an exception.

# Examples

Create an instance of RMxprt and connect to an existing RMxprt
design or create a new RMxprt design if one does not exist.

```pycon
>>> from ansys.aedt.core import Rmxprt
>>> app = Rmxprt()
```

Create an instance of Rmxprt and link to a project named
`"projectname"`. If this project does not exist, create one with
this name.

```pycon
>>> app = Rmxprt(projectname)
```

Create an instance of RMxprt and link to a design named
`"designname"` in a project named `"projectname"`.

```pycon
>>> app = Rmxprt(projectname,designame)
```

Create an instance of RMxprt and open the specified project,
which is `"myfile.aedt"`.

```pycon
>>> app = Rmxprt("myfile.aedt")
```

methods.name: ansys.aedt.core.q3d.QExtractor.get_traces_for_plot
documentation:
Get a list of traces of specified designs ready to use in plot reports.

# Parameters

get_self_terms

: Whether to get self terms. The default is `True`.

get_mutual_terms

: Whether to get mutual terms. The default is `True`.

first_element_filter

: Filter to apply to the first element of the equation.
  This parameter accepts `*` and `?` as special characters. The default is `None`.

second_element_filter

: Filter to apply to the second element of the equation.
  This parameter accepts `*` and `?` as special characters. The default is `None`.

category

: Plot category name as in the report, including operator.
  The default is `"C"`, which is the plot category name for capacitance.

# Returns

list

: Traces of specified designs ready to use in plot reports.

# Examples

```pycon
>>> from ansys.aedt.core import Q3d
>>> hfss = Q3d(project_path)
>>> hfss.get_traces_for_plot(first_element_filter="Bo?1",
...                           second_element_filter="GND*", category="C")
```

documentation:
Export mesh statistics to a file.

# Parameters

: Variation list. The default is `""`.

mesh_path

: Full path to the mesh statistics file. The default is `None`, in which
  case the working directory is used.

setup_type

: Setup type in Q3D. Options are `"CG"`, `"AC RL"`, and `"DC RL"`. The
  default is `"CG"`.

# Returns

str

: File path.

# References

```pycon

```

methods.name: ansys.aedt.core.q3d.QExtractor.edit_sources
documentation:
Set up the source loaded for Q3D or Q2D in multiple sources simultaneously.

# Parameters

cg

: Dictionary of input sources to modify the module and phase of a CG solution.
  Dictionary values can be:
  \- 1 Value to set up `0deg` as the default
  \- 2 Values tuple or list (magnitude and phase)

acrl

: Dictionary of input sources to modify the module and phase of an ACRL solution.
  Dictionary values can be:
  \- 1 Value to set up 0deg as the default
  \- 2 Values tuple or list (magnitude and phase)

dcrl

: Dictionary of input sources to modify the module and phase of a DCRL solution, This
  parameter is only available for Q3D. Dictionary values can be:
  \- 1 Value to set up `0deg` as the default
  \- 2 Values tuple or list (magnitude and phase)

# Returns

bool

: `True` when successful, `False` when failed.

# Examples

```pycon
>>> sources_cg = {"Box1": ("1V", "0deg"), "Box1_2": "1V"}
>>> sources_acrl = {"Box1:Source1": ("5A", "0deg")}
>>> sources_dcrl = {"Box1_1:Source2": ("5V", "0deg")}
>>> hfss.edit_sources(sources_cg,sources_acrl,sources_dcrl)
```

methods.name: ansys.aedt.core.q3d.QExtractor.export_matrix_data
documentation:
Export matrix data.

# Parameters

file_name

: Full path to save the matrix data to.
  Options for file extensions are: `*.m`, `*.lvl`, `*.csv`,
  and `*.txt`.

problem_type

: Problem type. The default value is `None`, in which case `"C"` is
  used. Options are `"C"`, `"AC RL"`, and `"DC RL"`.

variations

: Design variation. The default is `None`, in which case the
  current nominal variation is used.

setup

: Setup name. The default value is `None`, in which case the first
  analysis setup is used.

sweep

: Solution frequency. The default is `None`, in which case
  the default adaptive is used.

reduce_matrix

: Name of the matrix to display.
  Default value is `"Original"`.

r_unit

: Resistance unit value.
  The default value is `"ohm"`.

l_unit

: Inductance unit value.
  The default value is `"nH"`.

c_unit

: Capacitance unit value.
  Default value is `"pF"`.

g_unit

: Conductance unit value.
  The default value is `"mho"`.

freq

: Selected frequency.
  The default value is `"0Hz"`.

freq_unit

: Frequency unit. The default value is `None`, in which case the
  default unit is used.

matrix_type

: Matrix type. The default is `None`.
  Options are `"Couple"`, `"Maxwell"`, and `"Spice"`.

export_ac_dc_res

: Whether to add the AC and DC resistance.
  The default is `False`.

precision

: Precision format.
  The default value is `15`.

field_width

: Field Width.
  The default value is `20`.

use_sci_notation

: Use sci notation.
  Whether to use scientific notation.
  The default value is `True`. When `False`, the display format is used.

length_setting

: Length setting if the design si 2D.
  The default value is `"Distributed"`.

length

: Length.
  The default value is `"1meter"`.

# Returns

bool

: `True` when successful, `False` when failed.

methods.name: ansys.aedt.core.q3d.QExtractor.export_equivalent_circuit
documentation:
Export matrix data.

# Parameters

output_file

: Full path for saving the matrix data to.
  Options for file extensions are CIR, SML, SP, PKG, SPC, LIB, CKT, BSP,
  DML, and ICM.

setup

: Setup name.
  The default value is `None`, in which case the first analysis setup is used.

sweep

: Solution frequency. The default is `None`, in which case
  the default adaptive is used.

variations

: Design variation. The default is `None`, in which case the
  current nominal variation is used. If you provide a
  design variation, use the format `{Name}:{Value}`.

matrix

: Name of the matrix to show. The default is `"Original"`.

cells

: Number of cells in export.
  Default value is 2.

user_changed_settings

: Whether user has changed settings or not, defaulted to True.
  Default value is False.

include_cap

: Include Capacitance.
  Default value is True.

include_cond

: Include Conductance.
  Default value is True.

coupling_limit_type

: Coupling limit types.
  Values can be: `"By Value" -> 0` or `"By Fraction Of Self Term" -> 1`.
  If None, no coupling limits are set.
  Default value is None.

include_dcr

: Flag indicates whether to export DC resistance matrix.
  Default value is `False`.

include_dcl

: Flag indicates whether to export DC Inductance matrix.
  Default value is `False`.

include_acr

: Flag indicates whether to export AC resistance matrix.
  Default value is `False`.

include_acl

: Flag indicates whether to export AC inductance matrix.
  Default value is `False`.

include_r

: Flag indicates whether to export resistance.
  Default value is True.

include_l

: Flag indicates whether to export inductance.
  Default value is True.

add_resistance

: Adds the DC and AC resistance.
  Default value is True.

parse_pin_names

: Parse pin names.
  Default value is False.

export_distributed

: Flag to tell whether to export in distributed mode or Lumped mode.
  Default value is True.

lumped_length

: Length of the design.
  Default value is 1 meter.

rise_time_value

: Rise time to calculate the number of cells.
  Default value is 1e-09.

rise_time_unit

: Rise time unit.
  Default is s.

cap_limit

: Capacitance limit.
  Default value is 1pF if coupling_limit_type is 0.
  Default value is 0.01 if coupling_limit_type is 1.

cond_limit

: Conductance limit.
  Default value is 1mSie if coupling_limit_type is 0.
  Default value is 0.01 if coupling_limit_type is 1.

res_limit

: Resistance limit.
  Default value is 1ohm if coupling_limit_type is 0.
  Default value is 0.01 if coupling_limit_type is 1.

ind_limit

: Inductance limit.
  Default value is 1nH if coupling_limit_type is 0.
  Default value is 0.01 if coupling_limit_type is 1.

model

: Model name or name of the sub circuit (Optional).
  If None then file_name is considered as model name.

frequency

: Sweep frequency in Hz.
  Default value is 0.

file_type

: The type of file format.
  Type of HSPICE file format. (All HSPICE file formats have the same extension,
  which is `*.sp`.) Options are:
  "Hspice": simple HSPICE file format.
  "Welement": Nexxim/HSPICE W Element file format
  "RLGC": Nexxim/HSPICE RLGC W Element file format
  Default value is Hspice.

include_cpp

: Whether to include chip package control.
  Default value is False.

# Returns

bool

: `True` when successful, `False` when failed.

# References

```pycon
>>> oModule.ExportCircuit
```

# Examples

```pycon
>>> from ansys.aedt.core import Q3d
>>> aedtapp = Q3d()
>>> box = aedtapp.modeler.create_box([30, 30, 30],[10, 10, 10],name="mybox")
>>> net = aedtapp.assign_net(box,"my_net")
>>> source = aedtapp.assign_source_to_objectface(box.bottom_face_z.id, axisdir=0,
...     source_name="Source1", net_name=net.name)
>>> sink = aedtapp.assign_sink_to_objectface(box.top_face_z.id,direction=0,name="Sink1",net_name=net.name)
>>> aedtapp["d"] = "20mm"
>>> aedtapp.modeler.duplicate_along_line(objid="Box1",vector=[0, "d", 0])
>>> mysetup = aedtapp.create_setup()
>>> aedtapp.analyze_setup(mysetup.name)
>>> aedtapp.export_equivalent_circuit(output_file="test_export_circuit.cir",
...                                   setup=mysetup.name,sweep="LastAdaptive", variations=["d: 20mm"])
```

methods.name: ansys.aedt.core.q3d.Q3d.create_frequency_sweep
documentation:
Create a frequency sweep.

:::{deprecated} 0.7.12 This method is deprecated. To create a frequency sweep use ``create_frequency_sweep()`` from setup object. Example ------- >>> from ansys.aedt.core import Q3d >>> q3d = Q3d() >>> setup1 = q3d.create_setup(name="Setup1") >>> sweep1 = setup1.create_frequency_sweep(unit="GHz", freqstart=0.5, freqstop=1.5, sweepname="Sweep1") >>> q3d.release_desktop(True, True)
:::

# Parameters

setupname

: Name of the setup that is attached to the sweep.

units

: Units of the frequency. For example, `"MHz"` or
  `"GHz"`. The default is `None` which takes the Default Desktop units.

freqstart
: Starting frequency of the sweep. The default is `0`.

  : If a unit is passed with the number, such as `"1MHz"`, the unit is ignored.

freqstop

: Stopping frequency of the sweep. The default is `1`.
  If a unit is passed with the number, such as\`\`"1MHz", the unit is ignored.

freqstep

: Frequency step point.

sweepname

: Name of the sweep. The default is `None`, in which case the
  default name is used.

# Returns

{class}`ansys.aedt.core.modules.solve_sweeps.SweepHFSS3DLayout`

: Sweep object when successful, `False` when failed.

# References

```pycon
>>> oModule.InsertSweep
```

methods.name: ansys.aedt.core.q3d.Q3d.create_discrete_sweep
documentation:
Create a discrete sweep with a single frequency value.

:::{deprecated} 0.7.12 This method is deprecated. To create a discrete frequency sweep use ``create_frequency_sweep()`` from setup object. Example ------- >>> from ansys.aedt.core import Q3d >>> q3d = Q3d() >>> setup1 = q3d.create_setup(name="Setup1") >>> sweep1 = setup1.create_frequency_sweep(unit="GHz", ...                                        freqstart=0.5, ...                                        freqstop=1.5, ...                                        sweepname="Sweep1", ...                                        sweep_type="Discrete") >>> q3d.release_desktop(True, True)
:::

# Parameters

setupname

: Name of the setup that the sweeps belongs to.

freqstart

: Starting point for the discrete frequency.

freqstop

: Stopping point for the discrete frequency. If `None`,
  a single-point sweep is performed.

freqstep

: Step point for the discrete frequency. If `None`,
  11 points are created.

units

: Units of the discrete frequency. For example, `"MHz"` or
  `"GHz"`. The default is `"GHz"`.

sweepname

: Name of the sweep. The default is `None`, in which case
  the default name is used.

savefields

: Whether to save fields. The default is `False`.

# Returns

SweepMatrix

: Sweep option.

# References

```pycon
>>> oModule.InsertSweep
```

methods.name: ansys.aedt.core.q3d.Q3d.set_material_thresholds
documentation:
Set material threshold.

# Parameters

insulator_threshold

: Threshold for the insulator or conductor. The default is "None", in which
  case the threshold is set to 10000.

perfect_conductor_threshold

: Threshold that decides whether a conductor is perfectly conducting. This value
  must be higher than the value for the insulator threshold. The default is `None`,
  in which case the value is set to 1E+030.

magnetic_threshold

: Threshold that decides whether a material is magnetic. The default is "None",
  in which case the value is set to 1.01.

# Returns

bool

: `True` when successful, `False` when failed.

methods.name: ansys.aedt.core.q3d.Q3d.assign_thin_conductor

Assign a thin conductor to a sheet. The method accepts both a sheet name or a face id.
If a face it is provided, then a sheet will be created and the boundary assigned to it.

assignment

methods.name: ansys.aedt.core.q3d.Q3d.get_mutual_coupling
documentation:
Get mutual coupling between two terminals.
User has to provide the pair, source and sink of each terminal. If the provided sinks are not part of the
original matrix, a new matrix will be created.

# Parameters

source1

: First element source.

sink1

: First element sink.

source2

: Second element source.

sink2

: Second element sink.

calculation

: Calculation type.
  Available options are: `"ACL"`, `"ACR"`, `"DCL"`, `"DCR"`.
  The default is `"ACL"`.

setup_sweep_name

: Name of the setup. The default is `None`, in which case the `nominal_adaptive`
  setup is used. Be sure to build a setup string in the form of
  `"SetupName : SetupSweep"`, where `SetupSweep` is the sweep name to
  use in the export or `LastAdaptive`.

variations

: Dictionary of all families including the primary sweep.
  The default is `None` which uses all variations of the setup.

# Returns

{class}`pyaedt.modules.solutions.SolutionData` or bool

: Solution Data object if successful, `False` otherwise.

# Examples

```pycon
>>> from pyaedt import Q3d
>>> aedtapp = Q3d()
>>> data = aedtapp.modeler.get_mutual_coupling("a1", "a2", "b1", "b2", calculation="DCL")
```

Provides the Q3D app interface.

This class allows you to create an instance of Q3D and link to an
existing project or create a new one.

# Parameters

project

: Name of the project to select or the full path to the project
  or AEDTZ archive to open. The default is `None`, in which
  case an attempt is made to get an active project. If no
  projects are present, an empty project is created.

design

: Name of the design to select. The default is `None`, in
  which case an attempt is made to get an active design. If no
  designs are present, an empty design is created.

solution_type

: Solution type to apply to the design. The default is
  `None`, in which case the default type is applied.

setup

: Name of the setup to use as the nominal. The default is
  `None`, in which case the active setup is used or nothing
  is used.

version

: Version of AEDT to use. The default is `None`, in which case
  the active version or latest installed version is used.
  This parameter is ignored when Script is launched within AEDT.
  Examples of input values are `232`, `23.2`,\`\`2023.2\`\`,\`\`"2023.2".

non_graphical

: Whether to launch AEDT in non-graphical mode. The default
  is `False`, in which case AEDT is launched in graphical mode.
  This parameter is ignored when a script is launched within AEDT.

new_desktop

: Whether to launch an instance of AEDT in a new thread, even if
  another instance of the `specified_version` is active on the
  machine. The default is `False`. This parameter is ignored when
  a script is launched within AEDT.

close_on_exit

: Whether to release AEDT on exit. The default is `False`.

student_version

: Whether to open the AEDT student version. The default is `False`.
  This parameter is ignored when a script is launched within AEDT.

machine

: Machine name to connect the oDesktop session to. This works only in
  2022 R2 and later. The remote server must be up and running with the
  command `"ansysedt.exe -grpcsrv portnum"`. If the machine is `"localhost"`,
  the server also starts if not present.

port

: Port number on which to start the oDesktop communication on an already
  existing server. This parameter is ignored when a new server is created.
  It works only in 2022 R2 and later. The remote server must be up and
  running with the command `"ansysedt.exe -grpcsrv portnum"`.

aedt_process_id

: Process ID for the instance of AEDT to point PyAEDT at. The default is
  `None`. This parameter is only used when `new_desktop = False`.

remove_lock

: Whether to remove lock to project before opening it or not.
  The default is `False`, which means to not unlock
  the existing project if needed and raise an exception.

# Examples

Create an instance of Q3D and connect to an existing Q3D
design or create a new Q3D design if one does not exist.

```pycon
>>> from ansys.aedt.core import Q3d
>>> app = Q3d()
```

methods.name: ansys.aedt.core.q3d.Q2d.create_setup
documentation:
Create an analysis setup for 2D Extractor.

Optional arguments are passed along with the `setup_type` and `name`
parameters. Keyword names correspond to the `setup_type`
corresponding to the native AEDT API. The list of
keywords here is not exhaustive.

# Parameters

name

: Name of the setup. The default is "Setup1".

setup_type

: Type of the setup. Options are "IcepakSteadyState"
  and "IcepakTransient". The default is "IcepakSteadyState".

kwargs

: Available keys depend on the setup chosen.
  For more information, see {doc}`../SetupTemplatesQ3D`.

# Returns

{class}`ansys.aedt.core.modules.solve_setup.SetupHFSS`

: Solver Setup object.

# References

```pycon
>>> oModule.InsertSetup
```

# Examples

```pycon
>>> from ansys.aedt.core import Q2d
>>> app = Q2d()
>>> app.create_setup(name="Setup1",RLDataBlock__MinPass=2)
```

Provides the Q2D app interface.

This class allows you to create an instance of Q2D and link to an
existing project or create a new one.

# Parameters

project

: Name of the project to select or the full path to the project
  or AEDTZ archive to open. The default is `None`, in which
  case an attempt is made to get an active project. If no
  projects are present, an empty project is created.

design

: Name of the design to select. The default is `None`, in
  which case an attempt is made to get an active design. If no
  designs are present, an empty design is created.

solution_type

: Solution type to apply to the design. The default is
  `None`, in which case the default type is applied.

setup

: Name of the setup to use as the nominal. The default is
  `None`, in which case the active setup is used or
  nothing is used.

version

: Version of AEDT to use. The default is `None`, in which case
  the active version or latest installed version is used. This
  parameter is ignored when a script is launched within AEDT.
  Examples of input values are `232`, `23.2`,\`\`2023.2\`\`,\`\`"2023.2".

non_graphical

: Whether to launch AEDT in non-graphical mode. The default
  is `False`, in which case AEDT is launched in graphical mode.
  This parameter is ignored when a script is launched within AEDT.

new_desktop

: Whether to launch an instance of AEDT in a new thread, even if
  another instance of the `specified_version` is active on the
  machine. The default is `False`. This parameter is ignored
  when a script is launched within AEDT.

close_on_exit

: Whether to release AEDT on exit. The default is `False`.

student_version

: Whether to open the AEDT student version. This parameter is
  ignored when a script is launched within AEDT.

machine

: Machine name to connect the oDesktop session to. This works only in 2022 R2
  and later. The remote server must be up and running with the command
  `"ansysedt.exe -grpcsrv portnum"`. If the machine is `"localhost"`,
  the server also starts if not present.

port

: Port number on which to start the oDesktop communication on an already existing server.
  This parameter is ignored when creating a new server. It works only in 2022 R2 or later.
  The remote server must be up and running with the command `"ansysedt.exe -grpcsrv portnum"`.

aedt_process_id

: Process ID for the instance of AEDT to point PyAEDT at. The default is
  `None`. This parameter is only used when `new_desktop = False`.

remove_lock

: Whether to remove lock to project before opening it or not.
  The default is `False`, which means to not unlock
  the existing project if needed and raise an exception.

# Examples

Create an instance of Q2D and link to a project named
`projectname`. If this project does not exist, create one with
this name.

```pycon
>>> from ansys.aedt.core import Q2d
>>> app = Q2d(projectname)
```

Create an instance of Q2D and link to a design named
`designname` in a project named `projectname`.

```pycon
>>> app = Q2d(projectname,designame)
```

Create an instance of Q2D and open the specified project,
which is named `myfile.aedt`.

```pycon
>>> app = Q2d("myfile.aedt")
```

functions.name: ansys.aedt.core.generic.data_handlers.from_rkm
documentation:
Convert an RKM code string to a string with a decimal point.

# Parameters

code

: RKM code string.

# Returns

str

: String with a decimal point and an R value.

# Examples

```pycon
>>> from ansys.aedt.core.generic.data_handlers import from_rkm
>>> from_rkm('R47')
'0.47'
```

```pycon
>>> from_rkm('4R7')
'4.7'
```

```pycon
>>> from_rkm('470R')
'470'
```

```pycon
>>> from_rkm('4K7')
'4.7k'
```

```pycon
>>> from_rkm('47K')
'47k'
```

```pycon
>>> from_rkm('47K3')
'47.3k'
```

```pycon
>>> from_rkm('470K')
'470k'
```

```pycon
>>> from_rkm('4M7')
'4.7M'
```

functions.name: ansys.aedt.core.generic.data_handlers.str_to_bool
documentation:
Convert a `"True"` or `"False"` string to its corresponding Boolean value.

If the passed arguments are not relevant in the context of conversion, the argument
itself is returned. This method can be called using the `map()` function to
ensure conversion of Boolean strings in a list.

# Parameters

s: str

# Returns

bool or str

: The method is not case-sensitive.
  \- `True` is returned if the input is `"true"`, `"1"`,

  > `` "yes"` ``, or `"y"`,

  - `False` is returned if the input is `"false"`, `"no"`,
    `"n`, or `"0"`.
  - Otherwise, the input value is passed through the method unchanged.

methods.name: ansys.aedt.core.hfss3dlayout.Hfss3dLayout.export_touchstone_on_completion
documentation:
Enable or disable the automatic export of the touchstone file after completing frequency sweep.

# Parameters

export

: Whether to export the Touchstone file after the simulation. The default is `True`.

output_dir

: Path to the directory of exported file. The default is the project path.

# Returns

bool

: `True` when successful, `False` when failed.

# References

```pycon
>>> oDesign.DesignOptions
```

methods.name: ansys.aedt.core.hfss3dlayout.Hfss3dLayout.set_export_touchstone
documentation:
Set or disable the automatic export of the touchstone file after completing frequency sweep.

# Parameters

file_format

: Touchstone format. Available options are: `"TouchStone1.0"`, and `"TouchStone2.0"`.
  The default is `"TouchStone1.0"`.

enforce_passivity

: Enforce passivity. The default is `True`.

enforce_causality

: Enforce causality. The default is `False`.

use_common_ground

: Use common ground. The default is `True`.

show_gamma_comments

: Show gamma comments. The default is `True`.

renormalize

: Renormalize. The default is `False`.

impedance

: Impedance in ohms. The default is `50.0`.

fitting_error

: Fitting error. The default is `0.5`.

maximum_poles

: Maximum number of poles. The default is `10000`.

passivity_type

: Passivity type. Available options are: `"PassivityByPerturbation"`, `"IteratedFittingOfPV"`,
  `"IteratedFittingOfPVLF"`, and `"ConvexOptimization"`.

column_fitting_type

: Column fitting type. Available options are: `"Matrix"`, `` "Column"` ``, and `` "Entry"` ``.

state_space_fitting

: State space fitting algorithm. Available options are: `"IterativeRational"`, `` "TWA"` ``, and `` "FastFit"` ``.

relative_error_tolerance

: Relative error tolerance. The default is `True`.

ensure_accurate_fit

: Ensure accurate impedance fit. The default is `False`.

touchstone_output

: Touchstone output format. Available options are: `"MA"` for magnitude and phase in `deg`,
  `"RI"` for real and imaginary part, and `"DB"` for magnitude in `dB` and phase in `deg`.

units

: Frequency units. The default is `"GHz"`.

precision

: Touchstone precision. The default is `11`.

# Returns

bool

: `True` when successful, `False` when failed.

# References

```pycon
>>> oTool.SetExportTouchstoneOptions
```

# Examples

```pycon
>>> from ansys.aedt.core import Hfss3dlayout
>>> layout = Hfss3dlayout()
>>> layout.export_touchstone_on_completion()
>>> layout.export_touchstone_on_completion()
```

methods.name: ansys.aedt.core.hfss3dlayout.Hfss3dLayout.set_meshing_settings
documentation:
Define the settings of the mesh.

# Parameters

mesh_method

: Mesh method. The default is `"Phi"`. Options are `"Phi"`, `"PhiPlus"`,
  and `"Classic"`.

enable_intersections_check

: Whether to enable the alternative mesh intersections checks. The default is
  `True`.

use_alternative_fallback

: Whether to enable the alternative fall back mesh method. The default is `True`.

# Returns

bool

: `True` when successful, `False` when failed.

# References

```pycon
>>> oDesign.DesignOptions
```

methods.name: ansys.aedt.core.hfss3dlayout.Hfss3dLayout.create_linear_count_sweep
documentation:
Create a sweep with the specified number of points.

# Parameters

setup

: Name of the setup to attach to the sweep.

unit

: Unit of the frequency. For example, `"MHz"` or `"GHz"`.

start_frequency

: Starting frequency of the sweep.

stop_frequency

: Stopping frequency of the sweep.

num_of_freq_points

: Number of frequency points in the range.

name

: Name of the sweep. The default is `None`, in which
  case a name is automatically assigned.

save_fields

: Whether to save fields for a discrete sweep only. The
  default is `True`.

save_rad_fields_only

: Whether to save only radiated fields if
  `save_fields=True`. The default is `False`.

sweep_type

: Type of the sweep. Options are `"Fast"`,
  `"Interpolating"`, and `"Discrete"`. The default is
  `"Interpolating"`.

interpolation_tol_percent

: Error tolerance threshold for the interpolation process.
  The default is `0.5`.

interpolation_max_solutions

: Maximum number of solutions to evaluate for the
  interpolation process. The default is `250`.

use_q3d_for_dc

: Whether to use Q3D to solve the DC point. The default is `False`.

# Returns

{class}`ansys.aedt.core.modules.solve_sweeps.SweepHFSS3DLayout` or bool

: Sweep object if successful, `False` otherwise.

# References

```pycon
>>> oModule.AddSweep
```

methods.name: ansys.aedt.core.hfss3dlayout.Hfss3dLayout.create_linear_step_sweep
documentation:
Create a sweep with the specified frequency step.

# Parameters

setup

: Name of the setup to attach to the sweep.

unit

: Unit of the frequency. For example, `"MHz"` or `"GHz"`.

start_frequency

: Starting frequency of the sweep.

stop_frequency

: Stopping frequency of the sweep.

step_size

: Frequency size of the step.

name

: Name of the sweep. The default is `None`, in which
  case a name is automatically assigned.

save_fields

: Whether to save fields for a discrete sweep only. The
  default is `True`.

save_rad_fields_only

: Whether to save only radiated fields if
  `save_fields=True`. The default is `False`.

sweep_type

: Type of the sweep. Options are `"Fast"`,
  `"Interpolating"`, and `"Discrete"`.
  The default is `"Interpolating"`.

interpolation_tol_percent

: Error tolerance threshold for the interpolation
  process. The default is `0.5`.

interpolation_max_solutions

: Maximum number of solutions to evaluate for the
  interpolation process. The default is `250`.

use_q3d_for_dc

: Whether to use Q3D to solve the DC point. The default is `False`.

# Returns

{class}`ansys.aedt.core.modules.solve_sweeps.SweepHFSS3DLayout` or bool

: Sweep object if successful, `False` otherwise.

# References

```pycon
>>> oModule.AddSweep
```

methods.name: ansys.aedt.core.hfss3dlayout.Hfss3dLayout.create_single_point_sweep
documentation:
Create a sweep with a single frequency point.

# Parameters

setup

: Name of the setup.

unit

: Unit of the frequency. For example, `"MHz` or `"GHz"`.

freq

: Frequency of the single point or list of frequencies to create distinct single points.

name

: Name of the sweep. The default is `None`, in which
  case a name is automatically assigned.

save_fields

: Whether to save fields for all points and subranges defined in the sweep. The default is `False`.

save_rad_fields_only

: Whether to save only radiating fields. The default is `False`.

# Returns

{class}`ansys.aedt.core.modules.solve_sweeps.SweepHFSS` or bool

: Sweep object if successful, `False` otherwise.

# References

```pycon
>>> oModule.AddSweep
```

methods.name: ansys.aedt.core.hfss3dlayout.Hfss3dLayout.import_gds
documentation:
Import a GDS file into HFSS 3D Layout and assign the stackup from an XML file if present.

# Parameters

input_file

: Full path to the GDS file.

output_dir

: Full path to the AEDB folder. For example, `"c:\temp\test.aedb"`.

control_file

: Path to the XML or TECH file with the stackup information. The default is `None`, in
  which case the stackup is not edited.
  If a TECH file is provided and the layer name starts with `"v"`, the layer
  is mapped as a via layer.

set_as_active

: Whether to set the GDS file as active. The default is `True`.

close_active_project

: Whether to close the active project after loading the GDS file.
  The default is ''False\`\`.

# Returns

bool

: `True` when successful, `False` when failed.

# References

```pycon
>>> oModule.ImportGDSII
```

methods.name: ansys.aedt.core.hfss3dlayout.Hfss3dLayout.edit_cosim_options
documentation:
Edit cosimulation options.

# Parameters

simulate_missing_solution

: Whether the solver is to simulate a missing solution. The default is `True`. If
  `False`, the solver interpolates a missing solution.

align_ports

: Whether the solver is to align microwave parts. The default is `True`.

renormalize_ports

: Whether to renormalize port impendance. The default is `True`.

renorm_impedance

: Renormalization impedance in ohms. The default is `50`.

setup_override_name

: Setup name if there is a setup override. The default is `None`.

sweep_override_name

: Sweep name if there is a sweep override. The default is `None`.

use_interpolating_sweep

: Whether the solver is to use an interpolating sweep. The default is `True`.
  If `False`, the solver is to use a discrete sweep.

use_y_matrix

: Whether the interpolation algorithm is to use the Y matrix. The default is
  `True`.

interpolation_algorithm

: Interpolation algorithm to use. Options are `"auto"`, `"lin"`, `"shadH"`,
  and `"shadNH"`. The default is `"auto"`.

# Returns

bool

: `True` if successful and `False` if failed.

# References

```pycon
>>> oDesign.EditCoSimulationOptions
```

# Examples

```pycon
>>> from ansys.aedt.core import Hfss3dLayout
>>> h3d = Hfss3dLayout()
>>> h3d.edit_cosim_options(
...     simulate_missing_solution=True,
...     align_ports=True,
...     renormalize_ports=True,
...     renorm_impedance=50,
...     setup_override_name=None,
...     sweep_override_name=None,
...     use_interpolating_sweep=False,
...     use_y_matrix=True,
...     interpolation_algorithm="auto"
... )
```

methods.name: ansys.aedt.core.hfss3dlayout.Hfss3dLayout.set_differential_pair
documentation:
Add a differential pair definition.

# Parameters

assignment

: Name of the terminal to use as the positive terminal.

reference

: Name of the terminal to use as the negative terminal.

common_mode

: Name for the common mode. The default is `None`, in which case a unique name is assigned.

differential_mode

: Name for the differential mode. The default is `None`, in which case a unique name is assigned.

common_reference

: Reference impedance for the common mode in ohms. The default is `25`.

differential_reference

: Reference impedance for the differential mode in ohms. The default is `100`.

active

: Whether to set the differential pair as active. The default is `True`.

matched

: Whether to set the differential pair as active. The default is `False`.

# Returns

bool

: `True` when successful, `False` when failed.

# References

```pycon
>>> oModule.SetDiffPairs
```

methods.name: ansys.aedt.core.hfss3dlayout.Hfss3dLayout.load_diff_pairs_from_file
documentation:
Load differential pairs definition from a file.

You can use the `save_diff_pairs_to_file` method to obtain the file format.
The `File End Of Line` must be UNIX (LF).
New definitions are added only if compatible with the existing definition already defined in the project.

# Parameters

input_file

: Full path to the differential pairs definition file.

# Returns

bool

: `True` when successful, `False` when failed.

# References

```pycon
>>> oModule.LoadDiffPairsFromFile
```

methods.name: ansys.aedt.core.hfss3dlayout.Hfss3dLayout.save_diff_pairs_to_file
documentation:
Save differtential pairs definition to a file.

If a file with the specified name already exists, it is overwritten.

# Parameters

output_file

: Full path to the differential pairs definition file.

# Returns

bool

: `True` when successful, `False` when failed.

# References

```pycon
>>> oModule.SaveDiffPairsToFile
```

methods.name: ansys.aedt.core.hfss3dlayout.Hfss3dLayout.export_3d_model
documentation:
Export the Ecad model to a 3D file.

# Parameters

output_file

: Full name of the file to export. The default is `None`, in which case the file name is
  set to the design name and saved as a SAT file in the working directory.
  Extensions available are `"sat"`, `"sab"`, and `"sm3"` up to AEDT 2022 R2 and
  Parasolid format `"x_t"` from AEDT 2023R1.

# Returns

str

: File name if successful.

methods.name: ansys.aedt.core.hfss3dlayout.Hfss3dLayout.enable_rigid_flex
documentation:
Turn on or off the rigid flex of a board with bending if available.

This function is the same for both turning on and off rigid flex.

# Returns

bool

: `True` if rigid flex is turned off, `` False` `` if rigid flex is turned off.
  In non-graphical, `True` is always returned due to a bug in the native API.

methods.name: ansys.aedt.core.hfss3dlayout.Hfss3dLayout.edit_hfss_extents
documentation:
Edit HFSS 3D Layout extents.

# Parameters

diel_extent_type

: Dielectric extent type. The default is `None`. Options are `"BboxExtent"`,
  `"ConformalExtent"`, and `"ConvexHullExtent"`.

diel_extent_horizontal_padding

: Dielectric extent horizontal padding. The default is `None`.

diel_honor_primitives_on_diel_layers

: Whether to set dielectric honor primitives on dielectric layers. The default is `None`.

air_extent_type

: Airbox extent type. The default is `None`. Options are `"BboxExtent"`,
  `"ConformalExtent"`, and `"ConvexHullExtent"`.

air_truncate_model_at_ground_layer

: Whether to set airbox truncate model at ground layer. The default is `None`.

air_vertical_positive_padding

: Airbox vertical positive padding. The default is `None`.

air_vertical_negative_padding

: Airbox vertical negative padding. The default is `None`.

airbox_values_as_dim

: Either if inputs are dims or not. Default is `True`.

air_horizontal_padding

: Airbox horizontal padding. The default is `None`.

# Returns

bool

: `True` when successful, `False` when failed.

methods.name: ansys.aedt.core.hfss3dlayout.Hfss3dLayout.get_model_from_mesh_results
documentation:
Get the path for the parasolid file in the result folder.
The parasolid file is generated after the mesh is created in 3D Layout.

Parameters
----------
binary : str, optional
    Either if retrieve binary format of parasoli or not.
Returns
-------
str
    Path for the parasolid file in the results folder.

methods.name: ansys.aedt.core.hfss3dlayout.Hfss3dLayout.edit_source_from_file
documentation:
Edit a source from file data.
File data is a csv containing either frequency data or time domain data that will be converted through FFT.

# Parameters

source

: Source Name.

input_file

: Full name of the input file.

is_time_domain

: Whether the input data is time-based. The defaulti s `True`. If
  `False`, the input data is frequency-based. Frequency-based data
  is degrees in this format: `Mag/Phase`.

x_scale

: Scaling factor for x axis.

y_scale

: Scaling factor for y axis.

impedance

: Excitation impedance. Default is `50`.

data_format

: Either `"Power"`, `"Current"` or `"Voltage"`.

encoding

: Csv file encoding.

include_post_effects

: Either if include or not post-processing effects. Default is `True`,

incident_voltage

: Either if include or incident or total voltage. Default is `True`, for incident voltage.

window

: Fft window. Options are `"hamming"`, `"hanning"`, `"blackman"`, `"bartlett"` or `None`.

# Returns

bool

methods.name: ansys.aedt.core.hfss3dlayout.Hfss3dLayout.get_dcir_solution_data
documentation:
Retrieve dcir solution data. Available element_names are dependent on element_type as below.
Sources ["Voltage", "Current", "Power"]
"RL" ['Loop Resistance', 'Path Resistance', 'Resistance', 'Inductance']
"Vias" ['X', 'Y', 'Current', 'Limit', 'Resistance', 'IR Drop', 'Power']
"Bondwires" ['Current', 'Limit', 'Resistance', 'IR Drop']
"Probes" ['Voltage'].

# Parameters

setup

: Name of the setup.

show

: Type of the element. Options are ``` "Sources"`, ``"RL"`, ``"Vias" ```, `"Bondwires"`, and `"Probes"`.

category

: Name of the element. Options are ``` "Voltage"`, ``"Current"`, ``"Power" ```, `"Loop_Resistance"`,
  `"Path_Resistance"`, `"Resistance"`, `"Inductance"`, `"X"`, `"Y"`, `"Limit"` and `"IR Drop"`.

# Returns

from ansys.aedt.core.modules.solutions.SolutionData

methods.name: ansys.aedt.core.hfss3dlayout.Hfss3dLayout.import_table
documentation:
Import a data table as a solution.

# Parameters

input_file

: Full path to the file.

link

: Whether to link the file to the solution. The default is `False`.

header_rows

: Header rows. The default is `0`.

rows_to_read

: Rows to read. If `-1`, then reads until end of file. The default is `-1`.

column_separator

: Column separator type. Available options are `Space`, `Tab`, `Comma`, and `Period`.
  The default is `Space`.

data_type

: Data type. Available options are `real`, `real_imag`, `mag_ang_deg`, and `mag_ang_rad`.
  The default is `real`.

sweep_columns

: Sweep columns. The default is `0`.

total_columns

: Total number of columns. If `-1`, then reads the total number of columns. The default is `-1`.

real_columns

: Number of lefmotst real columns. The default is `1`.

# Returns

str

: `True` when successful, `False` when failed.

# References

```pycon
>>> oModule.ImportData
```

# Examples

```pycon
>>> from ansys.aedt.core import Hfss3dlayout
>>> h3d = Hfss3dlayout()
>>> h3d.import_table(input_file="my_file.csv")
```

methods.name: ansys.aedt.core.hfss3dlayout.Hfss3dLayout.delete_imported_data

```pycon
>>> from ansys.aedt.core import Hfss3dlayout
>>> h3d = Hfss3dlayout()
>>> table_name = h3d.import_table(input_file="my_file.csv")
>>> h3d.delete_imported_data(table_name)
```

Provides the HFSS 3D Layout application interface.

This class inherits all objects that belong to HFSS 3D Layout, including EDB
API queries.

# Parameters

project

: Name of the project to select or the full path to the project
  or AEDTZ archive to open or the path to the `aedb` folder or
  `edb.def` file. The default is `None`, in which case an
  attempt is made to get an active project. If no projects are present,
  an empty project is created.

design

: Name of the design to select. The default is `None`, in
  which case an attempt is made to get an active design. If no
  designs are present, an empty design is created.

solution_type

: Solution type to apply to the design. The default is
  `None`, in which case the default type is applied.

setup

: Name of the setup to use as the nominal. The default is
  `None`, in which case the active setup is used or
  nothing is used.

version

: Version of AEDT to use. The default is `None`, in which case
  the active version or latest installed version is used.
  Examples of input values are `232`, `23.2`,\`\`2023.2\`\`,\`\`"2023.2".

non_graphical

: Whether to launch AEDT in non-graphical mode. The default
  is `` True` ``, in which case AEDT is launched in graphical mode.
  This parameter is ignored when a script is launched within AEDT.

new_desktop

: Whether to launch an instance of AEDT in a new thread, even if
  another instance of the `specified_version` is active on the
  machine. The default is `False`.

close_on_exit

: Whether to release AEDT on exit. The default is `False`.

student_version

: Whether to open the AEDT student version. The default is `False`.

machine

: Machine name to connect the oDesktop session to. This works only in 2022 R2 or later.
  The remote server must be up and running with the command `"ansysedt.exe -grpcsrv portnum"`.
  If the machine is `"localhost"`. the server also starts if not present.

port

: Port number on which to start the oDesktop communication on an already existing server.
  This parameter is ignored when creating a new server. It works only in 2022 R2 or later.
  The remote server must be up and running with the command `"ansysedt.exe -grpcsrv portnum"`.

aedt_process_id

: Process ID for the instance of AEDT to point PyAEDT at. The default is
  `None`. This parameter is only used when `new_desktop = False`.

ic_mode

: Whether to set the design to IC mode or not. The default is `None`, which means to retain
  the existing setting.

remove_lock

: Whether to remove lock to project before opening it or not.
  The default is `False`, which means to not unlock
  the existing project if needed and raise an exception.

# Examples

Create an `Hfss3dLayout` object and connect to an existing HFSS
design or create a new HFSS design if one does not exist.

```pycon
>>> from ansys.aedt.core import Hfss3dLayout
>>> aedtapp = Hfss3dLayout()
```

Create an `Hfss3dLayout` object and link to a project named
`projectname`. If this project does not exist, create one with
this name.

```pycon
>>> aedtapp = Hfss3dLayout(projectname)
```

Create an `Hfss3dLayout` object and link to a design named
`designname` in a project named `projectname`.

```pycon
>>> aedtapp = Hfss3dLayout(projectname,designame)
```

Create an `Hfss3dLayout` object and open the specified project.

```pycon
>>> aedtapp = Hfss3dLayout("myfile.aedt")
```

Create an AEDT 2023 R1 object and then create a
`Hfss3dLayout` object and open the specified project.

```pycon
>>> aedtapp = Hfss3dLayout(version="2024.2", project="myfile.aedt")
```

Create an instance of `Hfss3dLayout` from an `Edb`

```pycon
>>> import ansys.aedt.core
>>> edb_path = "/path/to/edbfile.aedb"
>>> edb = ansys.aedt.core.Edb(edb_path, edbversion=231)
>>> edb.stackup.import_stackup("stackup.xml")  # Import stackup. Manipulate edb, ...
>>> edb.save_edb()
>>> edb.close_edb()
>>> aedtapp = ansys.aedt.core.Hfss3dLayout(version=231, project=edb_path)
```

documentation:
Verifies if a string content starts with a specific string or not.

This is identical to `str.startswith`, except that it includes
the `ignore_case` parameter.

# Parameters

src

: Current line content.

find

: Current line content.

ignore_case

: Case sensitive or not. The default value is `True`.

# Returns

bool

: `True` if the src string starts with the pattern.

# Parameters

functions.name: ansys.aedt.core.generic.design_types.FilterSolutions
documentation:
Initialize a FilterSolutions\` instance.

# Parameters

version

: Version of AEDT to use in `xxxx.x` format to use. The default is `None`, in which case the
  active setup or latest installed version is used.

implementation_type

: Type of filter implementation available from the `FilterImplementation` enum.
  The default is `None`, in which case the lumped implementation is used.

# Returns

{class}`ansys.aedt.core.filtersolutions.FilterSolutions`

# Examples

Define a band-pass Butterworth filter with a center frequency of 1 GHz and a pass band width of 500 MHz.

design = ansys.aedt.core.FilterSolutions(version="2025.1", implementation_type= FilterImplementation.LUMPED)
design.attributes.filter_class = FilterClass.BAND_PASS
design.attributes.filter_type = FilterType.BUTTERWORTH
design.attributes.pass_band_center_frequency = "1G"
design.attributes.pass_band_width_frequency = "500M"

functions.name: ansys.aedt.core.generic.design_types.launch_desktop
documentation:
Initializes AEDT based on the inputs provided.

# Parameters

version

: Version of AEDT to use. The default is `None`, in which case the
  active setup or latest installed version is used.

non_graphical

: Whether to launch AEDT in non-graphical mode. The default
  is `False`, in which case AEDT is launched in graphical mode.
  This parameter is ignored when a script is launched within AEDT.

new_desktop

: Whether to launch an instance of AEDT in a new thread, even if
  another instance of the `specified_version` is active on the machine.
  The default is `False`.

close_on_exit

: Whether to close AEDT on exit. The default is `True`.

student_version

: Whether to open the AEDT student version. The default is
  `False`.

machine

: Machine name to connect the oDesktop session to. This parameters works only in 2022 R2
  and later. The remote server must be up and running with the command
  `"ansysedt.exe -grpcsrv portnum"`. If the machine is `"localhost"`, the server also
  starts if not present.

port

: Port number on which to start the oDesktop communication on the already existing server.
  This parameter is ignored when creating a new server. It works only in 2022 R2 and
  later. The remote server must be up and running with the command `"ansysedt.exe -grpcsrv portnum"`.

aedt_process_id

: Process ID for the instance of AEDT to point PyAEDT at. The default is
  `None`. This parameter is only used when `new_desktop = False`.

# Returns

{class}`ansys.aedt.core.desktop.Desktop`

# Examples

Launch AEDT 2021 R1 in non-graphical mode and initialize HFSS.

```pycon
>>> import ansys.aedt.core
>>> desktop = ansys.aedt.core.launch_desktop("2022.2", non_graphical=True)
PyAEDT INFO: pyaedt v...
PyAEDT INFO: Python version ...
>>> hfss = ansys.aedt.core.Hfss(design="HFSSDesign1")
PyAEDT INFO: Project...
PyAEDT INFO: Added design 'HFSSDesign1' of type HFSS.
```

Launch AEDT 2021 R1 in graphical mode and initialize HFSS.

```pycon
>>> desktop = Desktop("2021.2")
PyAEDT INFO: pyaedt v...
PyAEDT INFO: Python version ...
>>> hfss = ansys.aedt.core.Hfss(design="HFSSDesign1")
PyAEDT INFO: No project is defined. Project...
```

documentation:
Write data to a CSV .

# Parameters

output_file

: Full path and name of the file to write the data to.

list_data

: Data to be written to the specified output file.

delimiter

: Delimiter. The default value is `"|"`.

quote_char

: Quote character. The default value is `"|"`

quoting

: Quoting character. The default value is `"csv.QUOTE_MINIMAL"`.
  It can take one any of the following module constants:

  - `"csv.QUOTE_MINIMAL"` means only when required, for example, when a
    : field contains either the quote char or the delimiter
  - `"csv.QUOTE_ALL"` means that quotes are always placed around fields.
  - `"csv.QUOTE_NONNUMERIC"` means that quotes are always placed around
    : fields which do not parse as integers or floating point
      numbers.
  - `"csv.QUOTE_NONE"` means that quotes are never placed around fields.

# Return

bool

: `True` when successful, `False` when failed.

functions.name: ansys.aedt.core.generic.general_methods.active_sessions
documentation:
Get information for the active AEDT sessions.

# Parameters

version

: Version to check. The default is `None`, in which case all versions are checked.
  When specifying a version, you can use a three-digit format like `"222"` or a
  five-digit format like `"2022.2"`.

student_version : bool, optional
non_graphical : bool, optional

# Returns

dict

: {AEDT PID: port}
  If the PID corresponds to a COM session port is set to -1
@pyaedt_function_handler()
def com_active_sessions(version=None, student_version=False, non_graphical=False):
"""Get information for the active COM AEDT sessions.
Parameters
version : str, optional
Version to check. The default is ``None``, in which case all versions are checked.
When specifying a version, you can use a three-digit format like ``"222"`` or a
five-digit format like ``"2022.2"``.
student_version : bool, optional
Whether to check for student version sessions. The default is ``False``.
non_graphical : bool, optional
Whether to check only for active non-graphical sessions. The default is ``False``.
Returns
list
List of AEDT PIDs.
if student_version:
keys = ["ansysedtsv.exe"]
else:
keys = ["ansysedt.exe"]
long_version = None
if len(version) > 6:
version = version[-6:]
if version and "." in version:
long_version = version
version = version[-4:].replace(".", "")
if version < "221":
version = version[:2] + "." + version[2]
long_version = "20{}".format(version)
sessions = []
for p in psutil.process_iter():
try:
if p.name() in keys:
if long_version and _check_installed_version(os.path.dirname(p.exe()), long_version):
sessions.append(p.pid)
continue
cmd = p.cmdline()
if non_graphical and "-ng" in cmd or not non_graphical:
if not version or (version and version in cmd[0]):
sessions.append(p.pid)
except Exception:
pass
return sessions
@pyaedt_function_handler()
def grpc_active_sessions(version=None, student_version=False, non_graphical=False):
"""Get information for the active gRPC AEDT sessions.
Parameters
version : str, optional
Version to check. The default is ``None``, in which case all versions are checked.
When specifying a version, you can use a three-digit format like ``"222"`` or a
five-digit format like ``"2022.2"``.
student_version : bool, optional
Whether to check for student version sessions. The default is ``False``.
non_graphical : bool, optional
Whether to check only for active non-graphical sessions. The default is ``False``.
Returns
list
List of gRPC ports.
if student_version:
keys = ["ansysedtsv.exe", "ansysedtsv"]
else:
keys = ["ansysedt.exe", "ansysedt"]
if version and "." in version:
version = version[-4:].replace(".", "")
sessions = []
for p in psutil.process_iter():
try:
if p.name() in keys:
cmd = p.cmdline()
if "-grpcsrv" in cmd:
if non_graphical and "-ng" in cmd or not non_graphical:
if not version or (version and version in cmd[0]):
try:
sessions.append(
int(cmd[cmd.index("-grpcsrv") + 1]),
except (IndexError, ValueError):
# default desktop grpc port.
sessions.append(50051)
except Exception:
pass
return sessions
def active_sessions(version=None, student_version=False, non_graphical=False):
"""Get information for the active AEDT sessions.
Parameters
version : str, optional
Version to check. The default is ``None``, in which case all versions are checked.
When specifying a version, you can use a three-digit format like ``"222"`` or a
five-digit format like ``"2022.2"``.
student_version : bool, optional
non_graphical : bool, optional
Returns
list
List of tuple (AEDT PIDs, port).
if student_version:
keys = ["ansysedtsv.exe", "ansysedtsv"]
else:
keys = ["ansysedt.exe", "ansysedt"]
if version and "." in version:
version = version[-4:].replace(".", "")
if version and version < "222":
version = version[:2] + "." + version[2]
sessions = []
for p in psutil.process_iter():
try:
if p.name() in keys:
cmd = p.cmdline()
if non_graphical and "-ng" in cmd or not non_graphical:
if not version or (version and version in cmd[0]):
if "-grpcsrv" in cmd:
if not version or (version and version in cmd[0]):
try:
sessions.append(
p.pid,
int(cmd[cmd.index("-grpcsrv") + 1]),
except (IndexError, ValueError):
# default desktop grpc port.
sessions.append(
p.pid,
else:
sessions.append(
p.pid,
except Exception:
pass
return sessions

functions.name: ansys.aedt.core.generic.general_methods.conversion_function
documentation:
Convert input data based on a specified function string.

The available functions are:

- `"dB10"`: Converts the data to decibels using base 10 logarithm.
- `"dB20"`: Converts the data to decibels using base 20 logarithm.
- `"abs"`: Computes the absolute value of the data.
- `"real"`: Computes the real part of the data.
- `"imag"`: Computes the imaginary part of the data.
- `"norm"`: Normalizes the data to have values between 0 and 1.
- `"ang"`: Computes the phase angle of the data in radians.
- `"ang_deg"`: Computes the phase angle of the data in degrees.

If an invalid function string is specified, the method returns `False`.

# Parameters

data

: Numerical values to convert. The format can be `list` or `numpy.array`.

function

: Conversion function. The default is `"dB10"`.

# Returns

numpy.array or bool

: Converted data, `False` otherwise.

# Examples

```pycon
>>> values = [1, 2, 3, 4]
>>> conversion_function(values,"dB10")
array([-inf, 0., 4.77, 6.02])
```

```pycon
>>> conversion_function(values,"abs")
array([1, 2, 3, 4])
```

```pycon
>>> conversion_function(values,"ang_deg")
array([ 0., 0., 0., 0.])
```

functions.name: ansys.aedt.core.generic.general_methods.parse_excitation_file
documentation:
Parse a csv file and convert data in list that can be applied to Hfss and Hfss3dLayout sources.

# Parameters

input_file

: Full name of the input file.

is_time_domain

: Either if the input data is Time based or Frequency Based. Frequency based data are Mag/Phase (deg).

x_scale

: Scaling factor for x axis.

y_scale

: Scaling factor for y axis.

data_format

: Either `"Power"`, `"Current"` or `"Voltage"`.

impedance

: Excitation impedance. Default is `50`.

encoding

: Csv file encoding.

out_mag

: Output magnitude format. It can be `"Voltage"` or `"Power"` depending on Hfss solution.

window

: Fft window. Options are `"hamming"`, `"hanning"`, `"blackman"`, `"bartlett"` or `None`.

# Returns

tuple

: Frequency, magnitude and phase.

modules.name: ansys.aedt.core.generic.settings
documentation:
This module contains the `Settings` and `_InnerProjectSettings` classes.

The first class encapsulates the settings associated with PyAEDT and AEDT including logging,
LSF, environment variables and general settings. Most of the default values used can be modified
using a YAML configuration file. An example of such file can be found in the documentation, see
[Settings YAML file](https://aedt.docs.pyansys.com/version/stable/User_guide/settings.html).
The path to the configuration file should be specified with the environment variable
`PYAEDT_LOCAL_SETTINGS_PATH`. If no environment variable is set, the class will look for the
configuration file `pyaedt_settings.yaml` in the user's `APPDATA` folder for Windows and
`HOME` folder for Linux.

The second class is intended for internal use only and shouldn't be modified by users.

classes.name: ansys.aedt.core.generic.python_optimizers.GeneticAlgorithm

methods.name: ansys.aedt.core.generic.python_optimizers.GeneticAlgorithm.run
documentation: Implements the genetic algorithm

Genetic Algorithm for Python

Basic implementation of elitist genetic algorithm for solving problems with integers, continuous, boolean
or mixed variables.

# Parameters

function

: The Objective function to be minimized. This implementation minimizes the given objective function.

dim

: Number of variables

reference_file

: Reference file to create the cromosomes. If it is not specified, the function should create the cromose.

goal

: If after 'max_iteration_no_improv' iterations the goal is not improvedaf, the algorithm stops

var_type: str

: Type of the optimization variables. The default is 'bool'.
  Other options are: 'int' if all variables are integer, and 'real' if all variables are
  real value or continuous

boundaries: \<numpy array/None>

: By default is None. None if var_type is 'bool', otherwise provide an array of tuples
  of length two as boundaries for each variable, the length of the array must be equal dimension.
  For example, np.array([0,100],[0,200]) determines lower boundary 0 and upper boundary 100 for first
  and upper boundary 200 for second variable where dimension is 2.

var_type_mixed: \<numpy array/None> -

: By default is None. None if all variables have the same type, otherwise this can be used to specify the type of
  each variable separately.
  For example if the first variable is integer but the second one is real the input is:
  np.array(['int'],['real']). NOTE: it does not accept 'bool'. If variable type is Boolean use 'int' and provide
  a boundary as [0,1] in variable_boundaries.

function_timeout: float

: If the given function does not provide output before function_timeout (unit is seconds)
  the algorithm raise error.
  For example, when there is an infinite loop in the given function.

algorithm_parameters: dict
: Genetic algorithm parameters:

  : max_num_iteration : int
    population_size : int
    crossover_prob: float
    parents_portion: float
    crossover_type: string

    > The default is 'uniform'. Other options are 'one_point' or 'two_point'

    mutation_prob : float
    elite_ration : float
    max_iteration_no_improv: int

    > Successive iterations without improvement. If None it is ineffective

progress_bar: bool

: Show progress bar. The default is True.

# Examples

Optimize a defined function using a genetic algorithm.

\>>>import numpy as np
\>>>from ansys.aedt.core.generic.python_optimizers import GeneticAlgorithm as ga
\>>> def f(X):
\>>> return np.sum(X)
\>>>varbound = np.array(\[[0, 10]\] * 3)
\>>>model = ga(function=f, dimension=3, var_type='real', variable_boundaries=varbound)
\>>>model.run()

functions.name: ansys.aedt.core.common_rpc.pyaedt_service_manager
documentation:
Starts the PyAEDT service manager using RPyC server on CPython.

This method, which must run on a server machine, is used as a service on the
server machine to listen on a dedicated port for inbound requests to launch
a new server connection and launch AEDT.

# Parameters

port

: Port that the RPyC server is to listen on.

aedt_version

: Version of Aedt to instantiate with server. Default is latest available version installed on the machine.

student_version

: Either to initialize Student version AEDT or Commercial version.

# Examples

```pycon
>>> from ansys.aedt.core.common_rpc import pyaedt_service_manager
>>> pyaedt_service_manager()
```

functions.name: ansys.aedt.core.common_rpc.launch_server
documentation:
Starts an RPyC server and listens on a specified port.

This method must run on a server machine only.

# Parameters

port

: Port that the RPyC server is to listen on.

ansysem_path

: Full path to the AEDT installation directory. The default is
  `None`. This parameter is needed for IronPython on Linux
  connections only.

non_graphical

: Whether to start AEDT in non-graphical mode. The default is `False`,
  which means AEDT starts in graphical mode. This parameter is needed
  for IronPython on Linux connections only.

threaded : bool, optional

# Examples

```pycon
>>> from ansys.aedt.core.common_rpc import launch_server
>>> launch_server()
```

functions.name: ansys.aedt.core.common_rpc.create_session
documentation:
Connect to an existing AEDT server session and create a new client session from it.

Parameters
----------
server_name : str
    Name of the remote machine to connect to.
client_port : int
    Port that the RPyC server will run.
launch_aedt_on_server : bool, optional
    Either if the method has to start AEDT after the connection is established or not. Default is  `False`.
aedt_port : int, optional
    Aedt Grpc port on server.
non_graphical : bool, optional
    Aedt Non Graphical Flag.

Returns
-------
RPyC client object.

functions.name: ansys.aedt.core.common_rpc.connect
documentation:
Connect to an existing AEDT server session.

Parameters
----------
server_name : str
    Name of the remote machine to connect to.
aedt_client_port : int
    Port that the RPyC server is running on inside AEDT.

Returns
-------
RPyC client object.

classes.name: ansys.aedt.core.mechanical.Mechanical

methods.name: ansys.aedt.core.mechanical.Mechanical.assign_em_losses
documentation:
Map EM losses to a Mechanical design.

# Parameters

design

: Name of the design of the source mapping. The default is `"HFSSDesign1"`.

setup

: Name of the EM setup. The default is `"Setup1"`.

sweep

: Name of the EM sweep to use for the mapping. The default is `"LastAdaptive"`.

map_frequency

: Frequency to map. The default is `None`. The value must be `None` for
  Eigenmode analysis.

surface_objects

: List objects in the source that are metals. The default is `None`.

source_project_name

: Name of the source project. The default is `None`, in which case
  the source from the same project is used.

parameters

: List of all parameters in the EM to map. The default is `None`.

assignment

: The default is `None`.

# Returns

{class}`ansys.aedt.core.modules.boundary.BoundaryObject`

# References

```pycon
>>> oModule.AssignEMLoss
```

methods.name: ansys.aedt.core.mechanical.Mechanical.assign_thermal_map
documentation:
Map thermal losses to a Mechanical design.

:::{note}
This method works in 2021 R2 only when coupled with Icepak.

object_list : list

design

: Name of the design with the source mapping. The default is `"IcepakDesign1"`.

sweep

: Name of the EM sweep to use for the mapping. The default is `"SteadyState"`.

source_project_name

: Name of the source project. The default is `None`, in which case the
  source from the same project is used.

parameters

: List of all parameters in the EM to map. The default is `None`.

# Returns

{class}`aedt.modules.boundary.Boundary object`

: Boundary object.

# References

```pycon
>>> oModule.AssignThermalCondition
```

methods.name: ansys.aedt.core.mechanical.Mechanical.assign_2way_coupling
documentation:
Assign two-way coupling to a setup.

# Parameters

setup

: Name of the setup. The default is `None`, in which case the active setup is used.

number_of_iterations

: Number of iterations. The default is `2`.

# Returns

bool

: `True` when successful, `False` when failed.

# References

```pycon
>>> oModule.AddTwoWayCoupling
```

# Examples

```pycon
>>> from ansys.aedt.core import Mechanical
>>> mech = Mechanical()
>>> setup = mech.create_setup()
>>> mech.assign_2way_coupling(setup.name, 1)
>>> mech.release_desktop()
```

methods.name: ansys.aedt.core.mechanical.Mechanical.create_setup
documentation:
Create an analysis setup for Mechanical.

Optional arguments are passed along with `setup_type` and `name`. Keyword
names correspond to the `setup_type` corresponding to the native AEDT API. The list of
keywords here is not exhaustive.

# Parameters

name

: Name of the setup. The default is `"Setup1"`.

setup_type

: Type of the setup. Options are `"IcepakSteadyState"` and
  `"IcepakTransient"`. The default is `"IcepakSteadyState"`.

kwargs

: Available keys depend on the setup chosen.
  For more information, see {doc}`../SetupTemplatesMechanical`.

# Returns

{class}`ansys.aedt.core.modules.solve_setup.SetupHFSS`

: Solver Setup object.

# References

```pycon
>>> oModule.InsertSetup
```

# Examples

```pycon
>>> from ansys.aedt.core import Mechanical
>>> app = Mechanical()
>>> app.create_setup(name="Setup1",MaxModes=6)
```

Provides the Mechanical application interface.

# Parameters

project

: Name of the project to select or the full path to the project
  or AEDTZ archive to open. The default is `None`, in which
  case an attempt is made to get an active project. If no
  projects are present, an empty project is created.

design

: Name of the design to select. The default is `None`, in
  which case an attempt is made to get an active design. If no
  designs are present, an empty design is created.

solution_type

: Solution type to apply to the design. The default is
  `None`, in which case the default type is applied.

setup

: Name of the setup to use as the nominal. The default is
  `None`, in which case the active setup is used or
  nothing is used.

version

: Version of AEDT to use. The default is `None`, in which case
  the active version or latest installed version is used.
  This parameter is ignored when a script is launched within AEDT.
  Examples of input values are `232`, `23.2`,\`\`2023.2\`\`,\`\`"2023.2".

non_graphical

: Whether to launch AEDT in the non-graphical mode. The default
  is `False`, in which case AEDT is launched in the graphical mode.
  This parameter is ignored when a script is launched within AEDT.

new_desktop

: Whether to launch an instance of AEDT in a new thread, even if
  another instance of the `specified_version` is active on the
  machine. The default is `False`. This parameter is ignored when
  a script is launched within AEDT.

close_on_exit

: Whether to release AEDT on exit. The default is `False`.

student_version

: Whether to open the AEDT student version. The default is `False`.
  This parameter is ignored when a script is launched within AEDT.

machine

: Machine name to connect the oDesktop session to. Works only in 2022R2 and
  later. The remote server must be up and running with the command
  `"ansysedt.exe -grpcsrv portnum"`. If the machine is `"localhost"`,
  the server also starts if not present.

port

: Port number on which to start the oDesktop communication on an already existing server.
  This parameter is ignored when creating a new server. It works only in 2022 R2 or
  later. The remote server must be up and running with the command `"ansysedt.exe -grpcsrv portnum"`.

aedt_process_id

: Process ID for the instance of AEDT to point PyAEDT at. The default is
  `None`. This parameter is only used when `new_desktop = False`.

remove_lock

: Whether to remove lock to project before opening it or not.
  The default is `False`, which means to not unlock
  the existing project if needed and raise an exception.

# Examples

Create an instance of Mechanical and connect to an existing
HFSS design or create a new HFSS design if one does not exist.

```pycon
>>> from ansys.aedt.core import Mechanical
>>> aedtapp = Mechanical()
```

Create an instance of Mechanical and link to a project named
`"projectname"`. If this project does not exist, create one with
this name.

```pycon
>>> aedtapp = Mechanical(projectname)
```

Create an instance of Mechanical and link to a design named
`"designname"` in a project named `"projectname"`.

```pycon
>>> aedtapp = Mechanical(projectname,designame)
```

Create an instance of Mechanical and open the specified
project, which is named `"myfile.aedt"`.

```pycon
>>> aedtapp = Mechanical("myfile.aedt")
```

Create a `Desktop on 2023 R2` object and then create an
`Mechanical` object and open the specified project, which is
named `"myfile.aedt"`.

```pycon
>>> aedtapp = Mechanical(version=23.2, project="myfile.aedt")
```

methods.name: ansys.aedt.core.icepak.Icepak.problem_type
documentation:
Problem type of the Icepak design. Options are `"TemperatureAndFlow"`, `"TemperatureOnly"`,
and `"FlowOnly"`.
