methods.name: ansys.aedt.core.emit_core.results.revision.Revision.get_license_session
documentation:
Get a license session.

A license session can be started with checkout(), and ended with check in().
The `with` keyword can also be used, where checkout() is called on enter, and check in() is called on exit.

Avoids having to wait for license check in and checkout when doing many runs.

Examples
--------
with revision.get_license_session():
    domain = aedtapp.interaction_domain()
    revision.run(domain)

Provides the `Revision` object.

# Parameters

parent_results :

: `Results` object that this revision is associated with.

emit_obj :

: `Emit` object that this revision is associated with.

name

: Name of the revision to create. The default is `None`, in which
  case the name of the current design revision is used.

# Raises

RuntimeError if the name given is not the name of an existing result set and a current result set already exists.

# Examples

Create a `Revision` instance.

```pycon
>>> aedtapp = Emit()
>>> rev = Revision(results, aedtapp, "Revision 1")
>>> domain = aedtapp.interaction_domain()
>>> rev.run(domain)
```

methods.name: ansys.aedt.core.generic.configurations.Configurations._update_object_properties
documentation: @pyaedt_function_handler()
def _update_face_coordinate_systems(self, name, props):
update = False
for cs in self._app.modeler.coordinate_systems:
if cs.name == name:
if not self.options.skip_import_if_exists:
cs.props = props
cs.update()
update = True
if update:
return True
cs = FaceCoordinateSystem(self._app.modeler, props, name)
try:
cs._modeler.oeditor.CreateFaceCS(cs._face_parameters, cs._attributes)
cs._modeler.coordinate_systems.append(cs)
self._app.logger.info("Face Coordinate System {} added.".format(name))
except Exception:
self._app.logger.warning("Failed to add CS {} ".format(name))

methods.name: ansys.aedt.core.generic.configurations.Configurations._export_objects_properties
documentation: @pyaedt_function_handler()
def _export_face_coordinate_systems(self, dict_out):
if self._app.modeler.coordinate_systems:
dict_out["facecoordinatesystems"] = {}
for cs in self._app.modeler.coordinate_systems:
if isinstance(cs, FaceCoordinateSystem):
dict_out["facecoordinatesystems"][cs.name] = cs.props

config_file

: Full path to json file. If `None`, then the config file will be saved in working directory.

overwrite

: If `True` the json file will be overwritten if already existing.
  If `False` and the version is compatible, the data in the existing file will be updated.
  Default is `False`.

str

: Exported config file.
Enables export and import of a JSON configuration file that can be applied to a new or existing design.

documentation: Define C++ API DLL functions.

methods.name: ansys.aedt.core.filtersolutions_core.multiple_bands_table.MultipleBandsTable.row_count
documentation:
Total number of rows present in the multiple bands table.
The default is `2`.

# Returns

int

: Current number of rows in the multiple bands table.

methods.name: ansys.aedt.core.filtersolutions_core.multiple_bands_table.MultipleBandsTable.row
documentation:
Get the lower and upper frequency values for a row in the multiple bands table.

# Parameters

row_index: int

: Index of the row from to get the frequency values from.
  Valid values range from `0` to `6`, inclusive.

# Returns

> tuple
>
> : The tuple contains three strings.The first is the lower frequency value,
>   and the second is the upper frequency value.

methods.name: ansys.aedt.core.filtersolutions_core.multiple_bands_table.MultipleBandsTable.update_row
documentation:
Update lower and upper frequency values for a row in the multiple bands table.

# Parameters

row_index: int

: Index of the row to update. Valid values range from `0` to `6`, inclusive.

lower_frequency: str, optional

: New lower frequency value to set for the row.
  If this value is not provided, the row's lower frequency remains unchanged.

upper_frequency: str, optional

: New upper frequency value to set for the row.
  If this value is not provided, the row's upper frequency remains unchanged.

methods.name: ansys.aedt.core.filtersolutions_core.multiple_bands_table.MultipleBandsTable.append_row
documentation:
Append a new row with specified lower and upper frequency values to the end of the multiple bands table.

Parameters
----------
lower_frequency: str
    Lower frequency value for the new row.
upper_frequency: str
    Upper frequency value for the new row.

methods.name: ansys.aedt.core.filtersolutions_core.multiple_bands_table.MultipleBandsTable.insert_row
documentation:
Insert lower and upper frequencies in a given row.

# Parameters

row_index: int

: Index of the row. Valid values range from `0` to `6`, inclusive.

lower_frequency: str

: Lower frequency value to insert.

upper_frequency: str

: Upper frequency value to insert.

methods.name: ansys.aedt.core.filtersolutions_core.multiple_bands_table.MultipleBandsTable.remove_row
documentation:
Remove a row specified by its index from the multiple bands table.

# Parameters

row_index: int

: The index of the row to be removed from the multiple bands table.
  Valid values range from ``` 0``to ``6 ```, inclusive.

methods.name: ansys.aedt.core.filtersolutions_core.multiple_bands_table.MultipleBandsTable.clear_table
documentation: Remove all rows from the multiple bands table.

Manipulates access to the entries of multiple bands table.

This class allows you to enter, edit, or remove entries in the multiple bands table.
The table includes the lower and upper frequencies of the bands.
To access the multiple bands table, use the `multiple_bands_table` attribute of the `FilterSolutions` class.
A valid multiple bands table must include both lower and upper frequencies for each band.

classes.name: ansys.aedt.core.aedt_logger.MessageList
documentation:
Collects and returns messages from the AEDT message manager for a specified project name and design name.

Parameters
----------
msg_list : list
    List of messages extracted from AEDT.

Attributes
----------
info_level : list of str
    List of strings representing the info messages of the message manager.

warning_level : list of str
    List of strings representing the warning messages of the message manager.

error_level : list of str
    List of strings representing the error messages of the message manager.

debug_level : list of str
    List of strings representing the debug messages of the message manager.

unknown_level : list of str
    List of strings representing the messages with no level of the message manager.

classes.name: ansys.aedt.core.aedt_logger.AppFilter

methods.name: ansys.aedt.core.aedt_logger.AppFilter.filter
documentation:
Modify the record sent to the logger.

Parameters
----------
record : class:`logging.LogRecord`
    Contains information related to the event being logged.

Specifies the destination of the logger.

AEDT exposes three different loggers, which are the global, project, and design loggers.

# Parameters

destination

: Logger to write to. Options are ``` "Global"`, ``"Project" ```, and `"Design"`.
  The default is `"Global"`.

extra

: Name of the design or project. The default is `""`.

methods.name: ansys.aedt.core.aedt_logger.AedtLogger.get_messages
documentation:
Get the message manager content for a specified project and design.

If the specified project and design names are invalid, they are ignored.

Parameters
----------
project_name : str
    Name of the project to read messages from. Leave empty string to get Desktop level messages.
design_name : str
    Name of the design to read messages from. Leave empty string to get Desktop level messages.
level : int
    Level of messages to read. 0 – info and above, 1 – warning and above, 2 – error and fatal
aedt_messages : bool
    Read content of message manager even if logger is disabled.


Returns
-------
list of str
    List of messages for the specified project and design.

methods.name: ansys.aedt.core.aedt_logger.AedtLogger.add_error_message
documentation:
Add a type 2 "Error" message to the message manager tree.

Also add an error message to the logger if the handler is present.

# Parameters

message_text

: Text to display as the error message.

level

: Level to add the error message to. Options are `"Global"`,
  `"Project"`, and `"Design"`. The default is `None`,
  in which case the error message gets added to the `"Design"`
  level.

# Examples

Add an error message to the AEDT message manager.

```pycon
>>> hfss.logger.project_logger.error("Project Error Message", "Project")
```

methods.name: ansys.aedt.core.aedt_logger.AedtLogger.add_warning_message
documentation:
Add a type 1 "Warning" message to the message manager tree.

Also add a warning message to the logger if the handler is present.

# Parameters

message_text

: Text to display as the warning message.

level

: Level to add the warning message to. Options are `"Global"`,
  `"Project"`, and `"Design"`. The default is `None`,
  in which case the warning message gets added to the `"Design"`
  level.

# Examples

Add a warning message to the AEDT message manager.

```pycon
>>> hfss.logger.warning("Global warning message")
```

methods.name: ansys.aedt.core.aedt_logger.AedtLogger.add_info_message
documentation:
Add a type 0 "Info" message to the active design level of the message manager tree.

Also add an info message to the logger if the handler is present.

# Parameters

message_text

: Text to display as the info message.

level

: Level to add the info message to. Options are `"Global"`,
  `"Project"`, and `"Design"`. The default is `None`,
  in which case the info message gets added to the `"Design"`
  level.

# Examples

Add an info message at the global level.

```pycon
>>> hfss.logger.info("Global warning message", "Global")
```

methods.name: ansys.aedt.core.aedt_logger.AedtLogger.add_debug_message
documentation:
Parameterized message to the message manager to specify the type and project or design level.

# Parameters

message_text

: Text to display as the message.

level

: Level to add the info message to. Options are `"Global"`,
  `"Project"`, and `"Design"`. The default value is `None`,
  in which case the info message gets added to the `"Design"`
  level.

methods.name: ansys.aedt.core.aedt_logger.AedtLogger.add_message
documentation:
Add a message to the message manager to specify the type and project or design level.

# Parameters

message_type

: Type of the message. Options are:
  \* `0` : Info
  \* `1` : Warning
  \* `2` : Error
  \* `3` : Debug

message_text

: Text to display as the message.

level

: Level to add the message to. Options are `"Global"`,
  `"Project"`, and `"Design"`. The default is `None`,
  in which case the message gets added to the
  `"Design"` level.

proj_name

: Name of the project.

des_name

: Name of the design.

methods.name: ansys.aedt.core.aedt_logger.AedtLogger.clear_messages
documentation:
Clear all messages.

# Parameters

proj_name

: Name of project. The default is `None`, in which case messages
  are cleared for the current project. If blank, messages are cleared
  for all projects.

des_name

: Name of the design within the specified project. The default
  is `None,` in which case the current design is used.
  If blank, all designs are used.

level

: Level of the messages to clear. Options are:

  - `0` : Clear all info messages.
  - `1` : Clear all info and warning messages.
  - `2` : Clear all info, warning, and error messages.
  - `3` : Clear all messages, which include info, warning,
    error, and fatal-error messages.

  The default is `2.`

# Examples

Clear all messages in the current design and project.

```pycon
>>> hfss.clear_messages(level=3)
```

Clear all messages.

```pycon
>>> hfss.clear_messages(proj_name="", des_name="", level=3)
```

documentation: Design logger.

Specifies the logger to use for each AEDT logger.

This class allows you to add a handler to write messages to a file and to indicate
whether to write mnessages to the standard output (stdout).

# Parameters

level

: Logging level to filter the message severity allowed in the logger.
  The default is `logging.DEBUG`.

filename

: Name of the file to write messages to. The default is `None`.

to_stdout

: Whether to write log messages to stdout. The default is `False`.

methods.name: ansys.aedt.core.filtersolutions_core.transmission_zeros.TransmissionZeros.row
documentation:
Get the transmission zero ratio or bandwidth and the position of the element
causing the transmission zero from a row in the transmission zeros table.

# Parameters

row_index: int

: Index of the row. Valid values range from `0` to `9`, inclusive.

# Returns

tuple

: The tuple contains two strings.The first is the transmission zero ratio or bandwidth value,
  and the second is the position of the element causing the transmission zero.

methods.name: ansys.aedt.core.filtersolutions_core.transmission_zeros.TransmissionZeros.update_row
documentation:
Update the transmission zero ratio or bandwidth and its position
for a row in the transmission zeros table.

# Parameters

row_index: int

: Index of the row. Valid values range from `0` to `9`, inclusive.

zero: str, optional

: New transmission zero ratio or bandwidth value to set.
  If no value is specified, the value remains unchanged.

position: str, optional

: New position of the element causing the transmission zero in the circuit.
  If no value is specified, the value remains unchanged.

methods.name: ansys.aedt.core.filtersolutions_core.transmission_zeros.TransmissionZeros.append_row
documentation:
Append a new row that includes the ratio or bandwidth and position.

Parameters
----------
zero: str
    Transmission zero ratio or bandwidth value.
position: str
    Position of the element creating transmission zero in the associated circuit.

methods.name: ansys.aedt.core.filtersolutions_core.transmission_zeros.TransmissionZeros.insert_row
documentation:
Insert a new row that includes the ratio or bandwidth and the position.

# Parameters

row_index: int

: Index for the new row in the transmission zeros table. Valid values range from `0` to `9`, inclusive.

zero: str

: Transmission zero ratio or bandwidth value.

position: str

: Position of the element creating transmission zero in the associated circuit.

methods.name: ansys.aedt.core.filtersolutions_core.transmission_zeros.TransmissionZeros.remove_row
documentation:
Remove a row, including the ratio or bandwidth and the position.

# Parameters

row_index: int

: Row index in the transmission zeros table. Valid values range from `0` to `9`, inclusive.

methods.name: ansys.aedt.core.filtersolutions_core.transmission_zeros.TransmissionZeros.clear_table
documentation: Clear all entries in the transmission zeros table.

methods.name: ansys.aedt.core.filtersolutions_core.transmission_zeros.TransmissionZeros.restore_default_positions
documentation: Restore default positions of transmissison zeros.

Manipulates access to ratio and bandwidth entries in the tranmsission zeros table.

This class lets you to enter, edit, or remove ratio and bandwidth entries
in the tranmsission zeros table.
The table includes the ratio or bandwidth and the position of the element creating
the transmission zero in the associated circuit.
The position of the transmission zero is the position of the element in
the associated circuit that creates the transmission zero.
The position is defined automatically if no value is provided.

classes.name: ansys.aedt.core.filtersolutions_core.lumped_termination_impedance_table.ComplexTerminationDefinition
documentation:
Selects type of complex presentation.

**Attributes:**

- POLAR: Represents polar definition.
- CARTESIAN: Represents Cartesian definition.
- PARALLEL: Represents parallel definition with real entry parallel to imaginary entry.
- REAL: Represents only real impedance definition.

documentation:
Selects type of complex impedance as reactance, equivalent inductance, or equivalent capacitance.

**Attributes:**

- REAC: Represents pure reactance of complex impedance.
- IND: Represents equivalent inductance in henries.
- CAP: Represents equivalent capacitance in farads.

classes.name: ansys.aedt.core.filtersolutions_core.lumped_termination_impedance_table.TerminationType
documentation:
Selects either source or load complex impedance table.

**Attributes:**

- SOURCE: Represents source impedenace table.
- LOAD: Represents load impedenace table.

classes.name: ansys.aedt.core.filtersolutions_core.ideal_response.FrequencyResponseColumn
documentation:
Provides an enum of frequency response parameters.

**Attributes:**

- MAGNITUDE_DB: Represents the frequency response magnitude in dB.
- PHASE_DEG: Represents the frequency response phase in degree.
- GROUP_DELAY: Represents the frequency response group delay.
- PHASE_RAD: Represents the frequency response phase in radian.
- MAGNITUDE_ARITH: Represents the frequency response magnitude.
- MAGNITUDE_REAL: Represents the real part of frequency response magnitude.
- MAGNITUDE_IMAG: Represents the imaginary part of frequency response magnitude.
- PHASE_DEV_DEG: Represents the frequency response phase deviation in degrees.
- PHASE_DEV_RAD: Represents the frequency response phase deviation in radian.
- FREQUENCY: Represents frequency parameter of the frequency response .

classes.name: ansys.aedt.core.filtersolutions_core.ideal_response.TimeResponseColumn
documentation:
Provides an enum of time response parameters.

**Attributes:**

- STEP_RESPONSE: Represents the step time response.
- RAMP_RESPONSE: Represents the ramp time response.
- IMPULSE_RESPONSE: Represents the impulse time response.
- STEP_RESPONSE_DB: Represents the step time response in dB.
- RAMP_RESPONSE_DB: Represents the ramp time response in dB.
- IMPULSE_RESPONSE_DB: Represents the impulse time response in dB.
- TIME: Represents time parameter of the time response .

classes.name: ansys.aedt.core.filtersolutions_core.ideal_response.SParametersResponseColumn
documentation:
Provides an enum of S parameters.

**Attributes:**

- S21_DB: Represents the S21 parameter in dB.
- S11_DB: Represents the S11 parameter in dB.
- S21_ARITH: Represents the S21 parameter.
- S11_ARITH: Represents the S11 parameter.
- FREQUENCY: Represents the S parameters' frequency parameter.

classes.name: ansys.aedt.core.filtersolutions_core.ideal_response.PoleZerosResponseColumn
documentation:
Provides an enum of pole zero x and y coordinates of transmission (TX) or reflection (RX) zeros.

**Attributes:**

- TX_ZERO_DEN_X: Represents the x coordinate of the filter transmission zero denominator.
- TX_ZERO_DEN_Y: Represents the y coordinate of the filter transmission zero denominator.
- PROTO_TX_ZERO_DEN_X: Represents the x coordinate of the prototype filter transmission zero denominator.
- PROTO_TX_ZERO_DEN_Y: Represents the y coordinate of the prototype filter transmission zero denominator.
- TX_ZERO_NUM_X: Represents the x coordinate of the filter transmission zero numerator.
- TX_ZERO_NUM_Y: Represents the y coordinate of the filter transmission zero numerator.
- PROTO_TX_ZERO_NUM_X: Represents the x coordinate of the prototype filter transmission zero numerator.
- PROTO_TX_ZERO_NUM_Y: Represents the y coordinate of the prototype filter transmission zero numerator.
- RX_ZERO_DEN_X: Represents the x coordinate of the filter reflection zero denominator.
- RX_ZERO_DEN_Y: Represents the y coordinate of the filter reflection zero denominator.
- PROTO_RX_ZERO_DEN_X: Represents the x coordinate of the prototype filter reflection zero denominator.
- PROTO_RX_ZERO_DEN_Y: Represents the y coordinate of the prototype filter reflection zero denominator.
- RX_ZERO_NUM_X: Represents the x coordinate of the filter reflection zero numerator.
- RX_ZERO_NUM_Y: Represents the y coordinate of the filter reflection zero numerator.
- PROTO_RX_ZERO_NUM_X: Represents the x coordinate of the prototype filter reflection zero numerator.
- PROTO_RX_ZERO_NUM_Y: Represents the y coordinate of the prototype filter reflection zero numerator.

methods.name: ansys.aedt.core.filtersolutions_core.ideal_response.IdealResponse.frequency_response
documentation:
Get the ideal filter frequency response for the given parameters.

# Parameters

y_axis_parameter: `FrequencyResponseColumn`, optional

: Frequency response column to return. The default is the frequency response magnitude in dB.

minimum_frequency: str, optional

: Minimum frequency to set for the frequency response.
  If the minimum frequency is not given, the existing minimum frequency of the graph is used.

maximum_frequency: str, optional

: Maximum frequency to set for the frequency response.
  If the maximum frequency is not given, the existing maximum frequency of the graph is used.

vsg_analysis_enabled: bool, optional

: The default is `False`.

# Returns

tuple

: The tuple contains two lists of strings. The first is a list
  of the defined frequency ranges, and the second is a
  list of the requested parameters.

methods.name: ansys.aedt.core.filtersolutions_core.ideal_response.IdealResponse.time_response
documentation:
Get the ideal filter time response for the given parameters.

# Parameters

y_axis_parameter: `TimeResponseColumn`, optional

: Time response column to get. The default is the step time response.

minimum_time: str, optional

: Minimum time to set for the time response.
  If the minimum time is not given, the existing minimum time of the graph is used.

maximum_time: str, optional

: Maximum time to set for the time response.
  If the maximum time is not given, the existing maximum time of the graph is used.

vsg_analysis_enabled: bool, optional

: The default is `False`.

# Returns

tuple

: The tuple contains two lists of strings. The first is a list
  of the defined time ranges, and the second is a
  list of the requested parameters.

methods.name: ansys.aedt.core.filtersolutions_core.ideal_response.IdealResponse.s_parameters
documentation:
Get the ideal filter S parameters response for the given parameters.

# Parameters

y_axis_parameter: `SParametersResponseColumn`, optional

: S parameter's response column to get. The default is the S21 parameter response in dB.

minimum_frequency: str, optional

: Minimum frequency to set for the S parameters response.
  If the minimum frequency is not given, the existing minimum frequency of the graph is used.

maximum_frequency: str, optional

: Maximum frequency to set for the S parameters response.
  If the maximum frequency is not given, the existing maximum frequency of the graph is used.

vsg_analysis_enabled: bool, optional

: The default is `False`.

# Returns

tuple

: The tuple contains two lists of strings. The first is a list
  of the defined frequency ranges, and the second is a
  list of the requested parameters.

methods.name: ansys.aedt.core.filtersolutions_core.ideal_response.IdealResponse.pole_zero_locations
documentation:
Get the ideal pole zero location for the given parameters.

Parameters
----------
x_axis_parameter: `PoleZerosResponseColumn`, optional
    X axis parameter of the pole zeros response column to get. The default is the x coordinate
    of the filter transmission zero denominator.
y_axis_parameter: `PoleZerosResponseColumn`, optional
    Y axis parameter of the pole zeros response column to get. The default is the y coordinate
    of the filter transmission zero denominator.

Returns
-------
tuple
    The tuple contains two lists of strings. The first is a list
    of the x coordinates of the requested parameter, and the second is a
    list of the y coordinates of the requested parameter.

Returns the data for available ideal filter responses.

Types of responses Include `frequency`, `time`, `S parameters`, `transfer function`,
and `pole zero location`.

This class allows you to construct all the necessary ideal response attributes for the `FilterDesign` class.

methods.name: ansys.aedt.core.maxwell.Maxwell.change_symmetry_multiplier
documentation:
Set the design symmetry multiplier to a specified value.
The symmetry multiplier is automatically applied to all input quantities.

# Parameters

value

: Value to use as the Design Symmetry Multiplier coefficient. The default value is `1`.

# Returns

bool

: `True` when successful, `False` when failed.

# References

```pycon
>>> oDesign.SetDesignSettings
```

methods.name: ansys.aedt.core.maxwell.Maxwell.change_inductance_computation
documentation:
Enable the inductance computation for the transient analysis and set the incremental matrix.

# Parameters

compute_transient_inductance

: Whether to enable the inductance calculation for the transient analysis.
  The default is `True`.

incremental_matrix

: Whether to set the inductance calculation to `Incremental` if
  `compute_transient_inductance=True`. The default is `False`.

# Returns

bool

: `True` when successful, `False` when failed.

# References

```pycon
>>> oDesign.SetDesignSettings
```

methods.name: ansys.aedt.core.maxwell.Maxwell.apply_skew
documentation:
Apply skew to 2D model.

# Parameters

skew_type

: Skew type.
  Possible choices are `Continuous`, `Step`, `V-Shape`, `User Defined`.
  The default value is `Continuous`.

skew_part

: Part to skew.
  Possible choices are `Rotor` or `Stator`.
  The default value is `Rotor`.

skew_angle

: Skew angle.
  The default value is `1`.

skew_angle_unit

: Skew angle unit.
  Possible choices are `deg`, `rad`, `degsec`, `degmin`.
  The default value is `deg`.

number_of_slices

: Number of slices to split the selected part into.
  The default value is `2`.

custom_slices_skew_angles

: List of custom angles to apply to slices.
  Only available if skew_type is `User Defined`.
  The length of this list must be equal to number_of_slices.
  The default value is `None`.

# Returns

bool

: `True` when successful, `False` when failed.

methods.name: ansys.aedt.core.maxwell.Maxwell.set_core_losses
documentation:
Whether to enable core losses for a set of objects.

For `EddyCurrent` and `Transient` solver designs, core losses calculations
may be included in the simulation on any object that has a corresponding
core loss definition (with core loss coefficient settings) in the material library.

# Parameters

assignment

: List of object to apply core losses to.

core_loss_on_field

: Whether to enable `Consider core loss effect on field` for the given list. The default is
  `False`.

# Returns

bool

: `True` when successful, `False` when failed.

# References

```pycon
>>> oModule.SetCoreLoss
```

# Examples

Set core losses in Maxwell 3D.

```pycon
>>> from ansys.aedt.core import Maxwell3d
>>> m3d = Maxwell3d()
>>> m3d.set_core_losses(assignment=["PQ_Core_Bottom", "PQ_Core_Top"],core_loss_on_field=True)
>>> m3d.release_desktop(True, True)
```

methods.name: ansys.aedt.core.maxwell.Maxwell.assign_matrix
documentation:
Assign a matrix to the selection.

Matrix assignment can be calculated based upon the solver type.
For 2D/3D solvers the available solution types are: `Magnetostatic`,
`Electrostatic`, `Eddy Current`, `DC Conduction` and `AC Conduction`.

# Parameters

assignment

: List of sources to assign a matrix to.

matrix_name

: Name of the matrix. The default is `None`.

turns

: Number of turns. The default is 1.

return_path

: Return path. The default is `infinite`

group_sources

: Dictionary consisting of `{Group Name: list of source names}` to add
  multiple groups. You can also define a list of strings. The default is `None`.

branches

: Number of branches. The default is `None`, which indicates that only one
  branch exists.

# Returns

{class}`ansys.aedt.core.modules.boundary.BoundaryObject`

: Boundary object.

# References

```pycon
>>> oModule.AssignMatrix
```

# Examples

Set matrix in a Maxwell magnetostatic analysis.

```pycon
>>> from ansys.aedt.core import Maxwell2d
>>> m2d = Maxwell2d(solution_type="MagnetostaticXY",version="2022.1",close_on_exit=True)
>>> coil1 = m2d.modeler.create_rectangle([0, 1.5, 0], [8, 3], is_covered=True, name="Coil_1")
>>> coil2 = m2d.modeler.create_rectangle([8.5, 1.5, 0], [8, 3], is_covered=True, name="Coil_2")
>>> coil3 = m2d.modeler.create_rectangle([16, 1.5, 0], [8, 3], is_covered=True, name="Coil_3")
>>> coil4 = m2d.modeler.create_rectangle([32, 1.5, 0], [8, 3], is_covered=True, name="Coil_4")
>>> current1 = m2d.assign_current(assignment="Coil_1",amplitude=1,swap_direction=False,name="Current1")
>>> current2 = m2d.assign_current(assignment="Coil_2",amplitude=1,swap_direction=True,name="Current2")
>>> current3 = m2d.assign_current(assignment="Coil_3",amplitude=1,swap_direction=True,name="Current3")
>>> current4 = m2d.assign_current(assignment="Coil_4",amplitude=1,swap_direction=True,name="Current4")
>>> group_sources = {"Group1_Test": ["Current1", "Current3"], "Group2_Test": ["Current2", "Current4"]}
>>> selection = ['Current1', 'Current2', 'Current3', 'Current4']
>>> turns = [5, 1, 2, 3]
>>> L = m2d.assign_matrix(assignment=selection,matrix_name="Test2",turns=turns,group_sources=group_sources)
```

Set matrix in a Maxwell DC Conduction analysis.
\>>> m2d.assign_voltage(["Port1"],amplitude=1,name="1V")
\>>> m2d.assign_voltage(["Port2"],amplitude=0,name="0V")
\>>> m2d.assign_matrix(assignment=['1V'],matrix_name="Matrix1",group_sources=['0V'])
\>>> m2d.release_desktop(True, True)

methods.name: ansys.aedt.core.maxwell.Maxwell.setup_ctrlprog
documentation:
Configure the transient design setup to run a specific control program.
The control program is executed from a temporary directory that Maxwell creates for every setup run.

:::{deprecated} 0.6.71
:::

Use {func}`enable_control_program` method instead.

# Parameters

setupname

: Name of the setup.
  It will become the name of the Python file.

file_str

: Name of the file. The default value is `None`.

keep_modifications

: Whether to save the changes. The default value is `False`.

python_interpreter

: Python interpreter to use. The default value is `None`.

aedt_lib_dir

: Full path to the `pyaedt` directory. The default value is `None`.

# Returns

bool

: `True` when successful and `False` when failed.

methods.name: ansys.aedt.core.maxwell.Maxwell.eddy_effects_on
documentation:
Assign eddy effects on a list of objects.

For Eddy Current solvers only, you must specify the displacement current on the model objects.

# Parameters

assignment

: List of objects to assign eddy effects to.

enable_eddy_effects

: Whether to activate eddy effects. The default is `True`.

enable_displacement_current

: Whether to activate the displacement current. The default is `True`.
  Valid only for Eddy Current solvers.

# Returns

bool

: `True` when successful and `False` when failed.

# References

```pycon
>>> oModule.SetEddyEffect
```

methods.name: ansys.aedt.core.maxwell.Maxwell.setup_y_connection
documentation:
Set up the Y connection.

# Parameters

assignment

: List of windings. For example, `["PhaseA", "PhaseB", "PhaseC"]`.
  The default is `None`, in which case the design has no Y connection.

# Returns

bool

: `True` when successful and `False` when failed.

# References

```pycon
>>> oModule.SetupYConnection
```

# Examples

Set up the Y connection for three existing windings named `PhaseA`, `PhaseB`, and `PhaseC`.
This creates one `YConnection` group containing these three phases.

```pycon
>>> from ansys.aedt.core import Maxwell2d
>>> m2d = Maxwell2d("Motor_EM_R2019R3.aedt")
>>> m2d.set_active_design("Basis_Model_For_Test")
>>> m2d.setup_y_connection(["PhaseA", "PhaseB", "PhaseC"])
>>> m2d.release_desktop(True, True)
```

methods.name: ansys.aedt.core.maxwell.Maxwell.assign_current
documentation:
Assign the source of the current.

# Parameters

assignment

: List of objects to assign the current source to.

amplitude

: Current amplitude. The default is `1A`.

phase

: Current phase.
  The default is `"0deg"`.

solid

: Specifies the type of conductor, which can be solid or stranded.
  The default is `True`, which means the conductor is solid\`\`.
  When `False`, it means the conductor is stranded.

swap_direction

: Reference direction.
  The default is `False` which means that current is flowing inside the object.

name

: Name of the current excitation.
  The default is `None` in which case a generic name will be given.

# Returns

{class}`ansys.aedt.core.modules.boundary.BoundaryObject`

: Boundary object.

# References

```pycon
>>> oModule.AssignCurrent
```

# Examples

```pycon
>>> from ansys.aedt.core import Maxwell3d
>>> m3d = Maxwell3d(solution_type="ElectroDCConduction")
>>> cylinder= m3d.modeler.create_cylinder("X",[0,0,0],10,100,250)
>>> current = m3d.assign_current(cylinder.top_face_x.id,amplitude="2mA")
>>> m3d.release_desktop(True, True)
```

methods.name: ansys.aedt.core.maxwell.Maxwell.assign_translate_motion
documentation:
Assign a translation motion to an object container.

For both rotational and translational problems, the band objects must always enclose all the moving objects.

# Parameters

assignment

: Object container.

coordinate_system

: Coordinate system name. The default is `"Global"`.

axis

: Coordinate system axis. The default is `"Z"`.
  It can be a `ansys.aedt.core.generic.constants.AXIS` enumerator value.

positive_movement

: Whether movement is positive. The default is `True`.

start_position

: Starting position of the movement. The default is `0`. If a float
  value is used, default modeler units are applied.

periodic_translate

: Whether movement is periodic. The default is `False`.

negative_limit

: Negative limit of the movement. The default is `0`. If a float
  value is used, the default modeler units are applied.

positive_limit

: Positive limit of the movement. The default is `0`. If a float
  value is used, the default modeler units are applied.

velocity

: Initial velocity.
  The default is `0`. If a float value is used, "m_per_sec" units are applied.

mechanical_transient

: Whether to consider the mechanical movement. The default is `False`.

mass

: Mechanical mass. The default is `1`. If a float value is used, "Kg" units
  are applied.

damping

: Damping factor. The default is `0`.

load_force

: Load force is positive if it's applied in the same direction as the moving vector and negative
  in the opposite direction.
  The default is `0`. If a float value is used, "newton" units are applied.

motion_name

: Motion name. The default is `None`.

# Returns

{class}`ansys.aedt.core.modules.boundary.BoundaryObject` or `False`

: Boundary object or bool if not successful.

# References

```pycon
>>> oModule.AssignBand
```

methods.name: ansys.aedt.core.maxwell.Maxwell.assign_rotate_motion
documentation:
Assign a rotation motion to an object container.

For both rotational and translational problems, the band objects must always enclose all the moving objects.

# Parameters

assignment

: Object container.

coordinate_system

: Coordinate system name. The default is `"Global"`.

axis

: Coordinate system axis. The default is `"Z"`.
  It can be a `ansys.aedt.core.generic.constants.AXIS` enumerator value.

positive_movement

: Whether the movement is positive. The default is `True`.

start_position

: Starting position of the movement. The default is `0`. If a float value
  is used, "deg" units are applied.

has_rotation_limits

: Whether there is a limit in rotation. The default is `False`.

negative_limit

: Negative limit of the movement. The default is `0`. If a float value is
  used, "deg" units are applied.

positive_limit

: Positive limit of the movement. The default is `360`. If a float value is used,
  "deg" units are applied.

non_cylindrical

: Whether to consider non-cylindrical rotation. The default is `False`.

angular_velocity

: Movement velocity. The default is `"0rpm"`. If a float value is used,
  "rpm" units are applied.

mechanical_transient

: Whether to consider mechanical movement. The default is `False`.

inertia

: Mechanical inertia. The default is `1`.

damping

: Damping factor. The default is `0`.

load_torque

: Load torque sign is determined based on the moving vector, using the right-hand rule.
  The default is `"0NewtonMeter"`. If a float value is used "NewtonMeter" units are applied.

# Returns

{class}`ansys.aedt.core.modules.boundary.BoundaryObject`

: Boundary object.

# References

```pycon
>>> oModule.AssignBand
```

methods.name: ansys.aedt.core.maxwell.Maxwell.assign_voltage
documentation:
Assign a voltage source to a list of faces in Maxwell 3D or a list of objects in Maxwell 2D.

# Parameters

assignment

: List of faces or objects to assign a voltage source to.

amplitude

: Voltage amplitude in mV. The default is `1`.

name

: Name of the boundary. The default is `None`.

# Returns

{class}`ansys.aedt.core.modules.boundary.BoundaryObject`

: Boundary object.
  `False` when failed.

# References

```pycon
>>> oModule.AssignVoltage
```

methods.name: ansys.aedt.core.maxwell.Maxwell.assign_voltage_drop
documentation:
Assign a voltage drop across a list of faces to a specific value.

The voltage drop applies only to sheet objects.

# Parameters

assignment

: List of faces to assign a voltage drop to.

amplitude

: Voltage amplitude in mV. The default is `1`.

swap_direction

: Whether to swap the direction. The default value is `False`.

name

: Name of the boundary. The default is `None`.

# Returns

{class}`ansys.aedt.core.modules.boundary.BoundaryObject`

: Boundary object.
  `False` when failed.

# References

```pycon
>>> oModule.AssignVoltageDrop
```

methods.name: ansys.aedt.core.maxwell.Maxwell.assign_floating
documentation:
Assign floating excitation to model conductors at unknown potentials
and specify the total charge on the conductor.

# Parameters

assignment

: > {class}`ansys.aedt.core.modeler.elements_3d.FacePrimitive` or str

  List of objects or faces to assign the excitation to.

charge_value

: Charge value.
  If not provided, The default is `0`.

name

: Name of the excitation.
  If not provided, a random name with prefix "Floating" will be generated.

# Returns

{class}`ansys.aedt.core.modules.Boundary.BoundaryObject`

: Boundary object.
  `False` when failed.

# References

```pycon
>>> oModule.AssignFloating
```

# Examples

Assign a floating excitation for a Maxwell 2d Electrostatic design

```pycon
>>> from ansys.aedt.core import Maxwell2d
>>> m2d = Maxwell2d(version="2024.2")
>>> m2d.solution_type = SOLUTIONS.Maxwell2d.ElectroStaticXY
>>> rect = self.aedtapp.modeler.create_rectangle([0, 0, 0], [3, 1], name="Rectangle1")
>>> floating = self.aedtapp.assign_floating(assignment=rect, charge_value=3, name="floating_test")
>>> m2d.release_desktop(True, True)
```

Assign a floating excitation for a Maxwell 3d Electrostatic design providing an object
\>>> from ansys.aedt.core import Maxwell3d
\>>> m3d = Maxwell3d(version="2024.2")
\>>> m3d.solution_type = SOLUTIONS.Maxwell3d.ElectroStatic
\>>> box = self.aedtapp.modeler.create_box([0, 0, 0], [10, 10, 10], name="Box1")
\>>> floating = self.aedtapp.assign_floating(assignment=box, charge_value=3)
Assign a floating excitation providing a list of faces
\>>> floating1 = self.aedtapp.assign_floating(assignment=\[box.faces[0], box.faces[1]\], charge_value=3)
\>>> m3d.release_desktop(True, True)

methods.name: ansys.aedt.core.maxwell.Maxwell.assign_winding
documentation:
Assign a winding to a Maxwell design.

# Parameters

assignment

: List of faces to create the coil terminal on.
  The default is `None`.

winding_type

: Type of the winding. Options are `"Current"`, `"Voltage"`,
  and `"External"`. The default is `"Current"`.

is_solid

: Whether the winding is the solid type. The default is `True`. If `False`,
  the winding is the stranded type.

current

: Value of the current in amperes. The default is `1`.

resistance

: Resistance in ohms. The default is `0`.

inductance

: Inductance in Henry (H). The default is `0`.

voltage

: Voltage value. The default is `0`.

parallel_branches

: Number of parallel branches. The default is `1`.

phase

: Value of the phase delay in degrees. The default is `0`.

name

: Name of the boundary. The default is `None`.

# Returns

{class}`ansys.aedt.core.modules.boundary.BoundaryObject`

: Bounding object for the winding, otherwise only the bounding object.
  `False` when failed.

# References

```pycon
>>> oModule.AssignWindingGroup
```

methods.name: ansys.aedt.core.maxwell.Maxwell.add_winding_coils
documentation:
Add coils to the winding.

# Parameters

assignment

: Name of the winding.

coils

: List of the coil names.

# Returns

bool

: `True` when successful, `False` when failed.

# References

```pycon
>>> oModule.AddWindingTerminals
>>> oModule.AddWindingCoils
```

methods.name: ansys.aedt.core.maxwell.Maxwell.assign_coil
documentation:
Assign coils to a list of objects or face IDs.

# Parameters

assignment

: List of objects or face IDs.

conductors_number

: Number of conductors. The default is `1`.

polarity

: Type of the polarity. The default is `"Positive"`.

name

: The default is `None`.

# Returns

{class}`ansys.aedt.core.modules.boundary.BoundaryObject`

: Bounding object for the winding, otherwise only the bounding object.
  `False` when failed.

# References

```pycon
>>> oModule.AssignCoil
```

methods.name: ansys.aedt.core.maxwell.Maxwell.assign_force
documentation:
Assign a force to one or more objects.

Force assignment can be calculated based upon the solver type.
For 3D solvers the available solution types are: `Magnetostatic`,
`Electrostatic`, `Eddy Current`, `Transient` and `Electric Transient`.
For 2D solvers the available solution types are: `Magnetostatic`,
`Electrostatic`, `Eddy Current` and `Transient`.

# Parameters

assignment

: One or more objects to assign the force to.

coordinate_system

: Name of the reference coordinate system. The default is `"Global"`.

is_virtual

: Whether the force is virtual. The default is `True.`

force_name

: Name of the force. The default is `None`, in which case the default
  name is used.

# Returns

bool

: `True` when successful, `False` when failed.

# References

```pycon
>>> oModule.AssignForce
```

# Examples

Assign virtual force to a magnetic object:

```pycon
>>> from ansys.aedt.core import Maxwell3d
>>> m3d = Maxwell3d()
>>> iron_object = m3d.modeler.create_box([0, 0, 0],[2, 10, 10],name="iron")
>>> magnet_object = m3d.modeler.create_box([10, 0, 0],[2, 10, 10],name="magnet")
>>> m3d.assign_material(iron_object,"iron")
>>> m3d.assign_material(magnet_object,"NdFe30")
>>> m3d.assign_force("iron",is_virtual=True,force_name="force_iron")
```

Assign Lorentz force to a conductor:

```pycon
>>> conductor1 = m3d.modeler.create_box([0, 0, 0],[1, 1, 10],name="conductor1")
>>> conductor2 = m3d.modeler.create_box([10, 0, 0],[1, 1, 10],name="conductor2")
>>> m3d.assign_material(conductor1,"copper")
>>> m3d.assign_material(conductor2,"copper")
>>> m3d.assign_force("conductor1",is_virtual=False,force_name="force_copper") # conductor, use Lorentz force
>>> m3d.release_desktop(True, True)
```

methods.name: ansys.aedt.core.maxwell.Maxwell.assign_torque
documentation:
Assign a torque to one or more objects.

Torque assignment can be calculated based upon the solver type.
For 3D solvers the available solution types are: `Magnetostatic`,
`Electrostatic`, `Eddy Current`, `Transient` and `Electric Transient`.
For 2D solvers the available solution types are: `Magnetostatic`,
`Electrostatic`, `Eddy Current` and `Transient`.

# Parameters

assignment

: One or more objects to assign the torque to.

coordinate_system

: Name of the reference coordinate system. The default is `"Global"`.

is_positive

: Whether the torque is positive. The default is `True`.

is_virtual

: Whether the torque is virtual. The default is `True`.

axis

: Axis to apply the torque to. The default is `"Z"`.

torque_name

: Name of the torque. The default is `None`, in which
  case the default name is used.

# Returns

bool

: `True` when successful, `False` when failed.

# References

```pycon
>>> oModule.AssignTorque
```

methods.name: ansys.aedt.core.maxwell.Maxwell.solve_inside
documentation:
Solve inside to generate a solution inside an object.

With this method, Maxwell will create a mesh inside the object and generate the solution from the mesh.

# Parameters

name

: Name of the object to generate the solution into.

activate

: The default value is `True`.

# Returns

bool

: `True` when successful, `False` when failed.

# References

```pycon
>>> oEditor.ChangeProperty
```

methods.name: ansys.aedt.core.maxwell.Maxwell.analyze_from_zero
documentation:
Force the next solve to start from time 0 for a given setup.

This method applies only to the Transient solution type.

# Returns

bool

: `True` when successful, `False` when failed.

# References

```pycon
>>> oModule.ResetSetupToTimeZero
```

methods.name: ansys.aedt.core.maxwell.Maxwell.set_initial_angle
documentation:
Set the initial angle.

# Parameters

motion_setup

: Name of the motion setup.

angle

: Value of the angle in degrees.

# Returns

bool

: `True` when successful, `False` when failed.

# References

```pycon
>>> oDesign.ChangeProperty
```

methods.name: ansys.aedt.core.maxwell.Maxwell.assign_symmetry
documentation:
Assign symmetry boundary.

This boundary condition defines a plane of geometric or magnetic symmetry in a structure.
Assign it only to the outer surfaces of the problem region.

# Parameters

assignment

: List IDs or {class}`ansys.aedt.core.modeler.elements_3d.EdgePrimitive` or
  {class}`ansys.aedt.core.modeler.elements_3d.FacePrimitive`.

symmetry_name

: Name of the symmetry.

is_odd

: Type of the symmetry. The default is ``` True`,` in which case the H field
  is tangential to the boundary. If ``False ```, the H field is normal to
  the boundary.

# Returns

bool

: `True` when successful, `False` when failed.

# References

```pycon
>>> oModule.AssignSymmetry
```

methods.name: ansys.aedt.core.maxwell.Maxwell.assign_current_density
documentation:
Assign current density to a single or list of entities.

This method specifies the x-, y-, and z-components of the current density in a conduction path.

# Parameters

assignment

: Objects to assign the current to.

current_density_name

: Current density name.
  If no name is provided a random name is generated.

phase

: Current density phase.
  Available units are 'deg', 'degmin', 'degsec' and 'rad'.
  Default value is 0deg.

current_density_x

: Current density X coordinate value.
  Default value is 0 A/m2.

current_density_y

: Current density Y coordinate value.
  Default value is 0 A/m2.

current_density_z

: Current density Z coordinate value.
  Default value is 0 A/m2.

current_density_2d

: Current density 2D value.
  Default value is 0 A/m2.

coordinate_system

: Coordinate system name.
  Default value is 'Global'.

coordinate_system_type

: Coordinate system cartesian.
  Possible values can be `"Cartesian"`, `"Cylindrical"`, and `"Spherical"`.
  Default value is `"Cartesian"`.

# Returns

bool

: `True` when successful, `False` when failed.

methods.name: ansys.aedt.core.maxwell.Maxwell.assign_radiation
documentation:
Assign radiation boundary to one or more objects.

Radiation assignment can be calculated based upon the solver type.
Available solution type is: `Eddy Current`.

# Parameters

assignment

: One or more objects to assign the radiation to.

radiation

: Name of the force. The default is `None`, in which case the default
  name is used.

# Returns

{class}`ansys.aedt.core.modules.boundary.BoundaryObject`

: Radiation objects. If the method fails to execute it returns `False`.

# References

```pycon
>>> oModule.Radiation
```

# Examples

Assign radiation boundary to one box and one face:

```pycon
>>> from ansys.aedt.core import Maxwell3d
>>> m3d = Maxwell3d()
>>> box1 = m3d.modeler.create_box([0, 0, 0],[2, 10, 10])
>>> box2 = m3d.modeler.create_box([10, 0, 0],[2, 10, 10])
>>> m3d.assign_radiation([box1, box2.faces[0]])
>>> m3d.release_desktop(True, True)
```

methods.name: ansys.aedt.core.maxwell.Maxwell.enable_harmonic_force
documentation:
Enable the harmonic force calculation for the transient analysis.

# Parameters

assignment

: List of object names for force calculations.

force_type

: Force type. Options are `0` for objects, `1` for surface, and `2` for volumetric.

window_function

: Windowing function. Default is `"Rectangular"`.
  Available options are: `"Rectangular"`, `"Tri"`, `"Van Hann"`, `"Hamming"`,
  `"Blackman"`, `"Lanczos"`, `"Welch"`.

use_number_of_last_cycles

: Use number of last cycles for force calculations. Default is `True`.

last_cycles_number

: Defines the number of cycles to compute if `use_number_of_last_cycle` is `True`.

calculate_force

: How to calculate force. The default is `"Harmonic"`.
  Options are `"Harmonic"` and `"Transient"`.

# Returns

bool

: `True` when successful, `False` when failed.

methods.name: ansys.aedt.core.maxwell.Maxwell.enable_harmonic_force_on_layout_component
documentation:
Enable the harmonic force calculation for the transient analysis.

# Parameters

assignment

: Name of layout component to apply harmonic forces to.

nets

: Dictionary containing nets and layers to enable harmonic forces on.

force_type

: Force Type. `0` for Objects, `1` for Surface, `2` for volumetric.

window_function

: Windowing function. Default is `"Rectangular"`.
  Available options are: `"Rectangular"`, `"Tri"`, `"Van Hann"`, `"Hamming"`,
  `"Blackman"`, `"Lanczos"`, `"Welch"`.

use_number_of_last_cycles

: Use number Of last cycles for force calculations. Default is `True`.

last_cycles_number

: Defines the number of cycles to compute if `use_number_of_last_cycle` is `True`.

calculate_force

: How to calculate force. The default is `"Harmonic"`.
  Options are `"Harmonic"` and `"Transient"`.

start_time

: Harmonic Force Start Time. Default is `"0s"`.

stop_time

: Harmonic Force Stop Time. Default is `"2ms"`.

use_number_of_cycles_for_stop_time

: Use number of cycles for force stop time calculations. Default is `True`.

number_of_cycles_for_stop_time

: Number of cycles for force stop time calculations. Default is `1`.

include_no_layer

: Whether to include `"<no-layer>"` layer or not (used for vias). Default is `True`.

# Returns

bool

: `True` when successful, `False` when failed.

methods.name: ansys.aedt.core.maxwell.Maxwell.export_element_based_harmonic_force
documentation:
Export an element-based harmonic force data to a .csv file.

# Parameters

output_directory

: Path for the output directory. If `None` pyaedt working dir will be used.

setup

: Name of the solution setup. If `None`, the nominal setup is used.

start_frequency

: When a float is entered the Start-Stop Frequency approach is used.

stop_frequency

: When a float is entered, the Start-Stop Frequency approach is used.

number_of_frequency

: When a number is entered, the number of frequencies approach is used.

# Returns

str

: Path to the export directory.

methods.name: ansys.aedt.core.maxwell.Maxwell.create_external_circuit
documentation:
Create the external circuit including all the windings of type `External` in the Maxwell design.

# Parameters

circuit_design

: Name of the created circuit design.
  If not provided the design name + `_ckt` is used.

# Returns

{class}`ansys.aedt.core.maxwellcircuit.MaxwellCircuit`

: MaxwellCircuit object if successful, `False` otherwise.

# Examples

```pycon
>>> from ansys.aedt.core import Maxwell2d
>>> m2d = Maxwell2d()
>>> m2d.modeler.create_circle([0, 0, 0], 10, name="Coil1")
>>> m2d.assign_coil(assignment=["Coil1"])
>>> m2d.assign_winding(assignment=["Coil1"], winding_type="External", name="Winding1")
>>> cir = m2d.create_external_circuit()
>>> m2d.release_desktop(True, True)
```

methods.name: ansys.aedt.core.maxwell.Maxwell.edit_external_circuit
documentation:
Edit the external circuit for the winding and allow editing of the circuit parameters.

# Parameters

netlist_file_path

: Path to the circuit netlist file.

schematic_design_name

: Name of the schematic design.

parameters

: Name and value of the circuit parameters.
  Parameters must be provided as a dictionary, where the key is the parameter name
  and the value is the parameter value.
  If the dictionary is provided, the `netlist_file_path` parameter is automatically
  set to an empty string.
  The default is `None`.

# Returns

bool

: `True` when successful, `False` when failed.

methods.name: ansys.aedt.core.maxwell.Maxwell.create_setup
documentation:
Create an analysis setup for Maxwell 3D or 2D.

Optional arguments are passed using the `setup_type` and `name`
parameters.
Keyword names correspond to the `setuptype` corresponding to the native AEDT API.
The list of keywords here is not exhaustive.

# Parameters

setup_type

: Type of the setup. Depending on the solution type, options are
  `"AC Conduction"`, `"DC Conduction"`, `"EddyCurrent"`,
  `"Electric Transient"`, `"Electrostatic"`, `"Magnetostatic"`,
  and `Transient"`.

name

: Name of the setup. The default is `"Setup1"`.

kwargs

: Available keys depend on the setup chosen.
  For more information, see {doc}`../SetupTemplatesMaxwell`.

# Returns

{class}`ansys.aedt.core.modules.solve_setup.SetupMaxwell`

: 3D Solver Setup object.

# References

```pycon
>>> oModule.InsertSetup
```

# Examples

```pycon
>>> from ansys.aedt.core import Maxwell3d
>>> m3d = Maxwell3d()
>>> m3d.create_setup(name="My_Setup",setup_type="EddyCurrent",MaximumPasses=10,PercentError=2)
>>> m3d.release_desktop(True, True)
```

methods.name: ansys.aedt.core.maxwell.Maxwell3d.assign_insulating
documentation:
Create an insulating boundary condition.

This boundary condition is used to model very thin sheets of perfectly insulating material between
touching conductors. Current cannot cross an insulating boundary.

# Parameters

assignment

: Objects or faces to apply the insulating boundary to.

insulation

: Name of the insulation. The default is `None`, in which case a unique name is assigned.

# Returns

{class}`ansys.aedt.core.modules.boundary.BoundaryObject`

: Boundary object if successful, `False` otherwise.

# References

```pycon
>>> oModule.AssignInsulating
```

# Examples

Create a box and assign insulating boundary to it.

```pycon
>>> from ansys.aedt.core import Maxwell3d
>>> m3d = Maxwell3d()
>>> insulated_box = m3d.modeler.create_box([50, 0, 50],[294, 294, 19],name="InsulatedBox")
>>> insulating_assignment = m3d.assign_insulating(assignment=insulated_box,insulation="InsulatingExample")
>>> m3d.release_desktop(True, True)
```

methods.name: ansys.aedt.core.maxwell.Maxwell3d.assign_impedance
documentation:
Create an impedance boundary condition for Transient or Eddy Current solvers.

This boundary condition is used to simulate the effect of induced currents in a conductor without
explicitly computing them.

# Parameters

assignment

: Faces or objects to apply the impedance boundary to.

material_name

: Material name. The default is `None`. If other than `None`, material properties values are extracted
  from the named material in the list of materials available. The default value is `None`.

permeability

: Permeability of the material.The default value is `0.0`.

conductivity

: Conductivity of the material. The default value is `None`.

non_linear_permeability

: If the option `material_name` is activated, the permeability can either be linear or not.
  The default value is `False`.

impedance

: Name of the impedance. The default is `None`, in which case a unique name is assigned.

# Returns

{class}`ansys.aedt.core.modules.boundary.BoundaryObject`

: Boundary object if successful, `False` otherwise.

# References

```pycon
>>> oModule.AssignImpedance
```

# Examples

Create a box and assign impedance boundary to the faces.

```pycon
>>> from ansys.aedt.core import Maxwell3d
>>> m3d = Maxwell3d()
>>> shield = m3d.modeler.create_box([-50, -50, -50],[294, 294, 19],name="shield")
>>> shield_faces = m3d.modeler.select_allfaces_fromobjects(["shield"])
>>> impedance_assignment = m3d.assign_impedance(assignment=shield_faces,impedance="ShieldImpedance")
>>> m3d.release_desktop(True, True)
```

methods.name: ansys.aedt.core.maxwell.Maxwell3d.assign_master_slave
documentation:
Assign dependent and independent boundary conditions to two faces of the same object.

# Parameters

independent

: ID of the master entity.

dependent

: ID of the slave entity.

u_vector_origin_coordinates_master

: Master's list of U vector origin coordinates.

u_vector_pos_coordinates_master

: Master's list of U vector position coordinates.

u_vector_origin_coordinates_slave

: Slave's list of U vector origin coordinates.

u_vector_pos_coordinates_slave

: Slave's list of U vector position coordinates.

reverse_master

: Whether to reverse the master edge to the V direction. The default is `False`.

reverse_slave

: Whether to reverse the master edge to the U direction. The default is `False`.

same_as_master

: Whether the B-Field of the slave edge and master edge are the same. The default is `True`.

bound_name

: Name of the master boundary. The default is `None`, in which case the default name
  is used. The name of the slave boundary has a `_dep` suffix.

# Returns

{class}`ansys.aedt.core.modules.boundary.BoundaryObject`,
{class}`ansys.aedt.core.modules.boundary.BoundaryObject`

> Master and slave objects. If the method fails to execute it returns `False`.

# References

```pycon
>>> oModule.AssignIndependent
>>> oModule.AssignDependent
```

methods.name: ansys.aedt.core.maxwell.Maxwell3d.assign_flux_tangential
documentation:
Assign a flux tangential boundary for a transient A-Phi solver.

# Parameters

assignment

: List of objects to assign the flux tangential boundary condition to.

flux_name

: Name of the flux tangential boundary. The default is `None`,
  in which case a random name is automatically generated.

# Returns

{class}`ansys.aedt.core.modules.boundary.BoundaryObject`

: Boundary object if successful, `False` otherwise.

# References

```pycon
>>> oModule.AssignFluxTangential
```

# Examples

Create a box and assign a flux tangential boundary to one of its faces.

```pycon
>>> from ansys.aedt.core import Maxwell3d
>>> m3d = Maxwell3d()
>>> box = m3d.modeler.create_box([50, 0, 50],[294, 294, 19],name="Box")
>>> flux_tangential = m3d.assign_flux_tangential(box.faces[0],"FluxExample")
>>> m3d.release_desktop(True, True)
```

methods.name: ansys.aedt.core.maxwell.Maxwell3d.assign_layout_force
documentation:
Assign the layout force to a component in a Transient A-Phi solver.
To access layout component features the Beta option has to be enabled first.

# Parameters

net_layers

: Each \<net, layer> pair represents the objects in the intersection of the corresponding net and layer.
  The layer name is from the list of layer names. The net name is the dictionary's key.

component_name

: Name of the 3D component to assign the layout force to.

coordinate_system

: Reference coordinate system.
  If not provided, the global one is used.

force_name

: Name of the layout force.
  If not provided a random name will be generated.

include_no_layer

: Whether to include `"<no-layer>"` layer or not (used for vias). Default is `True`.

# Returns

bool

: `True` when successful, `False` when failed.

# References

```pycon
>>> oModule.AssignLayoutForce
```

# Examples

Create a dictionary to give as an input to assign_layout_force method.
\>>> nets_layers = {"\<no-net>": ["PWR","TOP","UNNAMED_000","UNNAMED_002"],
\>>> "GND": ["LYR_1","LYR_2","UNNAMED_006"]}
\>>>

Assign layout force to a component.
\>>> from ansys.aedt.core import Maxwell3d
\>>> m3d = Maxwell3d()
\>>> m3d.assign_layout_force(net_layers=nets_layers,component_name="LC1_1")
\>>> m3d.release_desktop(True, True)

methods.name: ansys.aedt.core.maxwell.Maxwell3d.assign_tangential_h_field
documentation:
Assign a tangential H field boundary to a list of faces.

# Parameters

assignment

: List of objects to assign an end connection to.

x_component_real

: X component value real part. The default is `0`.

x_component_imag

: X component value imaginary part. The default is `0`.

y_component_real

: Y component value real part. The default is `0`.

y_component_imag

: Y component value imaginary part. The default is `0`.

coordinate_system

: Coordinate system to use for the UV vector.

origin

: Origin of the UV vector.
  The default is None\`, in which case the bottom left vertex is used.

u_pos

: Direction of the U vector.
  The default is `None`, in which case the top left vertex is used.

reverse

: Whether the vector is reversed. The default is `False`.

bound_name

: Name of the end connection boundary.
  The default is `None`, in which case the default name is used.

# Returns

{class}`ansys.aedt.core.modules.boundary.BoundaryObject`

: Newly created object when successful, `False` when failed.

# References

```pycon
>>> oModule.AssignTangentialHField
```

methods.name: ansys.aedt.core.maxwell.Maxwell3d.assign_resistive_sheet
documentation:
Assign a resistive sheet boundary between two conductors.

Available for Maxwell 3D Magnetostatic, Eddy Current and Transient designs.
For 3D Magnetostatic designs, the user can specify the nonlinear anode and cathode coefficients.
To understand the nonlinear relationship used by AEDT between the conductivity and current density,
please refer to Maxwell Help guide.

# Parameters

assignment

: List of objects to assign an end connection to.

resistance

: Resistance value with unit.
  For 3D Magnetostatic designs if non_linear is `True`, it is not available.
  The default is `1ohm`.

name

: Name of the boundary. The default is `None`, in which case the default name is used.

non_linear: bool, optional

: Whether the boundary is non-linear. The default is `False`.
  Valid for 3D Magnetostatic designs only.

anode_a

: Anode a value that corresponds to the a coefficient in the non-linear relationship
  between conductivity and current density.
  The default value is `"300000000"`.

anode_b

: Anode b value that corresponds to the b coefficient in the non-linear relationship
  between conductivity and current density.
  The default value is `"10"`.

anode_c

: Anode c value that corresponds to the c coefficient in the non-linear relationship
  between conductivity and current density.
  The default value is `"110000000000000"`.

anode_d

: Anode d value that corresponds to the d coefficient in the non-linear relationship
  between conductivity and current density.
  The default value is `"2"`.

cathode_a

: Cathode a value that corresponds to the a coefficient in the non-linear relationship
  between conductivity and current density.
  The default value is `"300000000"`.

cathode_b

: Cathode b value that corresponds to the b coefficient in the non-linear relationship
  between conductivity and current density.
  The default value is `"10"`.

cathode_c

: Cathode c value that corresponds to the c coefficient in the non-linear relationship
  between conductivity and current density.
  The default value is `"110000000000000"`.

cathode_d

: Cathode d value that corresponds to the d coefficient in the non-linear relationship
  between conductivity and current density.
  The default value is `"2"`.

# Returns

{class}`ansys.aedt.core.modules.boundary.BoundaryObject`

: Newly created object. `False` if it fails.

# References

```pycon
>>> oModule.AssignResistiveSheet
```

# Examples

```pycon
>>> import ansys.aedt.core
>>> from ansys.aedt.core.generic.constants import SOLUTIONS
>>> m3d = ansys.aedt.core.Maxwell3d(solution_type="Transient")
>>> my_box = m3d.modeler.create_box(origin=[0, 0, 0], sizes=[0.4, -1, 0.8], material="copper")
>>> resistive_face = my_box.faces[0]
>>> bound = self.aedtapp.assign_resistive_sheet(assignment=resistive_face, resistance="3ohm")
>>> self.aedtapp.solution_type = SOLUTIONS.Maxwell3d.Magnetostatic
>>> bound = self.aedtapp.assign_resistive_sheet(assignment=resistive_face, non_linear=True)
>>> m3d.release_desktop()
```

Provides the Maxwell 3D app interface.

This class allows you to connect to an existing Maxwell 3D design or create a
new Maxwell 3D design if one does not exist.

# Parameters

project

: Name of the project to select or the full path to the project
  or AEDTZ archive to open. The default is `None`, in which
  case an attempt is made to get an active project. If no
  projects are present, an empty project is created.

design

: Name of the design to select. The default is `None`, in
  which case an attempt is made to get an active design. If no
  designs are present, an empty design is created.

solution_type

: Solution type to apply to the design. The default is
  `None`, in which case the default type is applied.

setup

: Name of the setup to use as the nominal. The default is
  `None`, in which case the active setup is used or
  nothing is used.

version

: Version of AEDT to use. The default is `None`, in which case
  the active version or latest installed version is used. This
  parameter is ignored when a script is launched within AEDT.
  Examples of input values are `232`, `23.2`,\`\`2023.2\`\`,\`\`"2023.2".

non_graphical

: Whether to launch AEDT in non-graphical mode. The default
  is `False`, in which case AEDT is launched in graphical
  mode. This parameter is ignored when a script is launched within
  AEDT.

new_desktop

: Whether to launch an instance of AEDT in a new thread, even if
  another instance of the `specified_version` is active on the
  machine. The default is `False`. This parameter is ignored
  when a script is launched within AEDT.

close_on_exit

: Whether to release AEDT on exit. The default is `False`.

student_version

: Whether to open the AEDT student version. The default is
  `False`. This parameter is ignored when a script is launched
  within AEDT.

machine

: Machine name to connect the oDesktop session to. This works only in 2022 R2
  or later. The remote server must be up and running with the command
  `"ansysedt.exe -grpcsrv portnum"`. If the machine is `"localhost"`, the
  server also starts if not present.

port

: Port number on which to start the oDesktop communication on an already existing server.
  This parameter is ignored when a new server is created. It works only in 2022 R2 or later.
  The remote server must be up and running with the command `"ansysedt.exe -grpcsrv portnum"`.

aedt_process_id

: Process ID for the instance of AEDT to point PyAEDT at. The default is
  `None`. This parameter is only used when `new_desktop = False`.

remove_lock

: Whether to remove lock to project before opening it or not.
  The default is `False`, which means to not unlock
  the existing project if needed and raise an exception.

# Examples

Create an instance of Maxwell 3D and open the specified
project, which is named `mymaxwell.aedt`.

```pycon
>>> from ansys.aedt.core import Maxwell3d
>>> m3d = Maxwell3d("mymaxwell.aedt")
PyAEDT INFO: Added design ...
```

Create an instance of Maxwell 3D using the 2024 R1 release and open
the specified project, which is named `mymaxwell2.aedt`.

```pycon
>>> m3d = Maxwell3d(version="2024.2", project="mymaxwell2.aedt")
PyAEDT INFO: Added design ...
```

methods.name: ansys.aedt.core.maxwell.Maxwell2d.assign_balloon
documentation:
Assign a balloon boundary to a list of edges.

# Parameters


# Examples

```pycon
>>> from ansys.aedt.core import Maxwell2d

>>> region_id = m2d.modeler.create_region()
```

methods.name: ansys.aedt.core.maxwell.Maxwell2d.assign_vector_potential

```pycon
>>> from ansys.aedt.core import Maxwell2d

>>> region_id = m2d.modeler.create_region()
>>> region_edges = region_id.edges
```

methods.name: ansys.aedt.core.maxwell.Maxwell2d.assign_end_connection
documentation:


Provides the Maxwell 2D app interface.

This class allows you to connect to an existing Maxwell 2D design or create a
new Maxwell 2D design if one does not exist.

# Parameters

project

: Name of the project to select or the full path to the project
  or AEDTZ archive to open. The default is `None`, in which
  case an attempt is made to get an active project. If no
  projects are present, an empty project is created.

design

: Name of the design to select. The default is `None`, in
  which case an attempt is made to get an active design. If no
  designs are present, an empty design is created.

solution_type

: Solution type to apply to the design. The default is
  `None`, in which case the default type is applied.

setup

: Name of the setup to use as the nominal. The default is
  `None`, in which case the active setup is used or
  nothing is used.

version

: Version of AEDT to use. The default is `None`, in which case
  the active version or latest installed version is used.
  This parameter is ignored when a script is launched within AEDT.
  Examples of input values are `232`, `23.2`,\`\`2023.2\`\`,\`\`"2023.2".

non_graphical

: Whether to launch AEDT in non-graphical mode. The default
  is `False`, in which case AEDT is launched in graphical mode.
  This parameter is ignored when a script is launched within AEDT.

new_desktop

: Whether to launch an instance of AEDT in a new thread, even if
  another instance of the `specified_version` is active on the
  machine. The default is `False`. This parameter is ignored when
  a script is launched within AEDT.

close_on_exit

: Whether to release AEDT on exit. The default is `False`.

student_version

: Whether to open the AEDT student version. The default is `False`.
  This parameter is ignored when a script is launched within AEDT.

machine

: Machine name to connect the oDesktop session to. This works only in 2022 R2
  or later. The remote server must be up and running with the command
  `"ansysedt.exe -grpcsrv portnum"`. If the machine is `"localhost"`,
  the server also starts if not present.

port

: Port number of which to start the oDesktop communication on an already existing
  server. This parameter is ignored when creating a new server. It works only in 2022
  R2 or later. The remote server must be up and running with the command `"ansysedt.exe -grpcsrv portnum"`.

aedt_process_id

: Process ID for the instance of AEDT to point PyAEDT at. The default is
  `None`. This parameter is only used when `new_desktop = False`.

remove_lock

: Whether to remove lock to project before opening it or not.
  The default is `False`, which means to not unlock
  the existing project if needed and raise an exception.

# Examples

Create an instance of Maxwell 2D and connect to an existing
Maxwell 2D design or create a new Maxwell 2D design if one does
not exist.

```pycon
>>> from ansys.aedt.core import Maxwell2d
>>> m2d = Maxwell2d()
```

Create an instance of Maxwell 2D and link to a project named
`projectname`. If this project does not exist, create one with
this name.

```pycon
>>> m2d = Maxwell2d(projectname)
```

Create an instance of Maxwell 2D and link to a design named
`designname` in a project named `projectname`.

```pycon
>>> m2d = Maxwell2d(projectname,designname)
```

classes.name: ansys.aedt.core.filtersolutions_core.optimization_goals_table.OptimizationGoalParameter
documentation:
Enumeration of optimization goals parameters table.

**Attributes:**

- LOWER_FREQUENCY: Represents the lower frequency parameter, positioned
as the first value in the optimization goal table.
- UPPER_FREQUENCY: Represents the upper frequency parameter, positioned
as the second value in the optimization goal table.
- GOAL_VALUE: Represents the goal value parameter, positioned
as the third value in the optimization goal table.
- CONDITION: Represents the condition parameter, positioned
as the fourth value in the optimization goal table.
- PARAMETER_NAME: Represents the name of the parameter, positioned
as the fifth value in the optimization goal table.
- WEIGHT: Represents the weight parameter, positioned
as the sixth value in the optimization goal table.
- ENABLED: Represents the status of using the goal parameters, positioned
as the seventh value in the optimization goal table.

methods.name: ansys.aedt.core.filtersolutions_core.optimization_goals_table.OptimizationGoalsTable.row
documentation:
Get the values for one row of the optimization goals table.
The values are returned as a list: [value1, value2, ..., value7].

# Parameters

row_index: int

: Index of the row. Valid values range from `0` to `49`, inclusive.

# Returns

list

: A list of strings representing the row parameters.

methods.name: ansys.aedt.core.filtersolutions_core.optimization_goals_table.OptimizationGoalsTable.update_row
documentation:
Update the row parameters for an existing row in the optimization goals table.

# Parameters

row_index: int

: Index of the row. Valid values range from `0` to `49`, inclusive.

lower_frequency: str, optional

: New lower frequency value to set.
  If no value is specified, the value remains unchanged.

upper_frequency: str, optional

: New upper frequency value to set.
  If no value is specified, the value remains unchanged.

goal_value: str, optional

: New goal value to set.
  If no value is specified, the value remains unchanged.

condition: str, optional

: New condition value to set.
  If no value is specified, the value remains unchanged.

parameter_name: str, optional

: New parameter name value to set.
  If no value is specified, the value remains unchanged.

weight: str, optional

: New weight value to set.
  If no value is specified, the value remains unchanged.

enabled: str, optional

: New enabled value to set.
  If no value is specified, the value remains unchanged.

methods.name: ansys.aedt.core.filtersolutions_core.optimization_goals_table.OptimizationGoalsTable.clear_goal_entries
documentation: Clear the goal entries from optimization goals table.

Provides management of optimization goals within a table structure.

This class offers functionality to add, update, or delete entries in the optimization goals table,
facilitating the manipulation of optimization parameters for simulation tasks.
Each entry in the table can specify a range of parameters including lower and upper frequency limits,
a target value for the optimization goal, a condition that defines how the goal is evaluated,
the name of the parameter to optimize, the weight of the goal in the overall optimization process,
and whether the goal is active or not.

methods.name: ansys.aedt.core.rpc.rpyc_services.PyaedtServiceWindows.exposed_edb
documentation:
Starts a new Hfss session.

# Parameters

edbpath

: Full path to the `aedb` folder. The variable can also contain
  the path to a layout to import. Allowed formarts are BRD,
  XML (IPC2581), GDS, and DXF. The default is `None`.

cellname

: Name of the cell to select. The default is `None`.

isreadonly

: Whether to open `edb_core` in read-only mode. The default is `False`.

edbversion

: Version of `edb_core` to use. The default is `"2021.2"`.

# Returns

{class}`ansys.aedt.core.edb.Edb`

methods.name: ansys.aedt.core.rpc.rpyc_services.PyaedtServiceWindows.exposed_hfss
documentation:
Starts a new Hfss session.

# Parameters

project

: Name of the project to select or the full path to the project
  or AEDTZ archive to open. The default is `None`, in which
  case an attempt is made to get an active project. If no
  projects are present, an empty project is created.

design

: Name of the design to select. The default is `None`, in
  which case an attempt is made to get an active design. If no
  designs are present, an empty design is created.

solution_type

: Solution type to apply to the design. The default is
  `None`, in which case the default type is applied.

setup

: Name of the setup to use as the nominal. The default is
  `None`, in which case the active setup is used or
  nothing is used.

version

: Version of AEDT to use. The default is `None`, in which case
  the active version or latest installed version is used.

non_graphical

: Whether to launch AEDT in the non-graphical mode. The default
  is\`\`True\`\`, in which case AEDT is launched in the non graphical mode.

# Returns

{class}`ansys.aedt.core.hfss.Hfss`

methods.name: ansys.aedt.core.rpc.rpyc_services.GlobalService.edb
documentation:
Starts a new EDB Session.

# Parameters

edbpath

: Full path to the `aedb` folder. The variable can also contain
  the path to a layout to import. Allowed formats are BRD,
  XML (IPC2581), GDS, and DXF. The default is `None`.
  For GDS import, the Ansys control file, which is also XML, should have the same
  name as the GDS file. Only the extensions of the two files should differ.

cellname

: Name of the cell to select. The default is `None`.

isreadonly

: Whether to open `edb_core` in read-only mode when it is
  owned by HFSS 3D Layout. The default is `False`.

edbversion

: Version of `edb_core` to use. The default is `None`, in which case
  the latest installed version is used.

isaedtowned

: Whether to launch `edb_core` from HFSS 3D Layout. The
  default is `False`.

oproject

: Reference to the AEDT project object. The default is `None`.

student_version

: Whether to open the AEDT student version. The default is `False.`

use_ppe

: Whether to use PPE licensing. The default is `False`.

# Returns

{class}`ansys.aedt.core.edb.Edb`

: Edb class.
Global class to manage rpyc Server of PyAEDT.
